[[[128, 78], [388, 78], [388, 110], [128, 110]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 189], [428, 189], [428, 216], [130, 216]], '7.2.4 REPLACE statement']
[[[130, 242], [318, 242], [318, 268], [130, 268]], '7.2.4.1 General']
[[[130, 294], [720, 294], [720, 324], [130, 324]], 'The REPLACE statement modifies text in a compilation group:']
[[[130, 348], [402, 348], [402, 374], [130, 374]], '7.2.4.2 General format']
[[[129, 399], [340, 399], [340, 430], [129, 430]], 'Format 1 (replacing):']
[[[399, 502], [604, 502], [604, 532], [399, 532]], 'pseudo-text-1 == BY']
[[[639, 503], [807, 503], [807, 534], [639, 534]], 'pseudo-text-2 ==']
[[[144, 550], [330, 550], [330, 576], [144, 576]], 'REPLACE [ALSO ]']
[[[396, 566], [498, 566], [498, 625], [396, 625]], 'LEADING TRAILING']
[[[553, 578], [766, 578], [766, 610], [553, 610]], 'partial-word-1 == BY']
[[[799, 580], [977, 580], [977, 608], [799, 608]], 'partial-word-2 2=']
[[[130, 694], [276, 694], [276, 720], [130, 720]], 'Format 2 (offj:']
[[[132, 750], [358, 750], [358, 778], [132, 778]], 'REPLACE LAST ] OFF']
[[[130, 812], [368, 812], [368, 840], [130, 840]], '7.24.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 862], [1090, 862], [1090, 920], [130, 920]], '1) REPLACE statement may be  specified   anywhere in source text or in  library text that character-string or a separator; other than the closing delimiter ofa literal, may appear:']
[[[130, 942], [1092, 942], [1092, 1002], [130, 1002]], '2) REPLACE statement shall be preceded by a space except when it is the first statement in compilation group.']
[[[130, 1022], [1112, 1022], [1112, 1077], [130, 1077]], '3) Pseudo-text-1 shall contain one or more text-words, atleast one ofwhich shall be neither a separator comma nor a separator semicolon:']
[[[130, 1102], [718, 1102], [718, 1128], [130, 1128]], '4) Pseudo-text-2 shall contain zero, one,0r more text-words']
[[[130, 1154], [599, 1154], [599, 1181], [130, 1181]], '5) Partial-word-1 shall consist of one text-word.']
[[[130, 1208], [672, 1208], [672, 1234], [130, 1234]], '6) Partial-word-2 shall consist of zero or one text-word:']
[[[130, 1257], [1111, 1257], [1111, 1314], [130, 1314]], '7) An alphanumeric, boolean, or national literal shall not be specified as partial-word-1 or partial-word-2.']
[[[130, 1336], [1112, 1336], [1112, 1390], [130, 1390]], '8) Character-strings within pseudo-text-1 and pseudo-text-2 may be continued in accordance with the rules of reference format:']
[[[130, 1415], [1006, 1415], [1006, 1446], [130, 1446]], '9) The length ofa text-word within pseudo-text shall be from through 65,535 characters.']
[[[130, 1550], [160, 1550], [160, 1576], [130, 1576]], '50']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 81 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[163, 185], [1143, 185], [1143, 244], [163, 244]], '10) Compiler directive lines shall not be specified within pseudo-text-1, pseudo-text-2, partial-word-1, or partial-word-2 _']
[[[164, 268], [416, 268], [416, 294], [164, 294]], '7.24.4 General rules']
[[[164, 321], [1146, 321], [1146, 379], [164, 379]], "1) In subsequent general rules of the REPLACE statement; 'source text' refers to the conditionally- processed compilation group"]
[[[164, 397], [810, 397], [810, 429], [164, 429]], '2) Pseudo-text-1 specifies the text to be replaced by pseudo-text-2']
[[[164, 452], [826, 452], [826, 482], [164, 482]], '3) Partial-word-1 specifies the text to be replaced by partial-word-2.']
[[[164, 506], [906, 506], [906, 532], [164, 532]], '4) Once encountered, a format 1 REPLACE statement has one of three states:']
[[[200, 556], [1146, 556], [1146, 614], [200, 614]], 'a) active, meaning it is the current statement in use for replace processing for the compilation group;']
[[[200, 634], [1148, 634], [1148, 719], [200, 719]], 'b) inactive, meaning it is not currently in use for replace processing but is held in a last-in first-out queue, from which it may be popped and made active or canceled in accordance with the rules for subsequent REPLACE statements encountered in the compilation group;']
[[[203, 740], [1146, 740], [1146, 827], [203, 827]], 'c) canceled, meaning it is removed from use for replace processing for the remainder of the compilation group Or, if inactive, it is removed from the queue of inactive statements for the remainder of the compilation group:']
[[[164, 846], [1146, 846], [1146, 904], [164, 904]], '5) A REPLACE statementthat is placed in the active state remains active untilit is placed in the inactive state, it is canceled, or the end of the compilation group is reached, whichever occurs first:']
[[[167, 928], [742, 928], [742, 953], [167, 953]], '6) When there is no REPLACE statement in the active state:']
[[[201, 977], [1146, 977], [1146, 1036], [201, 1036]], 'a) A format 1 REPLACE statement is placed in the active state at the point = atwhich it is encountered in the compilation group. The ALSO phrase, if specified, has no effect:']
[[[200, 1058], [666, 1058], [666, 1088], [200, 1088]], 'b) A format 2 REPLACE statement has no effect:']
[[[166, 1112], [728, 1112], [728, 1138], [166, 1138]], '7) When there is a REPLACE statement in the active state:']
[[[201, 1162], [978, 1162], [978, 1192], [201, 1192]], 'a) A format 1 REPLACE statement with the ALSO phrase results in the following:']
[[[272, 1216], [1146, 1216], [1146, 1268], [272, 1268]], 'the active REPLACE statement is made inactive and is pushed into the queue of inactive REPLACE statements_']
[[[272, 1293], [1146, 1293], [1146, 1403], [272, 1403]], 'The current REPLACE statement is expanded into a single REPLACE statement; without the ALSO phrase, having as its operands all the operands of the current statement followed by the operands of the most recent statement pushed into the queue of inactive REPLACE statements The expanded REPLACE statement is placed in the active state_']
[[[924, 1549], [1076, 1549], [1076, 1578], [924, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1116, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1116, 1576]], '51']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 82 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[166, 186], [1112, 186], [1112, 270], [166, 270]], 'b) A format 1 REPLACE statement without the ALSO phrase cancels the active REPLACE statement and cancels any REPLACE statements in the queue of inactive REPLACE statements Then the current REPLACE statement is placed in the active state']
[[[167, 295], [1112, 295], [1112, 379], [167, 379]], 'c) A format 2 REPLACE statement with the LAST phrase cancels the active REPLACE statement and pops the last statement that was pushed into the queue of inactive REPLACE statements, if any The popped statement; if any, is placed in the active state.']
[[[166, 399], [1112, 399], [1112, 454], [166, 454]], 'd) A format 2 REPLACE statement without the LAST phrase cancels the active REPLACE statement and cancels all REPLACE statements in the queue of inactive REPLACE statements, if any.']
[[[130, 477], [1112, 477], [1112, 534], [130, 534]], '8) The   comparison   operation to determine text  replacement begins with the text   immediately following the REPLACE statement and occurs in the following manner:']
[[[166, 556], [1112, 556], [1112, 636], [166, 636]], 'a) Starting with the leftmost source text-word and the first pseudo-text-1 or partial-word-1, pseudo-text-1 or partial-word-1 is compared to an equivalent number of contiguous source text-words:']
[[[166, 664], [1114, 664], [1114, 902], [166, 902]], 'b) Pseudo-text-1 matches the source text if,and only if, the ordered sequence of text-words that forms pseudo-text-1 is equal, character for character; to the ordered sequence of source text-words. When the LEADING phrase is specified, partial-word-1 matches the source text- word only ifthe contiguous sequence of characters that forms partial-word-1 is equal, character for character; to an equal number of contiguous characters starting with the leftmost character position of that source text-word. When the TRAILING phrase is specified, partial-word-1 matches the source text-word only if the contiguous sequence of characters that forms partial- word-1 is equal, character for character, to an equal number of contiguous characters ending with the rightmost character position of that source text-word_']
[[[167, 924], [724, 924], [724, 956], [167, 956]], 'c) The following rules apply for the purpose of matching:']
[[[238, 977], [1112, 977], [1112, 1062], [238, 1062]], 'Each occurrence ofa separator comma, semicolon, or space in pseudo-text-1 or in the source text is considered to be a single space. Each sequence of one or more space separators is considered to be a single space:']
[[[240, 1085], [1016, 1085], [1016, 1113], [240, 1113]], 'Each operand and operator ofa concatenation expression is a separate text-word.']
[[[202, 1136], [1112, 1136], [1112, 1245], [202, 1245]], '3. Except when used in the non-hexadecimal formats of alphanumeric and national literals, each alphanumeric character is equivalent to its corresponding national character and each lowercase letter is equivalent to its corresponding uppercase letter, as specified for the COBOL character repertoire in 8.1.3, COBOL character repertoire']
[[[238, 1268], [704, 1268], [704, 1298], [238, 1298]], 'For alphanumeric; boolean, and national literals:']
[[[273, 1319], [1112, 1319], [1112, 1402], [273, 1402]], 'The two representations of the quotation symbol match when specified in the opening and  closing   delimiters of the literal and those delimiters shall be  in the same representation:']
[[[130, 1552], [158, 1552], [158, 1576], [130, 1576]], '52']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 83 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[308, 190], [1146, 190], [1146, 268], [308, 268]], 'In the content of the literal, two contiguous occurrences of the character used as the quotation symbol in the opening delimiter are treated as single occurrence of that character:']
[[[274, 295], [950, 295], [950, 324], [274, 324]], 'Each occurrence ofa compiler directive line is treated as a single space_']
[[[273, 347], [722, 347], [722, 377], [273, 377]], 'Comments, ifany, are treated as a single space:']
[[[274, 397], [1038, 397], [1038, 426], [274, 426]], 'NOTE 1 Because comments are removed during logical conversion, none are expected.']
[[[202, 448], [1148, 448], [1148, 530], [202, 530]], 'd) If no match occurs, the comparison is repeated with   each next  successive occurrence of pseudo-text-1 or partial-word-1, until either a match is found or there is no next successive occurrence of pseudo-text-1 or partial-word-1.']
[[[203, 552], [1146, 552], [1146, 662], [203, 662]], 'e) When all occurrences ofpseudo-text-1 or partial-word-1 have been compared and no match has occurred; the next successive source text-word is then considered as the leftmost source text-word, and the comparison cycle starts again with the first occurrence of pseudo-text-1 or partial-word-1.']
[[[201, 683], [1146, 683], [1146, 872], [201, 872]], '0) When match occurs between   pseudo-text-1 and the source text, the corresponding pseudo-text-2 replaces the matched text in the source text When match occurs between partial-word-1 and the source text-word, the matched characters of that source text-word are either replaced by partial-word-2 or deleted when partial-word-2 consists of zero text-words The source text-word immediately following the rightmost text-word that participated in the match is then considered as the leftmost source text-word: The comparison cycle starts again with the first occurrence of pseudo-text-1 or partial-word-1.']
[[[200, 896], [1146, 896], [1146, 982], [200, 982]], 'g) The comparison operation continues until the rightmost text-word in the source text that is within the scope of the REPLACE statement has either participated in match or been considered as a leftmost source text-word and participated in a complete comparison cycle:']
[[[164, 999], [1146, 999], [1146, 1055], [164, 1055]], '9) The text produced asa result of processing a REPLACE statement shall not contain a COPY statement; REPLACE statement; a SOURCE FORMAT directive, a comment; or a blank line']
[[[164, 1080], [1146, 1080], [1146, 1214], [164, 1214]], '10) The text that results from the processing of a REPLACE statement shall be in logical free-form reference format: Text-words inserted into the resultant text as a result of processing a REPLACE statement are placed in accordance with the rules of free-form reference format: When inserting text-words of pseudo-text-2 into the resultant text; additional spaces may be introduced only between text-words where there already exists a space or a space is assumed.']
[[[200, 1236], [688, 1236], [688, 1264], [200, 1264]], 'NOTE 2 A space is assumed at the end of a source line:']
[[[924, 1549], [1076, 1549], [1076, 1578], [924, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1116, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1116, 1576]], '53']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 84 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [168, 190], [168, 216], [130, 216]], '73']
[[[202, 190], [428, 190], [428, 218], [202, 218]], 'Compiler directives']
[[[130, 242], [298, 242], [298, 268], [130, 268]], '73.1 General']
[[[130, 294], [1094, 294], [1094, 348], [130, 348]], 'Compiler directives specify options for use by the compiler, define compilation-variables,and control conditional compilation_']
[[[130, 374], [382, 374], [382, 400], [130, 400]], '7.3.2 General format']
[[[129, 428], [358, 428], [358, 461], [129, 461]], '>>compiler-instruction']
[[[130, 486], [348, 486], [348, 514], [130, 514]], '733 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 534], [1113, 534], [1113, 592], [130, 592]], '1) compiler directive shall be specified on one line, except for the EVALUATE and the IF directives for which specific rules are specified.']
[[[130, 618], [156, 618], [156, 644], [130, 644]], '2)']
[[[183, 616], [952, 616], [952, 647], [183, 647]], 'compiler directive shall be preceded only by zero, one, or more space characters_']
[[[130, 668], [1112, 668], [1112, 724], [130, 724]], '3) When the reference format is fixed-form, a compiler directive shall be written in the program-text area and may be followed only by space characters and an optional inline comment_']
[[[130, 748], [1113, 748], [1113, 804], [130, 804]], '4) When the reference format is free-form, compiler directive may be followed only by space characters and an optional inline comment']
[[[130, 826], [1113, 826], [1113, 910], [130, 910]], '5) compiler directive is composed of the compiler directive indicator, optionally followed by the COBOL character space; followed by compiler-instruction. The compiler directive indicator shall be treated as though it were followed by a space ifno space is specified after the indicator_']
[[[130, 930], [1110, 930], [1110, 1014], [130, 1014]], '6) Compiler-instruction is composed of compiler-directive words, system-names, and user-defined words as specified in the syntax of each directive: Compiler-directive words are identified in 8.12, Compiler-directive words:']
[[[130, 1034], [1113, 1034], [1113, 1092], [130, 1092]], '7) When compiler-directive word is specified in the general format of a compiler directive, that compiler-directive word is reserved within the context of that directive_']
[[[130, 1113], [1114, 1113], [1114, 1172], [130, 1172]], '8) compiler directive may be specified anywhere in a compilation group, in source text or in library text; except']
[[[166, 1195], [772, 1195], [772, 1224], [166, 1224]], 'a) as restricted by the rules for the specific compiler directive,']
[[[166, 1250], [634, 1250], [634, 1278], [166, 1278]], 'b) within a source text manipulation statement;']
[[[166, 1301], [680, 1301], [680, 1330], [166, 1330]], 'c) between the lines ofa continued character-string:']
[[[130, 1354], [1112, 1354], [1112, 1408], [130, 1408]], "9) The compiler-directive word 'IMP' is reserved for use by the implementor If the implementor defines the IMP directive, the syntax rules for that directive shall be implementor-defined."]
[[[130, 1552], [158, 1552], [158, 1576], [130, 1576]], '54']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 85 ---

[[[886, 79], [1146, 79], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[200, 189], [1146, 189], [1146, 268], [200, 268]], 'NOTE >>IMP provides an optional place holder for all current and future implementor-defined directives. In this way the implementor can optionally support the use of >>IMP t0 indicate the start of one or more implementor-defined directives:']
[[[164, 288], [1147, 288], [1147, 349], [164, 349]], '10) A literal in a compiler directive shall not be specified as a concatenation expression;, a figurative constant; or a floating-_ point numeric literal']
[[[164, 372], [396, 372], [396, 398], [164, 398]], '7.3.4 General rules']
[[[164, 423], [1145, 423], [1145, 476], [164, 476]], '1) A compiler directive line is not affected by the replacing action of a COPY statement or a REPLACE statement']
[[[164, 499], [1149, 499], [1149, 612], [164, 612]], '2) Compiler directives are processed either in the text manipulation stage or the compilation stage of processing as specified  in 7.2, Text   manipulation: The order of processing  during the text manipulation stage is specified in 7.2, Text manipulation. During the compilation stage, compiler directives are processed in the order encountered in the structured compilation group:']
[[[164, 636], [1146, 636], [1146, 716], [164, 716]], "3) The state of compiler directives may be saved and restored with PUSH and POP directives A 'stack' is maintained that the state ofa directive may be pushed on to. A subsequent POP directive removes the item from the stack and restores the state ofthe directive to that when it was pushed."]
[[[199, 737], [1146, 737], [1146, 815], [199, 815]], 'NOTE The PUSH/POP sequence is especially useful when using COPY to introduce text that contains directives that can already be specified earlier: Changes to them in the added text can be overridden by subsequent POP directive so the remaining source text behaves as ifthe changes were not made:']
[[[164, 837], [1145, 837], [1145, 892], [164, 892]], '4) If the implementor defines the IMP   directive, the  general rules   for that   directive   shall be implementor-defined.']
[[[164, 916], [1148, 916], [1148, 968], [164, 968]], '5) A compiler directive applies to all of the source text and library text that follows and is independent of execution flow.']
[[[161, 991], [1143, 991], [1143, 1208], [161, 1208]], "7.3.5 Conditional compilation The use of certain compiler directives provides a means of including or omitting selected lines of source code: This is called conditional compilation. The compiler directives that are used for conditional compilation are the DEFINE directive; the EVALUATE directive,and the IF directive. The DEFINE directive is used to define compilation variables, which may be referenced in the EVALUATE and IF directives in order to select lines ofcode thatareto be 'compiled orare to be omitted during compilation. Compilation variables may be referenced in constant entries as specified in 13.10,Constant entry:"]
[[[164, 1232], [658, 1232], [658, 1262], [164, 1262]], '73.6 Compile-time arithmetic expressions']
[[[164, 1286], [354, 1286], [354, 1312], [164, 1312]], '7.3.6.1 General']
[[[164, 1336], [1124, 1336], [1124, 1392], [164, 1392]], 'A compile-time arithmetic expression may be specified in the DEFINE and EVALUATE directives, in a constant conditional expression, and in a constant entry.']
[[[924, 1549], [1076, 1549], [1076, 1578], [924, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1116, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1116, 1576]], '55']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 86 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [368, 190], [368, 218], [130, 218]], '73.6.2 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 240], [1112, 240], [1112, 298], [130, 298]], '1)  Compile-time arithmetic expressions shall be   formed in accordance with 8.8.1, Arithmetic expressions, with the following exceptions:']
[[[166, 319], [688, 319], [688, 351], [166, 351]], 'a) The exponentiation operator shall not be specified.']
[[[166, 372], [1112, 372], [1112, 429], [166, 429]], 'b) All operands shall be fixed-point numeric literals orarithmetic expressions in which all operands are fixed-point numeric literals.']
[[[167, 450], [998, 450], [998, 480], [167, 480]], 'c) The expression shall be specified in such a way that a division by zero cannot occur:']
[[[130, 502], [1112, 502], [1112, 589], [130, 589]], '2) The implementor shall define and document any rules restricting the precision and/or magnitude and/or range of permissible values for the intermediate results needed to evaluate the arithmetic expression: They shall also document which intermediate rounding method is used, ifapplicable']
[[[130, 610], [382, 610], [382, 638], [130, 638]], '73.6.3 General rules']
[[[130, 659], [1113, 659], [1113, 718], [130, 718]], '1) The order of precedence and the rules for evaluation of compile-time arithmetic expressions are shown in 8.8.1, Arithmetic expressions_']
[[[130, 740], [1114, 740], [1114, 827], [130, 827]], '2) The implementor shall define and document which mode ofarithmeticis to be used when evaluating compile-time arithmetic: This may be their native mode of arithmetic or a standard mode of arithmetic or a mode unique for processing compile-time arithmetic expressions']
[[[164, 846], [1112, 846], [1112, 944], [164, 944]], 'NOTE If portability is desired, then it is recommended that one of the standard modes of arithmetic is used. If consistency with evaluating runtime arithmetic expressions is desired, then native or the default runtime arithmetic mode should be used. In some cases, the resources available at compile-time may lead the implementor to use another mode of arithmetic']
[[[130, 968], [1112, 968], [1112, 1053], [130, 1053]], '3) The final result of the arithmetic expression shall be truncated to the integer part of the value as specified in 15.49, INTEGER-PART function, and the resultant value shall be considered to be an integer numeric literal.']
[[[130, 1074], [598, 1074], [598, 1104], [130, 1104]], '7.3.7 Compile-time boolean expressions']
[[[130, 1128], [318, 1128], [318, 1154], [130, 1154]], '73.7.1 General']
[[[129, 1178], [1038, 1178], [1038, 1234], [129, 1234]], 'A compile-time boolean expression may be specified where allowed by the general format ofan expression:']
[[[130, 1260], [358, 1260], [358, 1288], [130, 1288]], '73.7.2 Syntax rule']
[[[130, 1310], [1112, 1310], [1112, 1390], [130, 1390]], '1) Compile-time boolean expressions shall be formed in accordance with 8.8.2, Boolean expressions, except that all operands shall be boolean literals or boolean expressions in which all operands are boolean literals']
[[[130, 1550], [160, 1550], [160, 1576], [130, 1576]], '56']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 87 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [404, 190], [404, 216], [164, 216]], '73.73 General rule']
[[[164, 240], [1146, 240], [1146, 296], [164, 296]], '1) The order ofprecedenceandthe rules for evaluation ofcompile-time boolean expressions are shown in 8.8.2, Boolean expressions.']
[[[164, 320], [604, 320], [604, 350], [164, 350]], '73.8 Constant conditional expression']
[[[164, 374], [354, 374], [354, 400], [164, 400]], '7.3.8.1 General']
[[[164, 425], [1094, 425], [1094, 509], [164, 509]], 'A constant conditional expression is a conditional expression in which the operands are a defined condition, a literal, or an arithmetic or boolean expression containing only literal terms A defined condition tests whether a compilation-variable has a defined value:']
[[[164, 532], [402, 532], [402, 560], [164, 560]], '7.3.8.2 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 582], [805, 582], [805, 614], [164, 614]], '1) A constant conditional expression shall be one ofthe following:']
[[[200, 634], [1146, 634], [1146, 718], [200, 718]], 'a) A relation condition in which both operands are literals, arithmetic expressions containing only literal terms, or boolean expressions containing only literal terms The condition shall be formed according to the rules in 8.8.4.2, Simple relation conditions The following rules also apply:']
[[[272, 742], [1146, 742], [1146, 798], [272, 798]], 'The operands shall be of the same category. An arithmetic expression is of the category numeric A boolean expression is of the category boolean:']
[[[272, 819], [1146, 819], [1146, 876], [272, 876]], "If literals are specified and they are not numeric literals, the relational operator shall be 'IS EQUAL TO' , 'IS NOT EQUAL TO' , 'IS =', 'IS NOT =', or 'IS"]
[[[200, 898], [1146, 898], [1146, 952], [200, 952]], 'b) A boolean condition as specified in 8.8.4.3, Simple boolean condition, in which all operands are boolean literals_']
[[[240, 980], [430, 980], [430, 1004], [240, 1004]], 'A defined condition.']
[[[200, 1030], [1146, 1030], [1146, 1115], [200, 1115]], 'd) A complex condition as specified in 8.8.4.9, Complex conditions, formed by combining the above forms of simple conditions into complex conditions Abbreviated combined relation conditions shall not be specified.']
[[[164, 1138], [1146, 1138], [1146, 1193], [164, 1193]], '2) An arithmetic expression in a constant conditional expression shall be formed in accordance with 7.3.6, Compile-time arithmetic expressions:']
[[[164, 1216], [1142, 1216], [1142, 1272], [164, 1272]], '3) A boolean expression in a constant conditional expression shall be formed in accordance with 73.7, Compile-time boolean expressions']
[[[164, 1296], [416, 1296], [416, 1322], [164, 1322]], '7.3.83 General rules']
[[[164, 1343], [1033, 1343], [1033, 1379], [164, 1379]], '1) Complex conditions are evaluated according to the rules in 8.8.4.9, Complex conditions.']
[[[164, 1398], [1147, 1398], [1147, 1458], [164, 1458]], '2) For simple relation condition where the operands are not numeric or boolean, no collating sequence is used for the comparison A character by character comparison for equality based on the']
[[[924, 1549], [1076, 1549], [1076, 1578], [924, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1117, 1553], [1143, 1553], [1143, 1573], [1117, 1573]], '57']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 88 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[163, 185], [1113, 185], [1113, 245], [163, 245]], "binary value of each character's encoding is used. If the literals are of unequal length they are not equal:"]
[[[166, 266], [828, 266], [828, 294], [166, 294]], 'NOTE This means that uppercase and lowercase letters are not equivalent']
[[[130, 318], [430, 318], [430, 344], [130, 344]], '73.8.4 Defined condition']
[[[130, 370], [340, 370], [340, 396], [130, 396]], '7.3.8.4.1 General']
[[[132, 421], [832, 421], [832, 452], [132, 452]], 'A defined condition tests whether a given compilation-variable is defined.']
[[[130, 476], [422, 476], [422, 502], [130, 502]], '73.8.4.2 General format']
[[[131, 528], [609, 528], [609, 560], [131, 560]], 'compilation-variable-name-1 IS NOT DEFINED']
[[[130, 594], [378, 594], [378, 622], [130, 622]], '73.8.4.3 Syntax rule']
[[[130, 642], [939, 642], [939, 678], [130, 678]], '1) Compilation-variable-name-1 shall not be the same as a compiler-directive word,']
[[[130, 700], [390, 700], [390, 724], [130, 724]], '73.8.4.4 General rule']
[[[130, 748], [1112, 748], [1112, 806], [130, 806]], '1) A defined condition using the IS DEFINED syntax evaluates TRUE if compilation-variable-name-1 is currently defined:']
[[[130, 828], [1112, 828], [1112, 884], [130, 884]], '2) defined condition using the IS NOT DEFINED syntax evaluates TRUE if compilation- variable-name-l is not currently defined:']
[[[130, 1550], [160, 1550], [160, 1576], [130, 1576]], '58']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 89 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [566, 190], [566, 216], [164, 216]], '73.9 CALL-CONVENTION directive']
[[[164, 244], [354, 244], [354, 268], [164, 268]], '7.3.9.1 General']
[[[163, 294], [1142, 294], [1142, 376], [163, 376]], 'The CALL-CONVENTION directive instructs the compiler how to treat references to program-names and method-names and may be used to determine other details for interacting with a function, method, or program: This directive is processed during the compilation stage of processing:']
[[[164, 400], [436, 400], [436, 426], [164, 426]], '7.3.9.2 General format']
[[[416, 468], [646, 468], [646, 530], [416, 530]], 'COBOL call-convention-name-1']
[[[168, 480], [388, 480], [388, 506], [168, 506]], '>>CALL-CONVENTION']
[[[164, 566], [416, 566], [416, 590], [164, 590]], '73.93 General rules']
[[[164, 617], [994, 617], [994, 644], [164, 644]], "1) The default for the CALL-CONVENTION directive is '>>CALL-CONVENTION COBOL' ."]
[[[164, 667], [1146, 667], [1146, 780], [164, 780]], '2) The CALL-CONVENTION directive determines how program-names and method-names specified in subsequent INVOKE statements, inline method invocations, CALL statements, CANCEL statements and program-address-identifiers are processed by the compiler: This directive applies when program-name or method-name is referenced in those language constructs']
[[[200, 800], [1148, 800], [1148, 937], [200, 937]], 'a) When COBOL is specified, that program-name or method-name is treated as a COBOL word that maps to the externalized name ofthe method to be invoked or the program to be called, canceled, or referenced in the program-address-identifier, respectively, applying the same implementor- defined mapping rules as for a method-name or program-name for which no AS phrase is specified.']
[[[200, 957], [1146, 957], [1146, 1066], [200, 1066]], 'b) When call-convention-name-1 is specified, that program-name or method-name is treated as a literal that maps to the externalized name of the method to be invoked or the program to be called, canceled, or referenced in the program-address-identifier, respectively, in a manner defined by the implementor:']
[[[164, 1092], [1146, 1092], [1146, 1146], [164, 1146]], '3) The CALL-CONVENTION directive may also be used by the implementor to determine other details needed t0 interact with a function, method, or program:']
[[[924, 1549], [1076, 1549], [1076, 1578], [924, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1116, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1116, 1576]], '59']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 90 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 189], [496, 189], [496, 216], [130, 216]], '73.10 COBOL-WORDS directive']
[[[130, 242], [334, 242], [334, 268], [130, 268]], '73.10.1 General']
[[[127, 293], [1104, 293], [1104, 404], [127, 404]], 'The COBOL-WORDS directive provides the facility to modify which words may and may not be used as reserved words, context-sensitive words, and function names In addition, it allows the program to prohibit the use of specified user-defined names This directive is processed during the text manipulation stage of processing:']
[[[130, 426], [416, 426], [416, 452], [130, 452]], '73.10.2 General format']
[[[353, 504], [682, 504], [682, 639], [353, 639]], 'EQUATE literal-1 WITH literal-2 UNDEFINE literal-3 SUBSTITUTE literal-4 BY literal-5 RESERVE literal-6']
[[[144, 556], [324, 556], [324, 580], [144, 580]], '>>COBOL-WORDS']
[[[129, 681], [382, 681], [382, 710], [129, 710]], '73.4103 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 732], [1112, 732], [1112, 816], [130, 816]], '1) The COBOL-WORDS directive may be specified only before the first IDENTIFICATION DIVISION within a compilation group. There is no limit to the number of COBOL-WORDS directives that may be specified within a single compilation group_']
[[[130, 837], [1112, 837], [1112, 894], [130, 894]], '2) Each literal shall be an alphanumeric literal, shall not be specified in hexadecimal-alphanumeric format; shall not contain a space character, and shall be evaluated as case-insensitive']
[[[130, 918], [1112, 918], [1112, 974], [130, 974]], '3) The content of literal-1, literal-3,and literal-4 shall be a reserved word,a context-sensitive word, or an intrinsic function name The content ofthese literals shall not be a special-character word.']
[[[130, 998], [1112, 998], [1112, 1078], [130, 1078]], '4) The content of literal-2,literal-5,and literal-6 shall not be a reserved word,a context-sensitive word nor an intrinsic function-name_ The content of each of these literals shall be a COBOL word that meets the requirements for a user-defined data-name as specified in 8.3.2.2, User-defined words.']
[[[130, 1103], [1112, 1103], [1112, 1161], [130, 1161]], '5) The same COBOL word shall not be contained in a literal in more than one COBOL-WORDS directive within a single compilation group:']
[[[129, 1181], [396, 1181], [396, 1208], [129, 1208]], '7,3.10.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1234], [1112, 1234], [1112, 1316], [130, 1316]], '1) The content of each literal shall be processed as case-insensitive whenever the COBOL-WORDS directive is applied within a compilation group: Any use ofan equated or substituted literal shall be case-insensitive when used syntactically as a COBOL word.']
[[[130, 1338], [1112, 1338], [1112, 1444], [130, 1444]], '2) When the EQUATE option is specified, the COBOL word that is the content of literal-2 shall be treated as a synonym for the COBOL word that is the content of literal-1, and may be used in any syntax requiring the use of the reserved word, context-sensitive word, or intrinsic function name that is the content of literal-1.']
[[[131, 1553], [159, 1553], [159, 1573], [131, 1573]], '60']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 91 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 186], [1146, 186], [1146, 298], [164, 298]], '3) When the UNDEFINE option is specified, the COBOL word that is the content of literal-3 shall no longer be reserved or restricted in any way,and may be used as a user-defined intrinsic name, data- name or any other user-defined word, and any syntax requiring the use of the COBOL word that is the content of literal-3 shall not be available for use in this compilation group:']
[[[164, 319], [1146, 319], [1146, 456], [164, 456]], '4) When the SUBSTITUTE option is specified, the COBOL word that is the content of literal-5 shall be used in any syntax where the COBOL word that is the content of literal-4 is documented as required or optional The COBOL word that is the content of literal-4 may then be used as a user-defined word within this compilation group but the content of literal-4 shall no longer be a reserved word, context-sensitive word, nor an intrinsic function name within this compilation group:']
[[[164, 478], [1146, 478], [1146, 535], [164, 535]], '5) When the RESERVE option is used, then the content of literal-6 shall not be used as a user-defined word within this compilation group:']
[[[166, 553], [1146, 553], [1146, 612], [166, 612]], '6) A COBOL-WORDS directive   does not affect any Compiler  directing  statements or Compiler directives:']
[[[924, 1549], [1076, 1549], [1076, 1578], [924, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1117, 1553], [1143, 1553], [1143, 1573], [1117, 1573]], '61']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 92 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [410, 190], [410, 216], [130, 216]], '731 DEFINE directive']
[[[130, 242], [334, 242], [334, 268], [130, 268]], '73.1.1 General']
[[[130, 292], [1109, 292], [1109, 404], [130, 404]], 'The DEFINE directive specifies a symbolic name, called a compilation variable, for a particular literal value: This name may then be used in a constant conditional expression, an EVALUATE directive, 0r a constant entry:_ compilation variable may be set to a value obtained by the compiler from the operating environment: This directive is processed during the text manipulation stage of processing:']
[[[130, 426], [416, 426], [416, 452], [130, 452]], '7.3.1.2 General format']
[[[647, 540], [884, 540], [884, 653], [647, 653]], 'arithmetic-expression-1 boolean-expression-1 literal-1 PARAMETER']
[[[149, 593], [586, 593], [586, 622], [149, 622]], '>> DEFINE compilation-variable-name-1 As']
[[[912, 584], [1038, 584], [1038, 612], [912, 612]], '[OVERRIDE']
[[[614, 684], [658, 684], [658, 708], [614, 708]], 'OFF']
[[[130, 758], [384, 758], [384, 788], [130, 788]], '73.1.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 808], [938, 808], [938, 838], [130, 838]], '1) Compilation-variable-name-1 shall not be the same as a compiler-directive word.']
[[[130, 861], [978, 861], [978, 893], [130, 893]], '2) Ifa DEFINE directive specifies neither the OFF nor the OVERRIDE phrase, then either']
[[[201, 912], [1111, 912], [1111, 972], [201, 972]], 'compilation-variable-name-1 shall not have been declared previously within the same compilation group; or']
[[[200, 992], [1110, 992], [1110, 1051], [200, 1051]], 'the last previous DEFINE directive referring to compilation-variable-name-1 shall have been specified with the OFF phrase; Or']
[[[202, 1070], [1111, 1070], [1111, 1126], [202, 1126]], 'thelast previous DEFINE directive referring to compilation-variable-name-1 shall have specified the same value']
[[[130, 1154], [1112, 1154], [1112, 1209], [130, 1209]], '3) Arithmetic-expression-1 shall be formed in accordance with 7.3.6, Compile-time arithmetic expressions_']
[[[130, 1230], [1106, 1230], [1106, 1260], [130, 1260]], '4) Boolean-expression-1 shall be formed in accordance with 7.3.7, Compile-time boolean expressions:']
[[[130, 1286], [396, 1286], [396, 1312], [130, 1312]], '73.11.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1334], [1114, 1334], [1114, 1444], [130, 1444]], '1) In text that follows a DEFINE directive specifying compilation-variable-name-1 without the OFF phrase, compilation-variable-name-1 may be used in the compilation group in any compiler directive where a literal of the category associated with the name is permitted, in defined condition, Or in a constant entry where the FROM phrase is specified:']
[[[130, 1552], [158, 1552], [158, 1576], [130, 1576]], '62']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 93 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 186], [1146, 186], [1146, 268], [164, 268]], '2) Following a DEFINE directive in which the OFF phrase is specified, compilation-variable-name-1 shall not be used except in a defined condition unless it is redefined in subsequent DEFINE directive:']
[[[164, 292], [1145, 292], [1145, 351], [164, 351]], '3) Ifthe OVERRIDE phrase is   specified, compilation-variable-name-1 is   unconditionally set to reference the value ofthe specified operand.']
[[[166, 370], [1146, 370], [1146, 478], [166, 478]], '4) If the PARAMETER phrase is specified, the value referenced by compilation-variable-name-1 is obtained from the operating environment by an implementor-defined method when the DEFINE directive is processed. If no value is made available from the operating environment, compilation- variable-name-1 is not defined.']
[[[164, 505], [1146, 505], [1146, 561], [164, 561]], '5) If the operand ofthe DEFINE directive consists of a single numeric literal, that operand is treated as literal, not as an arithmetic-expression']
[[[200, 582], [1148, 582], [1148, 662], [200, 662]], 'If arithmetic-expression-1 is specified, arithmetic-expression-1 is evaluated according to 7.3.6, Compile-time arithmetic expressions, and compilation-variable-name-1 references the resultant value:']
[[[166, 687], [1146, 687], [1146, 744], [166, 744]], '7) If boolean-expression-1 is specified, boolean-expression-1 is evaluated according to 7.3.7, Compile- time boolean expressions,and compilation-variable-name-1 references the resultant value:']
[[[164, 764], [893, 764], [893, 800], [164, 800]], '8) If literal-1 is specified, compilation-variable-name-1 references literal-1.']
[[[924, 1549], [1076, 1549], [1076, 1578], [924, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1116, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1116, 1576]], '63']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 94 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 189], [422, 189], [422, 216], [130, 216]], '73.12 DISPLAY directive']
[[[130, 242], [334, 242], [334, 268], [130, 268]], '73.2.1 General']
[[[130, 292], [1078, 292], [1078, 351], [130, 351]], 'The DISPLAY directive transfers data to the source listing Or an implementor defined compile-time- device. The implementor defines the stage of processing for this directive:']
[[[130, 374], [416, 374], [416, 400], [130, 400]], '7.3.12.2 General format']
[[[299, 474], [714, 474], [714, 590], [299, 590]], 'arithmetic-expression-1 boolean-expression-1 literal-1 PARAMETER compilation-variable-name-1']
[[[154, 514], [270, 514], [270, 538], [154, 538]], '>>DISPLAY']
[[[276, 652], [336, 652], [336, 676], [276, 676]], 'UPON']
[[[371, 636], [592, 636], [592, 694], [371, 694]], 'compile-time-device-1 LISTING']
[[[129, 745], [382, 745], [382, 776], [129, 776]], '7.3.12.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 796], [1026, 796], [1026, 828], [130, 828]], '1) The DISPLAY directive shall begin on a new line and shall be specified entirely on that line:']
[[[130, 852], [1112, 852], [1112, 907], [130, 907]], '2) Arithmetic-expression-1 shall be formed in accordance with 7.3.6, Compile-time arithmetic expressions_']
[[[130, 929], [1106, 929], [1106, 959], [130, 959]], '3) Boolean-expression-1 shall be formed in accordance with 7.3.7, Compile-time boolean expressions']
[[[130, 984], [396, 984], [396, 1008], [130, 1008]], '7.3.12.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1032], [1113, 1032], [1113, 1116], [130, 1116]], '1) The DISPLAY directive causes the contents of each operand to be transferred to the source listing or compile-time-device-l or both in the order listed: Any conversion of data required is defined by the implementor:']
[[[130, 1140], [1112, 1140], [1112, 1218], [130, 1218]], '2) If the compiler does not produce a source listing, the result of the DISPLAY directive is defined by the implementor; otherwise the data transfer shall take place irrespective ofwhether it is suppressed by the LISTING directive.']
[[[130, 1244], [1112, 1244], [1112, 1352], [130, 1352]], '3) If the PARAMETER phrase is specified, the value referenced by compilation-variable-name-1 is obtained from the operating environment by an implementor-defined method when the DISPLAY directive is processed. Ifno value is made available from the operating environment no transfer shall take place:']
[[[130, 1376], [1112, 1376], [1112, 1432], [130, 1432]], '4) When DISPLAY directive contains more than one operand the values of the operands are transferred in the sequence in which the operands are encountered:']
[[[130, 1456], [470, 1456], [470, 1484], [130, 1484]], '5) Ifthe UPON phrase is specified:']
[[[131, 1553], [159, 1553], [159, 1573], [131, 1573]], '64']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 95 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[236, 186], [1111, 186], [1111, 219], [236, 219]], 'if LISTING is specified, data is transferred to the same device as that used for source listings.']
[[[200, 239], [1145, 239], [1145, 298], [200, 298]], 'b) if  compile-time-device-1 is   specified, data is   transferred to the device   defined by the implementor for receiving that data:']
[[[200, 318], [1003, 318], [1003, 351], [200, 351]], 'If the UPON phrase is not specified, the default is as if UPON LISTING were specified.']
[[[924, 1549], [1076, 1549], [1076, 1578], [924, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1116, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1116, 1576]], '65']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 96 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [446, 190], [446, 216], [130, 216]], '73.3 EVALUATE directive']
[[[130, 242], [334, 242], [334, 268], [130, 268]], '73.13.1 General']
[[[128, 293], [1106, 293], [1106, 351], [128, 351]], 'The EVALUATE directive provides for multi-branch conditional compilation. This directive is processed during the text manipulation stage of processing:']
[[[130, 374], [416, 374], [416, 400], [130, 400]], '7.3.13.2 General format']
[[[130, 426], [382, 426], [382, 454], [130, 454]], 'Format 1 (evaluate-value)']
[[[149, 523], [516, 523], [516, 600], [149, 600]], 'literal-1 >> EVALUATE arithmetic-expression-1 boolean-expression-1']
[[[309, 665], [526, 665], [526, 745], [309, 745]], 'literal-2 arithmetic-expression-2 boolean-expression-2']
[[[195, 687], [283, 687], [283, 707], [195, 707]], '>> WHEN']
[[[592, 674], [690, 674], [690, 732], [592, 732]], 'THROUGH THRU']
[[[734, 680], [952, 680], [952, 734], [734, 734]], 'literal-3 arithmetic-expression-3']
[[[1007, 687], [1063, 687], [1063, 707], [1007, 707]], 'text-1']
[[[189, 781], [456, 781], [456, 809], [189, 809]], '>> WHEN  OTHER text-2 ] ]']
[[[173, 828], [346, 828], [346, 852], [173, 852]], '>> END-EVALUATE']
[[[130, 894], [380, 894], [380, 922], [130, 922]], 'Format 2 (evaluate-truth)']
[[[148, 972], [354, 972], [354, 996], [148, 996]], '>> EVALUATE TRUE']
[[[154, 1012], [710, 1012], [710, 1040], [154, 1040]], '{>>WHEN constant-conditional-expression-1 text-1 ] }']
[[[190, 1058], [486, 1058], [486, 1086], [190, 1086]], '>> WHEN OTHER text-2 ] ]']
[[[172, 1108], [364, 1108], [364, 1132], [172, 1132]], '>> END-EVALUATE']
[[[130, 1178], [384, 1178], [384, 1206], [130, 1206]], '73.133 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 1230], [276, 1230], [276, 1254], [130, 1254]], 'ALL FORMATS']
[[[130, 1280], [1112, 1280], [1112, 1390], [130, 1390]], '1) For descriptive purposes in these syntax rules, operand-1 refers to literal-1,arithmetic-expression- 1, or boolean-expression-1 in format 1 and to the TRUE keyword in format 2; operand-2 refers to literal-2,arithmetic-expression-2,0r boolean-expression-2 in format 1 and t0 constant-conditional- expression-1 in format 2;and operand-3 refers to literal-3 or arithmetic-expression-3 in format 1.']
[[[130, 1411], [1026, 1411], [1026, 1443], [130, 1443]], '2) EVALUATE operand-1 shall begin on a new line and shall be specified entirely on that line:']
[[[131, 1553], [159, 1553], [159, 1573], [131, 1573]], '66']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 97 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 187], [1146, 187], [1146, 240], [164, 240]], '3) >>WHEN operand-2 [THROUGH operand-3] shall begin on a new line and shall be specified entirely on that line_']
[[[164, 267], [506, 267], [506, 299], [164, 299]], '4) Text-1 shall begin on a new line_']
[[[164, 319], [1008, 319], [1008, 351], [164, 351]], '5) >>WHEN OTHER shall begin on a new line and shall be specified entirely on that line']
[[[200, 373], [508, 373], [508, 403], [200, 403]], 'Text-2 shall begin on a new line.']
[[[166, 424], [1089, 424], [1089, 454], [166, 454]], '7) >>END-EVALUATE shall be specified on a new line and shall be specified entirely on that line']
[[[164, 476], [1146, 476], [1146, 534], [164, 534]], '8) Text-1 and text-2 may be any kind of source lines, including compiler directives. Text-1 and text-2 may consist of multiple lines:']
[[[164, 555], [1147, 555], [1147, 636], [164, 636]], '9)  Excluding text-1 and text-2, the phrases of a given EVALUATE directive shall all be specified in the same library text or all in source-text: A nested EVALUATE directive specified in text-1 or in text-2 is considered a new EVALUATE directive_']
[[[164, 664], [270, 664], [270, 688], [164, 688]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[164, 715], [1146, 715], [1146, 774], [164, 774]], '10) Literal-1, arithmetic-expression-1, and boolean-expression-1 are selection subjects. The operands specified in the WHEN phrase are selection objects']
[[[164, 794], [1148, 794], [1148, 851], [164, 851]], '11) All operands of one EVALUATE directive shall be of the same category. For this rule, an arithmetic expression is of category numeric and a boolean expression is of category boolean:']
[[[163, 871], [1147, 871], [1147, 926], [163, 926]], '12) If the THROUGH phrase is specified, all selection subjects and selection objects shall be of category numeric:']
[[[164, 950], [658, 950], [658, 983], [164, 983]], '13) The words THROUGH and THRU are equivalent']
[[[162, 1004], [1146, 1004], [1146, 1060], [162, 1060]], '14) Arithmetic-expression-1, arithmetic-expression-2, and arithmetic-expression-3 shall be formed in accordance with 7.3.6, Compile-time arithmetic expressions_']
[[[164, 1085], [1146, 1085], [1146, 1141], [164, 1141]], '15) Boolean-expression-1 and boolean-expression-2 shall be formed in accordance with 7.3.7 , Compile- time boolean expressions_']
[[[164, 1163], [1146, 1163], [1146, 1219], [164, 1219]], '16) Constant-conditional-expression-1 shall be formed in accordance with 7.3.8, Constant conditional expression:']
[[[164, 1243], [430, 1243], [430, 1271], [164, 1271]], '73.13.4 General rules']
[[[166, 1296], [310, 1296], [310, 1320], [166, 1320]], 'ALL FORMATS']
[[[164, 1347], [1148, 1347], [1148, 1426], [164, 1426]], '1) Text-1 and text-2 are not part of the EVALUATE compiler directive line: Any text words in text-1 or text-2 that do not form a compiler directive line are subject to the matching and replacing rules of the COPY statement and the REPLACE statement;']
[[[924, 1549], [1076, 1549], [1076, 1578], [924, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 98 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[128, 190], [236, 190], [236, 214], [128, 214]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[130, 240], [1110, 240], [1110, 297], [130, 297]], '2)  Ifan operand ofthe EVALUATE directive consists of a single numeric literal, that operand is treated as a literal, not as an arithmetic-expression_']
[[[130, 322], [1112, 322], [1112, 377], [130, 377]], '3) Boolean-expression-1 and boolean-expression-2 are evaluated in accordance with 7.3.7, Compile- time boolean expressions:']
[[[130, 398], [1112, 398], [1112, 452], [130, 452]], '4) The selection subject is compared against the values specified in each WHEN phrase in turn as follows:']
[[[166, 477], [1112, 477], [1112, 534], [166, 534]], 'a) Ifthe THROUGH phrase is not specified,a TRUE result is returned ifthe selection subject is equal to literal-2,arithmetic-expression-2, or boolean-expression-2']
[[[166, 556], [1112, 556], [1112, 638], [166, 638]], 'b) Ifthe THROUGH phrase is specified, a TRUE result is returned if the selection subject lies in the inclusive range determined by literal-2 or arithmetic-expression-2 and literal-3 or arithmetic- expression-3.']
[[[163, 664], [1112, 664], [1112, 768], [163, 768]], 'If a WHEN phrase evaluates to TRUE, all lines of text-1 associated with that WHEN phrase are included in the resultant text AllL lines of text-1 associated with other WHEN phrases in that EVALUATE directive and all lines of text-2 associated with a WHEN OTHER phrase are omitted from the resultant text:']
[[[130, 796], [1112, 796], [1112, 875], [130, 875]], '5) If no WHEN phrase evaluates to TRUE, all lines of text-2 associated with the WHEN OTHER phrase, if specified,are included in the resultant text: All lines of text-1 associated with other WHEN phrases are omitted from the resultant text']
[[[164, 898], [1112, 898], [1112, 981], [164, 981]], 'If the END-EVALUATE phrase is reached without any WHEN phrase evaluating to TRUE,and without encountering a WHEN OTHER phrase, all lines of text-1 associated with all WHEN phrases are omitted from the resultant text']
[[[130, 1002], [1112, 1002], [1112, 1087], [130, 1087]], "7) If literal-1 is an alphanumeric or national literal, a character by character comparison for equality based on the binary value of each character's encoding is used. If the literals are of unequal length they are not equal_"]
[[[130, 1112], [238, 1112], [238, 1136], [130, 1136]], 'FORMAT 2']
[[[130, 1163], [1112, 1163], [1112, 1218], [130, 1218]], '8) For each WHEN phrase in turn,the constant-conditional-expression is evaluated in accordance with 7.3.8, Constant conditional expression.']
[[[163, 1244], [1112, 1244], [1112, 1348], [163, 1348]], 'If a WHEN phrase evaluates to TRUE, all lines of text-1 associated with that WHEN phrase are included in the resultant text All lines of text-1 associated with other WHEN phrases of that EVALUATE directive and all lines of text-2 associated with a WHEN OTHER phrase are omitted from the resultant text']
[[[130, 1374], [1112, 1374], [1112, 1452], [130, 1452]], '9) If no WHEN phrase evaluates to TRUE, all lines of text-2 associated with the WHEN OTHER phrase, if specified,are included in the resultant text: All lines oftext-1 associated with other WHEN phrases are omitted from the resultant text:']
[[[131, 1553], [159, 1553], [159, 1573], [131, 1573]], '68']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 99 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[163, 185], [1149, 185], [1149, 269], [163, 269]], '10) Ifthe END-EVALUATE phrase is reached without any WHEN phrase evaluating to TRUE,and without encountering a WHEN OTHER phrase, all lines of text-1 associated with all WHEN phrases are omitted from the resultant text:']
[[[924, 1549], [1076, 1549], [1076, 1578], [924, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1119, 1553], [1145, 1553], [1145, 1573], [1119, 1573]], '69']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [420, 190], [420, 216], [130, 216]], '73.4 FLAG-02 directive']
[[[130, 242], [334, 242], [334, 268], [130, 268]], '73.4.1 General']
[[[128, 292], [1092, 292], [1092, 377], [128, 377]], 'The FLAG-02 directive specifies options to flag certain syntax for which the behavior might be incompatible between ISO 1989.2002 and ISO/IEC 1989.2014. This directive is processed during the compilation stage of processing']
[[[128, 397], [1112, 397], [1112, 446], [128, 446]], 'NOTE The FLAG-02 directive is an obsolete element in this Working Draft International Standard and is to be deleted from the next edition of standard COBOL']
[[[130, 473], [416, 473], [416, 501], [130, 501]], '73.4.2 General format']
[[[298, 568], [340, 568], [340, 592], [298, 592]], 'ALL']
[[[327, 608], [642, 608], [642, 778], [327, 778]], 'EC-PROGRAM-EXCEPTIONS [-0-STATUS-07 MOVE-TO-SAME-NAME RANGE-EXCEPTION-FOR-INDEX TERMINATE-WITH-VARYING']
[[[722, 648], [766, 648], [766, 704], [722, 704]], 'ON OFF']
[[[146, 659], [267, 659], [267, 684], [146, 684]], '>> FLAG-02']
[[[130, 842], [370, 842], [370, 872], [130, 872]], '73.14.3 Syntax rule']
[[[130, 893], [1112, 893], [1112, 950], [130, 950]], '1) The FLAG-02 directive may be specified only between clauses in divisions other than the procedure division and only between statements in the procedure division']
[[[130, 974], [396, 974], [396, 1000], [130, 1000]], '73.14.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1024], [1112, 1024], [1112, 1108], [130, 1108]], '1) The implementor shall provide warning mechanism that flags the incompatibilities potentially affecting existing programs for the selected option, where the incompatibility is between the specifications in ISO/IEC 1989.2002,and ISO/IEC 1989.2014_']
[[[130, 1130], [1114, 1130], [1114, 1241], [130, 1241]], '2) If ON is explicitly or implicitly specified for an option, the warning mechanism is enabled for that option for all text that follows until the end of the compilation group is reached,a FLAG-02 directive is encountered that turns offall flagging options, ora FLAG-02 directive is encountered that turns off that option:']
[[[130, 1262], [854, 1262], [854, 1294], [130, 1294]], '3) If OFF is specified, flagging for the selected option or options is disabled.']
[[[130, 1314], [882, 1314], [882, 1346], [130, 1346]], '4) The word or words following FLAG-02 indicate the syntax to be diagnosed:']
[[[166, 1370], [482, 1370], [482, 1398], [166, 1398]], 'a) ALL: All of the options apply:']
[[[166, 1420], [1112, 1420], [1112, 1478], [166, 1478]], 'b) EC-PROGRAM-EXCEPTIONS: A TURN directive for EC-ALL, EC-PROGRAM, EC-PROGRAM-ARG- OMITTED. or EC-PROGRAM-NOT-FOUND shall be flagged if']
[[[131, 1553], [159, 1553], [159, 1573], [131, 1573]], '70']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 101 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[272, 188], [662, 188], [662, 218], [272, 218]], 'the source element calls any function, or']
[[[272, 241], [658, 241], [658, 271], [272, 271]], 'the source element invokes any method:']
[[[234, 294], [1145, 294], [1145, 350], [234, 350]], 'IO-STATUS-07: A CLOSE statement shall be flagged if it specifies either the WITH NO REWIND phrase or the UNIT phrase']
[[[202, 371], [1147, 371], [1147, 429], [202, 429]], 'd) MOVE-TO-SAME-NAME: A MOVE statement shall be flagged when the sending and receiving operands are described by the same data description entry, and:']
[[[272, 452], [785, 452], [785, 483], [272, 483]], 'the operands are of category alphanumeric-edited, or']
[[[272, 503], [1146, 503], [1146, 587], [272, 587]], 'the data description entry for the operands includes a subordinate OCCURS DEPENDING clause, and the data item referenced by that OCCURS DEPENDING clause is subordinate to the data description entry referenced by the operands:']
[[[236, 609], [1146, 609], [1146, 688], [236, 688]], 'RANGE-EXCEPTION-FOR-INDEX: An index-assignment or index-arithmetic format SET statement that specifies an index as the receiving field shall be flagged when checking for EC- RANGE-INDEX is enabled:']
[[[203, 715], [1147, 715], [1147, 768], [203, 768]], 'f) TERMINATE-WITH-VARYING: A TERMINATE statement shall be flagged if the report being terminated contains a VARYING clause:']
[[[164, 792], [882, 792], [882, 825], [164, 825]], '5) If the FLAG-02 directive is not specified, the default for all options is off:']
[[[924, 1549], [1076, 1549], [1076, 1578], [924, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1117, 1553], [1143, 1553], [1143, 1573], [1117, 1573]], '71']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 102 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [420, 190], [420, 216], [130, 216]], '73.5 FLAG-14 directive']
[[[130, 242], [334, 242], [334, 268], [130, 268]], '73.15.1 General']
[[[128, 292], [1080, 292], [1080, 380], [128, 380]], 'The FLAG-14 directive specifies options to flag certain syntax for which the behavior might be incompatible between the previous COBOL standard and this Working Draft International Standard: This directive is processed during the compilation stage of processing:']
[[[129, 400], [416, 400], [416, 426], [129, 426]], '73.15.2 General format']
[[[298, 496], [340, 496], [340, 520], [298, 520]], 'ALL']
[[[340, 546], [772, 546], [772, 968], [340, 968]], 'COMPILE-TIME-ARITHMETIC-EXPRESSIONS EVALUATE [-0-DECLARATIVE 1-0-STATUS-04 1-0-STATUS-07 NUM-ED-ZERO-FIGCONST READ-PREVIOUS REF-MOD-ZERO-LENGTH VALUE EDITING VALUE-FIG-CON-LENGTH VALUE-ZERO WRITE-END-OF-PAGE']
[[[862, 710], [906, 710], [906, 764], [862, 764]], 'ON OFF']
[[[148, 721], [267, 721], [267, 745], [148, 745]], '>> FLAG-14']
[[[130, 1038], [372, 1038], [372, 1066], [130, 1066]], '73.15.3 Syntax rule']
[[[130, 1087], [1112, 1087], [1112, 1144], [130, 1144]], '1) The FLAG-14 directive may be specified only between clauses in divisions other than the procedure division and only between statements in the procedure division:']
[[[130, 1169], [396, 1169], [396, 1197], [130, 1197]], '73.15.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1219], [1112, 1219], [1112, 1304], [130, 1304]], '1) The implementor shall provide warning mechanism that flags the incompatibilities potentially affecting existing programs for the selected option, where the incompatibility is between the specifications in ISO/IEC 1989.2014,and this Working Draft International Standard.']
[[[165, 1322], [1112, 1322], [1112, 1376], [165, 1376]], 'NOTE Acomplete list of changes that potentially affect existing programs is given in E.2,Substantive changes potentially affecting existing programs.']
[[[130, 1397], [1112, 1397], [1112, 1456], [130, 1456]], '2) If ON is explicitly or implicitly specified for an option, the warning mechanism is enabled for that option for all text that follows until the end ofthe compilation group is reached,a FLAG-14 directive']
[[[130, 1552], [158, 1552], [158, 1576], [130, 1576]], '72']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 103 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[198, 188], [1148, 188], [1148, 244], [198, 244]], 'is encountered that turns offall flagging options, ora FLAG-14 directive is encountered that turns off that option:']
[[[164, 264], [888, 264], [888, 298], [164, 298]], '3) If OFF is specified, flagging for the selected option or options is disabled:']
[[[164, 318], [916, 318], [916, 351], [164, 351]], '4) The word or words following FLAG-14 indicate the syntax to be diagnosed:']
[[[238, 374], [510, 374], [510, 402], [238, 402]], 'ALL. All of the options apply_']
[[[200, 425], [1144, 425], [1144, 482], [200, 482]], 'b) COMPILE-TIME-ARITHMETIC-EXPRESSIONS. A compile-time arithmetic expression that could give a different result shall be flagged']
[[[201, 506], [1146, 506], [1146, 560], [201, 560]], 'c) EVALUATE directive A directive containing a WHEN phrase and a WHEN OTHER phrase shall be flagged:']
[[[202, 582], [1147, 582], [1147, 718], [202, 718]], 'd) I-0-DECLARATIVE: An input-output statement that can be specified with an INVALID KEY phrase is specified without that phrase when a declarative that specifies INPUT, OUTPUT,[-0,or EXTEND is specified shall be flagged. A READ statement that can be specified with an AT END phrase is specified without that phrase when a declarative that specifies INPUT or I-0 is specified shall be flagged_']
[[[236, 742], [1142, 742], [1142, 798], [236, 798]], "1-0-STATUS-04. A reference to a data item specified in a FILE STATUS clause that tests for '04 shall be flagged."]
[[[203, 819], [1140, 819], [1140, 878], [203, 878]], "0) 1-0-STATUS-07.A reference to a data item specified in a FILE STATUS clause that specifies '07 shall be flagged."]
[[[200, 900], [1146, 900], [1146, 956], [200, 956]], 'g) NUM-ED-ZERO-FIG-CONSTANT. The use of the figurative constant ZERO in the VALUE clause for a numeric-edited data item shall be flagged_']
[[[200, 979], [846, 979], [846, 1009], [200, 1009]], 'h) READ-PREVIOUS. A READ PREVIOUS statement shall be flagged.']
[[[201, 1032], [1018, 1032], [1018, 1062], [201, 1062]], 'i) REF-MOD-ZERO-LENGTH: A reference modification of a data-item shall be flagged']
[[[266, 1083], [1126, 1083], [1126, 1115], [266, 1115]], 'when the REF-MOD-ZERO-LENGTH directive is not explicitly specified as either ON or OFF']
[[[264, 1137], [1146, 1137], [1146, 1192], [264, 1192]], 'and when the TURN directive for EC-BOUND-REF-MOD is turned on, either implicitly or explicitly.']
[[[199, 1216], [1146, 1216], [1146, 1273], [199, 1273]], 'j) VALUE-EDITING. VALUE clause for a numeric-edited data item that does not contain any editing symbols and is specified as a literal shall be flagged.']
[[[200, 1293], [1146, 1293], [1146, 1352], [200, 1352]], 'k) VALUE-FIG-CON-NO-LENTH: figurative constant specified in the VALUE clause ofa data item with no specified length shall be flagged.']
[[[203, 1373], [1146, 1373], [1146, 1430], [203, 1430]], '1) VALUE-ZERO. A numeric-edited data item that has a VALUE clause that specifies the figurative constant ZERO shall be flagged.']
[[[924, 1549], [1076, 1549], [1076, 1578], [924, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1116, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1116, 1576]], '73']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 104 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[167, 186], [1112, 186], [1112, 245], [167, 245]], 'm) WRITE-END-OF-PAGE. A WRITE statement that allows an END-OF-PAGE phrase when the END- OF-PAGE phrase is not specified shall be flagged.']
[[[130, 266], [848, 266], [848, 296], [130, 296]], '5) If the FLAG-14 directive is not specified, the default for all options is off:']
[[[131, 1553], [157, 1553], [157, 1573], [131, 1573]], '74']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']
