[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[271, 190], [1146, 190], [1146, 244], [271, 244]], 'If the returning item in interface-2 is described with the ONLY phrase; the returning item in interface-1 shall be described with the ONLY phrase and the same object-class-name']
[[[272, 266], [1146, 266], [1146, 345], [272, 345]], 'If the returning item in interface-2 is described without the ONLY phrase, the returning item in interface-1 shall be described with the same object-class-name or a subclass of that object- class-name_']
[[[274, 370], [910, 370], [910, 402], [274, 402]], 'The presence or absence of the FACTORY phrase shall be the same']
[[[200, 424], [1146, 424], [1146, 508], [200, 508]], 'd) If the  returning item in interface-2 is described with the ACTIVE-CLASS phrase, the corresponding returning item in interface-1 is described with the ACTIVE-CLASS phrase,and the presence or absence of the FACTORY phrase is the same_']
[[[200, 530], [1146, 530], [1146, 612], [200, 612]], 'If the description of the returning item of a method in interface-1 directly or indirectly references interface-2, the description of the returning item of the corresponding method in interface-2 shall not directly or indirectly reference interface-l_']
[[[197, 634], [1146, 634], [1146, 718], [197, 718]], 'If the returning item of the method of interface-2 is not an object reference, the corresponding returning item has the same ALIGNED, ANY LENGTH, BLANK WHEN ZERO, DYNAMIC LENGTH, JUSTIFIED, PICTURE, SIGN, and USAGE clauses, with the following exceptions:']
[[[201, 740], [1066, 740], [1066, 773], [201, 773]], 'a) Currency symbols match ifand only ifthe corresponding currency strings are the same']
[[[200, 794], [1146, 794], [1146, 874], [200, 874]], 'b) Period picture symbols match ifand only ifthe DECIMAL-POINT IS COMMA clause is in effect for both or for neither ofthese interfaces. Comma picture symbols match ifand only ifthe DECIMAL- POINT IS COMMA clause is in effect for both or for neither ofthese interfaces']
[[[201, 898], [940, 898], [940, 928], [201, 928]], 'Additionally, locale specifications in the PICTURE clauses match ifand only if:']
[[[236, 954], [1036, 954], [1036, 982], [236, 982]], 'both specify the same SIZE phrase in the LOCALE phrase of the PICTURE clause, and']
[[[235, 1006], [1146, 1006], [1146, 1115], [235, 1115]], 'both specify the LOCALE phrase without a locale-name or both specify the LOCALE phrase with the same external identification, where the external identification is the external-locale-name or literal  value associated with a locale-name in the LOCALE clause of the SPECIAL-NAMES paragraph:']
[[[164, 1137], [1146, 1137], [1146, 1194], [164, 1194]], '7) If either of the corresponding formal parameters or returning items in interface-l or interface-2 is a strongly-typed group item, both are of the same type:']
[[[164, 1214], [1080, 1214], [1080, 1246], [164, 1246]], '8) The presence or absence of the OPTIONAL phrase is the same for corresponding parameters_']
[[[164, 1267], [1146, 1267], [1146, 1327], [164, 1327]], '9) If the RAISING phrase is specified in the procedure division header ofthe method in interface-1, the corresponding method in interface-2 specifies the RAISING phrase following these rules:']
[[[235, 1343], [1147, 1343], [1147, 1402], [235, 1402]], 'If an exception-name is specified in the RAISING phrase in interface-l, the corresponding RAISING phrase in interface-2 specifies the same exception-name']
[[[200, 1425], [1146, 1425], [1146, 1483], [200, 1483]], 'b) If an object-class-name is specified in the RAISING phrase in interface-1, the corresponding RAISING phrase in interface-2 specifies one ofthe following:']
[[[912, 1549], [1064, 1549], [1064, 1578], [912, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '249']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 280 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[229, 187], [1112, 187], [1112, 270], [229, 270]], 'the same object-class-name or the name of a superclass of the class identified by that object- class-name, including the FACTORY phrase if and only if the RAISING phrase in interface-1 specifies the FACTORY phrase,']
[[[228, 293], [1112, 293], [1112, 351], [228, 351]], 'the name ofan interface implemented by the factory object ofthat class, if the RAISING phrase in interface-1 specifies the FACTORY phrase,']
[[[230, 374], [1112, 374], [1112, 428], [230, 428]], 'the name of an interface implemented by the instance object of that class, if the RAISING phrase in interface-1 does not specify the FACTORY phrase:']
[[[202, 450], [1112, 450], [1112, 534], [202, 534]], 'If an interface-name is specified in the RAISING phrase in interface-1, the corresponding RAISING phrase in interface-2 specifies the same interface-name or the name of an interface inherited by that interface:']
[[[130, 555], [1077, 555], [1077, 614], [130, 614]], 'An alphanumeric group item that is not strongly typed is, for the purpose of conformance checking; considered to be equivalent to an elementary alphanumeric data item ofthe same length:']
[[[128, 634], [1048, 634], [1048, 696], [128, 696]], 'Variable-length groups conform ifthey are compatible groups, and all variable-length data items correspond to variable-length data items as specified in 8.5.1.12,Variable-length groups']
[[[129, 714], [1030, 714], [1030, 746], [129, 746]], '9.3.8.2.4 Conformance for parameterized classes and parameterized interfaces']
[[[129, 767], [1108, 767], [1108, 846], [129, 846]], 'When using a parameterized class or interface,the class or interface is treated as ifthe actual parameter classes or interfaces were substituted for the parameters throughout the class definition or interface definition']
[[[130, 874], [402, 874], [402, 900], [130, 900]], '93.9 Class inheritance']
[[[128, 924], [1111, 924], [1111, 1084], [128, 1084]], 'Class inheritance is a mechanism for using the interface and implementation of one or more classes as the basis for another class The inheriting class,also known as a subclass, inherits from one 0r more classes, known as superclasses: The subclass has all the methods defined for the inherited class definition or definitions, includingany methods thatthe inherited definition or definitions inherited The subclasshasallthedata definitions defined for theinherited class or classes, includinganydata definitions that the inherited class or classes inherited:']
[[[127, 1109], [1112, 1109], [1112, 1206], [127, 1206]], 'NOTE This does not mean that the actual source code that describes the data is accessible or that the data items described in that source code can be directly referenced in the subclass It means that the subclasses are treated as iftheir source code hada copy ofthe superclass definitions; in other words, the inherited data itemsare considered to be defined in the subclass;']
[[[128, 1232], [1108, 1232], [1108, 1498], [128, 1498]], 'The inherited data definitions define data for every instance object of the subclass and for its factory object: Each instance object has its own copy of inherited data, distinct from the copy belonging to an instance object ofan inherited class Each factory object has its own copy of inherited data, distinct from the copy belonging to a factory object ofan inherited class Names and attributes of inherited data items are not visible in the inheriting class: The inherited object data is initialized when an object is created: The inherited factory data isallocated independently from thefactorydata ofthe inheritedclass or classesand is initialized when the factory of the subclass is created, The inherited factory data is accessible only via methods and properties specified in the factory definition of the class that describes the data. The inherited object data is accessible only via methods and properties specified in the object definition of the class that describes the data: The subclass inherits all the file definitions in the same way as the data']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '250']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 281 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[162, 188], [1134, 188], [1134, 270], [162, 270]], 'definitions, subject to the same provisions as data definitions The subclass may define methods in addition to or in place of the inherited methods and may specify data definitions and file definitions in addition to, but not in place of,the inherited data definitions and file definitions.']
[[[162, 294], [1146, 294], [1146, 350], [162, 350]], 'The interface ofa subclass shall always conform to the interface of the inherited classes, although the subclass may override some of the methods ofthe inherited class to provide different implementations:']
[[[162, 371], [1108, 371], [1108, 426], [162, 426]], 'Ifa class is defined with the FINAL clause, that class shall not be used as a superclass Ifa method is defined with the FINAL clause, that method shall not be overridden in a subclass.']
[[[164, 451], [1138, 451], [1138, 508], [164, 508]], 'User-defined words in an inherited class are not visible in the subclass_ Any such word may be used as any type of user-defined word in the subclass definition']
[[[164, 532], [496, 532], [496, 558], [164, 558]], '9.3.10 Interface inheritance']
[[[160, 584], [1142, 584], [1142, 719], [160, 719]], 'Interface inheritance is a mechanism for using one or more interface definitions as the basis for another interface. The inheriting interface has all the method specifications defined for the inherited interface definition or definitions, including any method specifications that the inherited definition or definitions inherited: The inheriting interface may define new methods augmenting the set of inherited method specific cations. The inheriting interface shall always conform to each ofthe inherited interfaces:']
[[[164, 743], [545, 743], [545, 771], [164, 771]], '9.3.11 Interface implementation']
[[[161, 792], [1142, 792], [1142, 956], [161, 956]], 'Interface implementation is a mechanism for using one or more interface definitions as the basis for a class. The implementing class shall implementall the method specifications defined for the implemented interface definition or definitions, including any method specifications that the implemented definition or definitions inherited. Theinterface ofthe factory object ofthe implementing class shall conform to the interfaces to be implemented by the factory object; and the interface of the instance objects of the implementing class shall conform to the interfaces to be implemented by the instance object:']
[[[164, 980], [510, 980], [510, 1006], [164, 1006]], '9.3.12 Parameterized classes']
[[[163, 1031], [1146, 1031], [1146, 1140], [163, 1140]], 'A parameterized class is a generic Or skeleton class that has formal parameters that will be replaced by one or more object-class-names or interface-names When it is expanded by substituting specific object- class-names or interface-names as actual parameters, a class is created that functions as a non- parameterized class.']
[[[163, 1163], [1140, 1163], [1140, 1218], [163, 1218]], 'An expansion ofa parameterized class is treated in all respects the same as ifit were a class that is not a parameterized class.']
[[[162, 1242], [1142, 1242], [1142, 1350], [162, 1350]], 'When a class is specified as the parameterized class in an EXPANDS phrase in the REPOSITORY paragraph,a new class (an instance ofa parameterized class) is created based on the specification ofthe parameterized class This class has its own factory object and is completely separate from any other instance of the same parameterized class.']
[[[161, 1376], [1144, 1376], [1144, 1482], [161, 1482]], 'Within a run unit; two classes with the same externalized object-class-name that are created by expanding the same parameterized class with the same actual parameters arethe same class instance. If two classes expand a parameterized class with different actual parameters, they are not the same class instance and shall not have the same externalized object-class-name:']
[[[912, 1549], [1064, 1549], [1064, 1578], [912, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '251']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 282 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [510, 190], [510, 216], [130, 216]], '93.13 Parameterized interfaces']
[[[127, 241], [1090, 241], [1090, 350], [127, 350]], 'A parameterized interface is a generic or skeleton interface that has formal parameters that will be replaced by one or more object-class-names or interface-names When it is expanded by substituting specific object-class-names 0r interface-names as actual parameters, an interface is created that functions as a non-parameterized interface']
[[[129, 372], [1102, 372], [1102, 428], [129, 428]], 'An expansion ofa parameterized interface is treated in all respects as if it were an interface that is not parameterized.']
[[[127, 450], [1106, 450], [1106, 534], [127, 534]], 'When an interface is specified as the parameterized interface in an EXPANDS phrase in the REPOSITORY paragraph, a new interface (an instance of a parameterized interface) is created based on the specification ofthe parameterized interface']
[[[128, 558], [1104, 558], [1104, 662], [128, 662]], 'Within a run unit; two interfaces with the same externalized interface-name that are created by expanding the same parameterized interface with the same actual parameters are the same interface instance. Iftwo interfaces expand a parameterized interface with different actual parameters, they are not the same interface instance and shall not have the same externalized interface-name.']
[[[130, 687], [400, 687], [400, 721], [130, 721]], '9.3.14 Object life cycle']
[[[130, 744], [334, 744], [334, 768], [130, 768]], '9.3.14.1 General']
[[[130, 793], [914, 793], [914, 825], [130, 825]], 'The life cycle for an object begins when it is created and ends when it is destroyed.']
[[[129, 847], [558, 847], [558, 878], [129, 878]], '9.3.14.2 Life cycle for factory objects']
[[[146, 899], [770, 899], [770, 931], [146, 931]], 'factory object is created before it is first referenced by a run unit']
[[[146, 952], [746, 952], [746, 982], [146, 982]], 'factory object is deleted after it is last referenced by a run unit']
[[[130, 1003], [572, 1003], [572, 1037], [130, 1037]], '93.14.3 Life cycle for instance objects']
[[[130, 1056], [1038, 1056], [1038, 1089], [130, 1089]], 'An instance object is created as the result of the NEW method being invoked on a factory object_']
[[[129, 1112], [1066, 1112], [1066, 1164], [129, 1164]], 'An instance object is destroyed either when it is determined that the object cannot take part in the continued execution ofthe run unit; 0r when the run unit terminates, whichever occurs first:']
[[[130, 1188], [1108, 1188], [1108, 1244], [130, 1244]], 'The timing and algorithm for the mechanism that determines whether or not an instance object can take part in the continued execution of the run unit is implementor-defined.']
[[[127, 1266], [1112, 1266], [1112, 1320], [127, 1320]], 'NOTE The process of determining whether or not an instance object can take part in continued execution and reclaiming resources unique to the object is generally referred to as garbage collection:']
[[[130, 1344], [170, 1344], [170, 1368], [130, 1368]], '9.4']
[[[202, 1342], [464, 1342], [464, 1368], [202, 1368]], 'User-defined functions']
[[[128, 1395], [1108, 1395], [1108, 1478], [128, 1478]], 'A user-defined function is an entity that is defined by the user by specifying a FUNCTION-ID paragraph rather than a PROGRAM-ID paragraph: The rules and behavior ofa user-defined function are similar to those for a program except that a user-defined function returns a value as specified by the RETURNING']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '252']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 283 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[162, 188], [1142, 188], [1142, 294], [162, 294]], 'phrasein the procedure division header. Also,arguments and returned values foruser-defined functions may not use the word ALL as a subscript In addition, a user defined function always possesses the recursive attribute and may call itself: A user-defined function is invoked by specifying a function identifier as described in 8.4.3.2, Function-identifier.']
[[[912, 1549], [1064, 1549], [1064, 1578], [912, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '253']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 189], [561, 189], [561, 225], [130, 225]], '10 Structured compilation group']
[[[130, 242], [296, 242], [296, 268], [130, 268]], '10.1 General']
[[[126, 293], [1104, 293], [1104, 430], [126, 430]], 'A structured compilation group consists of zero, one, or more compilation units that have been processed by text manipulation. A structured compilation group may contain compiler directives affecting compilation processing or source listings,as specified in Clause 7, Compiler directing facility: Text manipulation compiler directives may be logically represented in a structured compilation group but have no effect on compilation processing:']
[[[130, 454], [408, 454], [408, 482], [130, 482]], '10.2 Compilation units']
[[[145, 501], [531, 501], [531, 538], [145, 538]], 'compilation unit is one of the following:']
[[[203, 557], [951, 557], [951, 717], [203, 717]], 'program-prototype definition function-prototype definition program definition for an outermost program, including its nested programs a class definition an interface definition function definition']
[[[130, 741], [1098, 741], [1098, 820], [130, 820]], 'Successful compilation of each compilation unit that is a program definition; a function definition, or a class definition results in executable code that; when included in a run unit; constitutes a runtime module']
[[[145, 843], [1021, 843], [1021, 880], [145, 880]], 'compilation unit may contain one or more source units, depending on the type of definition:']
[[[130, 899], [1052, 899], [1052, 954], [130, 954]], 'The compilation units in a compilation group may be all or part ofa run unit or may be unrelated compilation units_']
[[[130, 980], [346, 980], [346, 1006], [130, 1006]], '10.3 Source units']
[[[130, 1031], [1094, 1031], [1094, 1088], [130, 1088]], 'A source unit begins with an identification division and ends with an end marker or the end of the compilation group. A source unit includes any contained source units The following are source units:']
[[[202, 1108], [948, 1108], [948, 1379], [202, 1379]], 'an outermost program-definition, including its contained program-definitions contained program-definition, including its contained program-definitions program-prototype-definition function-definition function-prototype-definition a class-definition, including its factory definition and instance definition factory definition, including its method definitions an instance definition, including its method definitions method definition an interface definition, including its method prototypes']
[[[132, 1398], [906, 1398], [906, 1431], [132, 1431]], 'A source unit may contain one or more divisions, specified in the following order:']
[[[130, 1452], [380, 1452], [380, 1482], [130, 1482]], '1)   identification division']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '254']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 285 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [410, 190], [410, 270], [164, 270]], '2) environment division 3) data division 4) procedure division']
[[[164, 294], [1142, 294], [1142, 352], [164, 352]], 'The beginning ofa division is indicated by its division header 0r,in the case of the identification division when its header is omitted; by one of the paragraph headers permitted in the identification division_']
[[[164, 372], [1142, 372], [1142, 430], [164, 430]], 'The end ofa division is indicated by the beginning ofthe next division, an end marker for the source unit; or the end of the compilation group_']
[[[164, 454], [500, 454], [500, 480], [164, 480]], '10.4 Contained source units']
[[[164, 502], [666, 502], [666, 534], [164, 534]], 'Source units may be contained directly or indirectly:']
[[[164, 557], [1142, 557], [1142, 641], [164, 641]], 'The factory definition and instance definition in a class definition are directly contained within thatclass definition: The methods within a factory definition 0r an instance definition are directly contained within that factory definition or instance definition, respectively_']
[[[162, 660], [1143, 660], [1143, 772], [162, 772]], 'Program-definitions within another program-definition may be contained directly or indirectly. A program definition directly contains another program-definition that is immediately nested within it: A program-definition indirectly contains another program-definition when there is one or more levels of nesting between the two of them:']
[[[161, 796], [1106, 796], [1106, 877], [161, 877]], 'When source units are contained within other source units, names of resources described in the containing source unit may be referenced in the contained source unit in accordance with the rules specified in 8.4.6, Scope of names_']
[[[163, 900], [1120, 900], [1120, 959], [163, 959]], 'The executable code resulting from compilation of a contained source unit is considered inseparable from the executable code resulting from compilation ofthe containing source unit']
[[[164, 980], [680, 980], [680, 1006], [164, 1006]], '10.5 Source elements and runtime elements']
[[[166, 1027], [841, 1027], [841, 1063], [166, 1063]], 'A source element is a source unit excluding any contained source units.']
[[[165, 1084], [415, 1084], [415, 1131], [165, 1131]], 'NOTE In the example: PROGRAM-ID']
[[[312, 1162], [454, 1162], [454, 1186], [312, 1186]], 'PROGRAM-ID .']
[[[348, 1214], [490, 1214], [490, 1238], [348, 1238]], 'PROGRAM-ID .']
[[[275, 1269], [499, 1269], [499, 1344], [275, 1344]], 'END PROGRAM END PROGRAM END PROGRAM']
[[[161, 1369], [1146, 1369], [1146, 1445], [161, 1445]], 'Program B is directly contained in Program A; Program C is directly contained in Program B; and Program C is indirectly contained in Program Program C is a source element; Program B, devoid of Program C, is a source element; and Program A,devoid of programs B and C,is a source element']
[[[902, 1549], [1054, 1549], [1054, 1578], [902, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '255']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 286 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 187], [1028, 187], [1028, 245], [129, 245]], 'A runtime element is the result of successful compilation ofa function, method, or a program containing a procedure division and consists of executable code included in a run unit']
[[[130, 265], [497, 265], [497, 301], [130, 301]], '10.6 COBOL compilation group']
[[[130, 322], [396, 322], [396, 348], [130, 348]], '10.6.1 General format']
[[[170, 392], [364, 392], [364, 563], [170, 563]], 'program-prototype function-prototype program-definition function-definition class-definition interface-definition']
[[[130, 602], [412, 602], [412, 630], [130, 630]], 'where program-prototype is:']
[[[144, 678], [858, 678], [858, 834], [144, 834]], 'IDENTIFICATION DIVISION. PROGRAM-ID: program-prototype-name-1 AS literal-1 ] IS PROTOTYPE_ [options-paragraph] environment-division']
[[[144, 852], [572, 852], [572, 944], [144, 944]], 'data-division procedure-division ] END PROGRAM program-prototype-name-1,']
[[[130, 980], [408, 980], [408, 1011], [130, 1011]], 'where function-prototype is:']
[[[144, 1055], [854, 1055], [854, 1324], [144, 1324]], 'IDENTIFICATION DIVISION: FUNCTION-ID:  function-prototype-name-1 AS literal-1 ] IS PROTOTYPE [options-paragraph ] environment-division | data-division procedure-division ] END FUNCTION function-prototype-name-1.']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '256']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 187], [446, 187], [446, 219], [164, 219]], 'where program-definition is:']
[[[196, 267], [466, 267], [466, 292], [196, 292]], 'IDENTIFICATION DIVISION_']
[[[734, 350], [832, 350], [832, 376], [734, 376]], 'COMMON']
[[[190, 387], [640, 387], [640, 417], [190, 417]], 'PROGRAM-ID: program-name-l [ AS literal-1 ]']
[[[671, 393], [699, 393], [699, 413], [671, 413]], 'IS']
[[[759, 398], [878, 398], [878, 458], [759, 458]], 'INITIAL RECURSIVE']
[[[932, 392], [1048, 392], [1048, 416], [932, 416]], 'PROGRAM']
[[[191, 505], [649, 505], [649, 710], [191, 710]], 'options-paragraph_ environment-division | data-division ] procedure-division [program-definition ] _ ] END PROGRAM program-name-1. |']
[[[164, 748], [442, 748], [442, 774], [164, 774]], 'where function-definition is:']
[[[196, 826], [472, 826], [472, 850], [196, 850]], 'IDENTIFICATION DIVISION:']
[[[178, 866], [688, 866], [688, 892], [178, 892]], 'FUNCTION-ID. user-function-name-1 AS literal-1 ].']
[[[178, 908], [558, 908], [558, 1086], [178, 1086]], 'options-paragraph ] environment-division ] data-division ] procedure-division END FUNCTION user-function-name-1.']
[[[902, 1552], [1054, 1552], [1054, 1578], [902, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '257']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [374, 190], [374, 214], [130, 214]], 'where class-definition is:']
[[[144, 255], [698, 255], [698, 394], [144, 394]], 'IDENTIFICATION DIVISION_ CLASS-ID. object-class-name-1 AS literal-1 ] [ IS FINAL ] INHERITS FROM object-class-name-2 } _] USING parameter-name-1 } ]']
[[[150, 408], [464, 408], [464, 560], [150, 560]], '[options-paragraph environment-division ] factory-definition ] instance-definition ] END CLASS object-class-name-l_']
[[[129, 594], [394, 594], [394, 627], [129, 627]], 'where factory-definition is:']
[[[144, 665], [637, 665], [637, 880], [144, 880]], "IDENTIFICATION DIVISION . ] FACTORY IMPLEMENTS interface-name-1 } '] [options-paragraph ] environment-division ] data-division ] procedure-division ] END FACTORY_"]
[[[130, 920], [406, 920], [406, 946], [130, 946]], 'where instance-definition is:']
[[[144, 988], [620, 988], [620, 1204], [144, 1204]], 'IDENTIFICATION DIVISION. ] OBJECT IMPLEMENTS interface-name-2 } _.] [options-paragraph ] environment-division ] data-division ] procedure-division ] END OBJECT .']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '258']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [446, 190], [446, 214], [164, 214]], 'where interface-definition is:']
[[[178, 256], [646, 256], [646, 320], [178, 320]], 'IDENTIFICATION DIVISION. ] INTERFACE-ID. interface-name-1 AS literal-1 ]']
[[[222, 339], [622, 339], [622, 404], [222, 404]], 'INHERITS FROM interface-name-2 } _] USING parameter-name-1 } _].']
[[[178, 426], [517, 426], [517, 542], [178, 542]], '[options-paragraph ] [environment-division ] procedure-division END INTERFACE interface-name-1_']
[[[164, 582], [436, 582], [436, 608], [164, 608]], 'where method-definition is:']
[[[192, 649], [482, 649], [482, 678], [192, 678]], 'IDENTIFICATION DIVISION. ]']
[[[350, 710], [646, 710], [646, 738], [350, 738]], 'method-name-1 AS literal-1 ]']
[[[189, 759], [313, 759], [313, 785], [189, 785]], 'METHOD-ID']
[[[384, 770], [430, 770], [430, 830], [384, 830]], 'GET SET']
[[[780, 758], [1038, 758], [1038, 786], [780, 786]], '[ OVERRIDE ] [ IS FINAL ] _']
[[[458, 786], [740, 786], [740, 815], [458, 815]], 'PROPERTY property-name-1']
[[[178, 860], [512, 860], [512, 1004], [178, 1004]], 'options-paragraph ] environment-division ] data-division ] procedure-division ] END METHOD [ method-name-1 ] _']
[[[163, 1040], [1146, 1040], [1146, 1114], [163, 1114]], 'NOTE Method-definition is included here for completeness, because it is a type of source element Method- definition is referenced in the general format of Clause 14, Procedure division A method-definition in a class- definition defines a method. A method-definition in an interface-definition defines a method prototype']
[[[163, 1135], [967, 1135], [967, 1171], [163, 1171]], 'where the following meta-language terms are described in the indicated subclauses:']
[[[164, 1192], [226, 1192], [226, 1216], [164, 1216]], 'Term']
[[[382, 1192], [490, 1192], [490, 1216], [382, 1216]], 'Subclause']
[[[162, 1243], [630, 1243], [630, 1352], [162, 1352]], 'data-division 13,Data division environment-division 12,Environment division options-paragraph 11.9, OPTIONS paragraph procedure-division 14,Procedure division']
[[[164, 1376], [398, 1376], [398, 1406], [164, 1406]], '10.6.2 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 1427], [1148, 1427], [1148, 1484], [164, 1484]], '1) Within compilation group, function-prototypes and program-prototypes shall precede all other types of source units.']
[[[902, 1549], [1054, 1549], [1054, 1578], [902, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '259']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 290 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 187], [1110, 187], [1110, 248], [130, 248]], '2) Ifa compilation group contains both a program definition and a program prototype definition with the same externalized name, the signatures of these two compilation units shall be the same:']
[[[130, 267], [1112, 267], [1112, 324], [130, 324]], '3) Ifa compilation group contains both a function definition anda function prototype definition with the same externalized name, the signatures of these two compilation units shall be the same:']
[[[130, 344], [1112, 344], [1112, 401], [130, 401]], '4) The   following   restrictions apply to program prototypes, function prototypes, and method prototypes:']
[[[166, 425], [850, 425], [850, 454], [166, 454]], 'a) The identification division shall not contain an ARITHMETIC clause.']
[[[166, 480], [912, 480], [912, 509], [166, 509]], 'b) The environment division shall not contain an object-computer paragraph.']
[[[167, 529], [1111, 529], [1111, 610], [167, 610]], 'C) The only clauses that may be specified in the SPECIAL-NAMES paragraph are the ALPHABET clause, the CURRENCY clause, the  DECIMAL-POINT clause, the LOCALE clause, and the SYMBOLIC-CHARACTERS clause.']
[[[169, 637], [850, 637], [850, 667], [169, 667]], 'd) The environment division shall not contain an input-output section.']
[[[167, 689], [704, 689], [704, 719], [167, 719]], 'e) The data division may contain only a linkage section.']
[[[167, 742], [874, 742], [874, 770], [167, 770]], '0 The procedure division shall contain only a procedure division header']
[[[130, 791], [1113, 791], [1113, 846], [130, 846]], '5) Compiler directives may appear in a structured compilation group as specified in 7.3, Compiler directives']
[[[130, 874], [364, 874], [364, 900], [130, 900]], '10.6.3 General rule']
[[[130, 926], [1112, 926], [1112, 983], [130, 983]], '1) Compilation of program   prototype definition or function   prototype definition generates information required for the external repository, as specified in 8.13, External repository:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '260']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 291 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [386, 190], [386, 216], [164, 216]], '10.7 End markers']
[[[164, 242], [348, 242], [348, 268], [164, 268]], '10.7.1 General']
[[[164, 294], [588, 294], [588, 320], [164, 320]], 'End markers indicate the end ofa definition:']
[[[164, 348], [430, 348], [430, 374], [164, 374]], '10.7.2 General format']
[[[177, 426], [548, 426], [548, 683], [177, 683]], 'PROGRAM program-prototype-name-1 PROGRAM program-name-1 CLASS object-class-name-1 FACTORY END FUNCTION function-prototype-name-1 FUNCTION user-function-name-1 OBJECT METHOD method-name-1 INTERFACE interface-name-1']
[[[164, 728], [398, 728], [398, 758], [164, 758]], '10.7.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 780], [1146, 780], [1146, 834], [164, 834]], '1) An end marker shall be present in every source unit that contains, is contained in, 0r precedes another source unit:']
[[[164, 858], [1146, 858], [1146, 916], [164, 916]], '2) Program-name-1 shall be identical to the program-name declared in preceding PROGRAM-ID paragraph']
[[[164, 935], [1146, 935], [1146, 1022], [164, 1022]], '3) Ifa PROGRAM-ID paragraph declaring a specific program-name is stated between the PROGRAM-ID paragraph and the END PROGRAM marker for program-name-1, then an END PROGRAM marker referencing program-name shall precede the END PROGRAM marker referencing program-name-1.']
[[[164, 1045], [1146, 1045], [1146, 1100], [164, 1100]], '4) Object-class-name-1 shall be identical to the object-class-name declared in the corresponding CLASS-ID paragraph']
[[[164, 1123], [1146, 1123], [1146, 1202], [164, 1202]], '5) Method-name-1 shall be identical to the method-name declared in the corresponding METHOD-ID paragraph  If the PROPERTY phrase is specified in the METHOD-ID paragraph,method-name-1 shall be omitted:']
[[[200, 1229], [1146, 1229], [1146, 1284], [200, 1284]], 'Interface-name-1 shall be identical to the interface-name declared in the corresponding INTERFACE-ID paragraph:']
[[[166, 1303], [1149, 1303], [1149, 1364], [166, 1364]], '7) User-function-name-1 shall be identical to the user-function-name declared in the corresponding FUNCTION-ID paragraph']
[[[164, 1387], [1145, 1387], [1145, 1442], [164, 1442]], '8) Program-prototype-name-1 shall be identical to the program-prototype-name declared in the corresponding PROGRAM-ID paragraph_']
[[[902, 1552], [1054, 1552], [1054, 1578], [902, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '261']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 292 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 189], [1112, 189], [1112, 247], [130, 247]], '9) Function-prototype-name-1 shall be identical to the function-prototype-name declared in the corresponding FUNCTION-ID paragraph.']
[[[130, 268], [364, 268], [364, 294], [130, 294]], '10.7.4 General rule']
[[[130, 319], [744, 319], [744, 351], [130, 351]], '1) An end marker indicates the end ofthe specified source unit']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '262']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']
