[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [492, 190], [492, 220], [164, 220]], '11 Identification division']
[[[164, 242], [330, 242], [330, 268], [164, 268]], '11.1 General']
[[[162, 293], [1102, 293], [1102, 346], [162, 346]], 'The identification division identifies the program; function, class, factory object, object, method, or interface.']
[[[164, 373], [964, 373], [964, 405], [164, 405]], 'The paragraph header identifies the type Of information contained in the paragraph']
[[[164, 426], [600, 426], [600, 452], [164, 452]], '11.2 Identification division structure']
[[[164, 479], [432, 479], [432, 507], [164, 507]], '11.2.1 General format']
[[[192, 546], [478, 546], [478, 572], [192, 572]], 'IDENTIFICATION DIVISION. ]']
[[[178, 600], [424, 600], [424, 846], [178, 846]], 'program-id-paragraph function-id-paragraph class-id-paragraph factory-paragraph object-paragraph method-id-paragraph interface-id-paragraph [options-paragraph ]']
[[[164, 869], [967, 869], [967, 907], [164, 907]], 'where the following meta-language terms are described in the indicated subclauses:']
[[[164, 930], [226, 930], [226, 954], [164, 954]], 'Term']
[[[382, 928], [490, 928], [490, 954], [382, 954]], 'Subclause']
[[[160, 982], [687, 982], [687, 1196], [160, 1196]], 'class-id-paragraph 11.3,CLASS-ID paragraph factory-paragraph 11.4,FACTORY paragraph function-id-paragraph 11.5, FUNCTION-ID paragraph interface-id-paragraph11.6,INTERFACE-ID paragraph method-id-paragraph 11.7, METHOD-ID paragraph object-paragraph 11.8, OBJECT paragraph options-paragraph 11.9, OPTIONS paragraph program-id-paragraph 11.10, PROGRAM-ID paragraph']
[[[876, 1552], [1034, 1552], [1034, 1578], [876, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '263']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 294 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 189], [432, 189], [432, 222], [130, 222]], '113 CLASS-ID paragraph']
[[[130, 242], [314, 242], [314, 268], [130, 268]], '113.1 General']
[[[126, 292], [1098, 292], [1098, 351], [126, 351]], 'The CLASS-ID paragraph indicates that this identification division is introducing a class definition and specifies the name that identifies the class and assigns class attributes to the class.']
[[[130, 374], [396, 374], [396, 400], [130, 400]], '113.2 General format']
[[[144, 440], [712, 440], [712, 542], [144, 542]], 'CLASS-ID. object-class-name-1 [ AS literal-1 ] [IS FINAL ] INHERITS FROM object-class-name-2 } _] USING parameter-name-1 } _]-']
[[[130, 580], [364, 580], [364, 608], [130, 608]], '113.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 632], [1100, 632], [1100, 660], [130, 660]], '1) Literal-1 shall be an alphanumeric literal or a national literal and shall not be a figurative constant']
[[[130, 681], [1112, 681], [1112, 736], [130, 736]], '2)   Object-class-name-2 shall be the name of a class specified in the REPOSITORY paragraph of this source element']
[[[130, 762], [988, 762], [988, 790], [130, 790]], '3) Object-class-name-2 shall not be the name of the class declared by this class definition_']
[[[130, 815], [970, 815], [970, 844], [130, 844]], '4) Object-class-name-2 shall not inherit from object-class-name-1 directly or indirectly_']
[[[130, 868], [958, 868], [958, 896], [130, 896]], '5) Object-class-name-2 shall not be the name of a class defined with the FINAL clause:']
[[[131, 918], [1112, 918], [1112, 1003], [131, 1003]], '6) If two or more different methods with the same name are inherited, none of them may be specified with the FINAL clause. If the same method is inherited from one superclass through two or more intermediate superclasses, it may be specified with the FINAL clause:']
[[[130, 1026], [880, 1026], [880, 1054], [130, 1054]], '7) A given class name shall not appear more than once in an INHERITS clause:']
[[[130, 1076], [1112, 1076], [1112, 1134], [130, 1134]], '8) Parameter-name-1 shall be a name specified in class-specifier or an interface-specifier in the REPOSITORY paragraph ofthis class definition_']
[[[130, 1157], [894, 1157], [894, 1186], [130, 1186]], '9) A given parameter-name shall not appear more than once in a USING clause.']
[[[130, 1212], [376, 1212], [376, 1236], [130, 1236]], '11.3.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1264], [1110, 1264], [1110, 1318], [130, 1318]], '1) Object-class-name-1 names the class declared by this class definition. However, literal-1,if specified, is the name of the class that is externalized t0 the operating environment']
[[[130, 1340], [1110, 1340], [1110, 1399], [130, 1399]], '2) The INHERITS clause specifies the names of classes that are inherited by object-class-name-1 according to 9.3.9,Class inheritance']
[[[130, 1419], [992, 1419], [992, 1452], [130, 1452]], '3) If the FINAL clause is specified, the class shall not be the superclass for any other class:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '264']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 295 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [1146, 190], [1146, 244], [164, 244]], '4) If the same class is inherited more than once, then only one copy ofthe data for that class is added to object-class-name-1.']
[[[200, 265], [1148, 265], [1148, 367], [200, 367]], 'NOTE While the same class cannot be directly inherited more than once, a class can be indirectly inherited multiple times For example, suppose class D inherits from classes B and C, and classes B and € both inherit from class A. In this example; class D indirectly inherits class A twice, since class A is both a superclass of B,as well as a superclass of C']
[[[164, 387], [1146, 387], [1146, 470], [164, 470]], '5) The USING clause specifies that this is a parameterized class. Parameter-name-1 is the name given to the formal  parameter: See 9.3.12, Parameterized classes, for  details  of the behavior of parameterized class.']
[[[167, 493], [1146, 493], [1146, 550], [167, 550]], '6) Parameter-name-1 may be specified within this class definition only where an object-class-name or an interface-name is permitted:']
[[[876, 1552], [1034, 1552], [1034, 1578], [876, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1550], [1146, 1550], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '265']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 296 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 189], [436, 189], [436, 222], [130, 222]], '11.4 FACTORY paragraph']
[[[130, 242], [314, 242], [314, 268], [130, 268]], '114.1 General']
[[[129, 291], [1085, 291], [1085, 327], [129, 327]], 'The FACTORY paragraph indicates that this identification division is introducing a factory definition:']
[[[130, 348], [396, 348], [396, 374], [130, 374]], '11.4.2 General format']
[[[132, 404], [628, 404], [628, 432], [132, 432]], 'FACTORY_ [MPLEMENTS interface-name-1 } .]']
[[[130, 466], [364, 466], [364, 494], [130, 494]], '11.4.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 516], [1112, 516], [1112, 574], [130, 574]], '1) Interface-name-1 shall be the name of an interface specified in the REPOSITORY paragraph of the containing class definition:']
[[[130, 596], [1112, 596], [1112, 652], [130, 652]], '2) Each method prototype in each implemented interface shall be such thatthe factory interface of this class conforms to all implemented interfaces.']
[[[130, 676], [376, 676], [376, 702], [130, 702]], '11.4.4 General rules']
[[[130, 728], [1112, 728], [1112, 784], [130, 784]], '1) The IMPLEMENTS clause specifies the names of the interfaces that are implemented by the factory object ofthe containing class according to 9.3.11, Interface implementation:']
[[[130, 808], [156, 808], [156, 834], [130, 834]], '2)']
[[[183, 806], [822, 806], [822, 836], [183, 836]], 'factory object implements an interface int-1 in the following cases:']
[[[166, 860], [908, 860], [908, 890], [166, 890]], 'a) the factory object is defined with an IMPLEMENTS clause specifying int-1,']
[[[166, 912], [784, 912], [784, 943], [166, 943]], 'b) the factory object implements an interface that inherits int-1,']
[[[167, 965], [1082, 965], [1082, 996], [167, 996]], 'C) the class containing the factory object inherits a class whose factory object implements int-1_']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '266']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 297 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 189], [516, 189], [516, 222], [164, 222]], '11.5 FUNCTION-ID paragraph']
[[[164, 242], [348, 242], [348, 268], [164, 268]], '11.5.1 General']
[[[163, 292], [1134, 292], [1134, 346], [163, 346]], 'The FUNCTION-ID paragraph specifies the name by which a function is identified and assigns selected attributes to that function.']
[[[164, 374], [430, 374], [430, 400], [164, 400]], '11.5.2 General format']
[[[164, 426], [378, 426], [378, 454], [164, 454]], 'Format 1 (definition):']
[[[166, 482], [678, 482], [678, 510], [166, 510]], 'FUNCTION-ID_ user-function-name-1 AS literal-1 ]']
[[[164, 545], [380, 545], [380, 574], [164, 574]], 'Format 2 (prototype):']
[[[166, 598], [880, 598], [880, 631], [166, 631]], 'FUNCTION-ID . function-prototype-name-1 AS literal-1 ] IS PROTOTYPE_']
[[[164, 664], [386, 664], [386, 692], [164, 692]], '11.5.3 Syntax rule']
[[[164, 716], [1146, 716], [1146, 771], [164, 771]], '1) Literal-1 shall be an alphanumeric literal or a national literal and shall be neither figurative constant nor a zero-length literal:']
[[[164, 794], [410, 794], [410, 820], [164, 820]], '11.5.44 General rules']
[[[164, 848], [270, 848], [270, 872], [164, 872]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[164, 899], [1148, 899], [1148, 956], [164, 956]], '1) User-function-name-1 names the function declared by this function definition. However; literal-1,if specified, is the name of the function that is externalized to the operating environment']
[[[164, 980], [272, 980], [272, 1004], [164, 1004]], 'FORMAT 2']
[[[164, 1031], [1146, 1031], [1146, 1110], [164, 1110]], '2)   Function-prototype-name-1 names the function prototype declared by this definition. However, literal-1, if specified, is the name of the function prototype that is externalized to the operating environment;']
[[[876, 1552], [1034, 1552], [1034, 1578], [876, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '267']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 298 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 188], [493, 188], [493, 224], [130, 224]], '11.6 INTERFACE-ID paragraph']
[[[130, 242], [314, 242], [314, 268], [130, 268]], '11.6.1 General']
[[[128, 292], [1066, 292], [1066, 372], [128, 372]], 'The INTERFACE-ID paragraph indicates that this identification division is introducing an interface definition, specifies the name that identifies the interface, and assigns interface attributes to the interface:.']
[[[130, 400], [396, 400], [396, 426], [130, 426]], '11.6.2 General format']
[[[144, 468], [618, 468], [618, 568], [144, 568]], 'INTERFACE-ID interface-name-1 AS literal-1 ] INHERITS FROM interface-name-2 } _ ] USING parameter-name-1 } _]-']
[[[130, 606], [364, 606], [364, 634], [130, 634]], '11.6.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 654], [1112, 654], [1112, 713], [130, 713]], '1) Literal-1 shall be an alphanumeric literal or a national literal and shall be neither figurative constant nor a zero-length literal:']
[[[130, 734], [1112, 734], [1112, 788], [130, 788]], '2) Interface-name-2 shall be the name of an interface specified in the REPOSITORY paragraph of this source element:']
[[[130, 812], [915, 812], [915, 844], [130, 844]], '3) Interface-name-2 shall not inherit directly or indirectly from interface-name-1_']
[[[130, 866], [1112, 866], [1112, 925], [130, 925]], '4) Parameter-name-1 shall be a name specified in a class-specifier or an interface-specifier in the REPOSITORY paragraph of this interface definition:']
[[[130, 946], [1110, 946], [1110, 998], [130, 998]], '5) If a given method-name is inherited from more than one interface, the method prototype in each inherited interface shall be such that this interface conforms to all inherited interfaces.']
[[[130, 1026], [920, 1026], [920, 1054], [130, 1054]], '6) A given interface-name shall not appear more than once in an INHERITS clause']
[[[130, 1080], [898, 1080], [898, 1108], [130, 1108]], '7) A given parameter-name shall not appear more than once in a USING clause:']
[[[130, 1132], [376, 1132], [376, 1158], [130, 1158]], '11.6.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1184], [1114, 1184], [1114, 1240], [130, 1240]], '1) Interface-name-1 names the interface declared by this interface definition. However; literal-1, if specified, is the name ofthe interface that is externalized to the operating environment:']
[[[130, 1262], [1110, 1262], [1110, 1318], [130, 1318]], '2) The INHERITS clause specifies the names of interfaces that are inherited by interface-name-1 according to 9.3.10,Interface inheritance']
[[[130, 1340], [1112, 1340], [1112, 1398], [130, 1398]], '3) The USING clause specifies that this is a parameterized interface Parameter-name-1 is the name given to the formal parameter:']
[[[130, 1419], [1112, 1419], [1112, 1476], [130, 1476]], '4) Parameter-name-1 shall be specified within this interface definition only where an object-class- name 0r an interface-name is permitted:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '268']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 299 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 189], [500, 189], [500, 222], [164, 222]], '11.7 METHOD-ID paragraph']
[[[164, 242], [348, 242], [348, 268], [164, 268]], '11.7.1 General']
[[[161, 292], [1142, 292], [1142, 372], [161, 372]], 'The METHOD-ID paragraph indicates that this identification division is introducing a method definition, specifies the name that identifies the method or method prototype,and assigns method attributes to the method.']
[[[164, 400], [430, 400], [430, 426], [164, 426]], '11.7.2 General format']
[[[348, 492], [652, 492], [652, 520], [348, 520]], 'method-name-1 AS literal-1 ]']
[[[191, 540], [314, 540], [314, 568], [191, 568]], 'METHOD-ID_']
[[[376, 550], [418, 550], [418, 574], [376, 574]], 'GET']
[[[780, 538], [1036, 538], [1036, 566], [780, 566]], '[ OVERRIDE ] [ IS FINAL ]']
[[[448, 566], [728, 566], [728, 597], [448, 597]], 'PROPERTY property-name-1']
[[[374, 588], [416, 588], [416, 612], [374, 612]], 'SET']
[[[164, 670], [398, 670], [398, 698], [164, 698]], '11.73 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 721], [1145, 721], [1145, 778], [164, 778]], '1) Literal-1 shall be an alphanumeric literal or a national literal and shall be neither figurative constant nor a zero-length literal:']
[[[164, 796], [843, 796], [843, 832], [164, 832]], '2) The OVERRIDE phrase shall not be specified in a method prototype']
[[[164, 852], [1146, 852], [1146, 935], [164, 935]], '3) If the OVERRIDE phrase is specified, there shall be a method with the same method resolution signature as the method declared by this method definition defined in a superclass The method in the superclass shall not be defined with the FINAL clause:']
[[[164, 956], [588, 956], [588, 989], [164, 989]], '4) If the OVERRIDE phrase is not specified:']
[[[201, 1010], [1146, 1010], [1146, 1068], [201, 1068]], 'a) if this method definition is contained in a class definition, no inherited method shall have the same method resolution signature as the method declared by this method definition']
[[[200, 1090], [1146, 1090], [1146, 1168], [200, 1168]], 'b) if this method definition is contained in an interface definition, no inherited method prototype shall have the same method resolution signature as the method prototype declared by this method definition:']
[[[164, 1193], [1148, 1193], [1148, 1274], [164, 1274]], '5) If property-name-1 is specified as a data-name in the working-storage section of the containing object definition, the PROPERTY clause shall not be specified in the data description entry of that data-name:']
[[[200, 1298], [1146, 1298], [1146, 1382], [200, 1382]], 'If the GET phrase is specified, then the method shall have no USING phrase parameters specified in the procedure division header and shall have a single RETURNING phrase. The returning item shall not be an object reference described with the ACTIVE-CLASS phrase:']
[[[164, 1404], [1148, 1404], [1148, 1488], [164, 1488]], '7) Ifthe SET phrase is specified, then the method shall have a single USING parameter specified in the procedure division header and no RETURNING phrase The USING parameter shall not be an object reference described with the ACTIVE-CLASS phrase']
[[[876, 1552], [1034, 1552], [1034, 1578], [876, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '269']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 300 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 187], [763, 187], [763, 219], [130, 219]], '8) The FINAL clause shall not be specified in a method prototype']
[[[130, 239], [1112, 239], [1112, 402], [130, 402]], '9) If method-name-1 or literal-1 is the same as a method-name inherited or implemented by the containing definition, the parameter declarations, returning item, and exceptions that may be raised on the procedure division header shall obey the rules of conformance according to 9.3.8.2.3, Conformance between interfaces, such that the interface described by the factory 0r instance definition containing this method definition conforms to the interface described by the factory or instance definition containing the inherited or implemented method definition:']
[[[130, 428], [376, 428], [376, 452], [130, 452]], '11.7.4 General rules']
[[[130, 480], [978, 480], [978, 508], [130, 508]], '1) The name ofthe method declared by this method definition is determined as follows:']
[[[166, 528], [872, 528], [872, 560], [166, 560]], 'a) Ifthe PROPERTY clause is specified, the name is implementor-defined.']
[[[166, 582], [1112, 582], [1112, 640], [166, 640]], 'b) Otherwise, the name is method-name-1. However, literal-1,if specified, is the name of method that is externalized to the operating environment:']
[[[130, 664], [944, 664], [944, 692], [130, 692]], '2) The OVERRIDE phrase indicates that this method overrides the inherited method.']
[[[130, 714], [990, 714], [990, 744], [130, 744]], '3) The FINAL clause indicates that this method shall not be overridden in any subclasses.']
[[[130, 770], [1112, 770], [1112, 820], [130, 820]], '4) The name of this method may be referenced in the invocation of a method for an object of the class in which this method is defined.']
[[[130, 846], [1112, 846], [1112, 952], [130, 952]], '5) Ifa given user-defined word is defined in the data division ofthis method definition and in the data division of the containing object definition, the use of that word in this method refers to the declaration in this method The declaration in the containing object definition is inaccessible to this method']
[[[130, 977], [1012, 977], [1012, 1009], [130, 1009]], '6) If the GET phrase is specified, this method is a get property method for property-name-1.']
[[[130, 1028], [1013, 1028], [1013, 1064], [130, 1064]], '7) Ifthe SET phrase is specified, this method is a set property method for property-name-1.']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '270']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 301 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 189], [450, 189], [450, 222], [164, 222]], '11.8 OBJECT paragraph']
[[[164, 242], [348, 242], [348, 268], [164, 268]], '11.8.1 General']
[[[163, 290], [1091, 290], [1091, 346], [163, 346]], 'The OBJECT paragraph indicates that this identification division is introducing an instance object definition.']
[[[164, 374], [430, 374], [430, 400], [164, 400]], '11.8.2 General format']
[[[166, 432], [606, 432], [606, 458], [166, 458]], 'OBJECT IMPLEMENTS interface-name-1 }']
[[[164, 492], [398, 492], [398, 522], [164, 522]], '11.8.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 541], [1147, 541], [1147, 600], [164, 600]], '1) Interface-name-1 shall be the name of an interface specified in the REPOSITORY paragraph of the containing class definition']
[[[164, 624], [1146, 624], [1146, 678], [164, 678]], '2) Each method prototype in each implemented interface shall be such that the object interface of this class conforms to all implemented interfaces_']
[[[164, 704], [412, 704], [412, 728], [164, 728]], '11.8.4 General rules']
[[[164, 752], [1148, 752], [1148, 810], [164, 810]], '1) The IMPLEMENTS clause specifies the names ofthe interfaces that are implemented by the object of the containing class according to 9.3.11,Interface implementation_']
[[[164, 832], [888, 832], [888, 864], [164, 864]], '2) An instance object implements an interface intf-1 in the following cases:']
[[[200, 888], [958, 888], [958, 916], [200, 916]], 'a) the instance object is defined with an IMPLEMENTS clause specifying intf-1,']
[[[200, 936], [838, 936], [838, 969], [200, 969]], 'b) the instance object implements an interface that inherits intf-1,']
[[[203, 990], [1142, 990], [1142, 1022], [203, 1022]], 'c) the class containing the instance object inherits a class whose instance object implements intf-1_']
[[[876, 1552], [1034, 1552], [1034, 1578], [876, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '271']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 302 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 189], [432, 189], [432, 221], [130, 221]], '11.9 OPTIONS paragraph']
[[[130, 242], [314, 242], [314, 268], [130, 268]], '11.9.1 General']
[[[130, 294], [1108, 294], [1108, 346], [130, 346]], 'The OPTIONS paragraph specifies information for use by the compiler in generating executable code for a source unit']
[[[130, 374], [396, 374], [396, 400], [130, 400]], '11.9.2 General format']
[[[132, 440], [474, 440], [474, 710], [132, 710]], 'OPTIONS_ [arithmetic-clause default-rounded-clause entry-convention-clause float-binary-clause ] float-decimal-clause ] initialize-clause ] intermediate-rounding-clause ] []']
[[[130, 742], [352, 742], [352, 770], [130, 770]], '11.93 Syntax rule']
[[[130, 791], [971, 791], [971, 823], [130, 823]], '1) If any ofthe clauses are specified, then there shall be a terminating separator period.']
[[[130, 847], [364, 847], [364, 875], [130, 875]], '11.9.4 General rule']
[[[130, 896], [1112, 896], [1112, 983], [130, 983]], '1) The clauses in the OPTIONS paragraph apply to the source element in which they are specified and to allsource elements contained in that source element unless overridden by a clause in an OPTIONS paragraph in a contained source element;']
[[[130, 1004], [448, 1004], [448, 1032], [130, 1032]], '11.9.5 ARITHMETIC clause']
[[[130, 1055], [992, 1055], [992, 1087], [130, 1087]], 'The ARITHMETIC clause specifies the method used in developing the intermediate results.']
[[[130, 1110], [416, 1110], [416, 1136], [130, 1136]], '11.9.5.1 General format']
[[[329, 1188], [546, 1188], [546, 1286], [329, 1286]], 'NATIVE STANDARD-BINARY STANDARD-DECIMAL']
[[[143, 1223], [302, 1223], [302, 1248], [143, 1248]], 'ARITHMETIC IS']
[[[130, 1328], [396, 1328], [396, 1357], [130, 1357]], '11.9.5.2 General rules']
[[[130, 1377], [1113, 1377], [1113, 1486], [130, 1486]], '1) If the NATIVE phrase is specified, the techniques used in handling arithmetic expressions and intrinsic functions shall be those specified by the implementor,and the techniques used in handling arithmetic statements and the SUM clause shall be those specified for native arithmetic in 8.8.1.3, Native arithmetic:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '272']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 303 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 185], [1148, 185], [1148, 270], [164, 270]], '2) If the STANDARD-BINARY phrase is specified, the techniques used in handling   arithmetic expressions, arithmetic statements, the SUM clause, and integer and numeric functions shall be as described for standard-binary arithmetic in 8.8.1.4, Standard-binary arithmetic:']
[[[200, 292], [1148, 292], [1148, 339], [200, 339]], 'NOTE 1 Implementors are strongly encouraged to provide support for the STANDARD-DECIMAL phrase of the ARITHMETIC clause.']
[[[200, 366], [864, 366], [864, 390], [200, 390]], 'NOTE 2 The STANDARD-BINARY mode of arithmetic is an obsolete feature.']
[[[200, 412], [1148, 412], [1148, 485], [200, 485]], 'Unlike other obsolete features, it is intended that interest in this facility will be reevaluated during the next revision of standard COBOLand before any final decision is made on whether ornotto remove it from the next revision.']
[[[164, 509], [1148, 509], [1148, 593], [164, 593]], '3) If the STANDARD-DECIMAL   phrase is   specified, the techniques used in handling   arithmetic expressions, arithmetic statements, the SUM clause, and integer and numeric functions shall be as described for standard-decimal arithmetic in 8.8.1.5, Standard-decimal arithmetic.']
[[[164, 616], [1146, 616], [1146, 675], [164, 675]], '4) Ifthe ARITHMETIC clause is not specified in this source element or a containing source element,itis as if the ARITHMETIC clause were specified with the NATIVE phrase']
[[[164, 698], [560, 698], [560, 724], [164, 724]], '11.9.6 DEFAULT ROUNDED clause']
[[[164, 752], [368, 752], [368, 776], [164, 776]], '11.9.6.1 General']
[[[162, 800], [1144, 800], [1144, 858], [162, 858]], 'The DEFAULT ROUNDED clause specifies the type ofrounding that applies when ROUNDED phrases of arithmetic statements, SOURCE clauses, and SUM clauses do not specify a rounding mode']
[[[164, 882], [450, 882], [450, 908], [164, 908]], '11.9.6.2 General format']
[[[506, 966], [798, 966], [798, 1244], [506, 1244]], 'AWAY-FROM-ZERO NEAREST-AWAY-FROM-ZERO NEAREST-EVEN NEAREST-TOWARD-ZERO PROHIBITED TOWARD-GREATER TOWARD-LESSER TRUNCATION']
[[[178, 1090], [476, 1090], [476, 1114], [178, 1114]], 'DEFAULT ROUNDED MODE IS']
[[[164, 1290], [430, 1290], [430, 1316], [164, 1316]], '11.9.6.3 General rules']
[[[164, 1340], [1145, 1340], [1145, 1424], [164, 1424]], '1) The DEFAULT ROUNDED clause specifies the type of rounding that applies to any explicit ROUNDED phrase that does not include an explicit MODE phrase. The meaning ofthe rounding types associated with the DEFAULT ROUNDED clause is described in 14.7.4,ROUNDED phrase:']
[[[876, 1552], [1034, 1552], [1034, 1578], [876, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '273']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 304 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 188], [1112, 188], [1112, 244], [130, 244]], '2) If the DEFAULT ROUNDED clause is not specified, DEFAULT ROUNDED MODE IS NEAREST-AWAY- FROM-ZERO is implied.']
[[[166, 264], [1112, 264], [1112, 314], [166, 314]], 'NOTE NEAREST-AWAY-FROM-ZERO is the rounding mode provided by the ROUNDED phrase in earlier COBOL standards.']
[[[130, 341], [538, 341], [538, 368], [130, 368]], '11.9.7 ENTRY-CONVENTION clause']
[[[130, 394], [334, 394], [334, 420], [130, 420]], '11.9.7.1 General']
[[[130, 444], [1048, 444], [1048, 498], [130, 498]], 'The ENTRY-CONVENTION clause specifies the information to be used when activating a runtime element;']
[[[129, 525], [416, 525], [416, 552], [129, 552]], '11.9.7.2 General format']
[[[419, 606], [668, 606], [668, 670], [419, 670]], 'COBOL entry-convention-name-1']
[[[144, 618], [390, 618], [390, 644], [144, 644]], 'ENTRY-CONVENTION IS']
[[[130, 706], [370, 706], [370, 734], [130, 734]], '11.9.7.3 Syntax rule']
[[[130, 755], [1112, 755], [1112, 838], [130, 838]], '1) The ENTRY-CONVENTION clause may be specified only in a class definition, a function definition, a function-prototype definition, an interface definition, a program prototype definition, or a program definition that is not contained within another program:']
[[[130, 862], [396, 862], [396, 888], [130, 888]], '11.9.7.4 General rules']
[[[130, 912], [1112, 912], [1112, 971], [130, 971]], '1) The ENTRY-CONVENTION clause specifies the convention to be used for activating the runtime element corresponding to the source element in which this clause is specified.']
[[[163, 991], [1112, 991], [1112, 1069], [163, 1069]], 'NOTE All information required t0 interact successfully with a runtime element can be made available to the compiler via this entry convention association; this includes items such as name case sensitivity, how arguments are passed, and stack management']
[[[130, 1089], [1112, 1089], [1112, 1171], [130, 1171]], '2) When COBOL is specified, the naming convention and mapping of method-names and program- names are as specified in 8.3.2.2, User-defined words; other aspects of the entry convention are implementor-defined,']
[[[130, 1194], [1112, 1194], [1112, 1248], [130, 1248]], '3) When entry-convention-name-1 is specified, the meaning of the entry convention is implementor- defined.']
[[[130, 1273], [972, 1273], [972, 1305], [130, 1305]], '4) When ENTRY-CONVENTION is not specified, the entry convention used is as follows:']
[[[166, 1325], [1112, 1325], [1112, 1382], [166, 1382]], 'a) Ifa class definition includes the INHERITS clause, the entry convention is inherited from the first class specified in the INHERITS clause']
[[[166, 1405], [1112, 1405], [1112, 1462], [166, 1462]], 'b) If an interface definition includes the INHERITS clause, the entry convention is inherited from the first interface specified in the INHERITS clause:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '274']
[[[204, 1550], [356, 1550], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 305 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[236, 187], [708, 187], [708, 219], [236, 219]], 'In all other cases, the entry convention is COBOL.']
[[[164, 242], [506, 242], [506, 268], [164, 268]], '11.9.8 FLOAT-BINARY clause']
[[[163, 295], [368, 295], [368, 320], [163, 320]], '11.9.8.1 General']
[[[164, 345], [1084, 345], [1084, 403], [164, 403]], 'The FLOAT-BINARY clause specifies the endianness that is implied for data items described with standard binary floating-point usages:']
[[[164, 426], [450, 426], [450, 452], [164, 452]], '11.9.8.2 General format']
[[[484, 504], [688, 504], [688, 566], [484, 566]], 'HIGH-ORDER-LEFT HIGH-ORDER-RIGHT']
[[[178, 522], [456, 522], [456, 546], [178, 546]], 'FLOAT-BINARY DEFAULT IS']
[[[164, 610], [418, 610], [418, 638], [164, 638]], '11.9.8.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 660], [1148, 660], [1148, 768], [164, 768]], '1) When the FLOAT-BINARY clause is not specified, the implementor shall specify whether the HIGH- ORDER-LEFT phrase or the HIGH-ORDER-RIGHT phrase is implied for the data description entry of any data item described with a standard binary floating-point usage in which an endianness-phrase is not specified.']
[[[164, 791], [1147, 791], [1147, 876], [164, 876]], '2) When the HIGH-ORDER-LEFT phrase is specified, the HIGH-ORDER-LEFT phrase is implied for the USAGE clause in the data description entry of any data item described with standard binary floating-point usage in which an endianness-phrase is not specified.']
[[[164, 898], [1147, 898], [1147, 982], [164, 982]], '3) When the HIGH-ORDER-RIGHT phrase is specified, the HIGH-ORDER-RIGHT phrase is implied for the USAGE clause in the data description entry of any data item described with a standard binary floating-point usage in which an endianness-phrase is not specified.']
[[[164, 1004], [522, 1004], [522, 1030], [164, 1030]], '11.9.9 FLOAT-DECIMAL clause']
[[[164, 1058], [368, 1058], [368, 1084], [164, 1084]], '11.9.9.1 General']
[[[164, 1106], [1120, 1106], [1120, 1165], [164, 1165]], 'The FLOAT-DECIMAL clause specifies the encoding and the endianness that is implied for data items described with standard decimal floating-point usages']
[[[164, 1188], [450, 1188], [450, 1216], [164, 1216]], '11.9.9.2 General format']
[[[178, 1282], [470, 1282], [470, 1306], [178, 1306]], 'FLOAT-DECIMAL DEFAULT IS']
[[[505, 1272], [692, 1272], [692, 1331], [505, 1331]], 'encoding-phrase endianness-phrase']
[[[876, 1552], [1034, 1552], [1034, 1578], [876, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '275']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 306 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [388, 190], [388, 218], [130, 218]], 'where encoding-phrase is:']
[[[156, 258], [366, 258], [366, 318], [156, 318]], 'BINARY-ENCODING DECIMAL-ENCODING']
[[[129, 356], [408, 356], [408, 387], [129, 387]], 'where endianness-phrase is:']
[[[156, 426], [360, 426], [360, 486], [156, 486]], 'HIGH-ORDER-LEFT HIGH-ORDER-RIGHT']
[[[130, 526], [384, 526], [384, 556], [130, 556]], '11.9.93 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 577], [1112, 577], [1112, 664], [130, 664]], '1) When the BINARY-ENCODING phrase is specified,the BINARY-ENCODING phrase is implied for the USAGE clause in the data description entry of any data item described with a standard decimal floating-point usage in which an encoding-phrase is not specified.']
[[[130, 682], [1112, 682], [1112, 767], [130, 767]], '2) When the DECIMAL-ENCODING phrase is specified, the DECIMAL-ENCODING phrase is implied for the USAGE clause in the data description entry of any data item described with a standard decimal floating-point usage in which an encoding-phrase is not specified:']
[[[130, 787], [1112, 787], [1112, 900], [130, 900]], '3) When neither the BINARY-ENCODING phrase northe DECIMAL-ENCODING phrase is specified in the FLOAT-DECIMAL clause, the implementor shall specify which phrase is implied for the data description entry of any data item described with a standard decimal floating-point usage in which an encoding-phrase is not specified:']
[[[130, 919], [1112, 919], [1112, 1006], [130, 1006]], '4) When the HIGH-ORDER-LEFT phrase is specified, the HIGH-ORDER-LEFT phrase is implied for the USAGE clause in the data description entry of any data item described with a standard decimal floating-] ~point usage in which an endianness-phrase is not specified.']
[[[130, 1024], [1112, 1024], [1112, 1109], [130, 1109]], '5) When the HIGH-ORDER-RIGHT phrase is specified, the HIGH-ORDER-RIGHT phrase is in effect for the USAGE clause in the data description entry of any data item described with a standard decimal floating-point usage in which an endianness-phrase is not specified:']
[[[130, 1129], [1112, 1129], [1112, 1239], [130, 1239]], '6) When neither the HIGH-ORDER-LEFT phrase nor the HIGH-ORDER-RIGHT phrase is specified in the FLOAT-DECIMAL clause, the implementor  shall  specify which phrase is implied  for the data description entry of any data item described with a standard decimal floating-point usage in which an endianness-phrase is not specified.']
[[[130, 1264], [438, 1264], [438, 1290], [130, 1290]], '11.9.10 INITIALIZE clause']
[[[130, 1316], [348, 1316], [348, 1342], [130, 1342]], '11.9.10.1 General']
[[[129, 1366], [1106, 1366], [1106, 1425], [129, 1425]], 'The INITIALIZE clause specifies that during program initialization ofallocation of space the background is set to a specified character:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '276']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 307 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [466, 190], [466, 216], [164, 216]], '11.9.10.2 General format']
[[[359, 263], [399, 263], [399, 283], [359, 283]], 'ALL']
[[[814, 252], [982, 252], [982, 416], [814, 416]], 'BINARY ZEROES HIGH-VALUES literal-1 LOW-VALUES SPACES']
[[[388, 307], [558, 307], [558, 331], [388, 331]], 'LOCAL-STORAGE']
[[[214, 320], [328, 320], [328, 344], [214, 344]], 'INITIALIZE']
[[[660, 320], [786, 320], [786, 346], [660, 346]], 'SECTION TO']
[[[388, 344], [592, 344], [592, 404], [388, 404]], 'SCREEN WORKING-STORAGE']
[[[164, 470], [420, 470], [420, 500], [164, 500]], '11.9.103 Syntax rule']
[[[164, 522], [842, 522], [842, 552], [164, 552]], '1) Literal-1 shall specify a one-byte hexadecimal-alphanumeric literal.']
[[[164, 576], [446, 576], [446, 602], [164, 602]], '11.9.10.4 General rules']
[[[164, 626], [948, 626], [948, 658], [164, 658]], '1) If ALL is specified, LOCAL-STORAGE, SCREEN, and WORKING-STORAGE apply:']
[[[164, 680], [1144, 680], [1144, 732], [164, 732]], '2) If LOCAL-STORAGE is specified, all data items in the local-storage section are initialized as indicated in the rules for initial state_']
[[[164, 758], [1146, 758], [1146, 812], [164, 812]], '3) If SCREEN is specified, all data items in the screen section are initialized as indicated in the rules for initial state:']
[[[164, 836], [1146, 836], [1146, 890], [164, 890]], '4) If WORKING-STORAGE is specified, all data items in the working-storage section are initialized as indicated in the rules for initial state.']
[[[164, 913], [805, 913], [805, 949], [164, 949]], "5) The character following 'TO' creates the specified-fill-character."]
[[[200, 968], [1040, 968], [1040, 1001], [200, 1001]], 'a) If BINARY ZEROES is specified, string ofbinary zeros is the specified-fill-character:']
[[[200, 1020], [1146, 1020], [1146, 1076], [200, 1076]], 'b) If HIGH-VALUES is   specified, the alphanumeric high value character is the specified-fill- character:']
[[[236, 1100], [834, 1100], [834, 1130], [236, 1130]], 'If literal-1 is specified, that literal is the specified-fill-character']
[[[202, 1152], [1142, 1152], [1142, 1185], [202, 1185]], 'd) If LOW-VALUES is specified, the alphanumeric low value character is the specified-fill-character_']
[[[200, 1206], [958, 1206], [958, 1236], [200, 1236]], 'e) If SPACES is specified, the alphanumeric space is the specified-fill-character']
[[[200, 1258], [1146, 1258], [1146, 1343], [200, 1343]], 'The initializing of the data items takes place immediately after the space is allocated: If the INITIALIZE clause is not specified,the content of data items that do not have predefined initialization values (such as VALUE clauses) is undefined or specified by the implementor:']
[[[200, 1362], [1146, 1362], [1146, 1411], [200, 1411]], 'NOTE Data items without predefined settings will probably not have initial values that are valid for those data items_']
[[[166, 1436], [1146, 1436], [1146, 1494], [166, 1494]], '7) External items in the Working-storage section are not initialized when runtime elementsare put into the initial state, except for those with the CONSTANT RECORD clause:']
[[[876, 1552], [1034, 1552], [1034, 1578], [876, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '277']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 308 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [622, 190], [622, 216], [130, 216]], '11.9.11 INTERMEDIATE ROUNDING clause']
[[[129, 240], [1088, 240], [1088, 296], [129, 296]], 'The INTERMEDIATE ROUNDING clause specifies the rounding rules that are to be applied during the production of intermediate results in arithmetic statements and arithmetic expressions_']
[[[130, 322], [430, 322], [430, 348], [130, 348]], '11.9.11.1 General format']
[[[476, 400], [768, 400], [768, 532], [476, 532]], 'NEAREST-AWAY-FROM-ZERO NEAREST-EVEN PROHIBITED TRUNCATION']
[[[144, 452], [446, 452], [446, 476], [144, 476]], 'INTERMEDIATE ROUNDING IS']
[[[130, 576], [412, 576], [412, 602], [130, 602]], '11.9.11.2 General rules']
[[[130, 623], [1113, 623], [1113, 684], [130, 684]], '1) Ifnative arithmetic is in effect; the rounding rules that apply to intermediate data items are defined by the implementor']
[[[130, 704], [556, 704], [556, 736], [130, 736]], '2) If standard-binary arithmetic is in effect:']
[[[167, 758], [1110, 758], [1110, 789], [167, 789]], 'a) If the INTERMEDIATE ROUNDING clause is not specified, the NEAREST-EVEN phrase is implied.']
[[[166, 811], [814, 811], [814, 843], [166, 843]], 'b) The NEAREST-AWAY-FROM-ZERO phrase shall not be specified.']
[[[202, 863], [1112, 863], [1112, 943], [202, 943]], 'Ifthe NEAREST-EVEN phrase is specified or implied,the intermediate data item shall be rounded to the nearest value that can be represented exactly in SBIDI form in which the rightmost bit is zero']
[[[201, 970], [1112, 970], [1112, 1053], [201, 1053]], 'If the PROHIBITED phrase is specified and the intermediate value cannot be represented exactly in SBIDI form, the EC-SIZE-TRUNCATION exception condition is set t0 exist and the results ofthe operation are undefined.']
[[[202, 1074], [1112, 1074], [1112, 1154], [202, 1154]], 'Ifthe TRUNCATION phrase is specified and an intermediate value cannot be represented exactly in SBIDI form, the value shall be rounded to the nearest value that can be represented exactly in that format that is nearer to zero than the intermediate value']
[[[166, 1179], [818, 1179], [818, 1204], [166, 1204]], 'NOTE The STANDARD-BINARY mode ofarithmetic is an obsolete feature.']
[[[130, 1230], [570, 1230], [570, 1258], [130, 1258]], '3) If standard-decimal arithmetic is in effect:']
[[[166, 1282], [1110, 1282], [1110, 1338], [166, 1338]], 'a) If the INTERMEDIATE ROUNDING clause is not specified, the NEAREST-AWAY-FROM-ZERO phrase is implied.']
[[[166, 1360], [1112, 1360], [1112, 1465], [166, 1465]], 'b) Ifthe NEAREST-AWAY-FROM-ZERO phrase is specified,and an intermediate data item cannot be represented exactly in SDIDI form the value is rounded to the nearest value that can be represented in that format If two such values are equally near; the value farther from zero is chosen_']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '278']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 309 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[236, 187], [1146, 187], [1146, 271], [236, 271]], 'Ifthe NEAREST-EVEN phrase is specified or implied,the intermediate data item shall be rounded to the nearest value that can be represented exactly in SDIDI form: If two such values are equally near; the value in which the rightmost digit ofthe significand is even shall be delivered.']
[[[235, 292], [1146, 292], [1146, 377], [235, 377]], 'If the PROHIBITED phrase is specified and an intermediate value cannot be represented exactly in SDIDI form, the EC-SIZE-TRUNCATION exception condition is set to existand the results ofthe operation are undefined.']
[[[236, 398], [1146, 398], [1146, 478], [236, 478]], 'If the TRUNCATION phrase is specified and an intermediate data item cannot be represented exactly in SDIDI form, the value shall be the nearest value in that format that is nearer to zero than the intermediate value:']
[[[876, 1552], [1034, 1552], [1034, 1578], [876, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '279']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 310 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 189], [480, 189], [480, 222], [130, 222]], '11.10 PROGRAM-ID paragraph']
[[[130, 242], [326, 242], [326, 268], [130, 268]], '11.10.1 General']
[[[130, 294], [1100, 294], [1100, 350], [130, 350]], 'The PROGRAM-ID paragraph specifies the name by which a program is identified and assigns selected program attributes to that program:']
[[[130, 371], [1004, 371], [1004, 403], [130, 403]], 'The PROGRAM-ID paragraph specifies the name by which a program prototype is identified:']
[[[130, 426], [410, 426], [410, 452], [130, 452]], '11.10.2 General format']
[[[130, 480], [344, 480], [344, 506], [130, 506]], 'Format 1 (definition):']
[[[690, 580], [788, 580], [788, 604], [690, 604]], 'COMMON']
[[[144, 613], [648, 613], [648, 646], [144, 646]], 'PROGRAM-ID: program-name-1 [As literal-1 ] IS']
[[[716, 626], [834, 626], [834, 688], [716, 688]], 'INITIAL RECURSIVE']
[[[888, 620], [994, 620], [994, 644], [888, 644]], 'PROGRAM']
[[[128, 750], [346, 750], [346, 778], [128, 778]], 'Format 2 (prototype):']
[[[132, 804], [850, 804], [850, 835], [132, 835]], 'PROGRAM-ID: program-prototype-name-1 AS literal-1 ] IS PROTOTYPE']
[[[130, 868], [378, 868], [378, 896], [130, 896]], '11.10.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 920], [275, 920], [275, 944], [130, 944]], 'ALL FORMATS']
[[[130, 968], [1112, 968], [1112, 1026], [130, 1026]], '1) Literal-1 shall be an alphanumeric literal or national literal and shall be neither figurative constant nor a zero-length literal.']
[[[130, 1052], [236, 1052], [236, 1076], [130, 1076]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[130, 1101], [984, 1101], [984, 1133], [130, 1133]], '2) Literal-1 shall not be specified in a program that is contained within another program.']
[[[130, 1155], [1112, 1155], [1112, 1212], [130, 1212]], '3) A program contained within another program shall not be assigned the same name as that of any other program contained within the outermost program that contains this program_']
[[[130, 1233], [1076, 1233], [1076, 1265], [130, 1265]], '4) The COMMON clause may be specified only if the program is contained within another program_']
[[[130, 1285], [1112, 1285], [1112, 1344], [130, 1344]], '5) The INITIAL clause shall not be specified if any program that directly or indirectly contains this program is a recursive program']
[[[130, 1362], [1113, 1362], [1113, 1421], [130, 1421]], '6) The RECURSIVE clause shall not be specified ifany program that directly or indirectly contains this program is an initial program_']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '280']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 311 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [424, 190], [424, 216], [164, 216]], '11.10.4 General rules']
[[[165, 245], [269, 245], [269, 265], [165, 265]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[164, 292], [1146, 292], [1146, 351], [164, 351]], '1) Program-name-1 names the program declared by this program definition Literal-1, if specified, is the name of the program that is externalized to the operating environment']
[[[164, 371], [1146, 371], [1146, 455], [164, 455]], '2) The COMMON clause specifies thatthe program is common_ A common program is contained within another program but may be called from programs other than that containing it as stated in 8.4.6, Scope of names:']
[[[164, 478], [1146, 478], [1146, 558], [164, 558]], '3) The INITIAL clause specifies that the program is initial program When an initial program is activated, the data items and file connectors contained in it and any program contained within it are set to their initial states:']
[[[164, 582], [1146, 582], [1146, 688], [164, 688]], '4) The RECURSIVE clause specifies that the program and any programs contained within it are recursive: The program may be called while it is active and may call itself: If the RECURSIVE clause is not specified in a program Or implied for a program, the program shall not be called while it is active.']
[[[164, 716], [1144, 716], [1144, 768], [164, 768]], '5) Additional rules concerning initial and recursive programs are given in 8.6.6, Common, initial, and recursive attributes:']
[[[164, 796], [272, 796], [272, 820], [164, 820]], 'FORMAT 2']
[[[200, 846], [1146, 846], [1146, 904], [200, 904]], 'Program-prototype-name-1 identifies the program prototype. However; literal-1,if specified, is the name ofthe program prototype that is externalized to the operating environment']
[[[876, 1552], [1034, 1552], [1034, 1578], [876, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '281']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 312 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 188], [452, 188], [452, 220], [130, 220]], '12 Environment division']
[[[130, 242], [296, 242], [296, 268], [130, 268]], '12.1 General']
[[[128, 292], [1108, 292], [1108, 405], [128, 405]], 'The environment division specifies those aspects ofa data processing problem that are dependent upon thephysical characteristics ofa specific computer: This division allows specification ofthe configuration of the compiling computer and the object computer: In addition, information relative to input-output control, special hardware characteristics, and control techniques can be given:']
[[[130, 426], [560, 426], [560, 454], [130, 454]], '12.2 Environment division structure']
[[[130, 480], [396, 480], [396, 506], [130, 506]], '12.2.1 General format']
[[[144, 546], [400, 546], [400, 641], [144, 641]], 'ENVIRONMENT DIVISION._ configuration-section ] input-output-section ]']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '282']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 313 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [482, 190], [482, 220], [164, 220]], '12.3 Configuration section']
[[[164, 242], [348, 242], [348, 268], [164, 268]], '12.3.1 General']
[[[160, 292], [1117, 292], [1117, 377], [160, 377]], 'The configuration section specifies aspects ofthe data processing system that are dependent on the specific system as well as special control techniques and a means ofassociating a local name with an external resource: This section is divided into paragraphs:']
[[[235, 397], [1147, 397], [1147, 454], [235, 454]], 'the SOURCE-COMPUTER paragraph, which describes the computer configuration on which the source element is compiled;']
[[[235, 480], [1146, 480], [1146, 538], [235, 538]], 'the OBJECT-COMPUTER paragraph, which describes the computer configuration on which the runtime module produced by the compiler is to be run;']
[[[235, 556], [1147, 556], [1147, 667], [235, 667]], 'the SPECIAL-NAMES paragraph, which provides a means for specifying the currency sign, choosing the decimal point; specifying symbolic-characters,   relating   system-names to user-specified mnemonic-names,  relating alphabet-names to character sets or collating sequences, and relating class-names to sets of characters; and']
[[[236, 689], [1146, 689], [1146, 768], [236, 768]], 'the REPOSITORY paragraph, which provides a means for associating a local name with an external resource and specifying which intrinsic function names become reserved words for this source unit:']
[[[164, 796], [430, 796], [430, 822], [164, 822]], '12.3.2 General format']
[[[177, 862], [466, 862], [466, 1016], [177, 1016]], 'CONFIGURATION SECTION_ source-computer-paragraph [object-computer-paragraph special-names-paragraph ] repository-paragraph']
[[[164, 1046], [398, 1046], [398, 1074], [164, 1074]], '12.3.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 1095], [1146, 1095], [1146, 1154], [164, 1154]], '1) The configuration section shall not be specified in program that is contained within another program_']
[[[164, 1172], [869, 1172], [869, 1208], [164, 1208]], '2) The configuration section shall not be specified in a method definition_']
[[[164, 1227], [1147, 1227], [1147, 1284], [164, 1284]], '3) The SOURCE-COMPUTER, OBJECT-COMPUTER, and REPOSITORY paragraphs shall not be specified in a factory definition or an instance definition:']
[[[164, 1310], [398, 1310], [398, 1334], [164, 1334]], '12.3.4 General rule']
[[[164, 1359], [1145, 1359], [1145, 1420], [164, 1420]], '1) The entries explicitly or implicitly specified in the configuration section ofa source unit that contains other source units apply to each directly or indirectly contained source unit']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '283']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 314 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 186], [1112, 186], [1112, 264], [164, 264]], 'NOTE The functionality established in the CONFIGURATION SECTION applies to this source unit and all contained source units. The ability to reference explicitly any data item referenced in the CONFIGURATION SECTION is dependent upon the scope of that data item:']
[[[130, 286], [575, 286], [575, 322], [130, 322]], '12.3.5 SOURCE-COMPUTER paragraph']
[[[130, 342], [334, 342], [334, 368], [130, 368]], '12.3.5.1 General']
[[[130, 392], [1064, 392], [1064, 448], [130, 448]], 'The SOURCE-COMPUTER paragraph provides a means of describing the computer upon which the compilation unit is to be compiled.']
[[[130, 473], [410, 473], [410, 501], [130, 501]], '12.3.5.2 General format']
[[[132, 530], [550, 530], [550, 558], [132, 558]], 'SOURCE-COMPUTER. [computer-name-1 ]']
[[[130, 592], [366, 592], [366, 620], [130, 620]], '12.3.53 Syntax rule']
[[[130, 642], [1052, 642], [1052, 673], [130, 673]], '1) If computer-name-1 is not specified,the second period in the General format may be omitted:']
[[[130, 698], [390, 698], [390, 722], [130, 722]], '12.3.5.4 General rules']
[[[130, 746], [1112, 746], [1112, 805], [130, 805]], '1) Al] clauses ofthe SOURCE-COMPUTER paragraph apply to the source unitin which they are explicitly or implicitly specified and to any source unit contained within that source unit:']
[[[130, 824], [1112, 824], [1112, 911], [130, 911]], '2) When the SOURCE-COMPUTER paragraph is not specified and the source unit is not contained within a source unit including SOURCE-COMPUTER paragraph; the computer on which the source unit is being compiled is the source computer']
[[[130, 931], [1112, 931], [1112, 990], [130, 990]], '3) When the SOURCE-COMPUTER paragraph is specified, but computer-name-1 is not specified, the computer upon which the source unit is being compiled is the source computer_']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '284']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 315 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 189], [598, 189], [598, 222], [164, 222]], '12.3.6 OBJECT-COMPUTER paragraph']
[[[164, 242], [368, 242], [368, 268], [164, 268]], '12.3.6.1 General']
[[[163, 292], [1142, 292], [1142, 354], [163, 354]], 'The OBJECT-COMPUTER paragraph provides a means of describing the computer on which the runtime module created by the compiler is to be executed']
[[[164, 374], [446, 374], [446, 400], [164, 400]], '12.3.6.2 General format']
[[[175, 437], [340, 437], [340, 464], [175, 464]], 'OBJECT-COMPUTER']
[[[704, 506], [1050, 506], [1050, 594], [704, 594]], 'IS locale-phrase-1 locale-phrase-2 ] FOR ALPHANUMERIC IS locale-phrase-1 FOR NATIONAL IS locale-phrase-2']
[[[405, 539], [647, 539], [647, 559], [405, 559]], 'CHARACTER CLASSIFICATION']
[[[222, 602], [375, 602], [375, 622], [222, 622]], 'computer-name-1 ]']
[[[404, 633], [1066, 633], [1066, 720], [404, 720]], 'IS alphabet-name-1 [alphabet-name-2 ] PROGRAM COLLATING SEQUENCE FOR ALPHANUMERIC IS alphabet-name-1 FOR NATIONAL IS alphabet-name-2']
[[[164, 782], [410, 782], [410, 810], [164, 810]], 'where locale-phrase-1 is:']
[[[189, 851], [374, 851], [374, 980], [189, 980]], 'locae-name-1 LOCALE SYSTEM-DEFAULT USER-DEFAULT']
[[[163, 1020], [410, 1020], [410, 1051], [163, 1051]], 'where locale-phrase-2 is:']
[[[190, 1094], [374, 1094], [374, 1220], [190, 1220]], 'locale-name-2 LOCALE SYSTEM-DEFAULT USER-DEFAULT']
[[[164, 1264], [412, 1264], [412, 1292], [164, 1292]], '12.3.6.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 1311], [1103, 1311], [1103, 1347], [164, 1347]], '1) Alphabet-name-1 shall reference an alphabet that defines an alphanumeric collating sequence']
[[[164, 1366], [1032, 1366], [1032, 1398], [164, 1398]], '2) Alphabet-name-2 shall reference an alphabet that defines a national collating sequence']
[[[164, 1422], [1144, 1422], [1144, 1450], [164, 1450]], '3) Locale-name-1 and locale-name-2 shall be locale names defined in the SPECIAL-NAMES paragraph:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '285']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 316 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 186], [1112, 186], [1112, 244], [130, 244]], '4) If neither computer-name-1 nor any of the optional clauses is specified, the second period in the General format may be omitted:']
[[[130, 268], [390, 268], [390, 294], [130, 294]], '12.3.6.4 General rules']
[[[130, 318], [1112, 318], [1112, 377], [130, 377]], '1) All clauses ofthe OBJECT-COMPUTER paragraph apply to the source unit in which they are explicitly or implicitly specified and to any source unit contained within that source unit:']
[[[130, 399], [1112, 399], [1112, 456], [130, 456]], '2) Computer-name-1 may provide a means for identifying equipment configuration, in which case computer-name-l and its implied configuration are specified by each implementor_']
[[[130, 478], [1112, 478], [1112, 535], [130, 535]], '3) When the OBJECT-COMPUTER paragraph is specified, but computer-name-1 is not specified, the object computer is defined by the implementor_']
[[[130, 556], [1113, 556], [1113, 638], [130, 638]], '4) When the OBJECT-COMPUTER paragraph is not specified andthe source unit is not contained within a source unit including an OBJECT-COMPUTER paragraph, the object computer is defined by the implementor:']
[[[130, 662], [1112, 662], [1112, 714], [130, 714]], '5) When the CHARACTER CLASSIFICATION clause is specified; the initial character classification is as follows:']
[[[167, 740], [1112, 740], [1112, 794], [167, 794]], 'a) If locale-name-1 is specified, the initial alphanumeric character classification is the character classification associated with locale-name-1.']
[[[166, 819], [1112, 819], [1112, 874], [166, 874]], 'b) If LOCALE is specified as locale-phrase-1,the initial alphanumeric character classification is the character classification associated with the current locale']
[[[167, 898], [1112, 898], [1112, 957], [167, 957]], 'c) If SYSTEM-DEFAULT is   specified as   locale-phrase-1, the initial   alphanumeric character classification is the character classification associated with the system default locale']
[[[166, 977], [1112, 977], [1112, 1035], [166, 1035]], 'd) If USER-DEFAULT is specified as locale-phrase-1, the initial alphanumeric character classification is the character classification associated with the user default locale:']
[[[202, 1057], [1112, 1057], [1112, 1140], [202, 1140]], "If locale-phrase-1 is not   specified, the initial   alphanumeric   character classification is the character   classification associated with the computer's coded character set in effect for alphanumeric characters at runtime:"]
[[[169, 1161], [1112, 1161], [1112, 1216], [169, 1216]], '0) If locale-name-2 is specified, the initial   national character   classification is the character classification associated with locale-name-2_']
[[[166, 1240], [1112, 1240], [1112, 1294], [166, 1294]], 'g) If LOCALE is specified as locale-phrase-2, the initial national character classification is the character classification associated with the current locale.']
[[[166, 1319], [1110, 1319], [1110, 1376], [166, 1376]], 'h) If SYSTEM-DEFAULT is specified as locale-phrase-2, the initial national character classification is the character classification associated with the system default locale:']
[[[165, 1400], [1112, 1400], [1112, 1452], [165, 1452]], 'i) If USER-DEFAULT is specified as locale-phrase-2, the initial national character classification is the character classification associated with the user default locale.']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '286']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 317 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[199, 187], [1146, 187], [1146, 268], [199, 268]], "j) If locale-phrase-2 is not specified, the initial national character classification is the character classification associated with the computer's coded character set in effect for national characters at runtime"]
[[[200, 292], [1146, 292], [1146, 400], [200, 400]], "When the CHARACTER CLASSIFICATION clause is not specified and the source unit is not contained within a source unit for which a CHARACTER CLASSIFICATION clause is specified, the initial character classifications are the character classifications associated with the computer's coded character set in effect for alphanumeric and national characters at runtime:"]
[[[166, 424], [1146, 424], [1146, 483], [166, 483]], '7) When the CHARACTER CLASSIFICATION clause is specified, the cultural convention specification LC_CTYPE from the specified locales are used for:']
[[[201, 506], [1146, 506], [1146, 558], [201, 558]], 'a) the uppercase and lowercase mappings of characters for the UPPER-CASE and LOWER-CASE intrinsic functions.']
[[[200, 583], [1146, 583], [1146, 666], [200, 666]], 'b) the classification of characters for class tests ALPHABETIC,ALPHABETIC-LOWER,ALPHABETIC - UPPER, and for the class test specifying an alphabet-name that is associated with a locale in the SPECIAL-NAMES paragraph_']
[[[164, 688], [1146, 688], [1146, 796], [164, 796]], '8) The character   classifications explicitly or implicitly established by the OBJECT-COMPUTER paragraph are effective with the initial state of the runtime modules to which they apply. If locale- name-1 or locale-name-2 is specified, the associated character classification is defined by category LC_CTYPE in the locale identified by that locale-name']
[[[164, 818], [1147, 818], [1147, 983], [164, 983]], '9) When the PROGRAM COLLATING SEQUENCE clause is specified, the initial alphanumeric program collating sequence is the collating sequence associated with alphabet-name-1 and the initial national program collating sequence is the collating sequence associated with alphabet-name-2. When alphabet-name-1 is not specified, the initial alphanumeric program collating sequence is the native alphanumeric collating sequence; when alphabet-name-2 is not specified, the initial national program collating sequence is the native national collating sequence']
[[[164, 1003], [1148, 1003], [1148, 1114], [164, 1114]], '10) When the PROGRAM COLLATING SEQUENCE clause is not specified and the source unit is not contained within a source unit for which a PROGRAM COLLATING SEQUENCE clause is specified,the initial program collating sequences are the native alphanumeric collating sequence and the native national collating sequence:']
[[[161, 1133], [1147, 1133], [1147, 1216], [161, 1216]], '11) " The alphanumeric program collating sequence and national program collating sequence are used to determinethe truth value ofany alphanumeric comparisons and national comparisons,respectively, that are:']
[[[200, 1241], [630, 1241], [630, 1273], [200, 1273]], 'a) Explicitly specified in relation conditions_']
[[[200, 1293], [712, 1293], [712, 1325], [200, 1325]], 'b) Explicitly specified in condition-name conditions:']
[[[203, 1346], [1062, 1346], [1062, 1379], [203, 1379]], 'c) Implicitly specified by the presence ofa CONTROL clause in a report description entry.']
[[[200, 1400], [1146, 1400], [1146, 1458], [200, 1458]], 'When alphabet-name-1 or alphabet-name-2, or both, is associated with a locale, locale category LC_COLLATE is used to carry out these comparisons']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '287']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 318 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 186], [1113, 186], [1113, 351], [130, 351]], '12) The alphanumeric program collating sequence and national program collating sequence explicitly or implicitly established by the OBJECT-COMPUTER paragraph are effective with the initial state of the runtime modules to which they apply. If alphabet-name-1 or alphabet-name-2 references a locale; the associated   collating sequence is defined by category LC_COLLATE in the   specific locale associated with that alphabet-name Or, if none, in the locale current at the time the collating sequence is used at runtime']
[[[130, 370], [1112, 370], [1112, 481], [130, 481]], '13) The alphanumeric program collating sequence and national program collating sequence are applied to alphanumeric and national sort or merge keys, respectively, unless the sort r merge collating sequence has been modified by execution of a SET statement or a COLLATING SEQUENCE phrase is specified in the respective SORT or MERGE statement:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '288']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 189], [558, 189], [558, 222], [164, 222]], '12.3.7 SPECIAL-NAMES paragraph']
[[[164, 242], [368, 242], [368, 268], [164, 268]], '12.3.7.1 General']
[[[164, 293], [686, 293], [686, 325], [164, 325]], 'The SPECIAL-NAMES paragraph provides a means for:']
[[[233, 346], [1147, 346], [1147, 640], [233, 640]], 'specifying currency signs and symbols, choosing the decimal point; specifying symbolic-characters, relating dynamic-length-structure-names to the structure ofa dynamic-length elementary item, relating system-names to user-specified mnemonic-names, relating locale-names to the external identification of locales, relating alphabet-names to character sets or collating sequences or both relating class-names to a set of characters, relating an ordering table name to a cultural ordering table, relating a data item to the cursor position of a character addressable terminal, and relating a data item to the status ofa terminal input-output operation:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '289']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 320 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [416, 190], [416, 216], [130, 216]], '12.3.7.2 General format']
[[[142, 258], [373, 258], [373, 323], [142, 323]], 'SPECIAL-NAMES [alphabet-name-clause']
[[[460, 376], [626, 376], [626, 436], [460, 436]], 'ALPHANUMERIC NATIONAL']
[[[168, 388], [430, 388], [430, 416], [168, 416]], 'CLASS class-name-1 FOR']
[[[227, 515], [251, 515], [251, 535], [227, 535]], 'IS']
[[[280, 516], [364, 516], [364, 540], [280, 540]], 'literal-5']
[[[406, 504], [510, 504], [510, 564], [406, 564]], 'THROUGH THRU']
[[[536, 520], [620, 520], [620, 544], [536, 544]], 'literal-6']
[[[682, 512], [904, 512], [904, 540], [682, 540]], '[IN alphabet-name-4 ]']
[[[158, 600], [450, 600], [450, 628], [158, 628]], 'CRT STATUS IS data-name-2 ]']
[[[141, 641], [807, 641], [807, 791], [141, 791]], 'CURRENCY SIGN IS literal-7 [WITH PICTURE SYMBOL literal-8] ]  [CURSOR IS data-name-1 ]] DECIMAL-POINT IS COMMA dynamic-length-structure-clause']
[[[154, 824], [402, 824], [402, 848], [154, 848]], 'LOCALE locale-name-1 IS']
[[[430, 814], [658, 814], [658, 868], [430, 868]], 'external-locale-name-1 literal-4']
[[[154, 896], [300, 896], [300, 920], [154, 920]], 'switch-name-1']
[[[462, 956], [788, 956], [788, 1018], [462, 1018]], 'ON STATUS IS condition-name-1 OFF STATUS IS condition-name-2']
[[[218, 972], [426, 972], [426, 998], [218, 998]], 'IS mnemonic-name-1']
[[[238, 1046], [564, 1046], [564, 1108], [238, 1108]], 'ON STATUS IS condition-name-1 OFF STATUS IS condition-name-2']
[[[153, 1125], [601, 1125], [601, 1250], [153, 1250]], 'feature-name-1 IS mnemonic-name-2 device-name-1 IS mnemonic-name-3 symbolic-characters-clause ] ORDER TABLE ordering-name-1 IS literal-9 ]']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '290']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [472, 190], [472, 218], [164, 218]], 'where alphabet-name-clause is:']
[[[794, 308], [1044, 308], [1044, 514], [794, 514]], 'LOCALE [ locale-name-2 ] NATIVE STANDARD-1 STANDARD-2 code-name-1 literal-phrase }']
[[[329, 390], [766, 390], [766, 420], [329, 420]], 'alphabet-name-1 FOR ALPHANUMERIC IS']
[[[178, 530], [290, 530], [290, 554], [178, 554]], 'ALPHABET']
[[[330, 559], [962, 559], [962, 800], [330, 800]], 'LOCALE [ locale-name-2 ] NATIVE UCS-4 alphabet-name-2 FOR NATIONAL IS UTF-8 UTF-16 code-name-2 literal-phrase }']
[[[164, 866], [392, 866], [392, 892], [164, 892]], 'where literal-phrase is:']
[[[314, 964], [418, 964], [418, 1024], [314, 1024]], 'THROUGH THRU']
[[[444, 980], [528, 980], [528, 1004], [444, 1004]], 'literal-2']
[[[176, 992], [258, 992], [258, 1016], [176, 1016]], 'literal-1']
[[[298, 1044], [450, 1044], [450, 1070], [298, 1070]], 'ALSO literal-3 }']
[[[164, 1112], [572, 1112], [572, 1140], [164, 1140]], 'where dynamic-length-structure-clause is:']
[[[192, 1191], [860, 1191], [860, 1222], [192, 1222]], 'DYNAMIC LENGTH STRUCTURE dynamic-length-structure-name-1 IS']
[[[246, 1264], [560, 1264], [560, 1323], [246, 1323]], 'SIGNED [SHORT PREFIXED DELIMITED']
[[[213, 1336], [472, 1336], [472, 1368], [213, 1368]], 'physical-structure-name-1']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '291']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']
