--- Page 349 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[163, 189], [512, 189], [512, 216], [163, 216]], '12.4.5.4 ACCESS MODE clause']
[[[164, 242], [368, 242], [368, 268], [164, 268]], '12.4.5.5 General']
[[[164, 294], [1030, 294], [1030, 322], [164, 322]], 'The ACCESS MODE clause specifies the order in which records are to be accessed in the file']
[[[164, 348], [470, 348], [470, 374], [164, 374]], '12.4.5.5.1 General format']
[[[380, 426], [514, 426], [514, 522], [380, 522]], 'SEQUENTIAL RANDOM DYNAMIC']
[[[178, 458], [352, 458], [352, 484], [178, 484]], 'ACCESS MODE IS']
[[[163, 565], [438, 565], [438, 596], [163, 596]], '12.4.5.5.2 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 618], [1148, 618], [1148, 670], [164, 670]], '1) The RANDOM clause shall not be specified for file-names specified in the USING or GIVING phrase of a SORT or MERGE statement']
[[[164, 695], [950, 695], [950, 727], [164, 727]], '2) The DYNAMIC and RANDOM phrases shall not be specified for a sequential file.']
[[[164, 750], [450, 750], [450, 776], [164, 776]], '12.4.5.53 General rules']
[[[164, 803], [896, 803], [896, 831], [164, 831]], '1) If the ACCESS MODE clause is not specified, sequential access is assumed.']
[[[164, 854], [1146, 854], [1146, 913], [164, 913]], '2) If the access mode is sequential, records in the file are accessed in the sequence dictated by the file organization:']
[[[200, 934], [1146, 934], [1146, 990], [200, 990]], 'a) For sequential files this sequence is specified by predecessor-successor record relationships established by the execution of WRITE statements when the physical file is created or extended:']
[[[200, 1008], [1148, 1008], [1148, 1070], [200, 1070]], 'b) For relative files this sequence is the order of ascending relative record numbers of existing records in the physical file:']
[[[235, 1090], [1146, 1090], [1146, 1150], [235, 1150]], 'For indexed files this sequence is ascending within given key of reference according to the collating sequence for that key.']
[[[164, 1171], [486, 1171], [486, 1198], [164, 1198]], '3) If the access mode is random:']
[[[201, 1223], [1086, 1223], [1086, 1253], [201, 1253]], 'a) For a relative file, the value of a relative key data item indicates the record to be accessed_']
[[[200, 1274], [1094, 1274], [1094, 1308], [200, 1308]], 'b) For an indexed file, the value of a record key data item indicates the record to be accessed.']
[[[164, 1327], [1128, 1327], [1128, 1358], [164, 1358]], '4) If the access mode is dynamic, records in the file may be accessed sequentially, randomly, or both:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '319']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 350 ---

[[[127, 77], [389, 77], [389, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 189], [608, 189], [608, 216], [129, 216]], '12.4.5.6 ALTERNATE RECORD KEY clause']
[[[130, 242], [354, 242], [354, 268], [130, 268]], '12.4.5.6.1 General']
[[[128, 293], [1106, 293], [1106, 346], [128, 346]], 'The ALTERNATE RECORD KEY clause specifies an alternate record key access path to the records in an indexed file']
[[[130, 374], [436, 374], [436, 400], [130, 400]], '12.4.5.6.2 General format']
[[[144, 454], [928, 454], [928, 552], [144, 552]], 'data-name-1 ALTERNATE RECORD KEY IS record-key-name-1 SOURCE IS { data-name-2 } _ [WITH DUPLICATES ] SUPPRESS WHEN literal-1 ]']
[[[130, 588], [402, 588], [402, 618], [130, 618]], '12.4.5.63 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 642], [872, 642], [872, 670], [130, 670]], '1) Data-name-1 and data-name-2 shall not be subject to any OCCURS clauses']
[[[130, 694], [1112, 694], [1112, 780], [130, 780]], '2) Data-name-1 and data-name-2 shall be defined as a data item of category alphanumeric or national within a record description entry associated with the file-name to which the ALTERNATE RECORD KEY clause is subordinate: All occurrences of data-name-2 shall be of the same category.']
[[[130, 800], [910, 800], [910, 828], [130, 828]], '3) Data-name-1 and data-name-2 shall not reference a variable-length data item:']
[[[130, 850], [1112, 850], [1112, 935], [130, 935]], '4) Data-name-1 shall not reference an item whose leftmost byte position corresponds to the leftmost byte position of the prime record key, or of another alternate record key: This restriction does not apply in the case where either key is specified using the SOURCE phrase']
[[[130, 956], [1112, 956], [1112, 1038], [130, 1038]], '5) If the indexed file contains variable-length records, each data-name-1 and data-name-2 shall be contained within the first x bytes of the record, where X equals the minimum record size specified for the file: (See 13.18.43,RECORD clause:)']
[[[133, 1061], [762, 1061], [762, 1094], [133, 1094]], '6) Record-key-name-1has the class and category 0f data-name-2']
[[[130, 1116], [1114, 1116], [1114, 1197], [130, 1197]], '7) Literal-1 shall be an alphanumeric literal, a national literal, or a figurative constant; and shall be of the same category as data-name-1 or data-name-2. If ALL literal is specified, the literal shall be one character long:']
[[[130, 1222], [416, 1222], [416, 1246], [130, 1246]], '12.4.5.6.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1271], [1112, 1271], [1112, 1326], [130, 1326]], '1) An ALTERNATE RECORD KEY clause specifies an alternate record key for the file with which this clause is associated:']
[[[130, 1350], [1114, 1350], [1114, 1406], [130, 1406]], '2) Record-key-name-1 defines a record key consisting of the concatenation of all occurrences of data-name-2 in the order specified.']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '320']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 351 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [1146, 190], [1146, 271], [164, 271]], '3) The data description ofdata-name-1 or data-name-2 as well as their relative location within a record shall be the same as that used when the physical file was created_ The number of alternate record keys for the file shall also be the same as that used when the physical file was created.']
[[[164, 293], [1147, 293], [1147, 431], [164, 431]], '4) The DUPLICATES phrase specifies that the value of the associated alternate record key may be equal to the value ofthe same alternate record key in another record in the physical file. Ifthe DUPLICATES phrase is not specified,the value ofthe associated alternate record key- shall not be equal to the value of the same alternate record key in another record in the physical file The equality or inequality is based on the collating sequence used for the file according to the rules for a relation condition:']
[[[164, 451], [1146, 451], [1146, 509], [164, 509]], '5) The identical byte  positions  referenced by data-name-1 or data-name-2 in any one record description entry are implicitly referenced as keys for all other record description entries ofthat file:']
[[[198, 530], [1146, 530], [1146, 639], [198, 639]], 'Literal-1 establishes the key suppression value for this alternate record key for WRITE and REWRITE statements. Alternate record key suppression specifies thatan alternate record key- access path toa particular record shall not be provided when the value of data-name-1 orrecord-key-name- 1 in that record is equal to literal-1.']
[[[199, 661], [1146, 661], [1146, 713], [199, 713]], 'NOTE The suppression does not impact READ and START although the suppressed records will not be processed by these statements It is as if they did not exist']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '321']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 352 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [574, 190], [574, 220], [130, 220]], '12.4.5.7 COLLATING SEQUENCE clause']
[[[130, 242], [354, 242], [354, 268], [130, 268]], '12.4.5.7.1 General']
[[[128, 292], [1108, 292], [1108, 379], [128, 379]], 'The COLLATING SEQUENCE clause specifies the collating sequence to be used for the ordering ofrecord keys and alternate record keys for an indexed file. Multiple collating sequences may be used by specifying a collating sequence clause unique to the primary key or to specific alternate record keys:']
[[[130, 400], [446, 400], [446, 426], [130, 426]], '12.4.5.7.2 General formats']
[[[130, 454], [326, 454], [326, 480], [130, 480]], 'Format 1 (file-level)']
[[[143, 546], [840, 546], [840, 648], [143, 648]], 'IS alphabet-name-1 [alphabet-name-2 ] COLLATING SEQUENCE FOR ALPHANUMERIC IS alphabet-name-1 FOR NATIONAL IS alphabet-name-2']
[[[129, 707], [330, 707], [330, 736], [129, 736]], 'Format 2 (key-level)']
[[[142, 790], [628, 790], [628, 849], [142, 849]], 'data-name-1 COLLATING SEQUENCE OF record-key-name-1']
[[[678, 800], [872, 800], [872, 826], [678, 826]], 'IS alphabet-name-3']
[[[130, 886], [404, 886], [404, 916], [130, 916]], '12.4.5.73 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 940], [236, 940], [236, 964], [130, 964]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[130, 988], [1066, 988], [1066, 1021], [130, 1021]], '1) Alphabet-name-1 shall reference an alphabet that defines an alphanumeric collating sequence']
[[[130, 1042], [996, 1042], [996, 1074], [130, 1074]], '2) Alphabet-name-2 shall reference an alphabet that defines a national collating sequence']
[[[130, 1095], [1101, 1095], [1101, 1127], [130, 1127]], '3) Only one file-level format COLLATING SEQUENCE clause may be specified in one file control entry.']
[[[130, 1150], [238, 1150], [238, 1174], [130, 1174]], 'FORMAT 2']
[[[130, 1200], [1112, 1200], [1112, 1256], [130, 1256]], '4) Data-name-1 shall be a name specified as the data-name in an ALTERNATE RECORD KEY clause or in a RECORD KEY clause in the file control entry_']
[[[130, 1279], [1110, 1279], [1110, 1337], [130, 1337]], '5)   Record-key-name-1 shall be a name specified as a record-key-name in an ALTERNATE RECORD KEY clause or in a RECORD KEY clause in the file control entry']
[[[130, 1362], [758, 1362], [758, 1388], [130, 1388]], '6) Data-name-1 and record-key-name-1 shall not be subscripted.']
[[[133, 1414], [1112, 1414], [1112, 1494], [133, 1494]], '7) When the class of data-name-1 or record-key-name-1 is national, alphabet-name-3 shall reference an alphabet that defines a national collating sequence; otherwise, alphabet-name-3 shall reference an alphabet that defines an alphanumeric collating sequence']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '322']
[[[204, 1550], [356, 1550], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 353 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 187], [1146, 187], [1146, 244], [164, 244]], '8) Neither data-name-1 nor record-key-name-1 shall be specified in more than one COLLATING SEQUENCE clause:']
[[[164, 268], [450, 268], [450, 294], [164, 294]], '12.4.5.7.4 General rules']
[[[166, 322], [310, 322], [310, 346], [166, 346]], 'ALL FORMATS']
[[[164, 370], [1146, 370], [1146, 456], [164, 456]], '1) Each collating sequence is a fixed file attribute and the collating sequence is set upon the successful execution ofan OPEN statement that creates the physical file: The collating sequence used is the one that applies as specified in the following General rules:']
[[[164, 480], [270, 480], [270, 504], [164, 504]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[164, 527], [1149, 527], [1149, 612], [164, 612]], '2) An alphanumeric collating sequence referenced by alphabet-name-1 applies to any record keys of class alphanumeric, whether primary or alternate, not specified in a key-level format of another COLLATING SEQUENCE clause in the file control entry.']
[[[164, 634], [1146, 634], [1146, 719], [164, 719]], '3) A national collating sequence referenced by alphabet-name-2 applies to any record keys of class national, whether primary or alternate, not specified in a key-level format of another COLLATING SEQUENCE clause in the file control entry:']
[[[164, 739], [1147, 739], [1147, 825], [164, 825]], '4) If alphabet-name-1 is not specified in the file control entry, the native alphanumeric collating sequence applies to any record keys of class alphanumeric, whether primary or alternate, not specified in a key-level format ofanother COLLATING SEQUENCE clause:']
[[[164, 845], [1146, 845], [1146, 928], [164, 928]], '5) If alphabet-name-2 is not specified in the file control entry, the native national collating sequence applies to any record keys of class national, whether primary oralternate,not specified in a key-level format of another COLLATING SEQUENCE clause.']
[[[164, 954], [272, 954], [272, 978], [164, 978]], 'FORMAT 2']
[[[166, 1001], [1053, 1001], [1053, 1037], [166, 1037]], '6) Alphabet-name-3 applies to record keys identified by data-name-1 or record-key-name-1.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '323']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 354 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [464, 190], [464, 216], [130, 216]], '12.4.5.8 FILE STATUS clause']
[[[130, 242], [354, 242], [354, 268], [130, 268]], '12.4.5.8.1 General']
[[[130, 292], [1068, 292], [1068, 323], [130, 323]], 'The FILE STATUS clause specifies a data item that contains the status ofan input-output operation.']
[[[130, 348], [436, 348], [436, 374], [130, 374]], '12.4.5.8.2 General format']
[[[132, 404], [414, 404], [414, 430], [132, 430]], 'FILE STATUS IS data-name-1']
[[[130, 466], [402, 466], [402, 494], [130, 494]], '12.4.5.83 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 516], [710, 516], [710, 546], [130, 546]], '1) Data-name-1 shall not be subject to any OCCURS clauses:']
[[[130, 572], [1112, 572], [1112, 629], [130, 629]], '2) Data-name-1 shall reference a two-character data item of the category alphanumeric, defined in the working-storage, local-storage, or linkage section.']
[[[130, 648], [1066, 648], [1066, 678], [130, 678]], '3) Data-name-1 shall not reference a dynamic-length elementary item or a variable-length group.']
[[[130, 702], [874, 702], [874, 730], [130, 730]], '4) Data-name-1 shall not be subject to a BASED clause in its data description:']
[[[130, 756], [404, 756], [404, 782], [130, 782]], '12.4.5.8.4 General rule']
[[[130, 806], [1112, 806], [1112, 916], [130, 916]], '1) Ifthe FILE STATUS clause is specified,the data item referenced by data-name-1 is updated to contain the value of the I-0 status for the file connector referenced by the subject of the entry when the [-0 status associated with that file connector is updated as a result of an input-output statement A list ofthe possible I-0 status values is contained in 9.1.13,1-0 status.']
[[[166, 937], [1112, 937], [1112, 990], [166, 990]], 'NOTE 1 In the case where a file-name is global and data-name-1 isnot, data-name-1is updated by references to file-name in contained programs even though data-name-1 is a local name']
[[[166, 1010], [1112, 1010], [1112, 1086], [166, 1086]], "NOTE 2 While a programmer can alter the value of the FILE STATUS data item, this does not alter the state ofthe file: Consequently for example, if a sequential read produced the file status '10' and the value '00' was then moved to the file status data item, second read would not be successful"]
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '324']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 355 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[163, 189], [490, 189], [490, 216], [163, 216]], '12.4.5.9 LOCK MODE clause']
[[[164, 242], [388, 242], [388, 268], [164, 268]], '12.4.5.9.1 General']
[[[164, 292], [886, 292], [886, 324], [164, 324]], 'The LOCK MODE clause indicates the type of record locking for a shared file_']
[[[164, 348], [470, 348], [470, 374], [164, 374]], '12.4.5.9.2 General format']
[[[359, 435], [447, 435], [447, 455], [359, 455]], 'MANUAL']
[[[852, 436], [952, 436], [952, 498], [852, 498]], 'RECORD RECORDS']
[[[178, 448], [326, 448], [326, 472], [178, 472]], 'LOCK MODE IS']
[[[522, 452], [818, 452], [818, 480], [522, 480]], 'WITH LOCK ON MULTIPLE ]']
[[[356, 468], [482, 468], [482, 494], [356, 494]], 'AUTOMATIC']
[[[164, 544], [438, 544], [438, 574], [164, 574]], '12.4.5.9.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 596], [1146, 596], [1146, 681], [164, 681]], '1) This clause shall not be specified for a file that is the subject ofan APPLY COMMIT clause for which there is an implicit LOCK MODE IS AUTOMATIC WITH LOCK ON MULTIPLE RECORDS applied automatically, including for sequential files_']
[[[164, 700], [1146, 700], [1146, 759], [164, 759]], '2) The MULTIPLE phrase shall not be specified for a file described with sequential organization Or sequential access mode:']
[[[164, 782], [450, 782], [450, 808], [164, 808]], '12.4.5.9.4 General rules']
[[[164, 833], [780, 833], [780, 862], [164, 862]], '1) Ifthe LOCK MODE clause is omitted from a file control entry,']
[[[201, 885], [1148, 885], [1148, 942], [201, 942]], 'a) Ifthere is a SHARING clause in that file control entry, no record locks are set by the execution of 1-0 statements through the associated file connector.']
[[[200, 963], [759, 963], [759, 996], [200, 996]], 'b) If there is no SHARING clause in that file control entry,']
[[[272, 1018], [1146, 1018], [1146, 1074], [272, 1074]], 'Ifan OPEN statement for the associated file connectorhas a SHARING phrase,no record locks are set by the execution of [-0 statements for that opening of the associated file connector_']
[[[270, 1098], [1148, 1098], [1148, 1259], [270, 1259]], 'If an OPEN statement for the associated file connector has no SHARING phrase, the type of record locking for that opening ofa shared file associated with that file connector is defined by the implementor. The implementor may define the default in terms of standard LOCK MODE clause syntax, specify another type ofrecord locking as the default; or specify that the default is no record locking: If the default is defined in terms of standard syntax, then the semantics shall be as if that clause had been specified in the file control entry.']
[[[164, 1280], [1146, 1280], [1146, 1334], [164, 1334]], '2) Ifthe processor does not support record locking, record locks have no effect for the associated file connector:']
[[[164, 1358], [1144, 1358], [1144, 1414], [164, 1414]], '3) If a physical file is open in the sharing with no other mode, the LOCK MODE clause has no effect Otherwise, the LOCK MODE clause has the effects described in the General rules that follow:']
[[[164, 1438], [1146, 1438], [1146, 1492], [164, 1492]], '4) If the AUTOMATIC phrase is specified, the lock mode is automatic. Records are locked when any READ statement is executed.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '325']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 356 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 187], [1110, 187], [1110, 245], [130, 245]], '5) If the MANUAL phrase is specified, the lock mode is manual. Records locks are obtained only when the LOCK phrase is explicitly specified on an [-0 statement']
[[[164, 266], [1112, 266], [1112, 403], [164, 403]], 'Single record locking is specified explicitly by the LOCK ON phrase without the MULTIPLE phrase and implicitly when the LOCK MODE clause is specified with the LOCK ON phrase omitted: Single record locking allows only one record of a file to be locked at a given time through single file connector. Execution of any [-0 statement except START releases any previously locked record in that file for that file connector: Details of record locking are specified in 9.1.16,Record locking:']
[[[164, 424], [1114, 424], [1114, 693], [164, 693]], 'If the MULTIPLE phrase is specified in the LOCK ON phrase, then multiple record locking is said to have been specified and a file connector is permitted to have more than one record of a file locked: A file connector that has specified multiple record locking for a file may hold a number of record locks for that file simultaneously. This prevents other file connectors from accessing any member of the set of locked records, but will not deny them access to records that are not locked. The implementor shall specify the maximum number ofrecord locks that may be held by a file connector; that maximum shall be at least 15. The implementor shall specify the maximum number of record locks that may be held by a run unit; that maximum shall be at least 255_ Any 1-0 statement that attempts to obtain a record lock that would exceed either limit is unsuccessful and receives an [-0 status that indicates that condition: Details of record locking are specified in 9.1.16,Record locking:']
[[[130, 714], [842, 714], [842, 746], [130, 746]], '8) The setting ofa record lock is part of the operation of an [-0 statement']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '326']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 357 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [546, 190], [546, 216], [164, 216]], '12.4.5.10 ORGANIZATION clause']
[[[164, 242], [402, 242], [402, 268], [164, 268]], '12.4.5.10.1 General']
[[[164, 291], [789, 291], [789, 324], [164, 324]], 'The ORGANIZATION clause specifies the logical structure ofa file:']
[[[164, 348], [486, 348], [486, 374], [164, 374]], '12.4.5.10.2 General format']
[[[418, 452], [532, 452], [532, 582], [418, 582]], 'LINE RECORD RELATIVE INDEXED']
[[[558, 464], [692, 464], [692, 490], [558, 490]], 'SEQUENTIAL']
[[[192, 496], [388, 496], [388, 524], [192, 524]], 'ORGANIZATION IS ]']
[[[164, 640], [466, 640], [466, 668], [164, 668]], '12.4.5.10.3 General rules']
[[[164, 690], [1146, 690], [1146, 749], [164, 749]], '1) The ORGANIZATION clause specifies the logical structure ofa file The file organization is established at the time a physical file is created and cannot subsequently be changed:']
[[[164, 770], [1146, 770], [1146, 907], [164, 907]], '2) The LINE SEQUENTIAL phrase specifies that the file organization is line sequential. Line sequential organization is a permanent logical file structure in which a record is identified by a predecessor- successor relationship established when the record is placed into the file Each record in a line sequential file is terminated by an implementor-defined line delimiter. The range of allowable characters in a line sequential file is implementor-defined.']
[[[164, 928], [1148, 928], [1148, 1092], [164, 1092]], '3) The RECORD SEQUENTIAL phrase specifies that the file organization is record sequential. Record sequential organization is a permanent logical file structure in which a record is identified by a predecessor-successor relationship established when the record is placed into the file. Thelength of each record is determined by any information the implementor may add to the record on the physical storage medium (such as record length headers): The length of the record used by the COBOL programmer does not reflect these additions:']
[[[199, 1112], [1146, 1112], [1146, 1197], [199, 1197]], "The RELATIVE phrase specifies that the file organization is relative: Relative organization is permanent logical file structure in which each record is uniquely identified by an integer value greater than zero, that specifies the record's logical ordinal position in the file:"]
[[[164, 1218], [1146, 1218], [1146, 1298], [164, 1298]], '5) The INDEXED phrase specifies that the file organization is indexed. Indexed organization is permanent logical file structure in which each record is identified by the value of one or more keys within that record.']
[[[200, 1322], [1146, 1322], [1146, 1380], [200, 1380]], 'When the ORGANIZATION clause is not specified, sequential  organization with the RECORD SEQUENTIAL phrase is implied:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '327']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 358 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [564, 190], [564, 216], [130, 216]], '12.4.5.11 RECORD DELIMITER clause']
[[[130, 242], [368, 242], [368, 268], [130, 268]], '12.4.5.11.1 General']
[[[128, 292], [1080, 292], [1080, 346], [128, 346]], 'The RECORD DELIMITER clause indicates the method of determining the length ofa variable-length record on the external medium;']
[[[130, 374], [450, 374], [450, 400], [130, 400]], '12.4.5.11.2 General format']
[[[428, 464], [580, 464], [580, 524], [428, 524]], 'STANDARD-1 feature-name-1']
[[[156, 476], [400, 476], [400, 500], [156, 500]], 'RECORD DELIMITER IS']
[[[130, 568], [418, 568], [418, 596], [130, 596]], '12.4.5.11.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 616], [948, 616], [948, 649], [130, 649]], '1) The RECORD DELIMITER clause may be specified only for variable-length records.']
[[[166, 668], [780, 668], [780, 696], [166, 696]], 'NOTE There are three ways variable-length records can be specified:']
[[[238, 714], [1112, 714], [1112, 765], [238, 765]], 'The RECORD clause is not specified and the implementor has specified that variable-length records are obtained in this circumstance.']
[[[238, 788], [626, 788], [626, 819], [238, 819]], 'The RECORD IS VARYING clause is specified.']
[[[238, 836], [1112, 836], [1112, 892], [238, 892]], 'The fixed-or-variable-length format RECORD clause is specified and the implementor has specified that variable-length records are obtained in this circumstance:']
[[[130, 916], [906, 916], [906, 942], [130, 942]], '2) The implementor shall specify the names available for use as feature-name-1.']
[[[130, 968], [430, 968], [430, 994], [130, 994]], '12.4.5.11.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1018], [1112, 1018], [1112, 1101], [130, 1101]], '1) The RECORD DELIMITER clause indicates the method of determining the length ofa variable-length record on the external medium Any method used shall not be reflected in the record area or the record size used within the function, method, or program_']
[[[130, 1124], [836, 1124], [836, 1155], [130, 1155]], '2) If STANDARD-1 is specified, the external medium shall be a tape drive:']
[[[130, 1176], [1112, 1176], [1112, 1232], [130, 1232]], '3) If STANDARD-1 is specified, the method used for determining the length ofa variable-length record shall be that specified in ISO/IEC 1001.2012,7.1.2_']
[[[130, 1254], [1112, 1254], [1112, 1313], [130, 1313]], '4) If feature-name-1 is specified, the method used for determining the length of a variable-length record is thatassociated with feature-name-1 by the implementor']
[[[130, 1334], [1112, 1334], [1112, 1392], [130, 1392]], '5) If the RECORD DELIMITER clause is not specified, the method used for determining the length of a variable-length record is specified by the implementor:']
[[[130, 1413], [1111, 1413], [1111, 1494], [130, 1494]], '6) At the time of a successful execution ofan OPEN statement, the record delimiter is the one specified in the RECORD DELIMITER clause in the file control entry associated with the file-name specified in the OPEN statement:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '328']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 359 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [514, 190], [514, 216], [164, 216]], '12.4.5.12 RECORD KEY clause']
[[[164, 242], [402, 242], [402, 268], [164, 268]], '12.4.5.12.1 General']
[[[164, 294], [1112, 294], [1112, 323], [164, 323]], 'The RECORD KEY clause specifies the prime record key access path to the records in an indexed file_']
[[[164, 348], [486, 348], [486, 374], [164, 374]], '12.4.5.12.2 General format']
[[[190, 438], [832, 438], [832, 494], [190, 494]], 'data-name-1 RECORD KEY IS record-key-name-1 SOURCE IS { data-name-2 }']
[[[164, 570], [452, 570], [452, 600], [164, 600]], '12.4.5.12.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 620], [910, 620], [910, 650], [164, 650]], '1) Data-name-1 and data-name-2 shall not be subject to any OCCURS clauses.']
[[[164, 676], [1146, 676], [1146, 757], [164, 757]], '2) Data-name-1 and data-name-2 shall reference a data item of category alphanumeric or category national within a record description entry associated with the file-name specified in this file control entry. All occurrences of data-name-2 shall be of the same category_']
[[[164, 780], [940, 780], [940, 810], [164, 810]], '3) Data-name-1 and data-name-2 shall not reference a variable-length data item_']
[[[164, 832], [1147, 832], [1147, 912], [164, 912]], '4) If the indexed file contains variable-length records, data-name-1 and each data-name-2 shall be contained within the first n bytes of the record, where n equals the minimum record size specified for the file: (See 13.18.43,RECORD clause:)']
[[[164, 938], [796, 938], [796, 968], [164, 968]], '5) Record-key-name-1 has the class and category of data-name-2.']
[[[164, 992], [466, 992], [466, 1018], [164, 1018]], '12.4.5.12.4 General rules']
[[[164, 1041], [1146, 1041], [1146, 1152], [164, 1152]], '1) The RECORD KEY clause specifies the prime record key for the file that is the subject ofthe entry: The value of the associated record key shall not be equal to the value of the same record key in another record in the file: The equality or inequality is based on the collating sequence used for the file according to the rules for a relation condition_']
[[[164, 1172], [1148, 1172], [1148, 1231], [164, 1231]], '2)    Record-key-name-1 defines a record key consisting of the concatenation of all occurrences of data-name-2 in the order specified.']
[[[164, 1253], [1146, 1253], [1146, 1306], [164, 1306]], '3) The data description of data-name-1 or data-name-2 as well as their relative location within a record shall be the same as that used when the file was created.']
[[[164, 1332], [1147, 1332], [1147, 1440], [164, 1440]], '4) If the file has more than one record description entry, data-name-1 or data-name-2 need only be described in one of these record description entries: The identical byte positions referenced by data-name-1 or data-name-2 in any one record description entry are implicitly referenced as keys for all other record description entries of that file:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '329']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 360 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [500, 190], [500, 216], [130, 216]], '12.4.5.13 RELATIVE KEY clause']
[[[130, 242], [367, 242], [367, 268], [130, 268]], '12.4.5.13.1 General']
[[[127, 294], [1062, 294], [1062, 350], [127, 350]], 'The RELATIVE KEY clause identifies the data item that will contain the relative record number for accessing a relative file.']
[[[130, 374], [450, 374], [450, 400], [130, 400]], '12.4.5.13.2 General format']
[[[132, 430], [430, 430], [430, 456], [132, 456]], 'RELATIVE KEY IS data-name-1']
[[[130, 492], [418, 492], [418, 522], [130, 522]], '12.4.5.13.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 546], [710, 546], [710, 573], [130, 573]], '1) Data-name-1 shall not be subject to any OCCURS clauses:']
[[[130, 595], [1112, 595], [1112, 653], [130, 653]], "2) Data-name-1 shall reference an unsigned integer data item whose description does not contain the picture symbol 'P' _"]
[[[130, 675], [1112, 675], [1112, 728], [130, 728]], '3) Data-name-1 shall not be defined in a record description entry subordinate to the associated file-name']
[[[130, 756], [430, 756], [430, 782], [130, 782]], '12.4.5.13.4 General rules']
[[[130, 806], [1112, 806], [1112, 914], [130, 914]], "1) All records stored in a relative file are uniquely identified by relative record numbers The relative record number ofa given record specifies the record's logical ordinal position in the file: The first logical record has a relative record number of 1,and subsequent logical records have relative record numbers of 2,3,4,"]
[[[130, 939], [1112, 939], [1112, 1023], [130, 1023]], '2) The relative key data item associated with the execution of an input-output statement is the data item referenced by data-name-l; data-name-1 is used to communicate a relative record number between the user and the mass storage control system:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '330']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 361 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [472, 190], [472, 216], [164, 216]], '12.4.5.14 RESERVE clause']
[[[164, 242], [402, 242], [402, 268], [164, 268]], '12.4.5.14.1 General']
[[[164, 295], [1026, 295], [1026, 323], [164, 323]], 'The RESERVE clause allows the user to specify the number of input-output areas allocated:']
[[[164, 348], [486, 348], [486, 374], [164, 374]], '12.4.5.14.2 General format']
[[[190, 436], [468, 436], [468, 490], [190, 490]], 'AREA RESERVE integer-1 AREAS']
[[[164, 532], [454, 532], [454, 558], [164, 558]], '12.4.5.14.3 General rule']
[[[164, 581], [1146, 581], [1146, 668], [164, 668]], '1) If the RESERVE clause is specified, the number of input-output areas allocated is equal to the value of integer-1. If the RESERVE clause is not specified, the number of input-output areas allocated is specified by the implementor:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '331']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 362 ---

[[[128, 79], [390, 79], [390, 112], [128, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [438, 190], [438, 216], [130, 216]], '12.4.5.15 SHARING clause']
[[[130, 242], [368, 242], [368, 268], [130, 268]], '12.4.5.15.1 General']
[[[127, 292], [1098, 292], [1098, 372], [127, 372]], 'The SHARING clause indicates that a file is to participate in file sharing and record locking: It specifies the degree of file sharing (or non-sharing) to be permitted for a file and whether record locks have an effect:']
[[[130, 400], [450, 400], [450, 426], [130, 426]], '12.4.5.15.2 General format']
[[[344, 518], [466, 518], [466, 616], [344, 616]], 'ALL OTHER NO OTHER READ ONLY']
[[[154, 552], [314, 552], [314, 576], [154, 576]], 'SHARING WITH']
[[[130, 664], [420, 664], [420, 688], [130, 688]], '12.4.5.15.3 General rule']
[[[130, 712], [1112, 712], [1112, 799], [130, 799]], '1) The SHARING clause specifies the sharing mode to be used for the file unless it is overridden by the SHARING phrase of the OPEN statement: This clause also specifies whether record locks have an effect: Additional details are specified in 9.1.15, Sharing mode']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '332']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 363 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 188], [529, 188], [529, 224], [164, 224]], '12.4.6 I-0-CONTROL paragraph']
[[[164, 242], [368, 242], [368, 268], [164, 268]], '12.4.6.1 General']
[[[164, 294], [1132, 294], [1132, 376], [164, 376]], 'The I-0-CONTROL paragraph specifies which files and data-items are to be subject to commit and rollback: It also specifies that memory areas associated with different files are to be shared during file processing, record processing, or sort-merge processing:']
[[[163, 400], [450, 400], [450, 426], [163, 426]], '12.4.6.2 General format']
[[[186, 478], [586, 478], [586, 506], [186, 506]], '[-0-CONTROL [[apply-commit-clause]']
[[[616, 480], [822, 480], [822, 506], [616, 506]], '[[ same-clause}_1.]']
[[[164, 546], [1116, 546], [1116, 602], [164, 602]], 'where apply-commit-clause and same-clause are shown below. The apply-commit and same clauses may be specified in any order']
[[[164, 626], [530, 626], [530, 652], [164, 652]], '12.4.6.3 APPLY COMMIT clause']
[[[164, 678], [388, 678], [388, 704], [164, 704]], '12.4.6.3.1 General']
[[[164, 730], [1082, 730], [1082, 782], [164, 782]], 'The APPLY COMMIT clause specifies which files and data-items are to be subject to COMMIT and ROLLBACK']
[[[164, 810], [470, 810], [470, 836], [164, 836]], '12.4.6.3.2 General format']
[[[300, 892], [842, 892], [842, 922], [300, 922]], 'APPLY COMMIT ON [[ file-name-1][identifier-1]]-_']
[[[164, 956], [438, 956], [438, 984], [164, 984]], '12.4.6.33 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 1005], [1146, 1005], [1146, 1058], [164, 1058]], '1) File-name-1 shall be specified in the FILE-CONTROL paragraph of the source element that contains this clause.']
[[[164, 1088], [970, 1088], [970, 1116], [164, 1116]], '2) File-name-1 shall not represent a sort-merge file, nor a file used by report writer']
[[[164, 1138], [1146, 1138], [1146, 1196], [164, 1196]], '3) Multiple instances of Identifier-1 shall represent different non-overlapping 01 or 77 level data-items in the linkage, working-storage and local-storage sections. They shall not be reference-modified.']
[[[164, 1215], [644, 1215], [644, 1247], [164, 1247]], '4) At least one file or data-item shall be specified.']
[[[164, 1268], [802, 1268], [802, 1298], [164, 1298]], '5) This clause shall not be specified in a recursive source element:']
[[[200, 1321], [900, 1321], [900, 1353], [200, 1353]], 'This clause shall not be specified in a class, factory, or interface definition_']
[[[166, 1375], [1070, 1375], [1070, 1406], [166, 1406]], '7) APPLY COMMIT clauses shall not include data items containing object-reference data items']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '333']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 364 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 188], [1112, 188], [1112, 270], [130, 270]], '8) Where there is a dependency of one data item upon another; the 01 or 77 level data items that contain or refer to them shall each either or neither be specified in the list of identifiers, or have been specified in identifiers already subject to the same or another APPLY COMMIT clause']
[[[130, 293], [1112, 293], [1112, 402], [130, 402]], '9) When file descriptions are subject to an APPLY COMMIT clause, the file status data items and data- items specified in the linage or record clauses of those file descriptions are automatically subject to the APPLY COMMIT clause for the file connectors, and they shall only be specified in the linkage, working-storage or local-storage sections, 0r in the case of the record clause also in the file section']
[[[130, 425], [1112, 425], [1112, 480], [130, 480]], '10) No data items referenced by the FILE STATUS clause, the LINAGE clause, the RECORD clause, or separately specified in the APPLY COMMIT clause shall be subject to a BASED clause']
[[[130, 503], [1112, 503], [1112, 559], [130, 559]], '11) Where a file or record area is declared with the GLOBAL clause,it may only be specified in the APPLY COMMIT clause ofthe same source element:']
[[[130, 586], [416, 586], [416, 610], [130, 610]], '12.4.6.3.4 General rules']
[[[130, 635], [1112, 635], [1112, 693], [130, 693]], '1) This clause specifies that the files and data-items listed are to be subject to the commit and rollback facility:']
[[[130, 714], [1112, 714], [1112, 794], [130, 794]], '2) Ifthe same file connector is specified in more than one runtime element within the run unit; then all or none ofthe runtime elements shall have an associated APPLY COMMIT clause in effect for that file connector and that file connector shall be an external file connector']
[[[130, 821], [1112, 821], [1112, 880], [130, 880]], '3) Ifa runtime element that contains an APPLY COMMIT clause is attempted to be activated under the control ofa recursive runtime element, then the EC-FLOW-APPLY exception condition is set to exist:']
[[[130, 900], [1112, 900], [1112, 960], [130, 960]], '4) Ifa runtime element that contains an APPLY COMMIT clause is attempted to be invoked under the control ofa file sort or a merge, then the EC-FLOW-APPLY exception condition is set to exist']
[[[130, 980], [1110, 980], [1110, 1060], [130, 1060]], '5) An APPLY COMMIT clause is active when the runtime element in which it is contained has been activated and, in the case of canceled runtime elements and exited initial programs, has not been deactivated by a COMMIT or ROLLBACK statement:']
[[[164, 1082], [1112, 1082], [1112, 1166], [164, 1166]], 'Ifan attemptis made to change the address ofa data item containinga data-item subject to an APPLY COMMIT clause, the EC-FLOW-APPLY-COMMIT exception condition is set to exist: The ability to detect this is processor dependent:']
[[[166, 1188], [1112, 1188], [1112, 1288], [166, 1288]], "NOTE 1 This will prevent the situation where a data item subject to the APPLY COMMIT clause is passed to called program, which doesn't contain an APPLY COMMIT clause, which then attempts to change the address of that data item before then calling another program with that changed linkage address that does contain an APPLY COMMIT clause and attempts to access that data item_"]
[[[166, 1309], [1112, 1309], [1112, 1405], [166, 1405]], "NOTE 2 In the event that it is not possible to detect this situation, it is the programmer's responsibility to ensure that linkage passed between multiple source elements doesn't have its address changed between source elements that contain the APPLY COMMIT clause by a source element that doesn't have an APPLY COMMIT clause for the same data items"]
[[[166, 1431], [1112, 1431], [1112, 1484], [166, 1484]], 'NOTE 3 Rather than pass data items subject to APPLY COMMIT statements in linkage, such data items can be defined as external, which would protect them from inadvertent addressing changes:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '334']
[[[204, 1550], [356, 1550], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 365 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[163, 189], [418, 189], [418, 216], [163, 216]], '12.4.6.4 SAME clause']
[[[164, 242], [388, 242], [388, 268], [164, 268]], '12.4.6.4.1 General']
[[[164, 292], [1140, 292], [1140, 351], [164, 351]], 'The SAME clause specifies files for which memory areas are to be shared during file processing record processing, or sort-merge processing:']
[[[164, 374], [482, 374], [482, 400], [164, 400]], '12.4.6.4.2 General formats']
[[[164, 426], [363, 426], [363, 454], [164, 454]], 'Format 1 (file-area):']
[[[166, 482], [596, 482], [596, 510], [166, 510]], 'SAME AREA FOR file-name-1 file-name-2 }']
[[[164, 546], [396, 546], [396, 572], [164, 572]], 'Format 2 (record-area):']
[[[166, 601], [680, 601], [680, 630], [166, 630]], 'SAME RECORD AREA FOR file-name-1 { file-name-2 }']
[[[164, 663], [440, 663], [440, 693], [164, 693]], 'Format 3 (sort-merge-area):']
[[[268, 742], [406, 742], [406, 802], [268, 802]], 'SORT SORT-MERGE']
[[[178, 756], [238, 756], [238, 780], [178, 780]], 'SAME']
[[[438, 755], [800, 755], [800, 784], [438, 784]], 'AREA FOR file-name-1 { file-name-2 }']
[[[164, 846], [438, 846], [438, 874], [164, 874]], '12.4.6.4.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 897], [578, 897], [578, 926], [164, 926]], '1) SORT and SORT-MERGE are equivalent:']
[[[164, 948], [1146, 948], [1146, 1002], [164, 1002]], '2) File-name-1 and file-name-2 shall be specified in the FILE-CONTROL paragraph of the source element that contains this SAME clause_']
[[[164, 1030], [906, 1030], [906, 1058], [164, 1058]], '3) File-name-1 and file-name-2 shall not reference an external file connector']
[[[164, 1079], [1073, 1079], [1073, 1115], [164, 1115]], '4) The files specified in a given SAME clause need not all have the same organization or access:']
[[[164, 1134], [1146, 1134], [1146, 1191], [164, 1191]], '5) A given file-name that represents a report file may be specified in one file-area format SAME clause and shall not be specified in a record-area format or sort-merge-area format SAME clause:']
[[[167, 1212], [1148, 1212], [1148, 1292], [167, 1292]], '6) A given file-name that represents a sort or merge file may be specified in one record-area format SAME clause and in one sort-merge-area SAME clause,and shall not be specified in a file-area format SAME clause.']
[[[166, 1317], [1146, 1317], [1146, 1398], [166, 1398]], '7) Agiven file-name that representsa file other than a report file ora sort or merge file may be specified in one file-area format; in one record-area format; and in one 0r more sort-merge-area format SAME clauses']
[[[164, 1420], [1147, 1420], [1147, 1480], [164, 1480]], '8) At least one file-name specified in a sort-merge-area format SAME clause shall represent a sort or merge file:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '335']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 366 ---

[[[128, 80], [390, 80], [390, 112], [128, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 187], [1112, 187], [1112, 296], [130, 296]], '9) If one or more file-names specified in a file-area format SAME clause are also specified in record-area format SAME clause, all of the file-names specified in the file-area format SAME clause shall also be specified in the record-area format SAME clause. Additional file-names not specified in the file-area format SAME clause may be specified in the record-area format SAME clause']
[[[130, 319], [1112, 319], [1112, 428], [130, 428]], '10) If a file-name that represents a file other than a sort or merge file is specified in a file-area format SAME clause and in one or more sort-merge-area format SAME clauses,all ofthe file-names specified in that file-area format SAME clause shall also be specified in those sort-merge-area format SAME clause(s):']
[[[130, 448], [1113, 448], [1113, 506], [130, 506]], '11)A file or record area that is subject to an APPLY COMMIT clause shall not be specified with another file or record area that is not subject to an APPLY COMMIT clause:']
[[[130, 532], [416, 532], [416, 558], [130, 558]], '12.4.6.4.4 General rules']
[[[130, 582], [1112, 582], [1112, 692], [130, 692]], '1) file-area format SAME clause   specifies that two or more   files   referenced by file-name-1, file-name-2 are to use the same memory area during processing: The area being shared includes all storage areas assigned to the files referenced by file-name-1, file-name-2. No more than one of these files may be in the open mode at a given time:']
[[[130, 714], [1112, 714], [1112, 952], [130, 952]], '2) A record-area format SAME clause specifies that two or more files referenced by file-name-1, file-name-2 are to share a memory area for processing the current logical record. All of these files may be in the open mode at the same time, except that only one file that is also specified in a file-area format SAME clause may be open atthat time A logical record in the shared memory area is a logical record ofeach file open in the output mode and ofthe most recently-read file open in theinput mode This is equivalent to an implicit redefinition of the area with records aligned on the leftmost byte position. The record area is available to the runtime element when any file connector referenced by file-name-1, file-name-2, is open: When none of the file connectors is open, the record area is not available to the runtime element']
[[[130, 980], [1110, 980], [1110, 1060], [130, 1060]], '3) The file and record area ofa file subject to commit and rollback may not be shared with another such file or record area unless it has been closed,a commit ora rollback has been executed and the APPLY COMMIT clause for the file has been deactivated:']
[[[130, 1084], [952, 1084], [952, 1114], [130, 1114]], '4) A sort-merge-area format SAME clause specifies that memory is shared as follows:']
[[[166, 1135], [1112, 1135], [1112, 1220], [166, 1220]], 'a) Any storage area allocated for the sorting or merging of a sort 0r merge file specified in sort-merge-area format SAME clause is available for reuse in sorting Or merging any of the other sort or merge files specified in that sort-merge-area format SAME clause']
[[[166, 1242], [1112, 1242], [1112, 1350], [166, 1350]], 'b)   Storage areas assigned to files specified in a sort-merge-area format SAME clause that do not represent sort or merge files may be allocated as needed for sorting or merging the sort or merge files named in that sort-merge-area format SAME clause. The extent of such allocation shall be specified by the implementor.']
[[[167, 1374], [1112, 1374], [1112, 1431], [167, 1431]], 'c) Storage areas assigned to files specified in a sort-merge-area format SAME clause other than sort or merge files do not share the same storage area with each other.']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '336']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 367 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 186], [1146, 186], [1146, 270], [164, 270]], '5)   During the processing of a SORT or MERGE statement that refers to a sort or merge file named in a sort-merge-area format SAME clause, any non-sort and non-merge files associated with file-names specified in that clause shall not be in the open mode']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '337']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [350, 190], [350, 220], [130, 220]], '13 Data division']
[[[130, 242], [296, 242], [296, 268], [130, 268]], '13.1 General']
[[[130, 294], [1076, 294], [1076, 351], [130, 351]], 'The data division describes the data that the runtime module is to acceptas input; to manipulate, to create, or to produce as output: The data division is optional:']
[[[129, 372], [1080, 372], [1080, 428], [129, 428]], 'The following is the general format of the sections in the data division and defines the order of their presentation in the source element']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '338']

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [500, 190], [500, 216], [164, 216]], '13.2 Data division structure']
[[[164, 242], [430, 242], [430, 268], [164, 268]], '13.2.1 General format']
[[[190, 320], [350, 320], [350, 344], [190, 344]], 'DATA DIVISION_']
[[[190, 390], [948, 390], [948, 590], [190, 590]], 'constant-entry file-description-entry record-description-entry type-declaration-entry FILE SECTION_ constant- entry sort-merge-file-description-entry record-description-entry type-declaration-entry']
[[[194, 641], [772, 641], [772, 760], [194, 760]], '77-level-description-entry WORKING-STORAGE SECTION_ constant-entry record-description-entry type-declaration-entry']
[[[192, 787], [738, 787], [738, 906], [192, 906]], '77-level-description-entry LOCAL-STORAGE SECTION constant-entry record-description-entry type-declaration-entry']
[[[190, 937], [660, 937], [660, 1055], [190, 1055]], '77-level-description-entryl LINKAGE SECTION_ constant-entry record-description-entry type-declaration-entry']
[[[190, 1090], [964, 1090], [964, 1148], [190, 1148]], 'REPORT SECTION _ report-description-entry constant-entry report-group-description-entry']
[[[188, 1196], [368, 1196], [368, 1222], [188, 1222]], 'SCREEN SECTION']
[[[400, 1188], [642, 1188], [642, 1245], [400, 1245]], 'constant-entry screen-description-entry']
[[[164, 1288], [584, 1288], [584, 1320], [164, 1320]], '13.3 Explicit and implicit attributes']
[[[163, 1338], [1142, 1338], [1142, 1449], [163, 1449]], 'Attributes may be implicitly or explicitly specified  Any attribute that has been explicitly specified is called an explicit attribute. Some explicit attributes in the description ofa group item apply to its subordinate items; these attributes are implicit attributes ofthe subordinate item. Ifan attributehasnot been explicitly specified for an item or inherited from a group item, the attribute takes on the default']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '339']

[[[127, 188], [1054, 188], [1054, 245], [127, 245]], 'specification, which is also known as an implicit attribute: An implicit attribute is treated as if the attribute had been explicitly specified:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '340']

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [368, 190], [368, 216], [164, 216]], '13.4 File section']
[[[164, 242], [348, 242], [348, 268], [164, 268]], '13.4.1 General']
[[[164, 294], [990, 294], [990, 325], [164, 325]], 'The purpose of the file section is to describe the structure of data, sort, and merge files:']
[[[164, 348], [430, 348], [430, 374], [164, 374]], '13.4.2 General format']
[[[365, 450], [836, 450], [836, 538], [365, 538]], 'constant-entry file-description-entry record-description-entry type-declaration-entry']
[[[178, 535], [322, 535], [322, 562], [178, 562]], 'FILE SECTION']
[[[366, 576], [962, 576], [962, 664], [366, 664]], 'constant-entry sort-merge-file-description-entry record-description-entry type-declaration-entry']
[[[164, 713], [967, 713], [967, 750], [164, 750]], 'where the following meta-language terms are described in the indicated subclauses:']
[[[164, 772], [220, 772], [220, 796], [164, 796]], 'Term']
[[[424, 772], [526, 772], [526, 796], [424, 796]], 'Subclause']
[[[163, 821], [726, 821], [726, 906], [163, 906]], 'constant-- entry 13.10,Constant entry record-description-entry 13.11,Record description entry type-declaration-entry 13.12, Type declaration entry']
[[[164, 930], [386, 930], [386, 958], [164, 958]], '13.4.3 Syntax rule']
[[[164, 979], [1148, 979], [1148, 1062], [164, 1062]], '1) The file section may be specified in a function definition or a program definition: Within a class definition, the file section may be specified only in a factory definition 0r an instance definition, but not in a method definition. The file section shall not be specified within an interface definition.']
[[[164, 1088], [398, 1088], [398, 1114], [164, 1114]], '13.4.4 General rule']
[[[164, 1137], [1146, 1137], [1146, 1222], [164, 1222]], '1) A data-item format or table format VALUE clause specified in the file section is ignored except in the execution ofthe INITIALIZE statement The initial value ofa data item in the file section is undefined, The initial length ofa dynamic-length elementary item is zero.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '341']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 372 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [460, 190], [460, 218], [130, 218]], '13.44.5 File description entry']
[[[130, 242], [334, 242], [334, 268], [130, 268]], '13.4.5.1 General']
[[[130, 293], [1068, 293], [1068, 325], [130, 325]], 'The file description entry (FD entry) represents the highest level of organization in the file section:']
[[[128, 344], [1108, 344], [1108, 482], [128, 482]], 'The file description entry furnishes information concerning the physical structure, identification,and the internal or externalattributes ofa file connector, oftheassociated records,and ofthe associated data items The file description entry also determines whether a file-name is a local name or a global name In addition, for a report file the file description entry furnishes information concerning the physical structure, identification, and report-names pertaining to a report file:']
[[[130, 506], [426, 506], [426, 532], [130, 532]], '13.4.5.2 General formats']
[[[130, 557], [348, 557], [348, 589], [130, 589]], 'Format 1 (sequential):']
[[[132, 626], [278, 626], [278, 652], [132, 652]], 'FD file-name-1']
[[[157, 669], [451, 669], [451, 748], [157, 748]], 'IS EXTERNAL [ AS literal-1 ] | IS GLOBAL']
[[[282, 784], [410, 784], [410, 882], [282, 882]], 'BIT CHARACTER NUMERIC']
[[[157, 821], [245, 821], [245, 841], [157, 841]], 'FORMAT']
[[[442, 820], [504, 820], [504, 844], [442, 844]], 'DATA']
[[[612, 922], [752, 922], [752, 976], [612, 976]], 'CHARACTERS RECORDS']
[[[156, 936], [582, 936], [582, 968], [156, 968]], 'BLOCK CONTAINS integer-1 TO ] integer-2']
[[[160, 996], [304, 996], [304, 1054], [160, 1054]], 'record-clause linage-clause ]']
[[[154, 1090], [730, 1090], [730, 1194], [154, 1194]], 'IS alphabet-name-1 [ alphabet-name-2] CODE-SET FOR ALPHANUMERIC IS alphabet-name-1 FOR NATIONAL IS alphabet-name-2']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '342']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [470, 190], [470, 216], [164, 216]], 'Format 2 (relative-or-indexed):']
[[[166, 258], [312, 258], [312, 282], [166, 282]], 'FD file-name-1']
[[[194, 302], [484, 302], [484, 332], [194, 332]], 'IS EXTERNAL AS literal-1 ] |']
[[[194, 354], [306, 354], [306, 380], [194, 380]], 'IS GLOBAL']
[[[654, 416], [792, 416], [792, 472], [654, 472]], 'CHARACTERS RECORDS']
[[[196, 429], [624, 429], [624, 460], [196, 460]], 'BLOCK CONTAINS integer-1 TQ ] integer-2']
[[[196, 500], [332, 500], [332, 526], [196, 526]], 'record-clause']
[[[164, 564], [346, 564], [346, 590], [164, 590]], 'Format 3 (report):']
[[[166, 629], [312, 629], [312, 657], [166, 657]], 'FD file-name-1']
[[[194, 671], [487, 671], [487, 709], [194, 709]], 'IS EXTERNAL AS literal-1] |']
[[[194, 728], [306, 728], [306, 752], [194, 752]], 'IS GLOBAL']
[[[654, 790], [792, 790], [792, 844], [654, 844]], 'CHARACTERS RECORDS']
[[[196, 802], [624, 802], [624, 834], [196, 834]], 'BLOCK CONTAINS integer-1 TQ ] integer-2']
[[[196, 870], [332, 870], [332, 896], [196, 896]], 'record-clause']
[[[196, 939], [766, 939], [766, 1046], [196, 1046]], 'IS alphabet-name-1 [alphabet-name-2] CODE-SET FOR ALPHANUMERIC IS alphabet-name-1 FOR NATIONAL IS alphabet-name-2']
[[[202, 1090], [346, 1090], [346, 1152], [202, 1152]], 'REPORT IS REPORTS ARE']
[[[381, 1110], [544, 1110], [544, 1138], [381, 1138]], 'report-name-1 }']
[[[164, 1197], [967, 1197], [967, 1234], [164, 1234]], 'where the following meta-language terms are described in the indicated subclauses:']
[[[164, 1256], [220, 1256], [220, 1280], [164, 1280]], 'Term']
[[[346, 1256], [448, 1256], [448, 1280], [346, 1280]], 'Subclause']
[[[161, 1307], [300, 1307], [300, 1360], [161, 1360]], 'linage-clause record-clause']
[[[349, 1308], [596, 1308], [596, 1360], [349, 1360]], '13.18.34,LINAGE clause 13.18.43,RECORD clause']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '343']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 374 ---

[[[128, 79], [390, 79], [390, 112], [128, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [382, 190], [382, 218], [130, 218]], '13.44.5.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 242], [276, 242], [276, 266], [130, 266]], 'ALL FORMATS']
[[[130, 294], [658, 294], [658, 322], [130, 322]], '1) File-name-1 shall be specified in a file control entry_']
[[[130, 348], [744, 348], [744, 376], [130, 376]], '2) The clauses that follow file-name-1 may appear in any order.']
[[[130, 400], [314, 400], [314, 424], [130, 424]], 'FORMATS 1 AND 2']
[[[130, 450], [642, 450], [642, 480], [130, 480]], '3) When no record description entries are specified:']
[[[166, 503], [806, 503], [806, 535], [166, 535]], 'a) a RECORD clause shall be specified in the file description entry,']
[[[166, 556], [1112, 556], [1112, 610], [166, 610]], 'b) a FILE phrase specifying file-name-1 and the FROM phrase shall be specified on all WRITE and REWRITE statements associated with the file; and']
[[[167, 635], [980, 635], [980, 667], [167, 667]], 'c) an INTO phrase shall be specified on all READ statements associated with the file:']
[[[130, 690], [314, 690], [314, 714], [130, 714]], 'FORMATS 1 AND 3']
[[[131, 742], [1112, 742], [1112, 822], [131, 822]], '4) If the LINE SEQUENTIAL phrase ofthe ORGANIZATION clause ofthe sequential format ofthe 12.4.5, File control entry is specified neither the BLOCK CONTAINS clause nor the RECORD CONTAINS clause shall be specified.']
[[[130, 848], [236, 848], [236, 872], [130, 872]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[130, 899], [706, 899], [706, 929], [130, 929]], '5) Format 1 is the file description entry for a sequential file']
[[[131, 950], [990, 950], [990, 983], [131, 983]], '6) If the FORMAT clause is specified, variable-length records shall be specified for the file']
[[[130, 1006], [238, 1006], [238, 1030], [130, 1030]], 'FORMAT 2']
[[[130, 1057], [1112, 1057], [1112, 1115], [130, 1115]], '7) Format 2 is the file description entry for a relative file or an indexed file For an indexed file, one or more record description entries shall be associated with the file description entry.']
[[[130, 1138], [238, 1138], [238, 1162], [130, 1162]], 'FORMAT 3']
[[[130, 1187], [1112, 1187], [1112, 1244], [130, 1244]], '8) Format 3 is the file description entry for a report file: No record description entries or constant entries shall be associated with the file description entry for a report file:']
[[[130, 1267], [1112, 1267], [1112, 1350], [130, 1350]], '9) The subject of a file description entry that specifies a REPORT clause may be referenced in the procedure division only by the USE statement; the WHEN phrase of a PERFORM statement; the CLOSE statement; or the OPEN statement with the OUTPUT or EXTEND phrase']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '344']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 375 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[163, 189], [430, 189], [430, 216], [163, 216]], '13.4.5.4 General rules']
[[[166, 242], [308, 242], [308, 266], [166, 266]], 'ALL FORMATS']
[[[164, 293], [840, 293], [840, 325], [164, 325]], '1) A file description entry associates file-name-1 with a file connector_']
[[[164, 345], [1146, 345], [1146, 403], [164, 403]], '2) Ifthe EXTERNAL clause is specified,all file description entries in the run unitthat reference the same file connector as file-name shall obey the following rules:']
[[[201, 425], [1146, 425], [1146, 482], [201, 482]], 'a) If any of the file description entries has a BLOCK CONTAINS clause, all shall have a BLOCK CONTAINS clause specifying the same minimum and maximum size of the physical record:']
[[[200, 505], [1146, 505], [1146, 560], [200, 560]], 'b) If  any of the file description entries has a CODE-SET clause, all shall have a CODE-SET clause specifying the same character set:']
[[[200, 583], [1146, 583], [1146, 614], [200, 614]], 'c) Ifany of the file description entries has a LINAGE clause,all shall have a LINAGE clause specifying']
[[[272, 635], [807, 635], [807, 667], [272, 667]], 'the same corresponding values for any literals specified']
[[[272, 690], [702, 690], [702, 718], [272, 718]], 'the same corresponding external data items:']
[[[200, 741], [984, 741], [984, 773], [200, 773]], 'd) All file description entries shall have the same smallest and largest record size:']
[[[233, 793], [1082, 793], [1082, 825], [233, 825]], 'If any of the file description entries has a REPORT clause,all shall have a REPORT clause:']
[[[200, 844], [1146, 844], [1146, 896], [200, 896]], 'NOTE Ifa record description entry is not specified for a file description for an external file, the results can be undefined and can result in data corruption:']
[[[164, 922], [270, 922], [270, 946], [164, 946]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[164, 971], [1146, 971], [1146, 1077], [164, 1077]], '3) If the file description entry for a sequential file contains the LINAGE clause and the EXTERNAL clause, the LINAGE-COUNTER data item is an external data item: If the file description entry for a sequential file contains the LINAGE clause and the GLOBAL clause, LINAGE-COUNTER is a global name:']
[[[164, 1106], [272, 1106], [272, 1130], [164, 1130]], 'FORMAT 3']
[[[164, 1157], [1146, 1157], [1146, 1212], [164, 1212]], '4) The report writer logical record structure of the file associated with file-name-1 is defined by the implementor.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '345']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 376 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 188], [587, 188], [587, 221], [130, 221]], '13.4.6  Sort-merge file description entry']
[[[130, 242], [334, 242], [334, 268], [130, 268]], '13.4.6.1 General']
[[[128, 293], [1108, 293], [1108, 456], [128, 456]], 'The sort-merge file description entry (SD entry) represents the highest level of organization in the file section: The sort-merge file description entry furnishes information concerning the physical structure pertaining to a sort or merge file. The clauses ofa sort-merge file description entry (SD entry) specify the size and the names ofthe records associated with a sort file 0r a merge file. The set of records in the file may reside on external media or in the internal storage of the processor: Storage allocation and record management associated with the file are under control of the SORT/MERGE implementation:']
[[[130, 479], [416, 479], [416, 507], [130, 507]], '13.44.6.2 General format']
[[[132, 548], [308, 548], [308, 610], [132, 610]], 'SD file-name-1 [record-clause ]']
[[[130, 642], [718, 642], [718, 666], [130, 666]], 'where record-clause is described in 13.18.43,RECORD clause_']
[[[130, 694], [382, 694], [382, 724], [130, 724]], '13.4.6.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 744], [658, 744], [658, 774], [130, 774]], '1) File-name-1 shall be specified in a file control entry']
[[[130, 797], [1110, 797], [1110, 855], [130, 855]], '2) One or more record description entries shall be associated with the sort-merge file description entry.']
[[[130, 875], [774, 875], [774, 907], [130, 907]], '3) File-name-1 shall not be specified in an input-output statement']
[[[130, 929], [1112, 929], [1112, 986], [130, 986]], '4) A record description entry associated with file-name-1 shall not be specified in an input-output statement other than following the word FROM or the word INTO.']
[[[130, 1010], [384, 1010], [384, 1036], [130, 1036]], '13.4.6.4 General rule']
[[[130, 1060], [692, 1060], [692, 1090], [130, 1090]], '1) The number of characters is specified in terms of bytes']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '346']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 186], [517, 186], [517, 224], [164, 224]], '13.5 Working-storage section']
[[[164, 242], [348, 242], [348, 268], [164, 268]], '13.5.1 General']
[[[164, 292], [1142, 292], [1142, 352], [164, 352]], 'The working-storage section describes recordsand subordinate data items thatare not part offiles Data described in the working-storage section is static or initial data.']
[[[164, 374], [430, 374], [430, 400], [164, 400]], '13.5.2 General format']
[[[180, 456], [768, 456], [768, 574], [180, 574]], '77-level-description-entry WORKING-STORAGE SECTION_ constant-entry record-description-entry type-declaration-entry']
[[[163, 607], [967, 607], [967, 643], [163, 643]], 'where the following meta-language terms are described in the indicated subclauses:']
[[[164, 664], [220, 664], [220, 688], [164, 688]], 'Term']
[[[424, 664], [526, 664], [526, 688], [424, 688]], 'Subclause']
[[[163, 715], [782, 715], [782, 825], [163, 825]], '77-level-entry 13.13,77-level data description entry constant-entry 13.10,Constant entry record-description-entry 13.11_ Record description entry type-declaration-entry 13.12, Type declaration entry']
[[[164, 848], [386, 848], [386, 878], [164, 878]], '13.5.3 Syntax rule']
[[[164, 898], [1147, 898], [1147, 1009], [164, 1009]], '1) The working-storage section may be specified in function definition or a program definition Within a class definition, the working-storage section may be specified only in a factory definition or an instance definition, but not in a method definition. The working-storage section shall not be specified within an interface definition:']
[[[164, 1034], [410, 1034], [410, 1058], [164, 1058]], '13.5.4 General rules']
[[[164, 1084], [1146, 1084], [1146, 1141], [164, 1141]], '1) Data items in the working-storage section of a program that does not have the initial attribute, a function, a factory, or an object are static data.']
[[[164, 1163], [1146, 1163], [1146, 1216], [164, 1216]], '2) Data items in the working-storage section ofa program that does have the initial attribute are initial data_']
[[[164, 1242], [1146, 1242], [1146, 1296], [164, 1296]], '3) Data items in the working-storage section are initialized as indicated in 11.9.10, INITIALIZE clause, 13.18.63,VALUE clause, and 14.6.2.3.2, Initial state.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '347']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 188], [444, 188], [444, 220], [130, 220]], '13.6 Local-storage section']
[[[130, 242], [314, 242], [314, 268], [130, 268]], '13.6.1 General']
[[[130, 293], [616, 293], [616, 325], [130, 325]], 'The local-storage section describes automatic data.']
[[[130, 348], [396, 348], [396, 374], [130, 374]], '13.6.2 General format']
[[[144, 430], [699, 430], [699, 546], [144, 546]], '77-level-description-entry LOCAL-STORAGE SECTION constant-entry record-description-entry type-declaration-entry']
[[[130, 582], [932, 582], [932, 615], [130, 615]], 'where the following meta-language terms are described in the indicated subclauses:']
[[[130, 638], [186, 638], [186, 662], [130, 662]], 'Term']
[[[390, 637], [490, 637], [490, 661], [390, 661]], 'Subclause']
[[[127, 687], [747, 687], [747, 799], [127, 799]], '77-level-entry 13.13,77-level data description entry constant-entry 13.10,Constant entry record-description-entry 13.11,Record description entry type-declaration-entry 13.12, Type declaration entry']
[[[130, 822], [352, 822], [352, 850], [130, 850]], '13.6.3 Syntax rule']
[[[130, 872], [1112, 872], [1112, 926], [130, 926]], '1) The local-storage section may be specified in program definition or function definition 0r in a method definition contained in a class definition.']
[[[130, 954], [376, 954], [376, 980], [130, 980]], '13.6.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1005], [716, 1005], [716, 1034], [130, 1034]], '1) Data items in the local-storage section are automatic data']
[[[130, 1057], [1112, 1057], [1112, 1112], [130, 1112]], '2) Data items in the local-storage section are initialized as indicated in 11.9.10, INITIALIZE clause, 13.18.63,VALUE clause, and 14.6.2.3.2, Initial state:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '348']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
