[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 185], [417, 185], [417, 222], [164, 222]], '13.7 Linkage section']
[[[164, 242], [348, 242], [348, 268], [164, 268]], '13.7.1 General']
[[[164, 290], [826, 290], [826, 326], [164, 326]], 'The linkage section describes formal parameters and returning items']
[[[164, 344], [1142, 344], [1142, 402], [164, 402]], 'Formal parameters and returning items described in the linkage section ofa source element are referred to both by that source element; when it is activated, and by the activating source element:']
[[[164, 428], [430, 428], [430, 452], [164, 452]], '13.7.2 General format']
[[[178, 507], [658, 507], [658, 626], [178, 626]], '77-level-description-entry LINKAGE SECTION_ constant-entry record-description-entry type-declaration-entry']
[[[164, 662], [966, 662], [966, 693], [164, 693]], 'where the following meta-language terms are described in the indicated subclauses:']
[[[164, 718], [220, 718], [220, 742], [164, 742]], 'Term']
[[[424, 716], [526, 716], [526, 742], [424, 742]], 'Subclause']
[[[163, 767], [783, 767], [783, 876], [163, 876]], '77-level-entry 13.13,77-level data description entry constant-entry 13.10,Constant entry record-description-entry 13.11,Record description entry type-declaration-entry 13.12, Type declaration entry']
[[[164, 902], [398, 902], [398, 930], [164, 930]], '13.7.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 951], [1146, 951], [1146, 1008], [164, 1008]], '1) The linkage section may be specified in a program definition, function definition, method definition, program prototype definition, or function prototype definition']
[[[164, 1031], [1146, 1031], [1146, 1115], [164, 1115]], '2) The description of the formal parameters and the returning item that appear in the linkage section ofa function prototype or a program prototype shall match the description of the formal parameters and the returning item in the corresponding function definition Or program definition, respectively:']
[[[164, 1134], [1146, 1134], [1146, 1193], [164, 1193]], '3) The description ofthe parametersand the returning item thatappear in a linkage section shall follow the rules specified in 14.8.2, Parameters and 14.8.3,Returning items,']
[[[164, 1216], [1146, 1216], [1146, 1298], [164, 1298]], '4) A based data item may be referenced as described in 13.18.5,BASED clause; otherwise, a data item defined in the linkage section ofa source element may be referenced within the procedure division ofthat source element if,and only if,it satisfies one of the following conditions:']
[[[200, 1320], [1142, 1320], [1142, 1350], [200, 1350]], 'a) Itis an operand ofthe USING phrase or the RETURNING phrase ofthe procedure division header']
[[[200, 1374], [1146, 1374], [1146, 1426], [200, 1426]], 'b) It is subordinate to an operand ofthe USING phrase or the RETURNING phrase of the procedure division header:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '349']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 380 ---

[[[128, 80], [390, 80], [390, 112], [128, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[202, 189], [1112, 189], [1112, 240], [202, 240]], 'It is defined with a REDEFINES or RENAMES clause, the object of which satisfies one of the above conditions.']
[[[202, 268], [852, 268], [852, 296], [202, 296]], 'It is subordinate to any item that satisfies the condition in subrule €']
[[[166, 322], [1112, 322], [1112, 372], [166, 372]], 'e) It is a condition-name or index-name associated with a data item that satisfies one ofthe above conditions.']
[[[130, 398], [866, 398], [866, 430], [130, 430]], '5) A formal parameter ofa function shall not be used as a receiving operand_']
[[[130, 454], [376, 454], [376, 478], [130, 478]], '13.7.4 General rules']
[[[130, 506], [828, 506], [828, 532], [130, 532]], '1) The access t0 a based data item is described in 13.18.5,BASED clause.']
[[[130, 556], [1113, 556], [1113, 693], [130, 693]], '2) The mechanism by which correspondence is established between the formal parameters and returning items described in the linkage section and data items described in the activating element is described in 14.2.3, General rules ofthe procedure division. In the case of index-names, no such correspondence is established and index-names in the activated and activating source elements always refer to separate indices']
[[[130, 715], [1112, 715], [1112, 878], [130, 878]], '3) Ina program definition,access to formal parameters and the returningitem is guaranteed ifand only if the program is to execute under the control ofa CALL statement; and the CALL statement contains a USING phrase: Ifa formal parameter or a returning item is accessed in a program thatis nota called program, such as a program that is activated by the operating system, the effect is undefined. If a program is activated by a non-COBOL runtime element; the implementor defines whether access to formal parameters and the returning item is guaranteed.']
[[[130, 898], [1113, 898], [1113, 957], [130, 957]], '4) In a function definition and in a method definition, access to formal parameters and the returning item is always guaranteed:']
[[[130, 976], [1113, 976], [1113, 1218], [130, 1218]], '5) Adata-item format or table format VALUE clause specified in the linkage section is ignored except in the execution of an explicit or implicit INITIALIZE statement: If the runtime element containing the linkage section is activated by a COBOL runtime element; the initial value ofa data item in thelinkage section is determined by the value of the corresponding formal parameter in the activating runtime element; as described in 14.2.3, General rules of the procedure division_ If the runtime element containing the linkage section is activated by the operating system, the initial value of a linkage section data item is undefined. If the runtime element containing the linkage section is activated by non-COBOL runtime element; the initial value of a linkage section data item is defined by the implementor:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '350']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 189], [404, 189], [404, 219], [164, 219]], '13.8 Report section']
[[[164, 242], [348, 242], [348, 268], [164, 268]], '13.8.1 General']
[[[164, 295], [1132, 295], [1132, 350], [164, 350]], 'The report section describes the reports to be written t0 report files: The description of each report begins with a report description (RD) entry and is followed by one or more report group descriptions.']
[[[164, 374], [430, 374], [430, 400], [164, 400]], '13.8.2 General format']
[[[178, 476], [624, 476], [624, 508], [178, 508]], 'REPORT SECTION_ report-description-entry']
[[[652, 470], [958, 470], [958, 528], [652, 528]], 'constant-entry report-group-description-entry']
[[[164, 570], [966, 570], [966, 603], [164, 603]], 'where the following meta-language terms are described in the indicated subclauses:']
[[[164, 626], [220, 626], [220, 650], [164, 650]], 'Term']
[[[456, 626], [556, 626], [556, 650], [456, 650]], 'Subclause']
[[[162, 678], [835, 678], [835, 735], [162, 735]], 'constant-entry 13.10, Constant entry report-group-description-entry13.8.5,Report group description entry']
[[[164, 758], [386, 758], [386, 786], [164, 786]], '13.8.3 Syntax rule']
[[[164, 807], [1146, 807], [1146, 890], [164, 890]], '1) The report section may be specified in a function definition or a program definition. Within a class definition, the report section may be specified only in a factory definition 0r an instance definition, butnotin a method definition. The report section shall not be specified within an interface definition.']
[[[164, 915], [536, 915], [536, 945], [164, 945]], '13.8.4 Report description entry']
[[[164, 966], [1078, 966], [1078, 1022], [164, 1022]], 'The report description (RD) entry gives a name to the report and defines its physical and logical subdivisions. (See 13.8.6.2, Physical subdivisions ofa report:)']
[[[162, 1045], [1142, 1045], [1142, 1178], [162, 1178]], 'An RD entry shall be followed by one 0r more report group description entries The RD entry and the report group description entries that follow fully describe one report; Areport is in the active state after the successful execution ofan INITIATE statement referencing that report description entry and before the successful execution ofa TERMINATE statement referencing that report description entry. At any other time;it is in the inactive state;']
[[[163, 1203], [608, 1203], [608, 1236], [163, 1236]], '13.8.5 Report group description entry']
[[[164, 1257], [1143, 1257], [1143, 1366], [164, 1366]], 'Areport group is a block ofzero, one, or more report lines that is treated, both logically and visually,as single unit Each report group description shall consist ofa level 1 report description entry followed by zero, one, or more subordinate entries, describing the vertical and horizontal layout ofthe report group and the content or origin of each of its printed data items, known as printable items']
[[[161, 1387], [1142, 1387], [1142, 1472], [161, 1472]], 'The report groupsassociated with the report are specified immediately following the report description entry. If several report groups are specified, the order in which they are defined is not significant: The first entry of each report group description has level number 1 and a TYPE clause and,ifa data-name is']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '351']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 382 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[128, 188], [1094, 188], [1094, 245], [128, 245]], 'also specified, this may be used subsequently to identify the report group. Further subordinate group and elementary entries may be specified to describe additional elements of the report group.']
[[[130, 268], [450, 268], [450, 298], [130, 298]], '13.8.6 Report subdivisions']
[[[130, 321], [332, 321], [332, 349], [130, 349]], '13.8.6.1 General']
[[[147, 370], [1068, 370], [1068, 403], [147, 403]], 'report has physical and logical subdivisions that interact to determine what is printed on a page']
[[[130, 426], [608, 426], [608, 457], [130, 457]], '13.8.6.2 Physical subdivisions ofa report']
[[[130, 477], [332, 477], [332, 513], [130, 513]], '13.8.6.2.1 Pages']
[[[128, 530], [1107, 530], [1107, 667], [128, 667]], "Each report is composed ofpages ofequal size, defined by the PAGE clause, or one page ofindefinite size_ Each page heading body group (detail, control hea ding' or control footing),and page footing appears in separate subdivision ofthe page. Each reportheading or report footing may appear in any position on the page: Advancing to a new page is executed automatically for body groups, including the printing ofa page footing and page heading;"]
[[[130, 690], [326, 690], [326, 716], [130, 716]], '13.8.6.2.2 Lines']
[[[126, 742], [1104, 742], [1104, 825], [126, 825]], 'Each report group is divided vertically into zero, one, or more lines. Each line, or multiple line set, is represented in the report group description by a LINE clause: The NEXT GROUP clause, where defined, specifies additional vertical spacing following the report group:']
[[[130, 848], [412, 848], [412, 878], [130, 878]], '13.8.6.23 Report Items']
[[[126, 899], [1108, 899], [1108, 1058], [126, 1058]], 'Each line of each report group is divided horizontally into zero, one, or more printable items Each printable item; or adjacent set of printable items, is defined in the report group description by an elementary entry containing a COLUMN clause. The value that is placed in a printable item is determined solely by a SOURCE, SUM, or VALUE clause in its data description entry. In addition, unprintable items may be specified whose entries contain no COLUMN or LINE clause but from which printable items may be derived,']
[[[129, 1085], [1106, 1085], [1106, 1140], [129, 1140]], 'Report items shall not be accessed or referred to by any clauses in any other section ofthe data division or by any other procedure division statements, except in the following cases:']
[[[130, 1162], [816, 1162], [816, 1192], [130, 1192]], '1) Sum counters may be inspected or altered in the procedure division.']
[[[130, 1217], [862, 1217], [862, 1246], [130, 1246]], '2) Report groups of type DETAIL are accessed by the GENERATE statement']
[[[127, 1268], [1074, 1268], [1074, 1325], [127, 1325]], 'report item referenced in a function identifier or inline method invocation shall be an elementary report item:']
[[[130, 1348], [602, 1348], [602, 1379], [130, 1379]], '13.8.6.3 Logical Subdivisions ofa Report']
[[[129, 1401], [1108, 1401], [1108, 1457], [129, 1457]], 'Report groups of type DETAIL may be structured into a nested set of control groups. Each control group may begin with a control heading and end with a control footing:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '352']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 383 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[163, 188], [1144, 188], [1144, 320], [163, 320]], 'A control break occurs when a change of value is detected in a control data item during the execution of a GENERATE statement The hierarchy of control data items is used to check automatically for any such change in value: The detection ofa control break causes the same GENERATE statement to print each defined control footing, in reverse hierarchical order,and each defined control heading, in hierarchical order:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '353']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [368, 190], [368, 216], [130, 216]], '13.9 Screen section']
[[[130, 244], [314, 244], [314, 268], [130, 268]], '13.9.1 General']
[[[130, 292], [1044, 292], [1044, 346], [130, 346]], 'The screen section describes the screens to be displayed during terminal I-O. The screen section describes screen records and subordinate screen items']
[[[130, 374], [396, 374], [396, 400], [130, 400]], '13.9.2 General format']
[[[142, 468], [322, 468], [322, 492], [142, 492]], 'SCREEN SECTION_']
[[[353, 460], [598, 460], [598, 516], [353, 516]], 'constant-entry screen-description-entry']
[[[130, 552], [932, 552], [932, 583], [130, 583]], 'where the following meta-language terms are described in the indicated subclauses:']
[[[130, 608], [190, 608], [190, 632], [130, 632]], 'Term']
[[[388, 606], [498, 606], [498, 632], [388, 632]], 'Subclause']
[[[128, 657], [689, 657], [689, 715], [128, 715]], 'constant-entry 13.10,Constant entry screen-description-entry 13.17, Screen description entry']
[[[130, 738], [352, 738], [352, 768], [130, 768]], '13.9.3 Syntax rule']
[[[130, 788], [1112, 788], [1112, 894], [130, 894]], '1) The screen section may be specified in a function definition or a program definition. Within a class definition, the screen section may be specified only in a factory definition Or an instance definition_ but not in a method definition: The screen section shall not be specified within an interface definition']
[[[130, 922], [364, 922], [364, 948], [130, 948]], '13.9.4 General rule']
[[[130, 975], [1108, 975], [1108, 1028], [130, 1028]], '1) Data items in the screen section are initialized as indicated in 11.9.10, INITIALIZE clause, 13.18.63, VALUE clause, and 14.6.2.3.2,Initial state.']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '354']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 189], [406, 189], [406, 218], [164, 218]], '13.10 Constant entry']
[[[164, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [164, 268]], '13.10.1 General']
[[[166, 294], [918, 294], [918, 322], [166, 322]], 'A constant entry defines a constant: A constant may be used in place of a literal:']
[[[164, 348], [446, 348], [446, 374], [164, 374]], '13.10.2 General format']
[[[686, 446], [1046, 446], [1046, 566], [686, 566]], 'arithmetic-expression-1 BYTE-LENGTH OF data-name-1 AS literal-1 LENGTH OF data-name-2']
[[[190, 504], [660, 504], [660, 552], [190, 552]], 'constant-name-1 CONSTANT [IS GLOBAL] 01']
[[[684, 584], [1032, 584], [1032, 614], [684, 614]], 'FROM compilation-variable-name-1']
[[[164, 664], [412, 664], [412, 694], [164, 694]], '13.103 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 715], [1146, 715], [1146, 772], [164, 772]], '1) If the operand of the constant entry consists of a single numeric literal, that operand is treated as a literal, not as an arithmetic-expression:']
[[[164, 794], [1146, 794], [1146, 879], [164, 879]], '2) Except in a compiler directive, constant-name-1 may be used anywhere that a format specifies a literal of the class and category of constant-name-1. If constant-name-1 is an integer; it may also be used to specify repetition in a picture character-string, as specified in 13.18.40,PICTURE clause:']
[[[164, 900], [802, 900], [802, 930], [164, 930]], '3) All subscripts of data-name-l and data-name-2 shall be literals']
[[[164, 953], [1146, 953], [1146, 1006], [164, 1006]], '4) The length of data-name-1 or data-name-2 shall not be dependent, directly 0r indirectly, upon the value of constant-name-1.']
[[[164, 1033], [1146, 1033], [1146, 1088], [164, 1088]], '5) Neither the value of literal-1 nor the value of any of the literals in arithmetic-expression-1 shall be dependent; directly or indirectly, upon the value of constant-name-l_']
[[[167, 1112], [1106, 1112], [1106, 1142], [167, 1142]], '6) Neither literal-1 nor any of the literals in arithmetic-expression-1 shall be a figurative constant:']
[[[166, 1165], [1148, 1165], [1148, 1248], [166, 1248]], '7) Arithmetic-expression-1 shall be formed in accordance with 7.3.6, Compile-time arithmetic expressions, with the exception that all of the operands shall be literals and none shall be compilation-variable-names:']
[[[164, 1269], [1146, 1269], [1146, 1324], [164, 1324]], '8) Compilation-variable-name-1 shall be a compilation-variable-name for which the defined condition is currently true:']
[[[164, 1346], [1147, 1346], [1147, 1428], [164, 1428]], '9) If constant-name-1 duplicates another constant-name, the specification ofarithmetic-expression-l literal-1, data-name-1, data-name-2, or compilation-variable-name-1 shall be the same as specified in the other constant-name']
[[[164, 1456], [992, 1456], [992, 1484], [164, 1484]], '10) Data-name-1 and data-name-2 shall not be described with the ANY LENGTH clause_']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '355']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 386 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[127, 187], [1112, 187], [1112, 240], [127, 240]], '11) Data-name-1 and data-name-2, if defined in the report section, shall reference elementary report items.']
[[[130, 266], [1112, 266], [1112, 324], [130, 324]], '12) Data-name-1 and data-name-2 shall not be dynamic-length elementary items or variable-length groups:']
[[[130, 348], [390, 348], [390, 374], [130, 374]], '13.10.4 General rules']
[[[130, 398], [1112, 398], [1112, 478], [130, 478]], '1) If literal-1 or compilation-variable-name-1 is specified, the effect of specifying constant-name-1 in other than this entry is as if literal-l or the text represented by compilation-variable-name-1 were written where constant-name-1 is written_']
[[[130, 502], [1112, 502], [1112, 560], [130, 560]], '2) If literal-1 or compilation-variable-name-1 is specified, the class and category of constant-name-1 is the same as that of literal-1 or the literal represented by compilation-variable-name-1_']
[[[130, 582], [1112, 582], [1112, 668], [130, 668]], '3)   If arithmetic-expression-1, data-name-1,or data-name-2 is specified, the effect of writing constant- name-1 in other than this entry is as if an integer literal were written where constant-name-1 is written. This integer literal has the value specified in these general rules']
[[[130, 688], [1114, 688], [1114, 774], [130, 774]], '4) If arithmetic-expression-1 is specified, it is evaluated in accordance with 7.3.6, Compile-time arithmetic   expressions, to determine the value of constant-name-1, The class and category of constant-name-l is numeric Constant-name-1 is an integer:']
[[[130, 794], [1113, 794], [1113, 900], [130, 900]], '5) If the BYTE-LENGTH phrase is specified, the class and category of constant-name-1 is numeric_ Constant-name-1isan integer: The value of constant-name-1 is determined as specified in the BYTE- LENGTH intrinsic function with the exception that when data-name-1 is an occurs-depending group item, the maximum size of the data item is used,']
[[[130, 926], [1113, 926], [1113, 1032], [130, 1032]], '6) If the LENGTH phrase is specified, the class and category of constant-name-1 is numeric: Constant- name-1 is an integer. The value of constant-name-1 is determined as specified in the LENGTH intrinsic function with the exception that when data-name-2 is an occurs-depending group item; the maximum size ofthe data item is used.']
[[[130, 1550], [172, 1550], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '356']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 387 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 188], [523, 188], [523, 221], [164, 221]], '13.11 Record description entry']
[[[164, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [164, 268]], '13.11.1 General']
[[[164, 293], [1146, 293], [1146, 372], [164, 372]], 'Arecord description entry consists ofa set of data description entries, the first of which shall have level- number 1,that describe the characteristics ofa particular record. Any data item that has been described with level-number 1 and whose declaration does not include the TYPEDEF clause is a record:']
[[[164, 399], [1124, 399], [1124, 482], [164, 482]], 'Arecord description may have a hierarchical structure: The structure ofa record description and the elements allowed in a record description entry are explained in 8.5.1.3, Levels, and in 13.16,Data description entry.']
[[[162, 506], [1142, 506], [1142, 614], [162, 614]], 'Data elements that bear no hierarchical relationship to any other data item may be described as records that are single elementary items Alternatively,such data elements when defined in the working-storage section, local-storage section,and linkage section may be described as separate data description entries having level-number 77,as described in 13.13,77-level data description entry:']
[[[164, 636], [499, 636], [499, 669], [164, 669]], '13.12 Type declaration entry']
[[[164, 688], [1138, 688], [1138, 796], [164, 796]], 'A type declaration entry is a data description entry that contains a TYPEDEF clause A type declaration entry may have a hierarchical structure beginning with level-number 1. The structure of a type declaration entry and the elements allowed in a type declaration entry are explained in 8.5.1.3,Levels, and in 13.16, Data description entry.']
[[[166, 819], [718, 819], [718, 851], [166, 851]], 'A type declaration entry has no storage associated with it:']
[[[164, 872], [1072, 872], [1072, 926], [164, 926]], 'Further details regarding the specification ofa type declaration entry are described in 13.18.58 TYPEDEF clause:']
[[[164, 954], [586, 954], [586, 982], [164, 982]], '13.13 77-level data description entry']
[[[160, 1004], [1142, 1004], [1142, 1116], [160, 1116]], 'Items in the linkage section, local-storage, and the working-storage section that bear no hierarchical relationship to one another need not be grouped into records, provided they do not need to be further subdivided. Instead, they are classified and defined as noncontiguous elementary data items Each of these items is defined in a separate data description entry that begins with the special level-number 77']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '357']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 388 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 189], [484, 189], [484, 219], [130, 219]], '13.14 Report description entry']
[[[130, 242], [326, 242], [326, 268], [130, 268]], '13.14.1 General']
[[[130, 293], [1080, 293], [1080, 325], [130, 325]], 'The report description entry names a report and describes its general physical and logical structure_']
[[[130, 348], [410, 348], [410, 374], [130, 374]], '13.14.2 General format']
[[[132, 412], [312, 412], [312, 629], [132, 629]], 'RD report-name-1 IS GLOBAL ] [code-clause] [control-clause] [page-clause']
[[[129, 657], [927, 657], [927, 693], [129, 693]], 'where the following meta-language terms are described in the indicated subclauses']
[[[130, 714], [186, 714], [186, 738], [130, 738]], 'Term']
[[[274, 714], [376, 714], [376, 738], [274, 738]], 'Subclause']
[[[129, 766], [534, 766], [534, 849], [129, 849]], 'code-clause 13.18.12,CODE clause control-clause 13.18.16,CONTROL clause page-clause 13.1839,PAGE clause']
[[[130, 872], [378, 872], [378, 900], [130, 900]], '13.14.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 921], [1110, 921], [1110, 978], [130, 978]], '1) There shall be one and only one REPORT clause specifying report-name-1 in a given file description entry.']
[[[130, 1002], [776, 1002], [776, 1033], [130, 1033]], '2) The clauses that follow report-name-1 may appear in any order:']
[[[130, 1056], [380, 1056], [380, 1082], [130, 1082]], '13.14.4 General rule']
[[[130, 1106], [1112, 1106], [1112, 1164], [130, 1164]], '1) If GLOBAL is specified, report-name-1 and all its constituent report groups, its PAGE-COUNTER and LINE-COUNTER, and any sum counters defined in report-name-1 are global']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '358']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 389 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 188], [591, 188], [591, 221], [164, 221]], '13.15 Report group description entry']
[[[164, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [164, 268]], '13.15.1 General']
[[[162, 292], [1138, 292], [1138, 351], [162, 351]], 'The report group description entry specifies the characteristics ofa report group and of the individual items within a report group.']
[[[164, 374], [446, 374], [446, 400], [164, 400]], '13.15.2 General format']
[[[164, 439], [506, 439], [506, 658], [164, 658]], 'level-number entry-name-clause ] [type-clause] [next-group-clause] [line-clause] [picture-clause] DISPLAY USAGE IS ] NATIONAL']
[[[178, 674], [402, 674], [402, 788], [178, 788]], '[sign-clause] [justified-clause] [column-clause] [BLANK WHEN ZERO]']
[[[184, 808], [322, 808], [322, 892], [184, 892]], 'source-clause sum-clause value-clause']
[[[178, 910], [1088, 910], [1088, 1050], [178, 1050]], 'PRESENT WHEN condition-1 | [GROUP INDICATE] [OCCURS integer-1 TQ ] integer-2 TIMES DEPENDING ON data-name-1 ] [ STEP integer-3 ]] varying-clause]']
[[[164, 1086], [966, 1086], [966, 1118], [164, 1118]], 'where the following meta-language terms are described in the indicated subclauses:']
[[[164, 1144], [220, 1144], [220, 1168], [164, 1168]], 'Term']
[[[382, 1142], [484, 1142], [484, 1168], [382, 1168]], 'Subclause']
[[[161, 1196], [348, 1196], [348, 1484], [161, 1484]], 'column-clause entry-name-clause justified-clause line-clause next-group-clause picture-clause sign-clause source-clause sum-clause type-clause value-clause']
[[[381, 1195], [840, 1195], [840, 1487], [381, 1487]], '13.18.14, COLUMN clause 13.18.20, Entry-name clause 13.18.32, JUSTIFIED clause 13.18.35, LINE clause 13.18.37, NEXT GROUP clause 13.18.40, PICTURE clause 13.18.52, SIGN clause 13.18.53, SOURCE clause 13.18.54, SUM clause 13.18.57 , TYPE clause (report-group format) 13.18.63,VALUE clause (report-section format)']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '359']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 390 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[128, 190], [274, 190], [274, 218], [128, 218]], 'varying-clause']
[[[347, 188], [602, 188], [602, 216], [347, 216]], '13.18.64,VARYING clause']
[[[130, 242], [378, 242], [378, 272], [130, 272]], '13.15.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 295], [846, 295], [846, 324], [130, 324]], '1) Report group description entries may appear only in the report section']
[[[130, 345], [1112, 345], [1112, 428], [130, 428]], '2) If the entry-name clause is specified, the data-name format Or filler format shall be specified. The entry-name clause shall immediately follow the level-number AlL other clauses may be written in any order:']
[[[130, 453], [676, 453], [676, 483], [130, 483]], '3) Level-number shall be any integer from 1 through 49']
[[[130, 503], [895, 503], [895, 535], [130, 535]], '4) The first entry that follows a report description entry shall be a level 1 entry:']
[[[130, 556], [1112, 556], [1112, 586], [130, 586]], '5) The TYPE clause may be specified only in a level 1 entry and shall be specified in every level 1 entry.']
[[[133, 608], [778, 608], [778, 639], [133, 639]], '6) The NEXT GROUP clause may be specified only in a level 1 entry_']
[[[130, 661], [1112, 661], [1112, 772], [130, 772]], '7) The data-name format ofthe entry-name clause shall be specified when the data-name is referenced in a GENERATE statement; a USE BEFORE REPORTING statement; as a qualifier for a SUM counter, in the UPON phrase of the SUM clause, 0r as an operand in a SUM clause: The data-name shall not be referenced in any other way.']
[[[130, 793], [1112, 793], [1112, 846], [130, 846]], '8) No report group description entry with a LINE clause shall be subordinate to another entry with a LINE clause:']
[[[130, 874], [1112, 874], [1112, 926], [130, 926]], '9) Every elementary entry with a COLUMN clause but no LINE clause shall be subordinate to an entry with a LINE clause_']
[[[130, 951], [1110, 951], [1110, 1004], [130, 1004]], '10) Every elementary entry with a COLUMN clause shall also contain either a SOURCE, VALUE or SUM clause:']
[[[130, 1032], [1112, 1032], [1112, 1088], [130, 1088]], '11) The PICTURE, COLUMN, SOURCE, VALUE, SUM, and GROUP INDICATE clauses may be written only in an elementary entry:']
[[[130, 1109], [1084, 1109], [1084, 1141], [130, 1141]], '12)A PICTURE clause shall be specified in every elementary entry that has a SOURCE or SUM clause.']
[[[130, 1162], [984, 1162], [984, 1192], [130, 1192]], '13)A COLUMN clause shall be specified in each elementary entry that has a VALUE clause']
[[[130, 1217], [1112, 1217], [1112, 1298], [130, 1298]], '14) The PICTURE clause may be omitted for an elementary item when an alphanumeric, boolean or national literal that is not a zero-length literal is specified in the VALUE clause: A PICTURE clause is implied as follows:']
[[[166, 1321], [674, 1321], [674, 1353], [166, 1353]], "a) If the literal is alphanumeric, 'PICTURE X(length)'"]
[[[166, 1374], [621, 1374], [621, 1403], [166, 1403]], "b) If the literal is boolean, 'PICTURE 1(length)'"]
[[[202, 1428], [626, 1428], [626, 1456], [202, 1456]], "If the literal is national, 'PICTURE N(length)'"]
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '360']
[[[204, 1550], [356, 1550], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 391 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[200, 187], [846, 187], [846, 219], [200, 219]], 'where length is the length of the literal as specified in 8.3.3, Literals']
[[[164, 238], [1050, 238], [1050, 271], [164, 271]], '15) If BLANK WHEN ZERO or JUSTIFIED is specified, a COLUMN clause shall also be specified:']
[[[164, 294], [1148, 294], [1148, 346], [164, 346]], '16) Condition-1 shall not reference any sum counter; LINE-COUNTER, PAGE-COUNTER, or other report section data item.']
[[[161, 371], [1146, 371], [1146, 429], [161, 429]], '17) The GROUP INDICATE clause shall not be specified in an entry in which the PRESENT WHEN clause is specified.']
[[[164, 454], [424, 454], [424, 478], [164, 478]], '13.15.44 General rules']
[[[164, 503], [1146, 503], [1146, 557], [164, 557]], '1) Each level entry identifies a report group: The report group is defined by this entry and all its subordinate entries:']
[[[164, 584], [1146, 584], [1146, 690], [164, 690]], '2) Except for the additional restrictions defined under syntaxrules above,the USAGE, PICTURE,BLANK WHEN ZERO and JUSTIFIED clauses are the same clauses as those that are described under the general format for a data description entry and shall obey the syntax rules and general rules defined for each clause (See 13.18,Data division clauses.)']
[[[164, 715], [1148, 715], [1148, 878], [164, 878]], '3) An entry that contains either an OCCURS clause 0r a LINE or COLUMN clause with more than one operand is said to be a repeating entry, and the number of repetitions is defined to be integer-2 of the OCCURS clause or the number of operands of the LINE or COLUMN clause, whichever is applicable: The number of repetitions of an entry that is not a repeating entry is defined to be 1.A report item is a repeating item ifit is defined by a repeating entry or by an entry that is subordinate to a repeating entry.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '361']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 392 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [460, 190], [460, 218], [130, 218]], '13.16 Data description entry']
[[[130, 242], [326, 242], [326, 268], [130, 268]], '13.16.1 General']
[[[127, 292], [1108, 292], [1108, 376], [127, 376]], 'Adata description entry specifies thecharacteristics ofa particular item ofdata.Alevel 1 data description entry within the file, working-storage, local-storage, or linkage section determines whether the record and its subordinate data items have local names or global names:']
[[[128, 398], [1066, 398], [1066, 452], [128, 452]], 'A level 1 data description entry in the working-storage section determines the internal or external attribute of the record and its subordinate data items_']
[[[128, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [128, 1576]], '362']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 393 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [456, 190], [456, 216], [164, 216]], '13.16.2 General formats']
[[[164, 242], [442, 242], [442, 270], [164, 270]], 'Format 1 (data-description):']
[[[174, 317], [492, 317], [492, 536], [174, 536]], 'level-number entry-name-clause] REDEFINES data-name-1 ] [IS TYPEDEF STRONG ] ] ALIGNED ] ANY LENGTH ] BASED ] BLANK WHEN ZERO']
[[[205, 552], [894, 552], [894, 620], [205, 620]], 'CONSTANT RECORD | DYNAMIC LENGTH [dynamic-length-structure-name-1 ] LIMIT IS integer-1 ] ]']
[[[212, 642], [480, 642], [480, 669], [212, 669]], 'IS EXTERNAL AS literal-1 ]']
[[[202, 682], [314, 682], [314, 706], [202, 706]], 'IS GLOBAL ]']
[[[388, 737], [488, 737], [488, 789], [388, 789]], 'BIT NATIONAL']
[[[204, 750], [362, 750], [362, 774], [204, 774]], 'GROUP-USAGE IS']
[[[234, 828], [330, 828], [330, 886], [234, 886]], 'JUSTIFIED JUST']
[[[356, 842], [418, 842], [418, 866], [356, 866]], 'RIGHT']
[[[204, 900], [334, 900], [334, 952], [204, 952]], 'occurs-clause picture-clause']
[[[437, 985], [475, 985], [475, 1005], [437, 1005]], 'GET']
[[[203, 997], [410, 997], [410, 1022], [203, 1022]], 'PROPERTY WITH NO']
[[[514, 994], [602, 994], [602, 1018], [514, 1018]], 'IS FINAL']
[[[436, 1016], [474, 1016], [474, 1040], [436, 1040]], 'SET']
[[[203, 1058], [414, 1058], [414, 1118], [203, 1118]], 'SAME AS data-name-2] select-when-clause ]']
[[[334, 1138], [428, 1138], [428, 1194], [334, 1194]], 'LEADING TRAILING']
[[[224, 1154], [308, 1154], [308, 1180], [224, 1180]], 'SIGN IS ]']
[[[464, 1153], [692, 1153], [692, 1178], [464, 1178]], 'SEPARATE CHARACTER ]']
[[[235, 1233], [381, 1233], [381, 1253], [235, 1253]], 'SYNCHRONIZED']
[[[429, 1233], [477, 1233], [477, 1253], [429, 1253]], 'LEFT']
[[[234, 1264], [288, 1264], [288, 1288], [234, 1288]], 'SYNC']
[[[428, 1264], [492, 1264], [492, 1288], [428, 1288]], 'RIGHT']
[[[203, 1304], [386, 1304], [386, 1418], [203, 1418]], 'TYPE type-name-1 ] usage-clause ] validation-clauses ] value-clause ] _']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '363']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [398, 190], [398, 214], [130, 214]], 'where validation-clauses is:']
[[[144, 258], [1064, 258], [1064, 488], [144, 488]], 'class-clause default-clause DESTINATION IS { identifier-1 } [{INVALID WHEN condition-2 } PRESENT WHEN condition-3 VARYING { data-name-3 FROM arithmetic-expression-1 ] BY arithmetic-expression-2 ] } _] [validate-status-clause']
[[[130, 513], [933, 513], [933, 550], [130, 550]], 'where the following meta-language terms are described in the indicated subclauses:']
[[[130, 572], [186, 572], [186, 596], [130, 596]], 'Term']
[[[347, 571], [449, 571], [449, 595], [347, 595]], 'Subclause']
[[[129, 624], [314, 624], [314, 728], [129, 728]], 'class-clause default-clause entry-name-clause occurs-clause']
[[[128, 624], [1112, 624], [1112, 888], [128, 888]], '13.18.11,CLASS clause 13.18.17,DEFAULT clause 13.18.20, Entry-name clause 13.18.38, OCCURS clause   (fixed-table, occurs-depending-table, or   dynamic- capacity-table-format) picture-clause 13.18.40, PICTURE clause select-when clause 13.18.51, SELECT WHEN clause usage-clause 13.18.60,USAGE clause validate-status-clause 13.18.62,VALIDATE-STATUS clause value-clause 13.18.63,VALUE clause data-item or table format)']
[[[130, 913], [326, 913], [326, 942], [130, 942]], 'Format 2 (renames)=']
[[[562, 990], [668, 990], [668, 1052], [562, 1052]], 'THROUGH THRU']
[[[132, 1004], [530, 1004], [530, 1030], [132, 1030]], '66 data-name-1 RENAMES data-name-4']
[[[696, 1008], [822, 1008], [822, 1034], [696, 1034]], 'data-name-5']
[[[129, 1095], [394, 1095], [394, 1124], [129, 1124]], 'Format 3 (condition-name)']
[[[132, 1152], [462, 1152], [462, 1178], [132, 1178]], '88 condition-name-1 value-clause']
[[[130, 1207], [938, 1207], [938, 1235], [130, 1235]], 'where value-clause is described in 13.18.63,VALUE clause (condition-name format):']
[[[129, 1259], [346, 1259], [346, 1287], [129, 1287]], 'Format 4 (validation):']
[[[132, 1318], [492, 1318], [492, 1342], [132, 1342]], '88 condition-name-2 value-clause']
[[[130, 1370], [1016, 1370], [1016, 1400], [130, 1400]], 'where value-clause is described in 13.18.63,VALUE clause (content-validation-entry format)']
[[[130, 1421], [786, 1421], [786, 1449], [130, 1449]], 'NOTE The validation format ofthe data description is an obsolete feature:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '364']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 395 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [412, 190], [412, 218], [164, 218]], '13.163 Syntax rules']
[[[165, 245], [269, 245], [269, 265], [165, 265]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[164, 296], [598, 296], [598, 324], [164, 324]], '1) Level-number may be 77 or 1 through 49']
[[[164, 345], [1026, 345], [1026, 377], [164, 377]], '2) The data-name format of the entry-name clause shall be specified if level-number is 77_']
[[[164, 397], [1146, 397], [1146, 452], [164, 452]], '3) The REDEFINES clause shall not be specified in the same data description entry as the BASED, CONSTANT RECORD, or TYPEDEF clause.']
[[[164, 477], [1146, 477], [1146, 667], [164, 667]], '4) Ifthe entry-name clause is specified,either the data-name format or the filler format may be specified and the entry-name clause shall immediately follow the level-number: If no entry-name clause is specified, it is as though the filler format of the entry-name clause were specified. The REDEFINES clause, if specified, shall immediately follow the entry-name clause, if specified; otherwise, the REDEFINES clause shall immediately follow the level-number: Ifthe TYPEDEF clause is specified,the data-name format of the entry-name clause shall also be specified and the TYPEDEF clause shall immediately follow the entry-name clause: The rema ining clauses may be written in any order:']
[[[164, 687], [1146, 687], [1146, 742], [164, 742]], '5) The EXTERNAL clause shall not be specified in the same data description entry as the REDEFINES or BASED clause:']
[[[200, 767], [1146, 767], [1146, 820], [200, 820]], 'The CONSTANT RECORD and GLOBAL clauses may be specified only in data description entries whose level-number is 1.']
[[[166, 845], [1148, 845], [1148, 926], [166, 926]], '7) The data-name format of the entry-name clause shall be specified for any entry containing the GLOBAL or EXTERNAL clause, or for record descriptions associated with a file description entry that contains the EXTERNAL or GLOBAL clause.']
[[[164, 952], [1146, 952], [1146, 1086], [164, 1086]], '8) The PICTURE clause shall not be specified for the subject ofa RENAMES clause or for an item whose usage is binary-char; binary-short; binary-long, binary-double, float-short; float-long, float-extended, index, message-tag, object reference, pointer, function-pointer, Or program-pointer: For any other entry describing an elementary item, a PICTURE clause shall be specified except as indicated in Syntax rule 9.']
[[[164, 1110], [1146, 1110], [1146, 1192], [164, 1192]], '9) The PICTURE clause may be omitted for an elementary item when an alphanumeric; boolean, or national literal that is not a zero-length literal is specified in the data-item format of the VALUE clause. A PICTURE clause is implied as follows:']
[[[201, 1216], [709, 1216], [709, 1245], [201, 1245]], "a) if the literal is alphanumeric, 'PICTURE X(length)'"]
[[[200, 1268], [654, 1268], [654, 1298], [200, 1298]], "b) if the literal is boolean, 'PICTURE 1(length)'"]
[[[236, 1321], [656, 1321], [656, 1352], [236, 1352]], "if the literal is national, 'PICTURE N(length)'"]
[[[200, 1372], [846, 1372], [846, 1404], [200, 1404]], 'where length is the length ofthe literal as specified in 8.3.3, Literals']
[[[164, 1425], [1142, 1425], [1142, 1457], [164, 1457]], '10) The VALUE clause shallnot be specified for data items ofclass index, message-tag, object; Or pointer_']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1550], [1146, 1550], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '365']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 396 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 187], [1112, 187], [1112, 239], [130, 239]], '11) The PICTURE, JUSTIFIED,and BLANK WHEN ZERO clauses may be specified only for an elementary data item:']
[[[130, 266], [1112, 266], [1112, 322], [130, 322]], '12) The SAME AS clause shall not be specified in the same data description entry with any clauses except CONSTANT RECORD, entry-name, EXTERNAL, GLOBAL; level-number;and OCCURS']
[[[130, 347], [1112, 347], [1112, 457], [130, 457]], '13) The ANY LENGTH, BASED, BLANK WHEN ZERO, DYNAMIC LENGTH, select-when, SYNCHRONIZED and TYPEDEF clauses and validation-clauses shallnot be specified in the same data description entry with the CONSTANT RECORD clause, or in any data description entry subordinate to data description entry with the CONSTANT RECORD clause.']
[[[164, 478], [1112, 478], [1112, 536], [164, 536]], 'Ifthe CONSTANT RECORD clause is specified with the EXTERNAL clause, there shall also be a TYPE clause that specifies a strongly typed definition:']
[[[166, 554], [1108, 554], [1108, 632], [166, 632]], 'NOTE Further requirements and restrictions are specified in 13.18.38, OCCURS clause, Syntax rules 19,23, and 33; 13.18.42, PROPERTY clause, Syntax rule 5; 13.18.44, REDEFINES clause, Syntax rule 13; 13.18.49, SAME AS clause, Syntax rule 10; and 13.18.60, USAGE clause, Syntax rules 4 and 21.']
[[[130, 653], [1112, 653], [1112, 760], [130, 760]], '14) The TYPE clause shall not be specified in the same data description entry with any clauses except BASED, CLASS, CONSTANT RECORD, DEFAULT, DESTINATION, entry-name, EXTERNAL, GLOBAL, INVALID, level-number, OCCURS, PRESENT WHEN; PROPERTY, TYPEDEF, VALIDATE-STATUS VALUE, and VARYING.']
[[[129, 782], [1112, 782], [1112, 843], [129, 843]], '15) The TYPEDEF clause may be specified only in a data description entry whose level-number is 1 and for which the data-name format of the entry-name clause is specified.']
[[[130, 864], [1112, 864], [1112, 944], [130, 944]], '16) The BASED clause may be specified only in data description entries in the linkage section, in the working-storage section, and in the local-storage section. The level number of such data description entries shall be 1 or 77.']
[[[130, 969], [1111, 969], [1111, 1024], [130, 1024]], '17) If the ANY LENGTH clause is specified, the only other clauses permitted are level-number, entry- name, PICTURE, USAGE,and VALUE_']
[[[130, 1050], [1112, 1050], [1112, 1104], [130, 1104]], '18) Ifa DYNAMIC LENGTH clause is specified,the only other clauses permitted are level-number, entry- name, PICTURE, USAGE,and VALUE:']
[[[130, 1127], [1062, 1127], [1062, 1159], [130, 1159]], '19) If the LOCALE phrase of the PICTURE clause is specified,the SIGN clause shall not be specified:']
[[[130, 1180], [1112, 1180], [1112, 1234], [130, 1234]], '20) The PRESENT WHEN clause shall not be specified in a data description entry that has a level number of 1 or 77']
[[[130, 1259], [947, 1259], [947, 1291], [130, 1291]], '21) The PROPERTY clause shall not be specified in the same data description entry as:']
[[[166, 1314], [360, 1314], [360, 1342], [166, 1342]], 'a) a BASED clause,']
[[[166, 1368], [380, 1368], [380, 1394], [166, 1394]], 'b) a TYPEDEF clause:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '366']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [272, 190], [272, 214], [164, 214]], 'FORMAT 2']
[[[161, 239], [658, 239], [658, 270], [161, 270]], '22) The words THROUGH and THRU are equivalent']
[[[164, 296], [348, 296], [348, 320], [164, 320]], 'FORMATS 3 AND 4']
[[[164, 348], [972, 348], [972, 374], [164, 374]], '23) Each condition-name is subordinate to the data-name with which it is associated.']
[[[161, 397], [1146, 397], [1146, 509], [161, 509]], '24) Format 3 or 4 is used for each condition-name Each condition-name requires a separate entry with level-number 88. The condition-name entries for a particular conditional variable shall immediately follow the entry describing the item with which the condition-name is associated. A condition-name may be associated with any data description entry that contains a level-number exceptthe following:']
[[[200, 529], [463, 529], [463, 562], [200, 562]], 'a) Another level 88 entry:']
[[[200, 586], [396, 586], [396, 612], [200, 612]], 'b) A level 66 entry.']
[[[200, 635], [936, 635], [936, 666], [200, 666]], 'c) An alphanumeric group containing items with a usage other than display:']
[[[200, 686], [1004, 686], [1004, 718], [200, 718]], 'd) A group containing items described with a JUSTIFIED or SYNCHRONIZED clause']
[[[200, 741], [820, 741], [820, 772], [200, 772]], 'e) A data item ofthe class index, message-tag, object, or pointer.']
[[[203, 796], [736, 796], [736, 823], [203, 823]], '0) A data item described with the ANY LENGTH clause.']
[[[200, 848], [1146, 848], [1146, 904], [200, 904]], 'g) A type declaration described with the STRONG phrase, or a group item subordinate to such a type declaration.']
[[[200, 928], [474, 928], [474, 956], [200, 956]], 'h) Avariable-length group:']
[[[164, 980], [424, 980], [424, 1006], [164, 1006]], '13.16.4 General rules']
[[[164, 1032], [1146, 1032], [1146, 1086], [164, 1086]], '1) If the subject ofthe entry is a group item that is subordinate to a bit group and a GROUP-USAGE BIT clause is not specified,a GROUP-USAGE BIT clause is implied for the subject of the entry']
[[[164, 1109], [1146, 1109], [1146, 1192], [164, 1192]], '2) Ifthe subject ofthe entry is a group item that is subordinate to a national group and a GROUP-USAGE NATIONAL clause is not specified,a GROUP-USAGE NATIONAL clause is implied for the subject of the entry']
[[[164, 1216], [1146, 1216], [1146, 1268], [164, 1268]], '3) Format 3 contains the name ofthe condition and the value; values,or range of values associated with the condition-name:']
[[[164, 1294], [1148, 1294], [1148, 1457], [164, 1457]], '4) Format 4 may contain the name of a condition and the value, values, or range of values associated with the condition-name; in which case the condition-namehas the same meaningas in Format 3 and may be used in the same way: During the content validation stage of a VALIDATE statement that references the subject of the entry or a superordinate data item; the value or values specified in this format define the value, values, or range of values that make the subject of the entry valid if VALID is specified or invalid if INVALID is specified:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '367']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 188], [483, 188], [483, 221], [130, 221]], '13.17 Screen description entry']
[[[130, 242], [326, 242], [326, 268], [130, 268]], '13.17.1 General']
[[[130, 292], [1108, 292], [1108, 402], [130, 402]], 'A screen description entry specifies attributes, behavior; size,and location ofa screen item s0 thatit can be referenced by an ACCEPT screen or a DISPLAY screen statement: The screen description entry allows data items to be associated with the screen item so that the contents f the data item are displayed within the screen item or the value keyed into a screen item by the operator is placed in the data item']
[[[130, 426], [422, 426], [422, 452], [130, 452]], '13.17.2 General formats']
[[[130, 480], [300, 480], [300, 508], [130, 508]], 'Format 1 (group)']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [458, 190], [458, 214], [130, 214]], 'where screen-attribute-clauses is:']
[[[173, 275], [243, 275], [243, 354], [173, 354]], 'BELL ] BLINK']
[[[172, 378], [290, 378], [290, 440], [172, 440]], 'HIGHLIGHT LOWLIGHT']
[[[174, 464], [340, 464], [340, 494], [174, 494]], 'REVERSE-VIDEQ']
[[[174, 516], [298, 516], [298, 540], [174, 540]], 'UNDERLINE']
[[[446, 580], [560, 580], [560, 640], [446, 640]], 'identifier-3 integer-3']
[[[172, 592], [418, 592], [418, 616], [172, 616]], 'FOREGROUND-COLOR IS']
[[[446, 678], [562, 678], [562, 736], [446, 736]], 'identifier-4 integer-4']
[[[172, 688], [418, 688], [418, 712], [172, 712]], 'BACKGROUND-COLOR IS']
[[[130, 780], [728, 780], [728, 808], [130, 808]], 'where picture-clause is described in 13.18.40, PICTURE clause']
[[[130, 834], [482, 834], [482, 858], [130, 858]], 'where source-destination-clauses is:']
[[[184, 942], [392, 942], [392, 999], [184, 999]], 'identifier-5 FROM literal-1']
[[[150, 1014], [332, 1014], [332, 1120], [150, 1120]], 'TQ identifier-6 USING identifier-7 VALUE IS literal-2']
[[[130, 1160], [378, 1160], [378, 1190], [130, 1190]], '13.17.33 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 1213], [275, 1213], [275, 1238], [130, 1238]], 'ALL FORMATS']
[[[130, 1263], [1112, 1263], [1112, 1372], [130, 1372]], '1) The entry-name clause, if specified, shall immediately follow level-number: Ifthe entry-name clause is specified, only the screen-name format or filler format may be specified. If the entry-name clause is not specified, it is as if the FILLER format ofthe entry-name clause were specified: The remaining clauses may be specified in any order:']
[[[130, 1395], [1112, 1395], [1112, 1450], [130, 1450]], '2) Ifthe GLOBAL clause is specified,the screen-name format ofthe entry-name clause shall be specified and level-number shall be 1']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '370']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 401 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [270, 190], [270, 214], [164, 214]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[164, 241], [694, 241], [694, 270], [164, 270]], '3) Level-number shall be a number from 1 through 48.']
[[[164, 293], [706, 293], [706, 325], [164, 325]], '4) The subject of the entry shall be a group screen item:']
[[[164, 348], [272, 348], [272, 372], [164, 372]], 'FORMAT 2']
[[[164, 399], [694, 399], [694, 428], [164, 428]], '5) Level-number shall be a number from 1 through 49.']
[[[200, 451], [770, 451], [770, 483], [200, 483]], 'The subject of the entry shall be an elementary screen item:']
[[[166, 503], [1148, 503], [1148, 560], [166, 560]], '7) For an elementary screen item; the associated screen description entry shall include at least one of the following:']
[[[264, 583], [950, 583], [950, 742], [264, 742]], 'PICTURE clause and a FROM, TO, or USING clause; a PICTURE clause and a VALUE clause with a numeric literal; a VALUE clause specifying an alphanumeric; boolean, or national literal; a BLANK clause; an ERASE clause; a BELL clause:']
[[[164, 766], [900, 766], [900, 799], [164, 799]], '8) If the FULL clause is specified, the JUSTIFIED clause shall not be specified.']
[[[200, 819], [1097, 819], [1097, 851], [200, 851]], 'If the LOCALE phrase of the PICTURE clause is specified, the SIGN clause shall not be specified.']
[[[164, 876], [1146, 876], [1146, 930], [164, 930]], '10) The PICTURE clause may be omitted when an alphanumeric; boolean, or national literal that is not a zero-length literal is specified in the VALUE clause.A PICTURE clause is implied as follows:']
[[[201, 954], [716, 954], [716, 982], [201, 982]], "a) if the literal is alphanumeric, 'PICTURE X(length)' ,"]
[[[200, 1004], [686, 1004], [686, 1037], [200, 1037]], "b) if the literal is boolean, 'PICTURE 1(length)' , or"]
[[[200, 1060], [662, 1060], [662, 1088], [200, 1088]], "c) if the literal is national, 'PICTURE N(length)'."]
[[[202, 1109], [846, 1109], [846, 1141], [202, 1141]], 'where length is the length of the literal as specified in 8.3.3, Literals']
[[[164, 1164], [424, 1164], [424, 1190], [164, 1190]], '13.17.4 General rules']
[[[166, 1218], [308, 1218], [308, 1242], [166, 1242]], 'ALL FORMATS']
[[[164, 1267], [1146, 1267], [1146, 1348], [164, 1348]], '1) If the same clause, other than an OCCURS clause, is specified at more than one level in the hierarchy of a screen item; the clause that appears at the lowest level of the hierarchy is the one that takes effect;']
[[[164, 1374], [1146, 1374], [1146, 1430], [164, 1430]], '2) If the HIGHLIGHT and LOWLIGHT clauses are both specified in the hierarchy of a screen item; the clause that appears at the lowest level of the hierarchy is the one that takes effect:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '371']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 402 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [442, 190], [442, 216], [130, 216]], '13.18 Data division clauses']
[[[130, 242], [416, 242], [416, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.1 ALIGNED clause']
[[[130, 296], [348, 296], [348, 320], [130, 320]], '13.18.1.1 General']
[[[128, 345], [1108, 345], [1108, 402], [128, 402]], 'The ALIGNED clause specifies thata bit group item or an elementary bit data item is to be aligned at the first bit of the first available byte']
[[[130, 426], [430, 426], [430, 452], [130, 452]], '13.18.1.2 General format']
[[[132, 484], [226, 484], [226, 508], [132, 508]], 'ALIGNED']
[[[130, 546], [386, 546], [386, 574], [130, 574]], '13.18.1.3 Syntax rule']
[[[130, 595], [1064, 595], [1064, 627], [130, 627]], '1) The ALIGNED clause may be specified only for a bit group item or an elementary bit data item:']
[[[130, 650], [410, 650], [410, 676], [130, 676]], '13.18.1.4 General rules']
[[[130, 702], [1112, 702], [1112, 784], [130, 784]], '1) An ALIGNED clause causes the subject of the entry to be aligned on the first bit of the first available byte boundary: Implicit filler bits may be generated to complete the assignment of bits as described in 8.5.1.6.3, Alignment of data items of usage bit:']
[[[130, 805], [1112, 805], [1112, 860], [130, 860]], '2) An ALIGNED clause specified for a multiple-occurrence data item applies to each occurrence ofthat item']
[[[130, 886], [1108, 886], [1108, 942], [130, 942]], '3) When an ALIGNED clause is not specified, bit data items are aligned in accordance with 8.5.1.6.3_ Alignment of data items of usage bit:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '372']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 403 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [490, 190], [490, 216], [164, 216]], '13.18.2 ANY LENGTH clause']
[[[164, 242], [381, 242], [381, 268], [164, 268]], '13.18.2.1 General']
[[[164, 291], [1128, 291], [1128, 351], [164, 351]], 'The ANY LENGTH clause specifies that the length ofa linkage section item may vary at runtime and is determined by the length ofthe argument in the calling program, method, or function:']
[[[164, 374], [466, 374], [466, 400], [164, 400]], '13.18.2.2 General format']
[[[180, 442], [314, 442], [314, 466], [180, 466]], 'ANY LENGTH']
[[[164, 506], [432, 506], [432, 536], [164, 536]], '13.18.2.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 556], [1148, 556], [1148, 615], [164, 615]], "1) A PICTURE clause shall be specified forthe subject ofthe entry: The character-string specified in that PICTURE clause shall be one instance ofthe picture symbol 'N= 'X',or '1'."]
[[[164, 635], [1146, 635], [1146, 694], [164, 694]], '2) The ANY LENGTH clause may be specified only in an elementary level 1 entry in the linkage section ofa function, ofa contained program, or ofa method that is not a property method.']
[[[199, 712], [1148, 712], [1148, 817], [199, 817]], 'NOTE The ANY LENGTH clause may not be specified in an outermost program: This is because an outermost program can be called with or without a program-prototype format CALL statement For calls without program-prototype, this Working Draft International Standard does not require an implementation to determine whether an argument corresponds to a formal parameter described with ANY LENGTH']
[[[164, 835], [1146, 835], [1146, 894], [164, 894]], '3) If the source element containing the ANY LENGTH clause is a contained program or is a method, the subject of the entry shall be referenced in its procedure division header as either:']
[[[201, 918], [766, 918], [766, 946], [201, 946]], 'a) a formal parameter with the BY REFERENCE phrase, or']
[[[200, 968], [422, 968], [422, 1000], [200, 1000]], 'b) the returning item:']
[[[164, 1020], [1146, 1020], [1146, 1104], [164, 1104]], '4) Ifthe source element containing the ANY LENGTH clause is a function, the subject of the entry shall be referenced in its procedure division header as a formal parameter with the BY REFERENCE phrase:']
[[[164, 1130], [434, 1130], [434, 1154], [164, 1154]], '13.18.2.4 General rule']
[[[164, 1179], [878, 1179], [878, 1211], [164, 1211]], '1) The ANY LENGTH clause specifies that the subject ofthe entry shall be:']
[[[201, 1231], [1146, 1231], [1146, 1288], [201, 1288]], 'a) zero-length item when the corresponding argument or returning item ofthe activating runtime element is a zero-length item, or']
[[[200, 1310], [1146, 1310], [1146, 1394], [200, 1394]], 'b) treated as though there were n repetitions of the picture symbol in the character-string in its PICTURE clause, where n is the length of the corresponding argument or returning item of the activating runtime element:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '373']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 404 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [374, 190], [374, 216], [130, 216]], '13.18.3 AUTO clause']
[[[130, 242], [348, 242], [348, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.3.1 General']
[[[128, 294], [1100, 294], [1100, 350], [128, 350]], 'The AUTO clause causes the cursor to be automatically moved to the next field declared for the screen item during execution ofan ACCEPT screen statement;']
[[[130, 374], [430, 374], [430, 400], [130, 400]], '13.18.3.2 General format']
[[[132, 432], [192, 432], [192, 456], [132, 456]], 'AUTO']
[[[129, 492], [410, 492], [410, 518], [129, 518]], '13.18.3.3 General rules']
[[[130, 540], [1035, 540], [1035, 576], [130, 576]], '1) An AUTO clause specified at the group level applies to each input screen item in that group:']
[[[130, 597], [762, 597], [762, 627], [130, 627]], '2) The AUTO clause is ignored for a field that is not an input field.']
[[[130, 648], [1112, 648], [1112, 704], [130, 704]], '3) The AUTO clause takes effect during the execution ofan ACCEPT screen statement that references the screen item for which the AUTO clause is specified.']
[[[130, 728], [1112, 728], [1112, 805], [130, 805]], '4) The AUTO clause causes the cursorto beautomatically moved to the next input field declared for the screen item when the last character of the input field whose definition contains this clause has data entered into it:']
[[[130, 832], [1113, 832], [1113, 938], [130, 938]], '5) When the AUTO clause is specified for an input field that has no logical next field during input; then when that field is available for input during an ACCEPT screen statement and data is entered into the last character of the screen item, successful completion with normal termination of the ACCEPT statement results']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '374']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 405 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [590, 190], [590, 216], [164, 216]], '13.18.4 BACKGROUND-COLOR clause']
[[[164, 244], [382, 244], [382, 268], [164, 268]], '13.18.4.1 General']
[[[164, 294], [970, 294], [970, 324], [164, 324]], 'The BACKGROUND-COLOR clause specifies the background color for the screen item_']
[[[164, 348], [466, 348], [466, 374], [164, 374]], '13.18.4.2 General format']
[[[165, 420], [548, 420], [548, 478], [165, 478]], 'BACKGROUND-COLOR IS identifier-1 integer-1']
[[[164, 512], [432, 512], [432, 542], [164, 542]], '13.18.4.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 561], [1146, 561], [1146, 621], [164, 621]], '1) Identifier-1 shall be described in the file, working-storage, local-storage, or linkage section as an unsigned integer data item:']
[[[164, 643], [668, 643], [668, 673], [164, 673]], '2)   Integer-1 shall have a value in the range of 0 to 7_']
[[[164, 696], [446, 696], [446, 722], [164, 722]], '13.18.4.4 General rules']
[[[164, 746], [1146, 746], [1146, 826], [164, 826]], '1) When an ACCEPT screen statement or a DISPLAY screen statement referencing the associated screen item is executed, the content of the data item referenced by identifier-1 shall be in the range of 0 to 7_']
[[[164, 852], [1146, 852], [1146, 938], [164, 938]], "2) Integer-1 or the content ofthe data item referenced by identifier-1 specifies the color number ofthe background color to be used for displaying the screen item. The color associated with the color number is 'specified in 9.2.7, Color number:"]
[[[164, 957], [1146, 957], [1146, 1016], [164, 1016]], '3) A BACKGROUND-COLOR clause specified at the group level applies to each elementary screen item in that group:']
[[[164, 1038], [1146, 1038], [1146, 1094], [164, 1094]], '4) When a BACKGROUND-COLOR clause is not specified or the value is not in the range 0 to 7, the background color is implementor-defined.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '375']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 406 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [386, 190], [386, 216], [130, 216]], '13.18.5 BASED clause']
[[[130, 242], [348, 242], [348, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.5.1 General']
[[[130, 294], [1108, 294], [1108, 351], [130, 351]], 'The BASED clause defines a based entry.A based entry isa template that; when associated with an actual data item or allocated storage, describes a based data item:']
[[[130, 374], [430, 374], [430, 400], [130, 400]], '13.18.5.2 General format']
[[[132, 432], [202, 432], [202, 456], [132, 456]], 'BASED']
[[[130, 492], [398, 492], [398, 522], [130, 522]], '13.18.5.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 544], [650, 544], [650, 572], [130, 572]], '1) The subject of the entry shall not be of class object:']
[[[130, 594], [1094, 594], [1094, 627], [130, 627]], '2) The subject of the entry shall not be a dynamic-length elementary item or a variable-length group_']
[[[130, 650], [410, 650], [410, 676], [130, 676]], '13.18.5.4 General rules']
[[[130, 699], [1112, 699], [1112, 758], [130, 758]], '1) The BASED clause specifies that the subject of the entry is a based entry defining a template that is dynamically associated with storage through an implicit data-address pointer:']
[[[130, 778], [1112, 778], [1112, 862], [130, 862]], '2) The implicit data-address pointer has an initial value of NULL The rules for setting the implicit address-pointer are described in 14.9.39,SET statement; General rules 17,18,and 19. The life cycle of this implicit data-address pointer is described in 8.6.5, Based entries and based data items_']
[[[130, 884], [1112, 884], [1112, 946], [130, 946]], '3) If the subject of the entry or any item subordinate to it is referenced directly or indirectly while its address is NULL, the EC-DATA-PTR-NULL exception condition is set to exist.']
[[[130, 966], [1114, 966], [1114, 1022], [130, 1022]], '4) If the subject of the entry is referenced while its address is not NULL and not a valid address of storage, the EC-BOUND-PTR exception condition is set to exist:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '376']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 407 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [402, 190], [402, 216], [164, 216]], '13.18.6 BELL clause']
[[[164, 242], [382, 242], [382, 268], [164, 268]], '13.18.6.1 General']
[[[164, 296], [710, 296], [710, 320], [164, 320]], 'The BELL clause causes the terminal audio tone t0 sound_']
[[[164, 348], [466, 348], [466, 374], [164, 374]], '13.18.6.2 General format']
[[[169, 407], [219, 407], [219, 427], [169, 427]], 'BELL']
[[[164, 466], [446, 466], [446, 492], [164, 492]], '13.18.6.3 General rules']
[[[164, 513], [1147, 513], [1147, 622], [164, 622]], '1) The use ofthis clause results in the audio tone sounding when the screen item in which itis specified is processed during the execution ofa DISPLAY screen statement The audio tone sounds once at the start of the execution of the DISPLAY screen statement regardless of how many entries specify the clause.']
[[[164, 647], [1104, 647], [1104, 680], [164, 680]], '2) A BELL clause specified at the group level applies to each elementary screen item in that group']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '377']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 408 ---

[[[128, 78], [388, 78], [388, 110], [128, 110]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [388, 190], [388, 216], [130, 216]], '13.18.7 BLANK clause']
[[[130, 242], [348, 242], [348, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.7.1 General']
[[[128, 292], [1078, 292], [1078, 346], [128, 346]], 'The BLANK clause clears a screen line or clears the whole screen during the execution ofa DISPLAY screen statement before data is transferred to the screen item.']
[[[130, 374], [430, 374], [430, 400], [130, 400]], '13.18.7.2 General format']
[[[232, 442], [316, 442], [316, 502], [232, 502]], 'LINE SCREEN']
[[[132, 458], [206, 458], [206, 482], [132, 482]], 'BLANK']
[[[130, 542], [412, 542], [412, 568], [130, 568]], '13.18.7.3 General rules']
[[[130, 593], [1112, 593], [1112, 672], [130, 672]], '1) When the BLANK LINE clause is specified, the entire line specified for the screen item that is the subject of the entry, columns 1 through the end of the line, is cleared during the execution of a DISPLAY screen statement before data is transferred to the screen item']
[[[130, 698], [1112, 698], [1112, 808], [130, 808]], '2) When the BLANK SCREEN clause is specified,the screen is cleared and the cursor is placed at line 1, column during the execution of a DISPLAY screen statement before data is transferred to the screen item. Upon clearing the screen, the background color for the entire screen is set to the value applicable at the time:']
[[[130, 832], [1112, 832], [1112, 915], [130, 915]], '3) The BLANK SCREEN clause in combination with the BACKGROUND-COLOR clause for the same screen item, or for a screen item to which itis subordinate, establishes the default background color to be used until the same combination is encountered specifying another background color:']
[[[130, 938], [1112, 938], [1112, 1020], [130, 1020]], '4) The BLANK SCREEN clause in combination with the FOREGROUND-COLOR clause for the same screen item, or for a screen item to which it is subordinate, establishes the default foreground color to be used until the same combination is encountered specifying another foreground color;']
[[[130, 1044], [1072, 1044], [1072, 1072], [130, 1072]], '5) The BLANK clause is ignored during all phases of the execution of an ACCEPT screen statement']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '378']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 409 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [566, 190], [566, 216], [164, 216]], '13.18.8 BLANK WHEN ZERO clause']
[[[164, 244], [382, 244], [382, 268], [164, 268]], '13.18.8.1 General']
[[[164, 291], [1146, 291], [1146, 325], [164, 325]], 'The BLANK WHEN ZERO clause causes the blanking ofan item when a value of zero is being stored in it']
[[[164, 348], [466, 348], [466, 374], [164, 374]], '13.18.8.2 General format']
[[[168, 404], [370, 404], [370, 428], [168, 428]], 'BLANK WHEN ZERO']
[[[164, 466], [432, 466], [432, 494], [164, 494]], '13.18.8.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 516], [1146, 516], [1146, 574], [164, 574]], "1) The BLANK WHEN ZERO clause may be specified only for an elementary item described by its picture character-string as category numeric-edited or as numeric without the picture symbol 'S'."]
[[[164, 595], [1144, 595], [1144, 627], [164, 627]], '2) The subject of the entry shall be implicitly or explicitly described as usage display or usage national.']
[[[164, 650], [446, 650], [446, 676], [164, 676]], '13.18.8.4 General rules']
[[[164, 700], [1148, 700], [1148, 759], [164, 759]], '1) When the BLANK WHEN ZERO clause is specified for a data item; the content of the data item is set to all spaces when the item is a receiving operand and the value being stored is zero.']
[[[164, 779], [1146, 779], [1146, 836], [164, 836]], '2) Ifthe subject ofthe entry is described by its picture character-string as category numeric, the BLANK WHEN ZERO clause defines the item as numeric-edited_']
[[[164, 857], [1146, 857], [1146, 938], [164, 938]], '3) If the subject of the entry is a sending data item, the object ofan operation is a numeric Or numeric- edited data item, and the content ofthe sending data item is all spaces, the value of the sending data item is considered to be zero.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '379']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 410 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [382, 190], [382, 216], [130, 216]], '13.18.9 BLINK clause']
[[[130, 242], [348, 242], [348, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.9.1 General']
[[[130, 292], [1078, 292], [1078, 325], [130, 325]], 'The BLINK clause specifies that each character of the field blinks when it is displayed on the screen:']
[[[130, 348], [430, 348], [430, 374], [130, 374]], '13.18.9.2 General format']
[[[132, 404], [198, 404], [198, 428], [132, 428]], 'BLINK']
[[[130, 466], [410, 466], [410, 492], [130, 492]], '13.18.9.3 General rules']
[[[130, 514], [1086, 514], [1086, 549], [130, 549]], '1) A BLINK clause specified at the group level applies to each elementary screen item in that group:']
[[[130, 568], [1110, 568], [1110, 622], [130, 622]], '2) When the BLINK clause is specified, the characters that constitute the screen item will blink when the screen item is referenced in an ACCEPT screen statement or a DISPLAY screen statement']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '380']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 411 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 189], [558, 189], [558, 216], [164, 216]], '13.18.10 BLOCK CONTAINS clause']
[[[164, 242], [396, 242], [396, 268], [164, 268]], '13.18.10.1 General']
[[[164, 294], [806, 294], [806, 324], [164, 324]], 'The BLOCK CONTAINS clause specifies the size ofa physical record.']
[[[164, 348], [478, 348], [478, 374], [164, 374]], '13.18.10.2 General format']
[[[624, 416], [764, 416], [764, 470], [624, 470]], 'CHARACTERS RECORDS']
[[[166, 430], [594, 430], [594, 458], [166, 458]], 'BLOCK CONTAINS integer-1 TO integer-2']
[[[164, 512], [434, 512], [434, 542], [164, 542]], '13.18.10.3 Syntax rule']
[[[164, 562], [818, 562], [818, 594], [164, 594]], '1) If integer-1 is specified, integer-2 shall be greater than integer-1.']
[[[164, 618], [460, 618], [460, 644], [164, 644]], '13.18.10.4 General rules']
[[[164, 668], [980, 668], [980, 699], [164, 699]], '1) This clause is required except when one or more of the following conditions exist:']
