[[[201, 719], [890, 719], [890, 752], [201, 752]], 'a) A physical record contains one and only one complete logical record:']
[[[200, 776], [1024, 776], [1024, 804], [200, 804]], 'b) The hardware device assigned to the file has one and only one physical record size.']
[[[236, 828], [760, 828], [760, 880], [236, 880]], 'The number of records or  characters contained in environment:']
[[[788, 825], [1145, 825], [1145, 857], [788, 857]], 'block is specified in the operating']
[[[164, 904], [1147, 904], [1147, 960], [164, 960]], '2) The size ofa physical record may be stated in terms of records unless one or more of the following situations exists, in which case the RECORDS phrase shall not be used:']
[[[201, 984], [928, 984], [928, 1015], [201, 1015]], 'a) In mass storage files, logical records may extend across physical records.']
[[[200, 1036], [978, 1036], [978, 1068], [200, 1068]], 'b) The physical record contains padding (area not contained in a logical record):']
[[[200, 1090], [1146, 1090], [1146, 1146], [200, 1146]], 'c) Logical records are grouped in such a manner that an inaccurate physical record size would be implied:']
[[[164, 1168], [1146, 1168], [1146, 1253], [164, 1253]], '3) Ifthe CHARACTERS phrase is specified, the physical record size is specified in terms of the number ofbytes required to store the physical record, regardless ofthe types of characters used to represent the items within the physical record.']
[[[164, 1273], [1146, 1273], [1146, 1355], [164, 1355]], '4) Ifinteger-1 is not specified,integer-2 represents the exact size ofthe physical record Ifinteger-1and integer-2 are both specified, they refer to the minimum and maximum size of the physical record, respectively.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '381']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 412 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 190], [392, 190], [392, 216], [130, 216]], '13.18.11 CLASS clause']
[[[130, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.11.1 General']
[[[130, 292], [1106, 292], [1106, 351], [130, 351]], 'The CLASS clause specifies a range of values for each character ofa data item; to be checked during the content validation stage of the execution of a VALIDATE statement']
[[[129, 373], [446, 373], [446, 400], [129, 400]], '13.18.11.2 General format']
[[[248, 452], [460, 452], [460, 619], [248, 619]], 'NUMERIC ALPHABETIC ALPHABETIC-LOWER ALPHABETIC-UPPER BOOLEAN']
[[[132, 552], [222, 552], [222, 576], [132, 576]], 'CLASS IS']
[[[246, 632], [414, 632], [414, 686], [246, 686]], 'alphabet-name-1 class-name-1']
[[[130, 730], [400, 730], [400, 758], [130, 758]], '13.18.113 Syntax rule']
[[[130, 779], [908, 779], [908, 811], [130, 811]], '1) The subject of the entry shall be of class alphabetic, alphanumeric, 0r national.']
[[[130, 836], [424, 836], [424, 860], [130, 860]], '13.18.11.4 General rules']
[[[130, 884], [1112, 884], [1112, 966], [130, 966]], '1) The CLASS clause takes effect during the content validation stage of the execution of a VALIDATE statement: The CLASS clause is ignored during the execution of any statement other than VALIDATE statement:']
[[[130, 992], [1112, 992], [1112, 1074], [130, 1074]], "2) If the subject of the entry is an elementary item, the CLASS clause takes effect only if the item's associated internal indicator is still set to its initial valid value, indicating that no errors occurred during the format validation stage_"]
[[[130, 1097], [1112, 1097], [1112, 1176], [130, 1176]], "3) The operand of the CLASS clause is used to check each character of the value of the data item as described in 8.8.4.4, Simple class condition. If the class condition is false, the data item's internal indicator is set to invalid on content:"]
[[[130, 1201], [1112, 1201], [1112, 1284], [130, 1284]], '4) Ifthe subject of the entry is an alphanumeric group item or national group item, General rules 2 and 3 are applied to each of its subordinate elementary items, except for any data items that are not processed as a result ofa PRESENT WHEN clause or an OCCURS clause with a DEPENDING phrase:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '382']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 413 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[163, 189], [422, 189], [422, 218], [163, 218]], '13.18.12 CODE clause']
[[[164, 242], [395, 242], [395, 268], [164, 268]], '13.18.12.1 General']
[[[164, 294], [1142, 294], [1142, 350], [164, 350]], 'The CODE clause specifies one or more characters used to separate multiple reports written to the same file or for specifying correct functioning ofthe printer device.']
[[[163, 373], [478, 373], [478, 400], [163, 400]], '13.18.12.2 General format']
[[[166, 446], [388, 446], [388, 500], [166, 500]], 'literal-1 CODE IS identifier-1']
[[[164, 538], [452, 538], [452, 568], [164, 568]], '13.18.12.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 591], [594, 591], [594, 618], [164, 618]], '1) Literal-1 shall be an alphanumeric literal:']
[[[164, 644], [1146, 644], [1146, 698], [164, 698]], '2) Identifier-1 shall reference an alphanumeric data item that shall not be an occurs-depending-on group item, a variable-length group, or a dynamic-length elementary item:']
[[[164, 720], [1146, 720], [1146, 776], [164, 776]], '3) If the CODE clause is specified for any report, it shall be specified for each report associated with the same report file:']
[[[164, 802], [460, 802], [460, 828], [164, 828]], '13.18.12.4 General rules']
[[[164, 852], [1149, 852], [1149, 910], [164, 910]], '1) When the CODE clause is specified, literal-1 or identifier-1 is automatically placed in the first characters of each logical record written to the report file for this report']
[[[164, 934], [1146, 934], [1146, 989], [164, 989]], '2) The characters occupied by literal-1 or identifier-1 are not included in the descriptions of the lines in the report, butare included in the logical record size.']
[[[164, 1010], [1146, 1010], [1146, 1094], [164, 1094]], '3) If identifier-1 is specified, it is evaluated at the start of the processing for each body group, either during page advance processing,as detailed in the GENERATE statement, or whenever page advance processing is not performed: The resultant value is used until the next evaluation:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '383']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 414 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 189], [436, 189], [436, 216], [129, 216]], '13.18.13 CODE-SET clause']
[[[130, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.13.1 General']
[[[130, 294], [1086, 294], [1086, 346], [130, 346]], 'The CODE-SET clause specifies the character code convention used to represent data on the external media.']
[[[129, 373], [444, 373], [444, 400], [129, 400]], '13.18.13.2 General format']
[[[132, 456], [700, 456], [700, 558], [132, 558]], 'IS alphabet-name-1 [ alphabet-name-2 ] CODE-SET FOR ALPHANUMERIC IS alphabet-name-1 FOR NATIONAL IS alphabet-name-2']
[[[130, 616], [412, 616], [412, 644], [130, 644]], '13.18.13.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 668], [1072, 668], [1072, 694], [130, 694]], '1) Alphabet-name-1 shall reference an alphabet that defines an alphanumeric coded character set_']
[[[130, 716], [1006, 716], [1006, 749], [130, 749]], '2)   Alphabet-name-2 shall reference an alphabet that defines a national coded character set_']
[[[130, 772], [1112, 772], [1112, 829], [130, 829]], '3) If there are record description entries associated with the file and no SELECT WHEN clauses are specified, either alphabet-name-1 or alphabet-name-2 may be specified, but not both; and:']
[[[167, 849], [1112, 849], [1112, 930], [167, 930]], 'a) if alphabet-name-1 is specified, all elementary data items of all record description entries associated with the file shall be described as usage display, and any signed numeric data items shall be described with the SIGN IS SEPARATE clause_']
[[[166, 956], [1112, 956], [1112, 1034], [166, 1034]], 'b) if alphabet-name-2 is specified, all elementary data items of all record description entries associated with the file shall be described as usage national, and any signed numeric data items shall be described with the SIGN IS SEPARATE clause']
[[[130, 1064], [424, 1064], [424, 1088], [130, 1088]], '13.18.13.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1112], [1112, 1112], [1112, 1197], [130, 1197]], '1) The CODE-SET clause identifies alphabets to be used for converting data from a coded character set on the storage medium to the native character set during input operations, and from the native character set to the coded character set on the storage medium during output operations.']
[[[130, 1218], [1112, 1218], [1112, 1329], [130, 1329]], '2) Upon successful processing ofan OPEN statement for the file referenced in this file description entry, the coded character set used to represent alphanumeric data on the storage medium is the one referenced by alphabet-name-1; the coded character set used to represent national data on the storage medium is the one referenced by alphabet-name-2_']
[[[130, 1349], [1112, 1349], [1112, 1406], [130, 1406]], '3) If the record description entries associated with the file do not contain a SELECT WHEN clause, the specified alphabet is used for code-set conversion ofall data items in each record:']
[[[130, 1432], [1110, 1432], [1110, 1487], [130, 1487]], '4) If record description entries associated with the file contain a SELECT WHEN clause, a record description entry is selected by evaluation of those SELECT WHEN clauses. If there are no record']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '384']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 415 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[200, 190], [1146, 190], [1146, 272], [200, 272]], 'description entries associated with the file, the record description used for conversion is the description of the identifier or literal specified in the FROM phrase of a WRITE or REWRITE statement specifying the FILE phrase_']
[[[198, 294], [1146, 294], [1146, 377], [198, 377]], 'Alphabet-name-1 is used for code-set conversion of each data item described with usage display in the selected record description entry. Alphabet-name-2 is used for code-set conversion of each data item described with usage national in the selected record description entry.']
[[[200, 395], [1120, 395], [1120, 427], [200, 427]], 'NOTE There is no conversion of data items described with other usages unless a FORMAT clause applies:']
[[[164, 446], [1146, 446], [1146, 478], [164, 478]], '5) If a FORMAT clause is also specified for the file; the logical order of processing for each data item is:']
[[[235, 498], [888, 498], [888, 530], [235, 530]], 'formatting first; then code-set conversion during output operations;']
[[[235, 549], [875, 549], [875, 585], [235, 585]], 'code-set conversion first; then formatting during input operations.']
[[[200, 608], [542, 608], [542, 632], [200, 632]], 'For each data item to be converted:']
[[[200, 659], [1146, 659], [1146, 715], [200, 715]], 'a) On input; each coded character from the storage medium is replaced with its associated native coded character as defined in the alphabet being used:']
[[[200, 737], [1146, 737], [1146, 795], [200, 795]], 'b) On output; each native coded character in the record is replaced for the storage medium with its associated coded character as defined in the alphabet being used:']
[[[164, 816], [1146, 816], [1146, 868], [164, 868]], '7) If the CODE-SET clause is not specified, the native character set is assumed for data on the external media_']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '385']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 416 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 189], [422, 189], [422, 216], [129, 216]], '13.18.14 COLUMN clause']
[[[130, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.14.1 General']
[[[128, 294], [1108, 294], [1108, 351], [128, 351]], 'The COLUMN clause identifies a printable item, or a set of printable items,and specifies their horizontal location in a report line, or specifies the horizontal screen coordinate for a screen item:']
[[[130, 374], [456, 374], [456, 400], [130, 400]], '13.18.14.2 General formats']
[[[130, 428], [380, 428], [380, 454], [130, 454]], 'Format 1 (report-writer):']
[[[168, 506], [366, 506], [366, 710], [168, 710]], 'COLUMN NUMBER COLUMN NUMBERS COLUMNS COL NUMBER COL NUMBERS COLS']
[[[418, 564], [504, 564], [504, 652], [418, 652]], 'LEFT CENTER RIGHT']
[[[647, 557], [846, 557], [846, 642], [647, 642]], 'integer-1 PLUS integer-2']
[[[552, 584], [600, 584], [600, 638], [552, 638]], 'IS ARE']
[[[129, 757], [364, 757], [364, 786], [129, 786]], 'Format 2 (screen-item):']
[[[432, 836], [488, 836], [488, 860], [432, 860]], 'PLUS']
[[[168, 866], [260, 866], [260, 928], [168, 928]], 'COLUMN COL']
[[[552, 872], [666, 872], [666, 930], [552, 930]], 'identifier-1 integer-3']
[[[288, 882], [406, 882], [406, 908], [288, 908]], 'NUMBER IS']
[[[434, 902], [504, 902], [504, 926], [434, 926]], 'MINUS']
[[[130, 1000], [412, 1000], [412, 1028], [130, 1028]], '13.18.14.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 1052], [276, 1052], [276, 1076], [130, 1076]], 'ALL FORMATS']
[[[130, 1104], [652, 1104], [652, 1132], [130, 1132]], '1) COLUMN, COL, COLUMNS,and COLS are synonyms_']
[[[130, 1158], [418, 1158], [418, 1186], [130, 1186]], '2) PLUS and +are synonyms']
[[[131, 1213], [235, 1213], [235, 1233], [131, 1233]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[130, 1260], [1112, 1260], [1112, 1318], [130, 1318]], '3) The COLUMN clause may be specified only in an elementary entry: The entry shall also contain a LINE clause or shall be subordinate to an entry containing a LINE clause']
[[[130, 1339], [980, 1339], [980, 1371], [130, 1371]], '4) The keyword ARE may be specified only if COLUMNS, COLS, or NUMBERS is specified.']
[[[130, 1392], [952, 1392], [952, 1422], [130, 1422]], '5) The keyword IS shall not be specified if COLUMNS, COLS, or NUMBERS is specified.']
[[[130, 1447], [1006, 1447], [1006, 1477], [130, 1477]], '6) Neither integer-1 nor integer-2 shall exceed the page width (See 13.18.39,PAGE clause)']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '386']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 417 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 189], [1146, 189], [1146, 244], [164, 244]], '7) Within a given report line, any two or more absolute items defined using column numbers that are not in increasing numerical order shall be subject to a different PRESENT WHEN clause:']
[[[164, 266], [1002, 266], [1002, 298], [164, 298]], '8) The set of printable items within a given report line is subject to the following rules:']
[[[200, 319], [1146, 319], [1146, 372], [200, 372]], 'a) If any two or more items overlap each other, they shall each be subject to a different PRESENT WHEN clause.']
[[[200, 398], [1044, 398], [1044, 430], [200, 430]], 'b) The rightmost column positions ofall absolute items shall not exceed the page width_']
[[[235, 451], [1146, 451], [1146, 535], [235, 535]], 'Ifthe report line ends in a set of relative printable items or consists only of such, they shall not cause the page width to be exceeded unless each ofthem is subject to a different PRESENT WHEN clause, in which case this rule applies only to the largest of them_']
[[[164, 556], [1146, 556], [1146, 615], [164, 615]], '9) If LEFT, CENTER, or RIGHT is specified, all the operands shall be absolute If any of the operands is absolute and neither LEFT, CENTER, nor RIGHT is specified, LEFT is assumed:']
[[[164, 635], [1146, 635], [1146, 692], [164, 692]], '10) If more than one integer-1 or integer-2 operand is specified the clause is referred to as a multiple COLUMN clause and the following additional rules apply:']
[[[200, 714], [756, 714], [756, 744], [200, 744]], 'a) No OCCURS clause shall be specified in the same entry']
[[[200, 766], [940, 766], [940, 799], [200, 799]], 'b) All the occurrences of integer-1 shall be in increasing order of magnitude:']
[[[164, 822], [272, 822], [272, 846], [164, 846]], 'FORMAT 2']
[[[164, 874], [466, 874], [466, 903], [164, 903]], '11) MINUS and are synonyms_']
[[[164, 924], [1146, 924], [1146, 984], [164, 984]], '12) Identifier-1 shall be described in the file, working-storage; local-storage, or linkage section as an elementary unsigned integer data item.']
[[[164, 1003], [1146, 1003], [1146, 1058], [164, 1058]], '13) Neither the PLUS phrase nor the MINUS phrase shall be specified for the first elementary item in a screen record:']
[[[163, 1085], [460, 1085], [460, 1112], [163, 1112]], '13.18.14.4 General rules']
[[[164, 1138], [270, 1138], [270, 1162], [164, 1162]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[164, 1188], [1146, 1188], [1146, 1244], [164, 1244]], '1) The COLUMN clause defines one or more printable items. If a COLUMN clause is not specified, the items are not printed:']
[[[164, 1267], [1146, 1267], [1146, 1324], [164, 1324]], '2) There is a fixed correspondence, specified by the implementor; between a column anda character in national character set:']
[[[164, 1346], [1146, 1346], [1146, 1454], [164, 1454]], "3) The printable-size ofa printable item is the number of columns required for printing the characters described by the item's PICTURE clause Or, in the absence of a PICTURE clause; the literal specified in the VALUE clause. There is a one-to-one correspondence between a column and a character in an alphanumeric character set:"]
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '387']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 418 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[166, 186], [1112, 186], [1112, 238], [166, 238]], 'NOTE 1 Columns might not line up in a report if the printable characters are not of fixed size encoding, such as UTF-8.']
[[[130, 264], [1112, 264], [1112, 350], [130, 350]], '4) Any report line shall be defined in such a way that any given column position is used for only one printable item when the line is printed: If this rule is violated the EC-REPORT-COLUMN-OVERLAP exception condition is set to exist and the results are undefined.']
[[[130, 366], [1112, 366], [1112, 451], [130, 451]], '5) Any - reportline shall be defined in such a way that; when printed,the final column position of the last printable item does not exceed the page width. If this rule is violated the EC-REPORT-PAGE-WIDTH exception condition is set to exist; the report line is truncated, and the report line is printed.']
[[[130, 470], [823, 470], [823, 506], [130, 506]], '6) Ifthe integer-1 phrase is specified, the following general rules apply:']
[[[166, 525], [656, 525], [656, 557], [166, 557]], 'a) Integer-1 specifies an absolute column number.']
[[[166, 578], [1110, 578], [1110, 637], [166, 637]], 'b) If LEFT is specified, integer-1 is the leftmost column ofthe printable item: The rightmost column ofthe printable item is integer-1 printable-size']
[[[166, 658], [1111, 658], [1111, 715], [166, 715]], 'c) If RIGHT is specified, integer-1 is the rightmost column of the printable item The leftmost column ofthe printable item is integer-1 printable-size + 1.']
[[[166, 736], [818, 736], [818, 766], [166, 766]], 'd) If CENTER is specified, the printable item is centered,as follows:']
[[[238, 788], [1112, 788], [1112, 873], [238, 873]], 'If the printable-size of the printable item is an odd number of columns, integer-1 identifies the center column ofthe printable item: The leftmost column is integer-1 ((printable-size 1) / 2J; the rightmost column is integer-1 (printable-size 2), truncated to an integer:']
[[[238, 895], [1112, 895], [1112, 1005], [238, 1005]], 'If the printable-size of the printable item is an even number of columns, integer-1 identifies the column of the printable item that is to the left of an imaginary line between the two middle columns of the printable item: The leftmost column is (integer-1 (printable-size 2)) 1; the rightmost column is integer-1 (printable-size 2).']
[[[130, 1027], [1112, 1027], [1112, 1106], [130, 1106]], '7) Within any given report line,a horizontal counter, representing the rightmost occupied column, is maintained and updated by each printable item in the line_ At the start of the line, the horizontal counter is zero']
[[[130, 1131], [1112, 1131], [1112, 1269], [130, 1269]], "8) Integer-2 specifies a relative column number: If integer-2 is specified for an item that is the first printable item in the current line, it specifies the leftmost column of that item: Otherwise, the value of integer-2 is the number of column positions between the rightmost column of the preceding printable item and the leftmost column of the item being defined: The position of the item's leftmost character is obtained by adding integer-2 to the current line's horizontal counter:"]
[[[165, 1288], [1112, 1288], [1112, 1337], [165, 1337]], 'NOTE 2 The value of integer-2 minus is the number of blank columns, if any, immediately preceding the item_']
[[[130, 1363], [1112, 1363], [1112, 1415], [130, 1415]], '9) The rightmost column position of each printable item becomes the new value of the horizontal counter']
[[[130, 1442], [880, 1442], [880, 1473], [130, 1473]], '10) Any unoccupied columns in each print line are filled with space characters:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '388']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 419 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 186], [1148, 186], [1148, 245], [164, 245]], '11) Ifan entry containing a LINE clause has no subordinate entry defining printable item, the resultant report line will be blank:']
[[[164, 267], [1146, 267], [1146, 375], [164, 375]], '12)A multiple COLUMN clause is functionally equivalent to a COLUMN clause with single operand, together with simple OCCURS clause whose integer is equal to the number of operands of the COLUMN clause, except that the multiple COLUMN clause allows the printable items to be defined at unequal horizontal intervals_']
[[[164, 400], [272, 400], [272, 424], [164, 424]], 'FORMAT 2']
[[[164, 452], [1146, 452], [1146, 584], [164, 584]], '13) The COLUMN clause specifies the column in which the leftmost character of the screen item is to appear on the screen during the execution of an ACCEPT screen or a DISPLAY screen statement Positioning of the screen record and within the screen record appears the same on the terminal display regardless of whether the whole screen record or just a portion of it is referenced in an ACCEPT screen or a DISPLAY screen statement']
[[[200, 606], [1147, 606], [1147, 658], [200, 658]], 'NOTE 3 Columns might not line up on a screen if the characters on the screen are not of fixed size encoding such as UTF-8.']
[[[162, 682], [1146, 682], [1146, 762], [162, 762]], '14) Ifthe COLUMN clause does not specify PLUS or MINUS,the clause gives the column number relative to the first column of the screen record. A column number of 1 represents the first column of the screen record_']
[[[164, 787], [1146, 787], [1146, 924], [164, 924]], '15) Ifthe PLUS or MINUS phrase is specified in the COLUMN clause, the column number is relative to the end ofthe preceding screen item in the same screen record, such that if COLUMN PLUS 1 is specified, the screen item starts immediately following the preceding screen item_ PLUS denotes a column position that is increased by the value of identifier-1 or integer-3. MINUS denotes a column position that is decreased by the value of identifier-1 or integer-3']
[[[164, 948], [1146, 948], [1146, 998], [164, 998]], '16) A setting of COLUMN 1 is assumed for screen descriptions that specify the LINE clause but omit the COLUMN clause_']
[[[163, 1021], [968, 1021], [968, 1058], [163, 1058]], '17) If both the LINE clause and the COLUMN clause are omitted, the following apply:']
[[[200, 1078], [1076, 1078], [1076, 1106], [200, 1106]], 'a) ifno previous screen item has been defined, LINE 1 COLUMN 1 ofthe screen is assumed:']
[[[200, 1129], [1146, 1129], [1146, 1184], [200, 1184]], 'b) ifa previous screen item has been defined, the line of that previous item and COLUMN PLUS 1 is assumed.']
[[[164, 1210], [1146, 1210], [1146, 1264], [164, 1264]], '18) If a column number of zero is specified, the EC-SCREEN-STARTING-COLUMN exception condition is set to exist and the results are as if 1 were specified for the column number:']
[[[164, 1290], [1146, 1290], [1146, 1394], [164, 1394]], '19) Ifthe explicit or implicit column number ofthe first character of the screen item exceeds the number of columns available for a terminal, the EC-SCREEN-STARTING-COLUMN exception condition is set to exist and that screen item does not take part in the execution of the ACCEPT screen or DISPLAY screen statement:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '389']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 420 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[165, 186], [1112, 186], [1112, 268], [165, 268]], 'Otherwise, if the starting column and length of a screen item are such that the field would extend outside the end ofthe terminal line, the EC-SCREEN-ITEM-TRUNCATED exception condition is set to exist and the field is truncated at the end of the line']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '390']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 421 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [582, 190], [582, 216], [164, 216]], '13.18.15 CONSTANT RECORD clause']
[[[164, 242], [395, 242], [395, 268], [164, 268]], '13.18.15.1 General']
[[[164, 294], [1130, 294], [1130, 346], [164, 346]], 'The CONSTANT RECORD clause identifies a structured constant: The content ofa structured constant cannot be modified.']
[[[163, 373], [478, 373], [478, 400], [163, 400]], '13.18.15.2 General format']
[[[166, 432], [370, 432], [370, 456], [166, 456]], 'CONSTANT RECORD']
[[[163, 492], [446, 492], [446, 522], [163, 522]], '13.18.15.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 542], [1146, 542], [1146, 596], [164, 596]], '1) The CONSTANT RECORD clause may be specified only in the local-storage or working-storage sections:']
[[[164, 621], [1146, 621], [1146, 678], [164, 678]], '2) Neither the data item described by the subject of the entry nor any data item subordinate to the subject of the entry shall be specified as a receiving data item_']
[[[164, 702], [448, 702], [448, 728], [164, 728]], '13.18.15.4 General rule']
[[[164, 756], [1146, 756], [1146, 839], [164, 839]], '1) The content of a record described with the CONSTANT RECORD clause is as though the clause had been omitted and the record had been the subject ofan INITIALIZE statement that is specified with the following phrases:']
[[[264, 862], [746, 862], [746, 942], [264, 942]], 'the FILLER phrase; the VALUE phrase with the category-name of ALL; and the DEFAULT phrase:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '391']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 422 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 189], [434, 189], [434, 216], [129, 216]], '13.18.16 CONTROL clause']
[[[130, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.16.1 General']
[[[130, 294], [862, 294], [862, 322], [130, 322]], 'The CONTROL clause establishes a hierarchy of control breaks for the report:']
[[[130, 348], [446, 348], [446, 374], [130, 374]], '13.18.16.2 General format']
[[[167, 426], [326, 426], [326, 486], [167, 486]], 'CONTROL IS CONTROLS ARE']
[[[380, 430], [598, 430], [598, 486], [380, 486]], 'data-name-1 FINAL [ data-name-1 ]']
[[[130, 530], [412, 530], [412, 560], [130, 560]], '13.18.16.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 582], [558, 582], [558, 610], [130, 610]], '1) CONTROL and CONTROLS are synonyms']
[[[130, 635], [686, 635], [686, 665], [130, 665]], '2) Data-name-1 shall not be defined in the report section.']
[[[130, 688], [710, 688], [710, 716], [130, 716]], '3) Data-name-1 shall not be subject to any OCCURS clauses:']
[[[130, 740], [1112, 740], [1112, 771], [130, 771]], '4) Data-name-1 may be reference-modified. Ifit is,leftmost-position and length shall be integer literals']
[[[130, 791], [1070, 791], [1070, 823], [130, 823]], '5) The entry specified by data-name-1 shall not have an occurs-depending table subordinate to it:']
[[[130, 845], [1110, 845], [1110, 902], [130, 902]], '6) Data-name-1 shall be unique in any given CONTROL clause. Two or more instances of data-name-1 in the same clause may, however, refer to the same physical data item or to overlapping data items']
[[[130, 922], [706, 922], [706, 955], [130, 955]], '7) Data-name-1 shall not reference a variable-length group.']
[[[130, 978], [424, 978], [424, 1004], [130, 1004]], '13.18.16.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1030], [1112, 1030], [1112, 1110], [130, 1110]], '1) The order of appearance of the operands of the CONTROL clause establishes the levels ofthe control hierarchy. The first data-name-l is the major control; the next recurrence of data-name-1 is an intermediate control, etc: The last recurrence of data-name-1 is the minor control.']
[[[130, 1133], [1112, 1133], [1112, 1215], [130, 1215]], '2) Specifying FINAL is equivalentto specifyinga data item whose value does not change throughout the processing of the report: Therefore FINAL, if specified, is associated with the highest level in the hierarchy:']
[[[130, 1238], [1113, 1238], [1113, 1455], [130, 1455]], '3) For each data-name-1 an internal data item, known as a prior control, is implicitly defined, having the same data description as the corresponding data item and a mechanism is established to save each control data item in the corresponding prior control and subsequently to compare these values to sense for control breaks. Execution of the chronologically first GENERATE statement for a given report saves each current control data item in the corresponding prior control. Subsequent executions of any GENERATE statement for that report automatically test the current value of each control data item, in order major to minor, for equality with the corresponding prior control. If a change of value in a control data item is detected, no further control data items are tested for the']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '392']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 423 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[199, 190], [1146, 190], [1146, 244], [199, 244]], 'current GENERATE statement; and control break processing for that level and any lower levels is performed automatically:']
[[[164, 267], [1146, 267], [1146, 322], [164, 322]], '4) Ifa control break has been detected during the execution ofa GENERATE statement, the control data items are processed as follows:']
[[[201, 344], [1147, 344], [1147, 639], [201, 639]], 'a) If any control footing is defined for the report; the current contents of control data items are saved and the corresponding prior controls are stored in the control data items before any control footing is printed. This ensures that any reference to a control data item in a control footing will always yield the value that the control data item had before the control break If a USE BEFORE REPORTING declarative   procedure is specified for the control footing  any reference to a control data item in this section will similarly yield the prior value: If the printing of the control footing causes a page advance and the page heading 0r page footing refers to a control data item, the value produced is similarly the prior value_ When the printing of each control footing has been accomplished, the control data items are restored from the prior controls to their new current values, with these new current values remaining in the prior controls. All subsequent references to the control data items will obtain the new current values']
[[[200, 663], [1146, 663], [1146, 721], [200, 721]], 'b) Ifno control footing is defined for the report; the new current values of the control data items are stored in the corresponding prior controls.']
[[[200, 743], [516, 743], [516, 770], [200, 770]], '(See also 13.18.57, TYPE clause:)']
[[[164, 792], [1148, 792], [1148, 904], [164, 904]], '5) When a TERMINATE statement is executed,if = any control footing is defined for the report; the prior controls are stored in the control data items before each control footing is printed, as though a highest-level control break had been detected: The result is the same as that of a control break detected during the execution ofa GENERATE statement']
[[[167, 928], [758, 928], [758, 956], [167, 956]], '6) Data-name-1 shall not reference a zero-length group item:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '393']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 424 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 189], [430, 189], [430, 216], [129, 216]], '13.18.17 DEFAULT clause']
[[[130, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.17.1 General']
[[[129, 294], [1108, 294], [1108, 371], [129, 371]], 'The DEFAULT clause specifies an explicit alternative value for a data item that contains all spaces 0r was found to be invalid on format during the format validation stage ofthe execution of a VALIDATE statement']
[[[130, 396], [856, 396], [856, 424], [130, 424]], 'NOTE The DEFAULT clause feature ofthe VALIDATE facility is an obsolete feature_']
[[[130, 450], [446, 450], [446, 476], [130, 476]], '13.18.17.22 General format']
[[[132, 522], [392, 522], [392, 606], [132, 606]], 'literal-1 DEFAULT IS identifier-1 NONE']
[[[130, 652], [412, 652], [412, 680], [130, 680]], '13.18.173 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 702], [944, 702], [944, 732], [130, 732]], '1) The subject ofthe entry shall not be of class index, message-tag, object; or pointer.']
[[[130, 756], [1112, 756], [1112, 813], [130, 813]], '2) Literal-L or the data item referenced by identifier-1 shall be valid as a sending operand for a MOVE statement specifying the subject of the entry as the receiving operand.']
[[[130, 836], [424, 836], [424, 862], [130, 862]], '13.18.17.4 General rules']
[[[130, 887], [1112, 887], [1112, 944], [130, 944]], '1) A default value is established during the format validation stage of the execution of a VALIDATE statement ifany ofthe following circumstances apply:']
[[[166, 966], [1112, 966], [1112, 1048], [166, 1048]], "a) In the case ofan elementary data item, if the item's associated internal indicator is set to invalid on format, or the item is implicitly or explicitly of usage display or national and contains all spaces_"]
[[[166, 1072], [1112, 1072], [1112, 1150], [166, 1150]], "b) In the case ofan alphanumeric group or strongly-typed group item, ifall the item's subordinate elementary data items are implicitly or explicitly of usage display and contain all spaces, and none of their associated internal indicators is set to invalid on format"]
[[[166, 1178], [1110, 1178], [1110, 1232], [166, 1232]], "c) In the case of a national group item, if all the item's subordinate elementary data items contain all spaces_"]
[[[164, 1254], [1112, 1254], [1112, 1362], [164, 1362]], 'In these cases, any subsequent reference to that data item during the execution of the current VALIDATE statement uses the default value rather than the actual content ofthe item: The data item itself remains unchanged The DEFAULT clause is ignored during the execution of any statement other than a VALIDATE statement;']
[[[130, 1388], [1114, 1388], [1114, 1470], [130, 1470]], '2) Except in the case of DEFAULT NONE, the DEFAULT clause specifies the default value explicitly. If literal-1 is specified, the default value used is the value of literal-1. If identifier-1 is specified, the default value used is the value ofthe data item referenced by identifier-1.']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '394']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 425 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 187], [1149, 187], [1149, 270], [164, 270]], "3) Ifthe DEFAULT clause is not specified in the data item's data description entry; or if DEFAULT NONE is specified, the default value is that which would be supplied for the data item by the execution of an implicit INITIALIZE statement without a REPLACING or VALUE phrase:"]
[[[164, 292], [1146, 292], [1146, 377], [164, 377]], '4) If NONE is specified, the effect of the clause is the same as if no DEFAULT clause were specified, except that; if the data item contains all spaces or the data item is elementary and is found to be invalid on format; any DESTINATION clause specified for the data item is ignored.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '395']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 426 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 189], [482, 189], [482, 216], [129, 216]], '13.18.18 DESTINATION clause']
[[[130, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.18.1 General']
[[[128, 292], [1111, 292], [1111, 351], [128, 351]], 'The DESTINATION clause specifies one or more dataitems to which data is to be implicitly moved during the execution ofthe input distribution stage ofa VALIDATE statement.']
[[[130, 369], [903, 369], [903, 399], [130, 399]], 'NOTE The DESTINATION clause feature of the VALIDATE facility is an obsolete feature:']
[[[130, 422], [446, 422], [446, 448], [130, 448]], '13.18.18.2 General format']
[[[132, 478], [440, 478], [440, 506], [132, 506]], 'DESTINATION IS { identifier-1 }']
[[[130, 542], [412, 542], [412, 570], [130, 570]], '13.18.183 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 592], [1113, 592], [1113, 674], [130, 674]], '1) The description of the data item referenced by identifier-1 shall be such that a syntactically correct MOVE statement results when that data item is the receiving operand and the subject ofthe entry, qualified and subscripted where necessary, is the sending operand']
[[[130, 697], [1112, 697], [1112, 750], [130, 750]], '2) The data description entry for the data item referenced by identifier-1 or any data item subordinate to identifier-1 shall not contain a VALIDATE-STATUS clause.']
[[[130, 775], [1113, 775], [1113, 829], [130, 829]], '3) Ifthe subject ofthe entry is a global name, every data-name appearing in identifier-1 shall bea global name:']
[[[130, 858], [424, 858], [424, 882], [130, 882]], '13.18.18.4 General rules']
[[[130, 906], [1112, 906], [1112, 1039], [130, 1039]], '1) The DESTINATION clause causes one or more implicit MOVE statements to be executed during the input distribution phase ofthe execution ofa VALIDATE statementthat references a data item whose description contains the DESTINATION   clause in the same or in subordinate entry. The DESTINATION clause is ignored during the execution of any statement other than VALIDATE statement']
[[[130, 1067], [1112, 1067], [1112, 1122], [130, 1122]], '2) If a default value has not been assigned to the item, as described in General rule 1 of the DEFAULT clause; the actual content of the item is moved to the data item referenced by identifier-1.']
[[[130, 1146], [1112, 1146], [1112, 1200], [130, 1200]], "3) Ifa default value has been assigned to the item and DEFAULT NONE is not specified in the entry, the item's default value is moved to the data item referenced by identifier-1."]
[[[130, 1223], [1112, 1223], [1112, 1281], [130, 1281]], '4) Ifa default value has been assigned to the item and DEFAULT NONE is specified in the entry, the data item referenced by identifier-1 is unchanged:']
[[[130, 1302], [1112, 1302], [1112, 1413], [130, 1413]], '5) Ifidentifier-1 contains subscripts defined in VARYING clauses thatare processed atthe same time as the DESTINATION clause, these subscripts may be used to store different occurrences of the data item in the same number of different occurrences ofthe destination item. Ifa VARYING clause is not used, any subscripts specified in the DESTINATION clause are unchanged.']
[[[164, 1433], [1112, 1433], [1112, 1492], [164, 1492]], 'The sending data determined by the above general rules is moved to the data item referenced by each identifier-1 in the order specified, according to the rules for the MOVE statement:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '396']
[[[204, 1550], [356, 1550], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 427 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 189], [564, 189], [564, 216], [164, 216]], '13.18.19 DYNAMIC LENGTH clause']
[[[164, 242], [396, 242], [396, 268], [164, 268]], '13.18.19.1 General']
[[[164, 294], [1012, 294], [1012, 324], [164, 324]], 'The DYNAMIC LENGTH clause specifies that the length ofa data item can vary at runtime']
[[[164, 348], [478, 348], [478, 374], [164, 374]], '13.18.19.2 General format']
[[[165, 403], [922, 403], [922, 433], [165, 433]], 'DYNAMIC LENGTH dynamic-length-structure-name-1 ] [ LIMIT IS integer-1 ]']
[[[164, 466], [446, 466], [446, 494], [164, 494]], '13.18.19.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 516], [1148, 516], [1148, 573], [164, 573]], "1) A PICTURE clause shall be specified for the subject ofthe entry: The character-string specified in that PICTURE clause shall be one instance ofthe picture symbol 'N', or 'X'."]
[[[164, 596], [1146, 596], [1146, 681], [164, 681]], '2) If dynamic-length-structure-name-1 is specified, it shall correspond to a dynamic-length-structure- name specified in the DYNAMIC LENGTH STRUCTURE clause in the SPECIAL-NAMES paragraph, as defined in 12.3.7 , SPECIAL-NAMES paragraph.']
[[[163, 699], [1147, 699], [1147, 758], [163, 758]], '3)  If dynamic-length-structure-name-1 is not specified,the structure ofthe dynamic-length elementary item is defined by the implementor:']
[[[164, 779], [1146, 779], [1146, 836], [164, 836]], '4) If the LIMIT phrase and dynamic-structure-name-1 are both specified, integer-1 shall not be greater than the maximum length associated with dynamic-length-structure-name-1_']
[[[164, 862], [460, 862], [460, 886], [164, 886]], '13.18.19.4 General rules']
[[[164, 911], [1142, 911], [1142, 969], [164, 969]], '1) The DYNAMIC LENGTH clause specifies that the length ofthe data item defined by the entry can vary_ The minimum length ofthe data item is zero. The picture symbol determines the class.']
[[[164, 990], [1146, 990], [1146, 1075], [164, 1075]], '2) Integer-1 specifies the maximum number of characters that can be contained by the subject of the entry. Ifthe LIMIT phrase is not specified,the maximum number of characters that can be contained by the subject of the entry is implementor-defined']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '397']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 428 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 189], [456, 189], [456, 222], [129, 222]], '13.18.20   Entry-name clause']
[[[130, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.20.1 General']
[[[130, 291], [1090, 291], [1090, 350], [130, 350]], 'The entry-name clause specifies the name of the item that is being described: A data-name or screen- name may be used to give the item a name']
[[[129, 373], [456, 373], [456, 400], [129, 400]], '13.18.20.2 General formats']
[[[130, 425], [346, 425], [346, 454], [130, 454]], 'Format 1 (data-name)']
[[[132, 484], [256, 484], [256, 508], [132, 508]], 'data-name-1']
[[[129, 539], [366, 539], [366, 566], [129, 566]], 'Format 2 (screen-name)']
[[[129, 595], [278, 595], [278, 622], [129, 622]], 'screen-name-1']
[[[130, 648], [288, 648], [288, 678], [130, 678]], 'Format 3 (filler)']
[[[133, 709], [205, 709], [205, 729], [133, 729]], 'FILLER']
[[[130, 768], [412, 768], [412, 796], [130, 796]], '13.18.203 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 818], [644, 818], [644, 850], [130, 850]], '1) Data-name-1 shall not be qualified or subscripted:']
[[[130, 871], [1109, 871], [1109, 929], [130, 929]], '2) Ifthe entry-name clause is not specified in a data description entry, report group description entry, or a screen description entry, the word FILLER is assumed,']
[[[130, 949], [1113, 949], [1113, 1033], [130, 1033]], '3) Ifthe entry-name clause is specified in a data description entry or a report group description entry, either data-name-1 or FILLER shall be specified. If the entry-name clause is specified in a screen description entry, either screen-name-1 or FILLER shall be specified.']
[[[130, 1058], [414, 1058], [414, 1084], [130, 1084]], '13.18.20.4 General rule']
[[[130, 1109], [1112, 1109], [1112, 1164], [130, 1164]], '1) The word FILLER may be used to name a data, report; or screen item. Under no circumstances shall a FILLER item be referred to explicitly.']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '398']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 429 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [432, 190], [432, 216], [164, 216]], '13.18.21 ERASE clause']
[[[164, 242], [396, 242], [396, 268], [164, 268]], '13.18.21.1 General']
[[[164, 292], [964, 292], [964, 324], [164, 324]], 'The ERASE clause clears part of the line or the screen starting at the cursor position']
[[[164, 348], [478, 348], [478, 374], [164, 374]], '13.18.21.2 General format']
[[[264, 416], [426, 416], [426, 548], [264, 548]], 'END OF LINE END OF SCREEN EOL EOS']
[[[168, 468], [236, 468], [236, 492], [168, 492]], 'ERASE']
[[[164, 588], [446, 588], [446, 618], [164, 618]], '13.18.21.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 638], [726, 638], [726, 671], [164, 671]], '1) The word EOL is equivalent to the words END OF LINE:']
[[[164, 690], [760, 690], [760, 723], [164, 723]], '2) The word EOS is equivalent to the words END OF SCREEN:']
[[[164, 746], [460, 746], [460, 772], [164, 772]], '13.18.21.4 General rules']
[[[164, 796], [1146, 796], [1146, 881], [164, 881]], '1) When the ERASE clause is specified, a portion of the screen is cleared during the execution of a DISPLAY screen statement before data is transferred to the screen item_ The clearing begins at the line and column coordinates specified for the subject of the entry and continues as follows:']
[[[236, 902], [840, 902], [840, 934], [236, 934]], 'If LINE is specified,the clearing continues to the end of the line:']
[[[236, 954], [896, 954], [896, 986], [236, 986]], 'If SCREEN is specified, the clearing continues to the end ofthe screen_']
[[[164, 1006], [1106, 1006], [1106, 1038], [164, 1038]], '2) The ERASE clause is ignored during all phases of the execution ofan ACCEPT screen statement:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '399']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 430 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 189], [446, 189], [446, 216], [129, 216]], '13.18.22 EXTERNAL clause']
[[[130, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.22.1 General']
[[[128, 294], [1108, 294], [1108, 372], [128, 372]], 'The EXTERNAL clause specifies that a type declaration; data item, or a file connector is external: The constituent data itemsand group data items ofan external data record areavailable ina run unitto every runtime element that describes the record as external.']
[[[130, 400], [446, 400], [446, 426], [130, 426]], '13.18.22.2 General format']
[[[130, 458], [408, 458], [408, 486], [130, 486]], 'IS EXTERNAL AS literal-1 ]']
[[[130, 518], [412, 518], [412, 548], [130, 548]], '13.18.22.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 570], [1110, 570], [1110, 628], [130, 628]], '1) The EXTERNAL clause may be specified only in file description entries, in level 1 data description entries in the working-storage section, and in level 1 type declarations.']
[[[130, 648], [1112, 648], [1112, 728], [130, 728]], '2) In the same source element; the externalized name of the subject of the entry that includes the EXTERNAL clause shall not be the same as the externalized name ofany other entry that includes the EXTERNAL clause:']
[[[130, 755], [1112, 755], [1112, 811], [130, 811]], '3) Literal-1 shall be an alphanumeric or national literal and shall be neither a figurative constant nor a zero-length literal.']
[[[130, 832], [970, 832], [970, 862], [130, 862]], '4) The EXTERNAL clause shall not be specified for a data item of class object or pointer_']
[[[130, 887], [1110, 887], [1110, 938], [130, 938]], '5) When a record description is an external item, any associated type declaration that is strongly typed shall also be external:']
[[[166, 963], [1112, 963], [1112, 1012], [166, 1012]], 'NOTE This covers the situation where a file is declared as external and the associated record descriptions have TYPE clauses;']
[[[130, 1040], [424, 1040], [424, 1066], [130, 1066]], '13.18.22.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1089], [1112, 1089], [1112, 1174], [130, 1174]], '1) If the EXTERNAL clause is specified in a record description entry, the data contained in the record is external and may be accessed within the run unit by any runtime element that describes the same record as external, subject to the following rules:']
[[[167, 1195], [1112, 1195], [1112, 1243], [167, 1243]], 'NOTE 1 Use of the EXTERNAL clause does not imply that the associated file-name or data-name is a global name_']
[[[130, 1265], [1111, 1265], [1111, 1326], [130, 1326]], '2) Ifthe EXTERNAL clause is specified in a type declaration entry,any data description conta Jining that type declaration shall be at level-number 1.']
[[[130, 1347], [1112, 1347], [1112, 1405], [130, 1405]], '3) If a type declaration is specified with the EXTERNAL phrase, the record descriptions in which it is specified are also external and subject to the same rules:']
[[[130, 1426], [758, 1426], [758, 1457], [130, 1457]], '4) Ifthe EXTERNAL clause is specified in a file description entry:']
[[[131, 1553], [171, 1553], [171, 1573], [131, 1573]], '400']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 431 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[201, 187], [1118, 187], [1118, 219], [201, 219]], 'a) the file connector associated with this file description entry is an external file connector; and']
[[[200, 241], [1146, 241], [1146, 324], [200, 324]], 'b) the data contained in all record description entries subordinate to that file description entry is external and may be accessed by any runtime element in the run unit that describes the same file and records as external, subject to the following rules']
[[[200, 344], [1146, 344], [1146, 398], [200, 398]], 'NOTE 2 13.18.57, TYPE clause, Syntax rule 4; and 13.18.33,Level-number; General rule 3; require thatifthe record description is strongly typed, it is the only record description for the file:']
[[[164, 417], [1146, 417], [1146, 529], [164, 529]], '5) Literal-1,if specified, is the name ofthe file connector or record that is externalized to the operating environment: If literal-1 is not specified, the externalized name of the file connector or record is the name specified in the file description entry or the data-name format of the entry-name clause, respectively.']
[[[167, 552], [1147, 552], [1147, 767], [167, 767]], '6) Within a run unit; iftwo or more source elements describe the same external data record, each name that is externalized to the operating environment for the record description entries shall be the same; the VALUE clause specification, if any, for each record name of the associated record description entries shall be identical; and the records shall define the same number of bytes: source element that describes an external record that is not strongly typed may contain a data description entry including the REDEFINES clause that redefines the complete external record, and this complete redefinition need not occur identically in other source elements in the run unit Additionally, for strongly typed external items, the rules in 8.5.3, Types also apply:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1105, 1553], [1143, 1553], [1143, 1573], [1105, 1573]], '401']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 432 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 189], [570, 189], [570, 216], [129, 216]], '13.18.23 FOREGROUND-COLOR clause']
[[[130, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.23.1 General']
[[[130, 293], [854, 293], [854, 325], [130, 325]], 'The FOREGROUND clause specifies the foreground color for the screen item_']
[[[130, 348], [446, 348], [446, 374], [130, 374]], '13.18.23.2 General format']
[[[131, 418], [514, 418], [514, 478], [131, 478]], 'identifier-1 FOREGROUND-COLOR IS integer-1']
[[[130, 512], [412, 512], [412, 542], [130, 542]], '13.18.23.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 561], [1110, 561], [1110, 621], [130, 621]], '1) Identifier-1 shall be described in the file, working-storage, local-storage, or linkage section as an unsigned integer data item_']
[[[130, 643], [634, 643], [634, 673], [130, 673]], '2)   Integer-1 shall have a value in the range of 0 to 7.']
[[[130, 696], [424, 696], [424, 722], [130, 722]], '13.18.23.4 General rules']
[[[130, 746], [1112, 746], [1112, 826], [130, 826]], '1) When an ACCEPT screen statement or a DISPLAY screen statement referencing the associated screen item is executed, the content of the data item referenced by identifier-1 shall be in the range of 0 to 7']
[[[130, 851], [1112, 851], [1112, 934], [130, 934]], '2) Integer-1 or the content ofthe data item referenced by identifier-1 specifies the color number ofthe foreground color to be used for displaying the screen item: The color associated with the color number is specified in 9.2.7, Color number:']
[[[130, 957], [1110, 957], [1110, 1014], [130, 1014]], '3) A FOREGROUND-COLOR clause specified at the group level applies to each elementary screen item in that group']
[[[130, 1038], [1112, 1038], [1112, 1094], [130, 1094]], '4) When a FOREGROUND-COLOR clause is not specified or the value is not in the range 0 to 7, the foreground color is implementor-defined.']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '402']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 433 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [458, 190], [458, 216], [164, 216]], '13.18.24 FORMAT clause']
[[[164, 242], [395, 242], [395, 268], [164, 268]], '13.18.24.1 General']
[[[163, 292], [1126, 292], [1126, 402], [163, 402]], 'The FORMAT clause specifies that records written to the file are to be formatted t0 an external representation during the output process and records read from the file are to be converted from the external format into internal representation during the input process The external representation is suitable for presentation or printing:']
[[[164, 426], [478, 426], [478, 452], [164, 452]], '13.18.24.2 General format']
[[[293, 497], [329, 497], [329, 517], [293, 517]], 'BIT']
[[[166, 530], [256, 530], [256, 554], [166, 554]], 'FORMAT']
[[[291, 530], [418, 530], [418, 592], [291, 592]], 'CHARACTER NUMERIC']
[[[448, 530], [508, 530], [508, 554], [448, 554]], 'DATA']
[[[164, 632], [446, 632], [446, 660], [164, 660]], '13.18.24.3 Syntax rules']
[[[164, 681], [1146, 681], [1146, 762], [164, 762]], '1) If more than one record description entry is associated with a file description entry in which the FORMAT clause is specified, each ofthese record description entries shall contain a SELECT WHEN clause:']
[[[164, 787], [1147, 787], [1147, 897], [164, 897]], '2) The REDEFINES clause and the RENAMES clause shall not be specified in a record description entry associated with a file description entry in which a FORMAT clause is specified or in the data description entry of the identifier specified in a FROM phrase in a REWRITE or WRITE statement referencing that file description entry']
[[[164, 918], [1146, 918], [1146, 1029], [164, 1029]], '3) Data items of class index, message-tag object; and pointer shall not be specified in a record description entry associated with a file description entry in which a FORMAT clause is specified or in the data description entry of the identifier specified in a FROM phrase in a REWRITE or WRITE statement referencing that file description entry.']
[[[164, 1054], [460, 1054], [460, 1078], [164, 1078]], '13.18.24.4 General rules']
[[[164, 1103], [1144, 1103], [1144, 1164], [164, 1164]], '1) The FORMAT clause specifies that external media format is to be used for records written to the file In external media format; data contains the appropriate encoding for presentation Or printing']
[[[200, 1182], [1022, 1182], [1022, 1208], [200, 1208]], 'NOTE The data in the record area is visible t0 the runtime module in internal representation:']
[[[164, 1230], [1146, 1230], [1146, 1310], [164, 1310]], '2) A record description entry or a data description entry is selected for use in formatting during an input-output operation referencing a file description entry in which a FORMAT clause is specified as follows:']
[[[200, 1336], [1144, 1336], [1144, 1392], [200, 1392]], 'a) If only one record description entry is associated with the file description entry, that record description entry is selected.']
[[[200, 1415], [1146, 1415], [1146, 1473], [200, 1473]], 'b) Ifmore than one record description entry is associated with the file description entry, the record description entry identified by the SELECT WHEN clause is selected:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '403']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 434 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[201, 187], [1112, 187], [1112, 271], [201, 271]], 'If there are no record description entries associated with the file description entry, the record description entry or data description entry that is selected depends on the input-output operation as follows:']
[[[238, 293], [1112, 293], [1112, 351], [238, 351]], 'If the input-output statement is a READ statement; it is the data description entry of the identifier that is specified in the INTO phrase ofthat READ statement']
[[[238, 371], [1113, 371], [1113, 429], [238, 429]], 'Ifthe input-output statementis a WRITE or REWRITE statementand an identifier is specified in the FROM phrase, it is the data description entry of that identifier:']
[[[238, 452], [1112, 452], [1112, 535], [238, 535]], 'Ifthe input-output statement is a WRITE or REWRITE statement and a literal is specified in the FROM phrase, an implicit data description entry that describes the literal is the data description entry as follows:']
[[[274, 557], [994, 557], [994, 586], [274, 586]], 'If the literal is an alphanumeric literal,the implicit data description entry is']
[[[312, 612], [461, 612], [461, 636], [312, 636]], '01 PIC X (n)']
[[[276, 660], [966, 660], [966, 692], [276, 692]], 'where n is the number of alphanumeric character positions in the literal.']
[[[274, 715], [926, 715], [926, 745], [274, 745]], 'If the literal is a boolean literal, the implicit data description entry is']
[[[312, 770], [461, 770], [461, 794], [312, 794]], '01 PIC 1 (n)']
[[[276, 818], [910, 818], [910, 850], [276, 850]], 'where n is the number of boolean character positions in the literal.']
[[[274, 874], [928, 874], [928, 902], [274, 902]], 'If the literal is a national literal,the implicit data description entry is']
[[[312, 928], [464, 928], [464, 954], [312, 954]], '01 PIC N (n)']
[[[276, 977], [912, 977], [912, 1008], [276, 1008]], 'where n is the number of national character positions in the literal:']
[[[130, 1031], [1112, 1031], [1112, 1084], [130, 1084]], '3) Elementary data description entries of the selected record description entry are used to determine the data items to be formatted']
[[[130, 1109], [1112, 1109], [1112, 1166], [130, 1166]], '4) If CHARACTER is specified, data items described with the following characteristics are selected for formatting:']
[[[201, 1188], [532, 1188], [532, 1352], [201, 1352]], 'class alphabetic class alphanumeric class boolean with usage display class national class numeric with usage display class numeric with usage national:']
[[[130, 1372], [1112, 1372], [1112, 1405], [130, 1405]], '5) If BIT is specified, data items described with class boolean and usage bit are selected for formatting:']
[[[164, 1425], [1112, 1425], [1112, 1484], [164, 1484]], 'If NUMERIC is specified, data items described with class numeric and a usage other than national or display are selected for formatting:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '404']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 435 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 186], [1146, 186], [1146, 244], [164, 244]], '7) If a data item is not selected for formatting it is transferred as is with no conversion or formatting taking place:']
[[[164, 268], [1146, 268], [1146, 350], [164, 350]], '8) For a WRITE or REWRITE statement; each selected data item is formatted as necessary in temporaryarea for outputin external media format Output forma tting does notappear in the record area. Signs are presented as SIGN IS LEADING SEPARATE_']
[[[164, 373], [1146, 373], [1146, 428], [164, 428]], '9) For a READ statement; each selected data item is formatted as necessary to the appropriate internal representation: The record area contains the resultant logical record in internal representation.']
[[[164, 452], [1146, 452], [1146, 535], [164, 535]], '10) The implementor shall specify the representation produced as a result of processing the FORMAT clause, including any control information that may be necessary for presentation or printing: This representation may be the same as internal representation']
[[[161, 553], [1147, 553], [1147, 638], [161, 638]], '11) Data items selected for formatting are restored to the same internal representation when read with the same FORMAT clause on the same implementation, except for exclusions specified by the implementor:']
[[[164, 664], [1146, 664], [1146, 718], [164, 718]], '12) Ifthe associated file connector is an external file connector,all file description entries in the run unit that are associated with that file connector shall specify the same FORMAT clause:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1146, 1552], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '405']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 436 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 189], [392, 189], [392, 216], [129, 216]], '13.18.25 FROM clause']
[[[130, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.25.1 General']
[[[128, 294], [1084, 294], [1084, 344], [128, 344]], 'The FROM clause specifies the source of data for an ACCEPT screen statement and a DISPLAY screen statement']
[[[129, 373], [446, 373], [446, 400], [129, 400]], '13.18.25.2 General format']
[[[132, 446], [334, 446], [334, 500], [132, 500]], 'identifier-1 FROM literal-1']
[[[130, 538], [412, 538], [412, 568], [130, 568]], '13.18.25.3 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 588], [1006, 588], [1006, 621], [130, 621]], '1) Identifier-1 shall be defined in the file, working-storage; local-storage, or linkage section_']
[[[130, 639], [1112, 639], [1112, 724], [130, 724]], '2) The category of identifier-1 and literal-1 shall be a permissible category as a sending operand in a MOVE statement where the receiving operand has the same PICTURE clause as the subject of the entry.']
[[[130, 746], [1112, 746], [1112, 828], [130, 828]], '3) Ifthe subject ofthis entry is subject to an OCCURS clause, identifier-1 shall be specified without the subscripting normally required. Additional requirements are specified in 13.18.38, OCCURS clause, Syntax rule 13.']
[[[130, 849], [697, 849], [697, 886], [130, 886]], '4) Identifier-1 shall not reference a variable-length group:']
[[[130, 908], [562, 908], [562, 936], [130, 936]], '5) Literal-1 shall not be a zero-length literal.']
[[[130, 960], [414, 960], [414, 986], [130, 986]], '13.18.25.4 General rule']
[[[130, 1011], [630, 1011], [630, 1041], [130, 1041]], '1) The subject of the entry is an output screen item_']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '406']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 437 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[163, 189], [416, 189], [416, 216], [163, 216]], '13.18.26 FULL clause']
[[[164, 242], [396, 242], [396, 268], [164, 268]], '13.18.26.1 General']
[[[164, 292], [1142, 292], [1142, 348], [164, 348]], 'The FULL clause specifies that the operator shall either leave the screen item completely empty or fill it entirely with data:']
[[[163, 373], [478, 373], [478, 400], [163, 400]], '13.18.26.2 General format']
[[[166, 432], [222, 432], [222, 456], [166, 456]], 'FULL']
[[[164, 492], [460, 492], [460, 518], [164, 518]], '13.18.26.3 General rules']
[[[164, 542], [1146, 542], [1146, 600], [164, 600]], '1) Ifa FULL clause is specified at the group level, it applies to each elementary input screen item in that group that does not have the JUSTIFIED clause specified:']
[[[164, 622], [1146, 622], [1146, 702], [164, 702]], '2) The FULL clause is effective during the execution of any ACCEPT screen statement that causes the screen item to be accepted, provided the cursor enters the screen item at some time during the execution ofthe ACCEPT statement:']
[[[164, 726], [1144, 726], [1144, 784], [164, 784]], '3) The effect of the FULL clause is to reject the normal termination key unless that clause is satisfied. To satisfy the clause for a screen item:']
[[[200, 805], [1146, 805], [1146, 862], [200, 862]], 'a) Ifit is alphanumeric Or alphanumeric-edited, either the entire item shall contain spaces; or both the first and last character positions shall contain non-space characters;']
[[[200, 888], [1144, 888], [1144, 943], [200, 943]], 'b) Ifitis national or national-edited, either the entire item shall contain spaces, or both the first and last character positions shall contain non-space characters;']
[[[236, 965], [1146, 965], [1146, 1023], [236, 1023]], 'Ifitis numeric or numeric-edited, either the value shall be zero, or there shall be no digit position in which zero suppression has taken effect;']
[[[202, 1044], [920, 1044], [920, 1072], [202, 1072]], "d) Ifit is boolean, the entire item shall contain boolean '0' or '1 characters_"]
[[[164, 1096], [1146, 1096], [1146, 1152], [164, 1152]], '4) For fields that are both input and output; the FULL clause may be satisfied by the contents of the literal or identifier referenced in the FROM or USING clause, as well as operator keyed data:']
[[[164, 1175], [1146, 1175], [1146, 1228], [164, 1228]], '5) The FULL clause shall not be effective if a function key is used to terminate the execution of the ACCEPT statement']
[[[199, 1252], [1146, 1252], [1146, 1311], [199, 1311]], 'The specification ofthe FULL and REQUIRED clauses together requires thatthe field be entirely filled before the normal termination key has any effect;']
[[[166, 1335], [790, 1335], [790, 1364], [166, 1364]], '7) The FULL clause is ignored for a field that is not an input field.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1105, 1553], [1143, 1553], [1143, 1573], [1105, 1573]], '407']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 438 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 189], [416, 189], [416, 216], [129, 216]], '13.18.27 GLOBAL clause']
[[[130, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.27.1 General']
[[[127, 292], [1108, 292], [1108, 372], [127, 372]], 'The GLOBAL clause specifies that a constant-name,a data-name,a file-name,a report-name, 0r a screen-name is a global name: A global nameisavailable to every program contained within the program that declares it:']
[[[130, 400], [446, 400], [446, 426], [130, 426]], '13.18.27.2 General format']
[[[130, 458], [240, 458], [240, 482], [130, 482]], 'IS GLOBAL']
[[[130, 518], [412, 518], [412, 548], [130, 548]], '13.18.273 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 568], [798, 568], [798, 600], [130, 600]], '1) The GLOBAL clause may be specified only in the following entries:']
[[[166, 623], [367, 623], [367, 653], [166, 653]], 'a) A constant entry:']
[[[166, 674], [1112, 674], [1112, 733], [166, 733]], 'b) A data description entry whose level-number is 1 that is specified in the file, working-storage, local-storage, or linkage section:']
[[[166, 758], [190, 758], [190, 784], [166, 784]], 'c)']
[[[220, 753], [700, 753], [700, 785], [220, 785]], 'screen-description entry whose level-number is 1.']
[[[166, 808], [428, 808], [428, 836], [166, 836]], 'd) A file description entry']
[[[167, 859], [458, 859], [458, 889], [167, 889]], 'e) A report description entry_']
[[[130, 911], [1112, 911], [1112, 968], [130, 968]], '2) If the SAME RECORD AREA clause is specified for several files, the record description entries or the file description entries for these files shall not include the GLOBAL clause:']
[[[130, 990], [1112, 990], [1112, 1096], [130, 1096]], '3) If the GLOBAL clause is not specified in the file description entry of a containing program; the file shall not be referenced directly or indirectly by any input-output statements in any contained program, unless they are defined as external files in each of the source elements in which they are referenced:']
[[[130, 1122], [1110, 1122], [1110, 1176], [130, 1176]], '4) The GLOBAL clause shall not be specified in a factory definition, an instance definition, Or a method definition.']
[[[130, 1204], [424, 1204], [424, 1230], [130, 1230]], '13.18.27.4 General rules']
[[[130, 1255], [1112, 1255], [1112, 1338], [130, 1338]], '1) A constant-name, data-name, file-name, report-name, or screen-name described using a GLOBAL clause is global name. All   data-names subordinate to global name are global names.  AII condition-names associated with a global name are global names_']
[[[130, 1358], [1113, 1358], [1113, 1417], [130, 1417]], '2) A statement in a program contained directly or indirectly within a program that describes a global name may reference that name without describing it again: (See 8.4.6,Scope of names:)']
[[[130, 1439], [1112, 1439], [1112, 1496], [130, 1496]], '3) Ifthe GLOBAL clause is used in a data description entry that contains the REDEFINES clause,it is only the subject of that REDEFINES clause that possesses the global attribute.']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '408']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 439 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[163, 189], [554, 189], [554, 216], [163, 216]], '13.18.28 GROUP INDICATE clause']
[[[164, 242], [396, 242], [396, 268], [164, 268]], '13.18.28.1 General']
[[[164, 294], [1142, 294], [1142, 372], [164, 372]], 'The GROUP INDICATE clause specifies that the associated printable item is printed only on the first occurrence ofits report group after execution ofan INITIATE statement; or after a control break or page advance:']
[[[164, 400], [478, 400], [478, 426], [164, 426]], '13.18.28.2 General format']
[[[166, 458], [344, 458], [344, 482], [166, 482]], 'GROUP INDICATE']
[[[164, 518], [434, 518], [434, 548], [164, 548]], '13.18.28.3 Syntax rule']
[[[164, 570], [1146, 570], [1146, 627], [164, 627]], '1) The GROUP INDICATE clause may be specified only within a detail report group description, in an elementary entry that also contains a COLUMN clause anda SOURCE or VALUE clause.']
[[[164, 650], [448, 650], [448, 676], [164, 676]], '13.18.28.4 General rule']
[[[164, 702], [1146, 702], [1146, 784], [164, 784]], '1) The GROUP INDICATE clause has the same effect as a PRESENT WHEN clause where the associated condition is true only on the first occasion that a GENERATE is issued for the current detail group after any of the following events. (See 13.18.41,PRESENT WHEN clause)']
[[[200, 807], [622, 807], [622, 836], [200, 836]], 'a) Execution ofan INITIATE for the report']
[[[200, 860], [992, 860], [992, 890], [200, 890]], 'b) A page advance, if the report is divided into pages: (See 13.18.39,PAGE clause:)']
[[[201, 911], [922, 911], [922, 943], [201, 943]], 'C) Any control break, if the report description contains a CONTROL clause:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1105, 1553], [1145, 1553], [1145, 1573], [1105, 1573]], '409']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 440 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 189], [488, 189], [488, 216], [129, 216]], '13.18.29 GROUP-USAGE clause']
[[[130, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.29.1 General']
[[[130, 292], [1082, 292], [1082, 372], [130, 372]], 'A GROUP-USAGE clause with a BIT phrase specifies that the group item defined by the subject ofthe entry is to be treated as an elementary item of usage bit and category boolean, unless specified otherwise']
[[[127, 398], [1110, 398], [1110, 480], [127, 480]], 'A GROUP-USAGE clause with a NATIONAL phrase specifies that the group item defined by the subject of the entryis to be treated asan elementary item ofusage nationaland category national, unless otherwise specified.']
[[[130, 506], [446, 506], [446, 532], [130, 532]], '13.18.29.2 General format']
