[[[130, 1372], [1112, 1372], [1112, 1431], [130, 1431]], '14) Format 4 defines dynamic-capacity table. Other rules and restrictions concerning dynamic- capacity tables are specified in 8.5.1.9, Dynamic-capacity tables']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '436']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 467 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 189], [1146, 189], [1146, 245], [164, 245]], '15) Data-name-3 defines a numeric data item that contains the current capacity of the associated table: Data-name-3 shall not be referenced as a receiving operand:']
[[[164, 268], [1146, 268], [1146, 298], [164, 298]], '16) Integer-4 is the minimum capacity of the table If integer-4 is absent,a value ofzero is assumed for it']
[[[164, 319], [642, 319], [642, 351], [164, 351]], '17) Integer-5 is the expected capacity ofthe table:']
[[[164, 372], [1146, 372], [1146, 428], [164, 428]], '18) If INITIALIZED is specified, any unreferenced locations of each new element added to the table are implicitly initialized, as defined in 8.5.1.9.5, Implicit initialization.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1105, 1553], [1143, 1553], [1143, 1573], [1105, 1573]], '437']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[129, 189], [384, 189], [384, 216], [129, 216]], '13.18.39 PAGE clause']
[[[130, 242], [362, 242], [362, 268], [130, 268]], '13.18.39.1 General']
[[[128, 294], [1018, 294], [1018, 350], [128, 350]], 'The PAGE clause defines the maximum length and width ofa page ofa report and the vertical subdivisions within which its report groups shall be printed.']
[[[129, 373], [446, 373], [446, 400], [129, 400]], '13.18.39.2 General format']
[[[420, 462], [512, 462], [512, 490], [420, 490]], 'integer-1']
[[[156, 516], [212, 516], [212, 540], [156, 540]], 'PAGE']
[[[246, 508], [366, 508], [366, 562], [246, 562]], 'LIMIT  IS LIMITS ARE']
[[[578, 524], [642, 524], [642, 584], [578, 584]], 'LINE LINES']
[[[834, 524], [938, 524], [938, 584], [834, 584]], 'COLS COLUMNS']
[[[452, 542], [546, 542], [546, 570], [452, 570]], 'integer-1']
[[[710, 542], [804, 542], [804, 570], [710, 570]], 'integer-2']
[[[540, 638], [618, 638], [618, 698], [540, 698]], 'DETAIL DE']
[[[190, 650], [508, 650], [508, 678], [190, 678]], 'HEADING IS integer-3 ] FIRST']
[[[644, 654], [762, 654], [762, 682], [644, 682]], 'IS integer-4']
[[[284, 740], [634, 740], [634, 800], [284, 800]], 'CONTROL HEADING IS integer-5 CH']
[[[198, 756], [254, 756], [254, 782], [198, 782]], 'LAST']
[[[286, 842], [364, 842], [364, 866], [286, 866]], 'DETAIL']
[[[200, 860], [254, 860], [254, 884], [200, 884]], 'LAST']
[[[392, 860], [514, 860], [514, 888], [392, 888]], 'IS integer-6']
[[[554, 856], [778, 856], [778, 884], [554, 884]], 'FOOTING IS integer-7 ]']
[[[286, 880], [320, 880], [320, 908], [286, 908]], 'DE']
[[[130, 954], [412, 954], [412, 982], [130, 982]], '13.18.393 Syntax rules']
[[[130, 1004], [1112, 1004], [1112, 1060], [130, 1060]], '1) FIRST DE is synonymous with FIRST  DETAIL, LAST CH is synonymous with LAST CONTROL HEADING, and LAST DE is synonymous with LAST DETAIL.']
[[[130, 1082], [686, 1082], [686, 1114], [130, 1114]], '2) Either integer-1 or integer-2 or both shall be specified.']
[[[130, 1136], [1112, 1136], [1112, 1193], [130, 1193]], '3) The HEADING, FIRST DETAIL, LAST CONTROL HEADING, LAST DETAIL, or FOOTING phrase may be specified only if integer-1 is specified.']
[[[130, 1215], [1112, 1215], [1112, 1270], [130, 1270]], '4) The HEADING , FIRST DETAIL, LAST CONTROL HEADING, LAST DETAIL, and FOOTING phrases may be written in any order:']
[[[130, 1296], [472, 1296], [472, 1324], [130, 1324]], '5) Integer-1 shall not exceed 9999']
[[[130, 1347], [1113, 1347], [1113, 1405], [130, 1405]], '6) Integer-3, integer-4, integer-5, integer-6, integer-7, and integer-1 shall be greater than zero. Wherever specified, they shall be in ascending order, with equality allowed:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '438']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 469 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 190], [460, 190], [460, 216], [164, 216]], '13.1839.4 General rules']
[[[164, 240], [1148, 240], [1148, 376], [164, 376]], '1) The printed page is regarded as a vertical arrangement ofhorizontal lines, each line being capable of displaying the content of one  report  line: On each page these lines are  numbered from (representing the first line on the page) in steps of 1. The PAGE clause and its phrases subdivide the printed page into vertical regions Depending on its type, each report group shall be confined within one or more of these regions']
[[[164, 398], [1142, 398], [1142, 431], [164, 431]], '2) The integers specified in the PAGE clause establish the vertical regions ofthe report page as follows_']
[[[200, 452], [1146, 452], [1146, 534], [200, 534]], 'a) Integer-1 is the page limit: It defines the last line position on each page of the report: No report line will appear below this position on any page: If integer-1 is not specified, the report consists ofa single page of indefinite length.']
[[[200, 556], [1146, 556], [1146, 612], [200, 612]], 'b) Integer-2 is the page width It defines the maximum number of print columns that may be accommodated in any line of the report:']
[[[235, 633], [1147, 633], [1147, 693], [235, 693]], 'Integer-3 is the HEADING integer: It defines the first line position on which a report heading or page heading may be printed: No report line will appear higher than this position on the page']
[[[202, 714], [1146, 714], [1146, 794], [202, 794]], 'd) Integer-4 is the FIRST DETAIL integer: It defines the first line position on which any line ofa body group may be printed: Any report heading (when not on a page by itself) or page heading shall be defined so that it terminates before this line.']
[[[234, 820], [1146, 820], [1146, 879], [234, 879]], 'Integer-5 is the LAST CONTROL HEADING integer: It defines the last line position on which any line ofa control heading may be printed.']
[[[201, 899], [1146, 899], [1146, 956], [201, 956]], '0) Integer-6 is the LAST DETAIL integer. It defines the last line position on which any line ofa detail may be printed.']
[[[200, 977], [1146, 977], [1146, 1064], [200, 1064]], 'g) Integer-7 is the FOOTING integer. It defines the last line position on which any line ofa control footing may be printed. Any page footing orreport footing (when not on a page by itself) shall be defined so that it begins after this line:']
[[[164, 1082], [1146, 1082], [1146, 1142], [164, 1142]], '3)   Ifinteger-1 is specified and any ofthe following phrases is omitted,a default value for the associated integer is supplied as follows:']
[[[201, 1164], [638, 1164], [638, 1192], [201, 1192]], 'a) If HEADING is omitted, integer-3 will be 1_']
[[[200, 1216], [838, 1216], [838, 1246], [200, 1246]], 'b) If FIRST DETAIL is omitted, integer-4 will be equal to integer-3']
[[[233, 1269], [1147, 1269], [1147, 1350], [233, 1350]], 'If LAST CONTROL HEADING is omitted, integer-5 will be equal to integer-6,if LAST DETAIL is specified,or equal to integer-7,if FOOTING is specified,or otherwise equal to the page limit given by integer-1 .']
[[[200, 1373], [1146, 1373], [1146, 1432], [200, 1432]], 'd) If LAST DETAIL is omitted, integer-6 will be equal to integer-7, if FOOTING is specified, or otherwise equal to the page limit given by integer-1.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '439']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 470 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[167, 188], [1112, 188], [1112, 245], [167, 245]], 'e) If FOOTING is omitted, integer-7 will be equal to integer-6, if LAST DETAIL is specified, or otherwise equal to the page limit given by integer-1.']
[[[130, 264], [1112, 264], [1112, 351], [130, 351]], '4) A report heading or report footing on page by itself may occupy any region of the page from HEADING integer-3 through the page limit integer-1 or,ifnot on a page by itself;,is constrained in the same way as a page heading or page footing respectively:']
[[[130, 373], [810, 373], [810, 403], [130, 403]], '5)   If integer-2 is omitted,a value of 999 is assumed for the page width:']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '440']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 471 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 189], [458, 189], [458, 216], [164, 216]], '13.18.40 PICTURE clause']
[[[164, 242], [395, 242], [395, 268], [164, 268]], '13.18.40.1 General']
[[[163, 292], [1140, 292], [1140, 350], [163, 350]], 'The PICTURE clause describes the General characteristics, editing requirements, and format validation profile ofan elementary item:']
[[[163, 373], [490, 373], [490, 400], [163, 400]], '13.18.40.2 General formats']
[[[164, 428], [334, 428], [334, 454], [164, 454]], 'Format 1 (basic):']
[[[768, 520], [872, 520], [872, 544], [768, 544]], 'IS literal-1']
[[[190, 538], [282, 538], [282, 602], [190, 602]], 'PICTURE PIC']
[[[310, 554], [738, 554], [738, 582], [310, 582]], 'IS character-string-1 EDITING character-1']
[[[848, 560], [1062, 560], [1062, 620], [848, 620]], 'NEGATIVE IS literal-2 POSITIVE IS literal-3']
[[[768, 576], [814, 576], [814, 600], [768, 600]], 'FOR']
[[[164, 682], [340, 682], [340, 710], [164, 710]], 'Format 2 (locale):']
[[[189, 749], [281, 749], [281, 773], [189, 773]], 'PICTURE']
[[[310, 765], [960, 765], [960, 795], [310, 795]], 'IS character-string-1 LOCALE [ IS locale-name-1 ] SIZE IS integer-1']
[[[190, 786], [228, 786], [228, 810], [190, 810]], 'PIC']
[[[164, 850], [446, 850], [446, 880], [164, 880]], '13.18.40.3 Syntax rules']
[[[166, 904], [308, 904], [308, 928], [166, 928]], 'ALL FORMATS']
[[[164, 953], [832, 953], [832, 985], [164, 985]], '1) The PICTURE clause may be specified only at the elementary level.']
[[[164, 1005], [1140, 1005], [1140, 1037], [164, 1037]], '2) Character-string-1 shall consist ofan allowable combination of characters used as picture symbols:']
[[[200, 1058], [1146, 1058], [1146, 1112], [200, 1112]], 'The allowable combinations of symbols for a PICTURE clauseare specified in 13.18.40.6,Precedence rules']
[[[198, 1136], [1146, 1136], [1146, 1213], [198, 1213]], "NOTE 1 The currency symbol can be selected from the set of allowable characters from the computer's compile-time coded character set: All picture symbols other than the currency symbol are from the COBOL character repertoire:"]
[[[164, 1237], [1146, 1237], [1146, 1368], [164, 1368]], '3) The equivalence between uppercase and lowercase letters appearing as occurrences of a currency symbol in character-string-1 is as specified in 12.3.7, SPECIAL-NAMES paragraph, Syntax rule 20_ The equivalence between uppercase and lowercase letters appearing as picture symbols other than currency symbols in character-string-1 is as specified in 8.1.3,COBOL character repertoire, General rule 3.']
[[[164, 1392], [890, 1392], [890, 1424], [164, 1424]], '4) The maximum number of characters allowed in character-string-1 is 63.']
[[[164, 1447], [540, 1447], [540, 1474], [164, 1474]], '5) PIC is an abbreviation for PICTURE.']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1105, 1553], [1143, 1553], [1143, 1573], [1105, 1573]], '441']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 472 ---

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[130, 187], [1112, 187], [1112, 298], [130, 298]], '6) An unsigned nonzero integer that is enclosed in parentheses indicates the number of consecutive occurrences of the symbol that immediately precedes the left parenthesis. The integer may be specified by a constant-name, in which case the length ofthe integer, not the length of the constant: name, is counted toward the maximum number of characters in character-string-1.']
[[[130, 319], [1112, 319], [1112, 403], [130, 403]], '7) Ifthe symbol or the symbol is the last symbol of character-string-1,the PICTURE clause shall be the last clause of the data  description entry and shall be followed immediately (without an intervening separator space) by the separator period.']
[[[130, 428], [236, 428], [236, 452], [130, 452]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[130, 478], [1112, 478], [1112, 558], [130, 558]], '8) Character-1 shall be any basic letter in the COBOL character set except those specified in CURRENCY-SIGN clause or a basic letter character A, B,C, D, E, N, P,R, S, V,X, Z or their lowercase equivalents']
[[[130, 582], [1112, 582], [1112, 688], [130, 688]], '9) If USAGE IS NATIONAL is specified for the subject of the entry or if character-string-1 contains the symbol \'N" literal-1,literal-2,and literal-3 shall be national literals. Otherwise, literall,literal-2,and literal-3 shall be alphanumeric literals. The total number of characters in literal-1, literal-2,0r literal- 3 shall not exceed 50.']
[[[130, 714], [1112, 714], [1112, 772], [130, 772]], '10) If the EDITING phrase is specified, the character identified by character-1 shall appear at least once in character-string-1.']
[[[130, 793], [1112, 793], [1112, 846], [130, 846]], '11) If more than one EDITING phrase is specified in one PICTURE clause, no two instances of character- 1 shall be the same character_']
[[[130, 872], [1112, 872], [1112, 956], [130, 956]], '12) If literal-1 is specified, character-1 is a fixed editing sign control symbol: If the FOR phrase is specified, character-1 is an extended editing sign control symbol, character-1 is limited to numeric or numeric-edited data items, and the following rules apply:']
[[[167, 977], [918, 977], [918, 1008], [167, 1008]], 'a) Literal-2 and literal-3 shall occupy the same number of character positions:']
[[[166, 1031], [1108, 1031], [1108, 1084], [166, 1084]], "b) Character-string-1 shall contain character-1and only the symbols '9' , the currency symbol, 'P', 'V', or 'Z' ."]
[[[167, 1109], [1114, 1109], [1114, 1216], [167, 1216]], 'c) If only POSITIVE is specified, the default character for the unspecified phrase is the space character repeated for the number of characters in literal-2. If only NEGATIVE is specified, the default character for the unspecified phrase is the space character repeated the number of characters in literal-3.']
[[[166, 1240], [1110, 1240], [1110, 1299], [166, 1299]], 'a) No editing sign control symbols may be specified in character-string-1. Extended editing sign control symbols shall not be specified for a floating-point edited item:']
[[[164, 1320], [734, 1320], [734, 1352], [164, 1352]], 'If the FOR phrase is not specified, the following rules apply:']
[[[166, 1376], [516, 1376], [516, 1404], [166, 1404]], 'a) Character-string-1 shall contain:']
[[[230, 1425], [879, 1425], [879, 1457], [230, 1457]], 'at least on one ofthe symbols from the set \'A, \'N\', \'X, "Z\',\'1\',\'9\' , or']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '442']
[[[203, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1579], [203, 1579]], 'OISO /IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[263, 187], [1000, 187], [1000, 245], [263, 245]], "at least two occurrences of one of the symbols from the set character-1, 'x', currency symbol."]
[[[1066, 190], [1146, 190], [1146, 214], [1066, 214]], 'and the']
[[[200, 266], [1147, 266], [1147, 299], [200, 299]], "b) Each of the symbols from the set 'CR', 'DB' 'E','S, 'V' may appear only once in character-string-"]
[[[235, 343], [1146, 343], [1146, 396], [235, 396]], "NOTE 2 The symbols 'CR' and 'DB' although consisting of two characters are each considered to be a symbol by itself:"]
[[[164, 418], [1146, 418], [1146, 503], [164, 503]], '13) When the DECIMAL-POINT IS COMMA clause is specified, the symbol comma is the decimal separator and the symbol period is the grouping separator: The rules for the symbol period apply to the symbol comma,and the rules for the symbol comma apply to the symbol period:']
[[[161, 523], [1146, 523], [1146, 580], [161, 580]], '14) For data items of category numeric, and for fixed-point data items of category numeric-edited, the number of digit positions described by character-string-1 shall range from 1 through 31.']
[[[164, 604], [1146, 604], [1146, 661], [164, 661]], '15) For floating-point data items of category numeric-edited, the number of digit positions in the significand shall range from through 36.']
[[[164, 683], [1148, 683], [1148, 741], [164, 741]], "16) The symbol may appear only as a continuous string of 'P's in the leftmost or rightmost digit positions in character ~string"]
[[[164, 760], [936, 760], [936, 792], [164, 792]], "17) The symbol 'P' and the symbol are mutually exclusive in character-string-1"]
[[[164, 813], [882, 813], [882, 845], [164, 845]], "18) The symbol 'S' , if present; shall be the first symbol in character-string-l"]
[[[164, 865], [1146, 865], [1146, 925], [164, 925]], "19) When the symbol 'V' and one or more symbols 'P'are used in character-string-1,the symbol 'V' shall either immediately precede the first symbol 'P' or immediately follow the last symbol 'P'."]
[[[164, 944], [936, 944], [936, 977], [164, 977]], "20) The symbol 'V' and the symbol are mutually exclusive in character-string-1"]
[[[161, 997], [940, 997], [940, 1029], [161, 1029]], '21) " The symbol \'Z\' and the symbol are mutually exclusive in character-string-1.']
[[[164, 1050], [1146, 1050], [1146, 1110], [164, 1110]], "22) Neither the symbol S' nor the symbol shall be specified in character-string-1 when the BLANK WHEN ZERO clause is  specified for the subject of the entry."]
[[[164, 1128], [1146, 1128], [1146, 1210], [164, 1210]], "23) The editing sign control symbols 'CR' , and 'DB are mutually exclusive in character-string-1 with the exception of a numeric-edited data item for a floating-point edited result as described in General rule 13b."]
[[[198, 1232], [1148, 1232], [1148, 1287], [198, 1287]], 'NOTE 3 For a floating-point edited result;the significand part ofthe character-string may contain a symbol and the exponent part always contains a symbol']
[[[161, 1307], [1147, 1307], [1147, 1392], [161, 1392]], '24) For fixed insertion with editing sign control symbols, only one currency symbol and only one editing sign control symbol may be used in character-string-1. For extended editing sign control symbols either one or two extended editing sign control symbols may be used in character-string-1']
[[[164, 1413], [1146, 1413], [1146, 1471], [164, 1471]], '25) The symbol or the symbol when used, shall be either the leftmost or the rightmost symbol in character-string-1. When extended editing sign control symbols are used and two are specified, the']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1552], [1144, 1552], [1144, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '443']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[163, 187], [1112, 187], [1112, 245], [163, 245]], 'first occurrence ofthe EDITING phrase shall be for the leftmost symbol in character-string-1 and the second occurrence shall be for the rightmost symbol in character-string-1']
[[[130, 266], [1112, 266], [1112, 430], [130, 430]], "26) For fixed editing sign control, the currency symbol, when used, shall be either the leftmost symbol in character-string-1, optionally preceded by one of the symbols or or the rightmost symbol in character-string-1, optionally followed by one of the symbols 'CR' , or DB' For extended editing sign control,the currency symbol when used shall be either the leftmost symbol in character- string-1, optionally preceded by character-1, or the rightmost  symbol in character-string-1 optionally followed by character-1."]
[[[166, 450], [818, 450], [818, 622], [166, 622]], "NOTE 4 This means that the following are valid picture character-strings: $999+ +9995' +5999' '999S+' +8s99' '$s99+'."]
[[[127, 647], [866, 647], [866, 679], [127, 679]], '27) No more than one of the following may be specified in character-string-l:']
[[[242, 701], [634, 701], [634, 837], [242, 837]], "string of two or more symbols string of two or more symbols string oftwo or more currency symbols; string of one or more symbols string of one or more symbols 'Z'_"]
[[[165, 858], [1112, 858], [1112, 910], [165, 910]], 'NOTE This means, for example, that the picture character-string +SSS is valid, but that the picture character-string +++- -SSS is invalid,']
[[[130, 930], [1112, 930], [1112, 989], [130, 989]], '28) For floating insertion, when the currency symbol is used as the floating insertion symbol, all occurrences ofthe currency symbol within character-string-1 shall be equivalent characters_']
[[[130, 1010], [1112, 1010], [1112, 1068], [130, 1068]], '29) For floating insertion,at least one insertion symbol shall be specified to the left of the decimal point position:']
[[[130, 1088], [1112, 1088], [1112, 1147], [130, 1147]], "30) The symbols 'A'and 'X' shall not be specified in character-string-1 when a USAGE NATIONAL clause is specified for the subject of the entry"]
[[[127, 1168], [1112, 1168], [1112, 1224], [127, 1224]], "31) The symbol 'S' shall not be specified in character-string-1 when the NO SIGN phrase of the USAGE Clause is specified for the subject of the entry_"]
[[[130, 1250], [238, 1250], [238, 1274], [130, 1274]], 'FORMAT 2']
[[[130, 1299], [1112, 1299], [1112, 1380], [130, 1380]], '32)A format 2 PICTURE clause shall not be specified in a data item described with the CONSTANT RECORD clause, or in any data item subordinate to data item described with the CONSTANT RECORD clause:']
[[[130, 1404], [812, 1404], [812, 1437], [130, 1437]], "33) Character-string-1 shall contain at least one of the symbols 'Z' or '9'"]
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '444']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']

[[[164, 187], [528, 187], [528, 244], [164, 244]], '34) Each of the symbols from the set string-13']
[[[588, 187], [1146, 187], [1146, 219], [588, 219]], 'the currency symbol may appear only once in character-']
[[[164, 268], [1082, 268], [1082, 298], [164, 298]], '35) The number of digit positions described by character-string-1 shall range from 1 through 31.']
[[[164, 319], [577, 319], [577, 376], [164, 376]], '36) The currency symbol and the symbol position.']
[[[610, 319], [1148, 319], [1148, 351], [610, 351]], 'may be specified only to the left of the decimal point']
[[[164, 398], [1050, 398], [1050, 431], [164, 431]], '37) Locale-name-1 shall be specified in the LOCALE clause in the SPECIAL-NAMES paragraph.']
[[[164, 454], [460, 454], [460, 480], [164, 480]], '13.18.40.4 General rules']
[[[166, 506], [308, 506], [308, 530], [166, 530]], 'ALL FORMATS']
[[[164, 557], [1146, 557], [1146, 640], [164, 640]], '1) When the usage ofthe subject ofthe entry is national, each symbol representinga character position defines a national character position. When the usage of the subject of the entry is display, each symbol representing a character position defines an alphanumeric character position.']
[[[164, 664], [1146, 664], [1146, 770], [164, 770]], "2) The value of insertion and replacement characters in a resultant edited item is the value of those characters in the computer's runtime coded character set: When the usage of the item being edited is national, the value is the national character  representation; otherwise, the value the alphanumeric character representation_"]
[[[164, 796], [270, 796], [270, 820], [164, 820]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[164, 846], [1146, 846], [1146, 878], [164, 878]], '3) A PICTURE clause defines the subject of the entry to fall into one ofthe following categories of data:']
[[[263, 898], [468, 898], [468, 1109], [263, 1109]], 'alphabetic alphanumeric alphanumeric-edited boolean national national-edited numeric numeric-edited']
[[[200, 1135], [1146, 1135], [1146, 1190], [200, 1190]], 'A BLANK WHEN ZERO clause specified for the subject of the entry defines the item as numeric- edited.']
[[[164, 1216], [1146, 1216], [1146, 1298], [164, 1298]], '4) The size in boolean positions or character positions ofan elementary data item that has been defined with a PICTURE clause is determined by the number of symbols in character-string-1 that represent either boolean positions or character positions.']
[[[164, 1320], [1146, 1320], [1146, 1377], [164, 1377]], "5) To define an item as alphabetic, character-string-1 shall contain only one or more occurrences of the symbol 'A'"]
[[[200, 1398], [1146, 1398], [1146, 1454], [200, 1454]], "To define an item as alphanumeric, character-string-1 shall contain a combination of symbols from the set 'A', 'X', and '9', that includes"]
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1104, 1550], [1146, 1550], [1146, 1576], [1104, 1576]], '445']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[230, 187], [646, 187], [646, 245], [230, 245]], "at least one symbol 'X', or at least two different symbols from this set"]
[[[130, 267], [874, 267], [874, 298], [130, 298]], '7) To define an item as alphanumeric-edited, character-string-1 shall include']
[[[230, 319], [988, 319], [988, 377], [230, 377]], "at least one symbol A' or one symbol 'X', and at least one instance of character-1 or one of the symbols from the set 'B', '0', ' /'_"]
[[[130, 398], [1112, 398], [1112, 455], [130, 455]], "8) To define an item as boolean, character-string-1 shall contain only one or more occurrences of the symbol '1'."]
[[[130, 479], [1112, 479], [1112, 535], [130, 535]], "9) To define an item as national, character-string-1 shall contain only one or more occurrences of the symbol 'N'."]
[[[130, 556], [820, 556], [820, 588], [130, 588]], '10) To define an item as national-edited, character-string-1 shall include']
[[[228, 610], [992, 610], [992, 667], [228, 667]], "at least one symbol 'N', and atleast one instance of character-1 or one of the symbols from the set 'B', '0', ' /'."]
[[[130, 688], [738, 688], [738, 719], [130, 719]], '11) To define an item as fixed-point numeric, character-string-1']
[[[230, 742], [850, 742], [850, 798], [230, 798]], "shall include at least one symbol '9', and may contain a combination of symbols from the set 'P' 'S',and 'V'"]
[[[130, 820], [1112, 820], [1112, 900], [130, 900]], '12) In the following general rules, for a PICTURE clause with the FOR phrase all references to apply to POSITIVE is literal-2, and all references to apply to NEGATIVE IS literal-3 unless otherwise indicated,']
[[[130, 924], [962, 924], [962, 957], [130, 957]], '13) To define an item as numeric-edited, one ofthe following options shall be specified:']
[[[167, 978], [936, 978], [936, 1009], [167, 1009]], 'a) To define a fixed-point numeric-edited item, character-string-1 shall include:']
[[[230, 1032], [478, 1032], [478, 1061], [230, 1061]], "at least one symbol 'Z'; or"]
[[[230, 1083], [475, 1083], [475, 1115], [230, 1115]], 'at least one symbol or']
[[[230, 1135], [638, 1135], [638, 1167], [230, 1167]], 'at least two identical symbols from the set']
[[[699, 1137], [898, 1137], [898, 1166], [699, 1166]], 'currency symbol; or']
[[[230, 1190], [1112, 1190], [1112, 1246], [230, 1246]], "at least one symbol '9' and at least one instance of character-1 or at least one of the symbols from the set 'B', 'CR'_ 'DB'_ 0' , the currency symbol"]
[[[166, 1266], [1112, 1266], [1112, 1350], [166, 1350]], "b) To define a floating-point numeric-edited item, characters-string-1 shall consist of two parts, separated without any spaces, by the symbol 'E'. The first part represents the significand; the second part represents the exponent:"]
[[[200, 1372], [1112, 1372], [1112, 1457], [200, 1457]], 'The significand shall be a valid character-string for either a numeric item or a numeric-edited item for fixed-point result: Neither floating insertion editing nor zero suppression with replacement shall be specified for the significand_']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '446']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 477 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[236, 190], [1008, 190], [1008, 218], [236, 218]], "The exponent shall be '+9', '+99', '+999', '+9999',or '+9(n)' where n = 1,2,3,or 4"]
[[[164, 240], [866, 240], [866, 272], [164, 272]], '14) The meaning of the symbols used in character-string-1 are as follows:']
[[[199, 293], [1146, 293], [1146, 352], [199, 352]], "Each symbol 'A represents a character position that shall contain any character from the computer's alphanumeric character set: Each symbol A' is counted in the size of the item;"]
[[[200, 371], [976, 371], [976, 406], [200, 406]], 'The characters represented may be graphic characters or non-graphic characters_']
[[[200, 422], [1144, 422], [1144, 477], [200, 477]], "NOTE 1 The symbol 'A' is equivalent to the symbol 'X' except within the execution ofa VALIDATE statement The meaning of the symbol 'A' within the VALIDATE statement is specified in General rule 15,below:"]
[[[198, 497], [1144, 497], [1144, 555], [198, 555]], "Each symbol 'B' represents a character position into which the character space will be inserted during editing: Each symbol 'B' is counted in the size of the item:"]
[[[198, 578], [1144, 578], [1144, 634], [198, 634]], "The symbol 'E' represents a character position into which the character 'E will be inserted during editing: The symbol 'E' is counted in the size ofthe item:"]
[[[200, 655], [1146, 655], [1146, 710], [200, 710]], "The symbol 'E' is used to separate the significand and the exponent of a floating-point numeric- edited item:"]
[[[200, 734], [1146, 734], [1146, 790], [200, 790]], "Each symbol 'N' represents a national character position that shall contain a character from the computer's national character set: Each symbol 'N' is counted in the size of the item"]
[[[200, 813], [1148, 813], [1148, 898], [200, 898]], "The symbol specifies the location ofan assumed decimal point when that point is not within the number that appears in the data item: The symbol 'P' is not counted in the size of the item, but each symbol P' is counted in the maximum number of digit positions"]
[[[202, 920], [798, 920], [798, 950], [202, 950]], "The symbol 'P' implies an assumed decimal point that is either"]
[[[264, 970], [1146, 970], [1146, 1082], [264, 1082]], "to the left of the string of 'P's if they indicate the leftmost digit positions in character-string- 1;or to the right of the string of 'P's if they indicate the rightmost digit positions in character- string-1."]
[[[199, 1102], [1148, 1102], [1148, 1240], [199, 1240]], "In certain operations that reference a data item whose picture character-string contains the symbol 'P', the algebraic value of the data item is used rather than the actual value of the data item: This algebraic value assumes the decimal point in the prescribed position and zero in place of each digit position specified by the symbol 'P'. The size ofthe value is the number of digit positions represented by the picture character-string:"]
[[[202, 1261], [786, 1261], [786, 1293], [202, 1293]], 'The operations that use the algebraic value are the following:']
[[[200, 1313], [740, 1313], [740, 1346], [200, 1346]], 'a) Any operation requiring a numeric sending operand:']
[[[200, 1364], [1147, 1364], [1147, 1425], [200, 1425]], "b) An elementary move operation where the sending operand is numeric and its picture character- string contains one Or more symbols 'P'_"]
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1105, 1553], [1143, 1553], [1143, 1573], [1105, 1573]], '447']
[[[126, 1622], [1146, 1622], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector. ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 478 ---

[[[128, 80], [390, 80], [390, 112], [128, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[167, 186], [1112, 186], [1112, 247], [167, 247]], "c) move operation where the sending operand is numeric-edited, its picture character-string contains one or more 'symbols 'P',and the receiving operand is numeric or numeric-edited"]
[[[166, 267], [768, 267], [768, 297], [166, 297]], 'd) A comparison operation where both operands are numeric:']
[[[166, 318], [1112, 318], [1112, 377], [166, 377]], 'In all other operations, the digit positions specified with the symbol are ignored and are not counted in the size of the operand']
[[[164, 398], [1112, 398], [1112, 508], [164, 508]], "The symbol S' indicates the presence, but neither the representation nor, necessarily, the position of an operational sign. For usages display and national, the symbol 'S' is counted in the size of the item only when the subject of the entry is described with a SIGN clause with the SEPARATE phrase For other numeric usages, the effect ofthe 'S' is described in the rules for the USAGE clause:"]
[[[165, 529], [1112, 529], [1112, 587], [165, 587]], "The symbol 'V' indicates the position ofthe assumed decimal point for alignment purposes: The symbol 'V' is not counted in the size of the item:"]
[[[166, 609], [1112, 609], [1112, 662], [166, 662]], "When the assumed decimal point position is to the right of the rightmost digit position, the symbol 'V' is redundant;"]
[[[165, 687], [1112, 687], [1112, 745], [165, 745]], "Each symbol 'X' represents a character position that shall contain any character from the computer's alphanumeric character set: Each symbol 'X' is counted in the size of the item:"]
[[[166, 768], [942, 768], [942, 800], [166, 800]], 'The characters represented may be graphic characters or non-graphic characters']
[[[166, 818], [1113, 818], [1113, 903], [166, 903]], "Each symbol 'Z' represents a lea ding numeric position that during editing will contain a numeric character in the range 0 through 9,or a character space when the content ofthat position is a leading zero. Each symbol 'Z' is counted in the size of the item:"]
[[[164, 926], [1112, 926], [1112, 983], [164, 983]], "Each symbol '0' (zero) represents a character position into which the character zero will be inserted during editing: Each symbol '0' is counted in the size of the item."]
[[[166, 1006], [1112, 1006], [1112, 1087], [166, 1087]], "Each symbol '1' represents boolean position that shall contain boolean character; Each boolean character can be represented in storage as a bit; an alphanumeric character, or a national character: Each symbol 1' is counted in the size of the item"]
[[[164, 1109], [1114, 1109], [1114, 1242], [164, 1242]], "Each symbol '9' represents a decimal digit position of the value of the item. For usages display and national, each 9' represents a character position that shall contain a numeric character in the range 0 through 9 with the possible inclusion ofan operational sign; each '9' is counted in the size of the item. For other numeric usages, the effect of the number of '9's is described in the rules for the USAGE clause:"]
[[[164, 1267], [1112, 1267], [1112, 1325], [164, 1325]], "Each symbol '/' (slant) represents a character position into which the character slant will be inserted during editing: Each symbol '/' is counted in the size of the item:"]
[[[164, 1348], [1112, 1348], [1112, 1405], [164, 1405]], 'Each symbol (comma) represents a character position into which the character comma will be inserted during editing: Each symbol is counted in the size of the item']
[[[164, 1425], [1112, 1425], [1112, 1484], [164, 1484]], 'The symbol (period) represents a character position into which the character period will be inserted during editing: The symbol is counted in the size ofthe item;']
[[[130, 1552], [172, 1552], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '448']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']
[[[126, 1621], [1146, 1621], [1146, 1650], [126, 1650]], 'Licensed to Brent Rector: ANSI order X_952804. Downloaded 9/28/2023. Single user license only. Copying and networking prohibited.']


--- Page 479 ---

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[200, 187], [1016, 187], [1016, 219], [200, 219]], 'In addition, the symbol indicates the decimal point position for alignment purposes']
[[[200, 240], [1146, 240], [1146, 320], [200, 320]], 'CR DB These symbols represent the character position(s) into which the editing sign control character(s) is (are) placed during editing: Each character used in the symbol is counted in the size of the item:']
[[[195, 344], [1148, 344], [1148, 431], [195, 431]], 'Each symbol represents a leading numeric position that during editing will contain a numeric character in the range 0 through 9,0r a character asterisk when the content of that position is a leading zero. Each symbol is counted in the size ofthe item']
[[[198, 451], [1146, 451], [1146, 535], [198, 535]], 'CS A currency symbol represents character positions into which the currency string will be placed during editing: A currency symbol is represented in character-string-1 either by the currency sign Or by the currency symbol specified in a CURRENCY SIGN clause in the SPECIAL-NAMES paragraph_']
[[[198, 557], [1146, 557], [1146, 636], [198, 636]], 'The first occurrence of the currency symbol adds the number of characters in the currency string to the size of the item. Each subsequent occurrence of the currency symbol adds one to the size of the item:']
[[[199, 661], [1147, 661], [1147, 852], [199, 852]], 'es Character-1 in character-string-1 represents a character position into which the associated literal-1, literal-2, or literal-3 is to be placed: If character-1 is a simple insertion symbol or a fixed insertion symbol, the size of literal-1 is counted in the size ofthe item. For fixed editing sign control symbols, each character used in the symbol is counted in the size of the item: For extended editing sign control symbols with fixed insertion, each occurrence of the character(s) specified in the associated literal are counted in the size of the item. For floating inserting one occurrence ofliteral- 2 or literal-3 is counted in the size ofthe item plus one character for each repetition of character-1.']
[[[198, 870], [1146, 870], [1146, 925], [198, 925]], 'NOTE 2 If reference modification is used to reference part of a data item described with floating extended editing sign control symbols, care is needed to account for the repetitions.']
[[[164, 944], [1146, 944], [1146, 1003], [164, 1003]], '15) The PICTURE clause takes effect during the format validation stage of the execution ofa VALIDATE statement that refers directly or indirectly to the subject ofthe entry.']
[[[200, 1024], [1146, 1024], [1146, 1108], [200, 1108]], 'If the usage of the subject of the entry is not display or national, character-string-1 is used in combination with the USAGE clause to check; where applicable, that the contents are compatible with character-string-1. The rules for compatibility are implementor-defined_']
[[[199, 1127], [1149, 1127], [1149, 1214], [199, 1214]], 'If the usage of the subject of the entry is binary, computational or packed-decimal, and the range of values permitted by character-string-1 is less than that used by the hardware for the representation of the item, the unused portion of the hardware allocation is checked to contain only the value zero']
[[[200, 1234], [1146, 1234], [1146, 1288], [200, 1288]], 'If the usage is display or national, the effects of each symbol in character-string-1 are explained as follows:']
[[[199, 1314], [1148, 1314], [1148, 1400], [199, 1400]], "Each symbol 'A' represents a character position that will be checked to contain an alphabetic character: The classification of characters as alphabetic is determined in accordance with the rules for the ALPHABETIC class condition as specified in 8.8.4.4,Simple class condition:"]
[[[201, 1419], [984, 1419], [984, 1451], [201, 1451]], "B Each symbol 'B' represents a character position that will be checked to contain"]
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']
[[[1105, 1553], [1145, 1553], [1145, 1573], [1105, 1573]], '449']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[228, 186], [1112, 186], [1112, 299], [228, 299]], "the character space if the symbol 'B' is neither part of floating insertion editing nor of zero suppression with replacement editing; otherwise, the character space Or, if no significant numeric character appears to its left; the corresponding floating insertion character or replacement character respectively:"]
[[[198, 319], [1100, 319], [1100, 351], [198, 351]], "The symbol 'E' represents a character position that will be checked to contain the character 'E'."]
[[[164, 371], [1110, 371], [1110, 429], [164, 429]], "N Each symbol 'N' represents a character position that will be checked to contain a character from the computer's national character set"]
[[[192, 451], [1108, 451], [1108, 483], [192, 483]], "The symbol 'S' specifies that the data item will be checked for the presence ofan operational sign_"]
[[[228, 503], [1114, 503], [1114, 742], [228, 742]], 'If the subject of the entry is not specified with a SIGN clause, the position and the mode of representation of this operational sign are checked according to the specifications of the implementor; If the subject of the entry is specified with a SIGN clause without the SEPARATE phrase, then either the first or the last digit position and the mode ofrepresentation ofthe operational sign are checked according to the specifications of the implementor; Ifthe subject of the entry is specified with a SIGN clause with the SEPARATE phrase, then the corresponding position of the data item is checked to contain the character or the character']
[[[198, 767], [952, 767], [952, 798], [198, 798]], "Each symbol 'Z' represents a character position that will be checked to contain:"]
[[[228, 819], [1112, 819], [1112, 956], [228, 956]], "the space character; if the symbol 'Z' is specified to the left of the decimal point position and the content ofthe character position is a leading zero, or the space character, if the symbol is specified at the rightmost digit position after the decimal point position and the content of the data item is zero, or a numeric character in the range 0 through 9 otherwise"]
[[[198, 977], [1010, 977], [1010, 1009], [198, 1009]], "Each symbol '0' (zero) represents a character position that will be checked to contain"]
[[[229, 1029], [1112, 1029], [1112, 1140], [229, 1140]], "the character zero if the symbol '0' is neither part of floating insertion editing nor of zero suppression with replacement editing; otherwise, the character zero Or, if no significant numeric character appears to its left; the corresponding floating insertion character or replacement character respectively_"]
[[[196, 1163], [1108, 1163], [1108, 1193], [196, 1193]], "Each symbol '1' represents a boolean position that will be checked to contain a boolean character_"]
[[[166, 1216], [1114, 1216], [1114, 1273], [166, 1273]], "Each symbol 9' represents a character position that will be checked to contain a numeric character in the range 0 through 9 with the possible inclusion ofan operational sign:"]
[[[196, 1293], [1012, 1293], [1012, 1325], [196, 1325]], "Each symbol /' (slant) represents a character position that will be checked to contain"]
[[[229, 1345], [1112, 1345], [1112, 1456], [229, 1456]], "the character slant if the symbol 1' is neither part of floa ating insertion editing nor of zero suppression with replacement editing; otherwise, the character slant Or, if no significant numeric character appears to its left; the corresponding floating insertion character or replacement character respectively:"]

[[[224, 187], [1058, 187], [1058, 219], [224, 219]], 'Each symbol (comma) represents a character position that will be checked to contain']
[[[263, 240], [1147, 240], [1147, 351], [263, 351]], 'the character comma if the symbol is neither part of floating insertion editing nor of zero suppression with replacement editing; otherwise, the character comma or,if no significant numeric character appears to its left; the corresponding floating insertion character or replacement character respectively:']
[[[200, 371], [1146, 371], [1146, 428], [200, 428]], 'The symbol (period) represents a character position that will be checked t0 contain the character period.']
[[[199, 451], [1146, 451], [1146, 508], [199, 508]], 'CR DB These symbols represent character positions that will be checked to contain a valid sign, according to the following:']
[[[262, 529], [1146, 529], [1146, 747], [262, 747]], "the character position corresponding to the symbol or when such a symbol is used as a fixed insertion symbol, will be checked to contain a valid sign character as defined in Table 8, Results of fixed insertion editing; the character positions corresponding to the symbols +'or when such symbols are used as floating insertion symbols, will all be checked to contain valid characters, including a valid sign character, as specified in 13.18.40.5, Editing rules, rule 6; the character positions corresponding to the symbols 'CR' and 'DB' will be checked to contain valid sign characters as defined in Table 8, Results of fixed insertion editing:"]
[[[224, 767], [976, 767], [976, 800], [224, 800]], 'Each symbol represents a character position that will be checked to contain:']
[[[264, 819], [1146, 819], [1146, 956], [264, 956]], "the character asterisk, if the symbol is specified to the left of the decimal point position and the content ofthe character position is a leading zero, or the character asterisk, if the symbol '* is specified at the rightmost digit position after the decimal point position and the content of the data item is zero, or a numeric character in the range 0 through 9 otherwise"]
[[[203, 977], [1106, 977], [1106, 1009], [203, 1009]], 'CS The character position(s) corresponding to the currency symbol will be checked as follows:']
[[[263, 1030], [1146, 1030], [1146, 1088], [263, 1088]], 'when used asa fixed insertion symbol, the character position(s) will be checked to contain the currency string']
[[[264, 1108], [1146, 1108], [1146, 1190], [264, 1190]], 'when used as floating insertion symbols, the character position(s) will be checked to contain valid characters, including a valid currency string, as specified in 13.18.40.5, Editing rules, rule 6.']
[[[200, 1214], [1147, 1214], [1147, 1323], [200, 1323]], 'es Ifthe FOR clause is not specified,the character position (s) correspondingto character-1 shall be checked to contain literal-1. [[[202, 1348], [714, 1348], [714, 1376], [202, 1376]], 'All other symbols have no effect on format validation.']

[[[130, 186], [838, 186], [838, 219], [130, 219]], '17) The number of character positions in the item is specified by integer-1_']
[[[130, 241], [832, 241], [832, 272], [130, 272]], '18) The meaning of the symbols used in character-string-1 are as follows:']
[[[198, 292], [1112, 292], [1112, 376], [198, 376]], "Each symbol 'Z' represents a leading numeric position that during editing will contain a numeric character in the range 0 through 9, 0r a character space when the content of that position is a leading zero"]
[[[202, 397], [1112, 397], [1112, 456], [202, 456]], "Each symbol '9' represents a digit position that shall contain a numeric character in the range 0 through 9."]
[[[202, 477], [1110, 477], [1110, 535], [202, 535]], 'The symbol (period) represents a character position into which the decimal separator taken from the locale will be inserted during editing:']
[[[166, 557], [982, 557], [982, 587], [166, 587]], 'In addition, the symbol indicates the decimal point position for alignment purposes:']
[[[200, 610], [1112, 610], [1112, 667], [200, 667]], "The symbol indicates that the item is to be signed in accordance with the specifications in the locale: If the symbol +'is not specified,the item will be unsigned."]
[[[167, 687], [1110, 687], [1110, 744], [167, 744]], 'CS The currency symbol indicates that the item is to include a currency string in accordance with the specifications in the locale']
[[[130, 766], [1112, 766], [1112, 850], [130, 850]], '19) The PICTURE clause takes effect during the format validation stage ofthe execution ofa VALIDATE statement that refers directly or indirectly to the subject ofthe entry: The validation is carried out in accordance with the character classification and monetary specification in the locale']
[[[130, 869], [419, 869], [419, 908], [130, 908]], '13.18.40.5 Editing rules']
[[[128, 928], [236, 928], [236, 952], [128, 952]], 'FORMAT 1']
[[[130, 975], [1083, 975], [1083, 1011], [130, 1011]], '1) There are two methods of performing editing: either insertion or suppression with replacement']
[[[165, 1028], [560, 1028], [560, 1064], [165, 1064]], 'There are four types of insertion editing:']
[[[200, 1086], [372, 1086], [372, 1194], [200, 1194]], 'simple insertion special insertion fixed insertion floating insertion']
[[[166, 1217], [686, 1217], [686, 1247], [166, 1247]], 'There are two types of suppression with replacement:']
[[[203, 1270], [682, 1270], [682, 1325], [203, 1325]], 'zero suppression with replacement with spaces zero suppression with replacement with asterisks']
[[[130, 1550], [172, 1550], [172, 1576], [130, 1576]], '452']
[[[204, 1549], [356, 1549], [356, 1578], [204, 1578]], '@ISO/IEC 2023']

[[[886, 80], [1146, 80], [1146, 112], [886, 112]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[164, 186], [1146, 186], [1146, 746], [164, 746]], '2) The type of editing that may be performed upon an item is dependent upon the category to which the item belongs: Table Category and type of editing, specifies which type of editing may be performed upon a given category: Table 7 Category and type of editing Category Type of editing Alphabetic None Alphanumeric None Boolean None National None Numeric None Alphanumeric-edited Simple insertion National-edited Simple insertion Numeric-edited (fixed-point edited result) AIL Numeric-edited (floating-point edited result) Simple insertion, special insertion, and fixed insertion for the significand part None for the exponent part']
[[[164, 774], [435, 774], [435, 806], [164, 806]], '3) Simple insertion editing']
[[[200, 827], [1146, 827], [1146, 886], [200, 886]], "Character-1 and the symbols 'B', '0', '/', '' and, if literal-1 is specified, character-1 are used as the simple insertion editing symbols"]
[[[200, 906], [1146, 906], [1146, 965], [200, 965]], 'Simple insertion editing results in the insertion character occupying the same character position in the edited item as the associated symbol occupies in character-string-1.']
[[[164, 984], [437, 984], [437, 1016], [164, 1016]], '4) Special insertion editing']
[[[200, 1037], [778, 1037], [778, 1070], [200, 1070]], 'The symbol is used as the special insertion editing symbol.']
[[[200, 1090], [1146, 1090], [1146, 1149], [200, 1149]], 'Special insertion editing results in the period character occupying the same character position in the edited item as the symbol occupies in character-string-1']
[[[164, 1167], [423, 1167], [423, 1203], [164, 1203]], '5) Fixed insertion editing']
[[[200, 1222], [874, 1222], [874, 1281], [200, 1281]], 'Character-1,the currency symbol and the editing sign control symbols as the fixed insertion editing symbols.']
[[[938, 1226], [1144, 1226], [1144, 1250], [938, 1250]], "CR' and 'DB' are used"]
[[[200, 1301], [1146, 1301], [1146, 1359], [200, 1359]], 'Fixed insertion editing results in the insertion character(s) occupyingthe same character position(s) in the edited item as the associated symbol occupies in character-string-1']
[[[200, 1383], [1146, 1383], [1146, 1436], [200, 1436]], 'When character-1 is used,and is not a simple insertion character; it represents literal-2,0r literal-3 as the insertion characters:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']

[[[164, 780], [1113, 780], [1113, 839], [164, 839]], 'The currency symbol, the extended editing sign control symbols, if specified, and the fixed editing sign control symbols and are used as the floating insertion symbols:']
[[[164, 857], [1113, 857], [1113, 945], [164, 945]], 'Floating insertion editing is indicated by specifying a string ofatleast two identical floating insertion editing symbols Any of the simple insertion editing symbols embedded in this string or to the immediate right ofthis string are part ofthe string:']
[[[164, 964], [1112, 964], [1112, 1050], [164, 1050]], 'The leftmost symbol of the insertion string represents the leftmost limit ofthe floating characters in the data item_ The rightmost symbol of the insertion string represents the rightmost limit of the floating characters in the data item']
[[[164, 1070], [1112, 1070], [1112, 1154], [164, 1154]], 'The second floating symbol represents the leftmost limit of the numeric data that may be stored in the item. During editing, nonzero numeric characters may replace all the insertion symbols at or to the right ofthis limit:']
[[[164, 1178], [1112, 1178], [1112, 1234], [164, 1234]], 'If truncation occurs, the value of the data that is used for editing is the value after truncation as specified in 14.6.8,Alignment and transfer of data into data items:']

[[[166, 1353], [756, 1353], [756, 1384], [166, 1384]], 'There are two ways of representing floating insertion editing:']

[[[166, 923], [618, 923], [618, 959], [166, 959]], '7) Zero suppression with replacement editing']
[[[200, 976], [1147, 976], [1147, 1058], [200, 1058]], "The symbols 'Z' and are used as the symbols for zero suppression with replacement Ifthe symbol 'Z' is used,the replacement character is the character space; ifthe symbol is used,the replacement character is the character asterisk"]
[[[199, 1081], [1146, 1081], [1146, 1166], [199, 1166]], 'Zero suppression with replacement is indicated by specifying a string of one or more identical zero- suppression characters_ Any of the simple insertion editing symbols embedded in this string or to the immediate right of this string are part of the string:']
[[[200, 1189], [892, 1189], [892, 1220], [200, 1220]], 'There are two ways of representing zero suppression with replacement:']
[[[200, 1240], [1146, 1240], [1146, 1353], [200, 1353]], 'a) One way is to represent any or all of the leading numeric character positions to the left of the decimal point position by the zero-suppression symbol. The result is that the corresponding replacement character is placed into any character position immediately preceding whichever of the following is encountered first:']
[[[264, 1376], [1099, 1376], [1099, 1456], [264, 1456]], 'the first nonzero numeric character in the item the first character position for which no zero suppression with replacement is specified the decimal point position:']
[[[878, 1552], [1030, 1552], [1030, 1578], [878, 1578]], '@ISO /IEC 2023']

[[[127, 77], [391, 77], [391, 113], [127, 113]], 'ISO /IEC 1989.2023 (E)']
[[[166, 187], [1112, 187], [1112, 377], [166, 377]], "b) The second way is to represent all of the numeric character positions by the zero-suppression symbol: The result depends upon the value of the data to be stored: If the value is not zero, the result is the sameas ifthe zero suppression with replacement were defined only to theleft ofthe decimal point position. If the value is zero and the zero-suppression symbol is the symbol 'Z' ,all character positions of the item will contain the character space. Ifthe value is zero and the zero- suppression symbol isthe symbol '* all character positions ofthe item will contain the character asterisk, but the decimal separator; when specified, will appear in the item:"]
[[[130, 397], [559, 397], [559, 431], [130, 431]], '8) Zero value of a floating-point edited item']
[[[164, 449], [1114, 449], [1114, 535], [164, 535]], 'If the value to be edited into a floating-_ point edited item is zero, then after editing all digit positions of the significand and all digit positions ofthe exponent shall be zero; the sign of the significand, if present, shall be positive; and the sign of the exponent shall be positive']
